BOOTH, LEONARD WM.—Writer, Ceylon service, 1878; pol. mag., Avisawella, 1880; office ast. to govt. agt., Uva, 1886; ditto, Mannar, 1891; pol. mag., Kandy, 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Oct., 1895; resumed duties as forest stlttn. offr., Dec., 1895; ag. astt. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar. to Oct., 1896; special offr. under Ordinance No. 1 of 1897; govt. agt. Sabaragamuna, June, 1899; govt. agt. N.C. Province, Feb., 1900.

BOOTH, ROBT. MALCOLM.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov., 1883; attached to col. sec. office, Feb., 1884; ditto stip. mag. office, Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua, Nov., 1884; att.-gen.'s office, June, 1885, to Oct., 1888; passed cadet, 1887; stip. mag., Tai Levu, 1888; ditto, Loma Viti, 1893; ditto, Savu Savu and Bua, 1893; confirmed as stip. mag. and comm. of sup. ct., 1894; called to the bar Mid. Tem., 1899; stip. mag., sub-coll. customs and tax inspr., Lautoka, 1901.

BOOTHSBY, JOSHUA, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1837; clk. col. sec.'s office, S. Australia, 1853; clk. in audit office, 1854; ch. clk., 1856; ch. clk. in ch. sec.'s office, 1859; also govt. statist. and supt. of census, 1860; asst. sec. and govt. statist. S. Australia, 1866; and under-sec. and govt. statist. 1868 to 1880; elected correspnd. mem. of the statistical soc., Lond., 1869; trustee, savings bks., S. Australia, 1869; a commr. for international exhibs., 1872; joint edit. of a work "S. Australia: its History, Resources, and Productions," pub. by authority of govt. 1876; exec. commr. representing S. Australia at the Paris univl. exhib. of 1878; elected hon. mem. of roy. geogr. soc. of Berlin and Vienna, 1878.

BORDEN, THE HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902), B.A., M.D.—B. 1847; grad. of Harvard Med. Sch.; has represented King's in the House of Commons since 1874, with the exception of one term, being defeated at the gen. election of 1882; min. of Militia and Defence in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.
BORN, EDWARD TURNER, M.B.—Asst. col. surgeon, St. Lucia, 1901; ditto, Falkland Is., 1903; col. surg., Falklands, 1904.
BOSMAN, JOHANNES JACOBUS.—Examiner of diagrams, surv.-gen.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1892; examiner of candidates for practical land surveying; geodetic offr., Jan., 1903.

BOSTON, REV. NATHANIEL HENRY.—Ed. C.M.S. Gram. Schl., Freetown, and Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone; Durham Univ., L.H., 1878; B.A., 1890; deacon, 1889; priest, 1891; sec. of S. Leon. C.M.S., 1880-1887; missionary and supt., Buller mission, 1880-1889; delegate of S. Leone natv. church to England, 1885; J.P., S. Leone, 1890; editor of "Missionary Friend Magazine," 1876. 1861; supt. of Lecce church and dist., 1898-1899; minister of St. Paul's church, Tasso Is., Jan.-Apr., 1900; priest in charge, St. Henry's parish, York, S. Leone, Apl. to Sep., 1900; asst. col. chaplain and regisr. of deaths, Cape Coast, 1st Sept., 1900; ag. col. chaplain 23rd Sept., 1900, to 7th Apl., 1901; also from 16th May, 1902.
BOTTOMLEY, W. C.—Ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; aschl., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., 22nd Oct., 1901.

BOUCAUT, THE HON. SIR JAMES PENN, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1831; entered parlmt., S. Australia, 1862. Held office in several ministries as atty.-gen., Oct., 1865, to Mar., 1866, and thence to May, 1867; as atty.-gen. and premier, and again as atty.-gen. in 1872; as premier and comsrr. of crown lands and pub. wks. from June, 1875, to June, 1876; as premier and treas., from 26th Oct., 1877, to 25th Sept., 1878, when he accepted a seat on the bench of the sup. ct.; ret. from bench, 1905.
BOULTON, J. F., Assoc. M.I.C.E. (1884).—Exec. engineer, p.w. dept., H. Kong, Apl., 1889; ag. asst. dir. p.w., July, 1902, to May, 1903, Apl., 1904, to Mar., 1905, and July to Sept., 1905.

BOURKE, SOUTHWELL G. J.—Ent. Royal Navy as cadet, 1864; served in "Aurora," Canada, during fenian raid (medal); in "Satellite" during Abyssinian campaign (medal); in "Orontes" during Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal, Khedive's star); called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1887; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1897; interpr. in French (1st class, Navy), ag. pol. mag., Georgetown, Oct., 1900.

BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, M.A. (Balliol Coll., Oxford).—Asst. sec., charity organization soc., 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1884; registr.-gen. and examr. of titles, Trinidad, 1893; acted as judge of sup. ct., 1896 and 1898; audr.-gen. Trinidad and Tobago, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1899, 1900, 1901, and 1902; admstd. govt., 10th to 13th Nov., 1901; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Oct., 1902; col. sec., Jamaica, June, 1904; admstd. govt., Sept., 1904.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYN, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2ndclass honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and escheator-gen., of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comsrr. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst. C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902.

BOVELL, JOHN R., F.L.S., F.C.S.—Parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, Apr., 1882; supt. reformatory and industrial schl., Apl., 1888; mem. local comtee. (Col. and Ind. exhib., 1888; supt. Botan. Stan., 1886; went to Antigua 1890 to start Skerrett's training schl.; vice-pres., Windward dist. agric. soc., 1890; mem. of comsrr. to inquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman emigrn. comtee., Apr., 1895.

BOVILL, ALFRED KARSLAKE.—Clk. of wks., pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1882-5; asst. to dir. of survey and principal forest officer, 1886-1896; inspr. of agric. industries, 1892-96; prin. forest offr., 1895.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G. (1835).—B. 1823; emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy coun., 19th Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of customs; min. of militia and defence, 1892; min. of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-col. trade, 1893; pres. of col. confoe. at Ottawa, 1894; prem. of Canada and vice-president of council, 1894-96.

BOWEN, ALFRED SYDNEY.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Newton Coll., S. Devon; 2nd clk. crown lands office, July, 1879; 4th clk. rec. gen.'s office, July, 1881; branch savings bk. clk., Jan., 1883; ch. clk., surg.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1885; ch. clk., G.P.O., May, 1885; warden St. Ann's and Diego Martin, Dec., 1889; J.P. 1890; ag. priv. sec. to Sir F. Broome, Sept., 1896; inspr. of prisons, 1903.

BOWEN, ERNEST F. S., F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk. audit office from Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1889; clk. post office from May, 1889, to Sep., 1890; ch. clk. and draughtsman, pub. wks. office from Oct., 1890, to Jan., 1895; supt. pub. wks., Barbados, Feb., 1896.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. commodr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high comsrr. for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high comsrr., 1884-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; admstd. govt., July, 1900, to 1901, and Nov., 1903, to Aug., 1904.

BOWHILL, J. O.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; asst. collr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Apl., 1892; collr., 1892; 1st cls. asst., July, 1898; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, 1902; ag. consul, Beira, 1903; ag. vice-consul, Tete, 1904.

BOWLEY, FRANCIS BULMER LYON—Sollr. of sup. ct., England, 1890; sollr. of sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1893; notary public, 1895; sec., librn. and curator of City Hall, 1899; ag. Crown sollr., May, 1899; Crown sollr. and Queen's Proctor, 1900.
BOWRING, WALTER ANDREW.—Ed. at Eton; asst. auditor, East Africa Prot., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; local auditor, ditto, 31st May, 1905.

BOYCE, ERNEST PEDDER.—Supernumerary clk., G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial clk. col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1879; sec. to comsrr. to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; ch. clk., pub. library, 1880; ag. librn., Sept., 1882; asst. clk., petty debt ct. of St. Michael's, Nov., 1882; ag. ch. clk., May, 1883, to June, 1884; inland rev. offr., 1887.

BOYD, A. D.—Teacher, preparatory dept., Dominica Grammar Sch., Sept., 1894; junr. govt. off. treas., Jan., 1895; clk. govt. off., Portsmouth dist., Jan., 1896; govt. off. treas., Roseau, Sept., 1896; quar. off., Oct., 1897.
BOYD, A. H.—Entered C.O. after exam., Apr., 1887; asst. clk., June, 1892; supplementary clk. in registry, Mar., 1900.

BOYLE, ALEXANDER GEORGE.—Ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puianae judge, S. Stiltns., 1893; resig., 1895; asst. treas., Uganda Prot., 18th Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comsrr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apl., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admn., May, 1901, to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sep., 1895, 1st April, 1902; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1902.

BOYLE, Sir CAVENDISH, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1849; ed. at Charterhouse; dist. mag., Leeward Is., Dec., 1879; col. sec. of Bermuda and regisr.-gen., 1882; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1888; govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1894; admstd. govt. at various times, 1894-1900; was also chairman of the Br. Guiana bk., Apr., 1897, to Nov., 1898; govnr. of Newfoundland, Jan., 1901; gov., Mauritius, 15th Apr., 1904.
BOYSE, V. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902.

BRABANT, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD YEWD, K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1838; Mem. of the legis. assem. for E. London, Cape of Good Hope; retired from Cape mounted rifles, as captain, 1870; field comdt. of col. forces, 1878; col., Cape yeomanry, 1879; in command of local forces in the S. African war, 1899-1901; ret. 1904; M.L.A. for East London, 1904.

BRACE, GEORGE KNIGHT KIRKWOOD.—Dispenser of medicines and gen. asst., N. P. asylum, Bahamas, 1885 to 1890; clk., registr. of records off., 1890 to 1896; 3rd clk., recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., 31st Dec., 1896; ag. registr. of records, 26th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd clk. and book-keeper, recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., Nov., 1903, to March, 1904, and from 5th May, 1904.

BRACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sept., 1882; clk. pol. office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1887; clk. to bd. of pub. wks., New Providence, Mar., 1900; ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Mar., 1904.
BRADFORD, M. G.—Asst., P.W.D., Sarawak, May, 1895; supt., July, 1901; ag. comntr., May, 1902.
BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, L.R.C.P. and S., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., Seychelles, 21st Aug., 1901; J.P., and chairman, local bd. of health, South Mahé.

BRADY, ALFRED BARTON, M.I.C.E.—Pupil and afterwards ch. draughtsman, ch. engrnr.'s dept., L. and Y. Rly., Manchester, Jan., 1872; asst. to G. W. Stevenson, M.I.C.E., 1879; survr. Docking Union and Hunstanton urban sanitary authority, Mar., 1881; engrnr. and survr., Maldon sanitary dist., Essex, Oct., 1882; asst. engrnr., rly. dept., Brisbane, Qnsld., Jan., 1885; asst. engrnr. of bridges (rlys.), July, 1887; engrnr. for bridges (main roads), P.W.D., June, 1889; govt. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Sept., 1891; under-secretary for public works, govt. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Feb., 1901.

BRAITHWAITE, H. W.—Petroleum locker and asst. excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; ch. clk. sub-treasury, 1874; supt. registr. of births, 1878; J.P.; elected mun. coun. 1888; now ch. clk. med. dept.; sec. quarantine, and sec. bd. of health.

BRAMSTON, SIR JOHN, G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1897), D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—B. 1832; graduated B.A. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls, 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as priv. sec. to Sir George Bowen; resig., 1861; was mem. of the legis. coun. of Queensland, 1863 to 1869, and of the exec. coun. 1863 to 1866. While in England in 1867, was asst. boundary commr. for Devon and Cornwall under the Reform Act of that year.

BRANCH, CHRISTIAN WM.—Ed. at St. Kitt's high school and Antigua gram. school, Edin. Univ., M.B., C.M., 1893; Leeward Is. med. offr. in charge, Virgin Is., 1894; M.L.C., Virgin Is., 1894; ag. comntr., V.I., 1894; med. offr. dist. 4, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1895; mem. bd. of health, St. Kitts, 1895; ag. supt. Cunn. Hosp., ag. surg., gaol, and ag. analyst, vital statistics, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1896; ag. med. offr., dists. 7 and 8, 1897; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis volrs., 1897; ag. asst. col. surg., St. Lucia, 1899; pub. phys., Inagua, Bahamas, 1900; ag. res. just., Inagua, 1901; med. offr., Kingston dist., med. offr., col. hosp. and supr., paper asylum, St. Vincent, 1902; also ag. med. offr., div. I., surg., gaol, and health offr., 1902; J.P., 1902; ag. mem., legis. and exec. couns., 1903; mem. bd. of educn., 1904; del. to W. I. Quarantine Confe., 1904.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.B.Ch.B., Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. supttd. Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis naval def. force, 1904; capt., 1905.

BRANCH, E. ST. J., B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1896; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1896; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen. Leeward Is., 12 Sept., 1902; ag. col. sec., 10 Oct., 1902.

BRANCH, E. W. R., M.B., C.M. (Edin.), B.A. (Durham).—Med. offr., Virgin Is., Oct., 1889; acted as comntr., Virgin Is.; J.P. and M.L.C., Virgin Is.; med. offr., Montserrat, 1890; M.L.C., Montserrat; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1894; dist. med. offr., Antigua, Jan., 1905.
BRANDON, THE VERY REV'D DEAN, M.A.—Colonial chaplain, Falkland Is., 1877.

BRASSEY, LOUI, 1st baron (created 1886); Sir THOMAS BRASSEY, K.C.B., D.C.L. (Hon. and M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1836; called to the bar Linc Inn, 1866; M.P. for Devonport, 1865, and for Hastings, 1868 to 1886; lieut. comdg. rov. naval art. volrs., 1873; hon. comdr., 1880; col. 2nd Cinque Ports art. volrs., 1891; dep.-lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; was civil lord of the admty., 1880 to 1884 and sec. to the admty., 1884-5; pres. of the statistical soc., 1879-80; comdr. of the legion of honour, 1889; chairman of the roy. comm. on opium traffic, 1894-5; lord-in-waiting, 1893-5; gov. of Victoria, 1895-1900.

BRATT, J. HICKS DAVSON.—5th cl. clk., and dept., British Guiana, 1889; 4th, 1892; 3rd, 1896; 2nd, 1897; sec. to comttee of inquiry into traveller expenses, 1893; sec. to comttee. on pension- 1898; asst. loc. auditor, N. Nigeria, Feb., 1901; loc. auditor, Sept., 1901; ch. asst. treas., Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Feb., 1903.

BREARLEY, L. J.—Passed Lond. matric. and Oxford sen. local exams., June, 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1900; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1900.
BRETT, F. W.—Ent. service of United African Co. (afterwards Roy. Niger Co.), Mar., 1882; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, 1883 to 1899; transferred to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.
BREW, SAM. H.—Ed. Wesleyan high schl., Cape Coast; clerical asst., gov. office, G. Coast, Apr., 1887; jun. clk., Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; now 2nd clk.

BREWIN, ARTHUR WINBOLD.—Ed. at Winchester; cadet, Hong Kong, 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. asst. regiar.-gen., May, 1891, to Mar., 1894, and again 1895; J.P., 1894; insp. of schools, 1897; regiar.-gen., 1901; M.L.C., 1903.

BREWSTER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. at Brewood and Chelt.; asst. dist. offr., Matang, Perak, 1878; asst. supt., Lower Perak, 1878; asst. mag., Krian, 1879; ditto, Salama, 1880; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 1883-4; ditto, Larut, 1885; ditto, Knita, 1888; dist. offr., Krian, 1889; mag., Krian, 1889; state comnr. of lands, and regiar. of mines, Jan., 1892; ag. state auditor, Apr., 1893; dist. mag., Lower Perak, Aug., 1893.

BRIDGES, W. F.—Sub admnstr.-gen., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1885; ag. admnstr.-gen., Feb., 1886, to Jan., 1887; comnr. to inquire into road arrears under rd. ordinance, May, 1889; and into land titles, Oct., 1890; admnstr.-gen., Br. G., Apl., 1899.
BRIDGMAN, ALFRED H.—Entered C.O. after exam., Oct., 1880; apptd. asst. clk., May, 1892; shorthand writer to C.O., Sept., 1896-1900; asst. supt. of printing, Mar., 1900.

BRIGHT, CHARLES E., C.M.G. (1883).—B.1829; a leading merchant in Victoria; has been chairman of the harbour trust and comnr. for the Melbourne exhibn.; has also been comnr. for Victoria at exhibns. in Europe.

BRINTON, ALFRED JOHN—Ck., col. sec.'s off., Cape Colony, May, 1884; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1885; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1892; prin. clk., statistical branch, July, 1897; ag. regiar.-gen. of statistics, July to Dec., 1904, and Apl. to June, 1905; regiar.-gen. of statistics, July, 1905.

BRISBANE, 3RD BISHOP (Australia), RT. REV. WILLIAM THOMAS THORNHILL WEBBER, D.D.—Consec. 1885; Pemb. Coll., Oxon, M.A., 1862; curate of Chiswick, 1860-4; vicar of St. John the Evang., Holborn, 1864-85; mem. Lon. schl. bd., 1882-5.
BRISTOW, CHAS. EDWIN.—Govt. printer, S. Australia, Aug., 1890.

BRISTOWE, LEONARD SYER, M.A. (Oxon.).—Called to the bar, In. Temp., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1903; joint author of "Law of Charities and Mortmain"; author of "The Mortmain and Charitable Uses Act, 1891," and of "The Hospital Legal Handbook"; joint editor of 2nd edtn. of "MacSwiney on Mines."

BRISTOWE, LINDSAY W.—Ck. survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-4; sec. crown lands bd., 1884; qualified land survr., clk., regiar.'s record office, clk. of the petty debt ct., July, 1884; clk. of coun., 1885; regiar. V.A. court, 1886; provost-marshal, 1887; regiar. and keeper of records, 1887-1890; offi. admnstr., 1890; dist. comnr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and comnr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. comnr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treasury, Apl., 1894; ag. ch. asst. treas., Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; dist. comnr., Accra, 1894; Elmina, 1896-96; chief regist. and sheriff, 1897; dist. comnr., Chama, Sekondi, 1898; Salt Pond, 1899; Winnebah, 1900; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1901; 2nd ditto, 1902; 1st ditto, 1903; ag. ch. asst., 9th Feb. to 3rd Mar., 15th July to 2nd Dec., 1904, and 20th June to 12th Nov., 1905; clk. ex. and leg. couns., Mar., 1904.

BRITTEN, HENRY.—Apptd. probationer, Dec., 1895; 3rd cl. clk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; clk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apl., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith.
BRITTER, CHAS. ALFRED.—Master, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 15th Nov., 1876; sec., ditto, 17th Feb., 1893; sec., college comtees., 12th May, 1896; inspr. and sec. to comtees., 13th Oct., 1903.

BROADRICK, EDWD. GEO.—Ed. at Sherborne schl.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; cadet S.S., 1887; dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of imgrts., May, 1896; 2nd mag. in sole charge of dept., Nov., 1897; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1897; ag. collr. land rev.; offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Sept., 1898; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Dec., 1898, to Nov., 1900; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Dec., 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Feb., 1902; capt. Singapore volr. rifles, July, 1900; p.s. cert., schl. of instruction, Chelsea barracks, July, 1901; major and ag. commt. Singapore volr. corps, May, 1902; sen. dist. offr., Province Wellesley, Aug., 1902; mag. for, and visited Coco Is., Nov., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., May to Aug., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. commt., Singapore vol. corps, 6th Nov., 1903; pres., mun. comnrs., 1st June, 1904.

BROCKMAN, EDWD. LEWIS.—Cadet, S. Shitlms, 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. col. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1895; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Aug., 1897; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; comnr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sep., 1903; ag. col. sec., Apl., 1905.
BROCKMAN, FREDERICK SLADE.—Survr., lands and survey dept., W. Aust., 1884; survr., P.W. dept., 1887; survr., lands and survey dept., 1890; inspecting survr., ditto, 1891; chief ditto, ditto, 1894.

BRODHURST, HENRY WM. FRED. COTTINGHAM.—Ed. at Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., Matale, 1880; asst. to gov't. agt., N.W. Provs., 1885; ditto, Kegalla, 1891; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, 1891; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1896; Matare, Mar., 1899; asst. gov't. agt., Putalam, Dec., 1899; ag. gov't. agt. and fiscal, S. Prov., Feb., 1902.
BRODIE, D. E.—Ed. at Winchester; entd. serv. of British South Africa Company, 1897; asst. sec., 1902; sec., Aug., 1906.
BRODIE, H. M.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur.; sworn ld. survr., Maur., Apr., 1900; asst. survr., Seychelles, May, 1901; town survr., 23rd Dec., 1901.

BROMLEY, SIR ROBERT, BART.—Apptd. hon. attache to the embassy at Washington, Sept. 23rd, 1897; resigned Feb., 1901; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, S. of S. for Colonies, Dec., 1901; admstr. and treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR.—Ag. clk., pol. office, Nassau, May to Oct., 1874; librn., Nassau pub. liby., reading room and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clk., pol. office, Oct., 1879; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. dept., May to Nov., 1881, and Oct., 1882; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Mar. to Apr., 1883; ch. clk. col. sec. dept., June, 1883; clk. bd. of pub. works and regir. of records, Sept., 1887; supt. Bahamas census, 1891; now regir. of records; is a J.P. and mem. of bd. of educn.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SIR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1829; Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKE, GILBERT E.—Ed. Pem. Coll., Cam., and Lond. Hosp.; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1901; L.R.C.S. and L.R.C.P. Edin.; L.E.P.S. Glas.; Fell. Roy. Inst. Pub. Health; Fell. Med. Soc., Lond., F.R.G.S.; late surg. Allan Line and Furness S.S. Co.; govt. med. offr. and med. offr. of health, Cockburn Harbour, Turks and Caicos Islds.; ag. G.M.O. and M.O.H., Turks Isld., Aug., 1899; J.P. for the col., June, 1899; ag. asst. comsrr., Cockburn Harbour, Dec., 1899-May, 1900; dist. comsrr., Caicos Is., June, 1901; health officer, Singapore, 31st Jan., 1902.

BROOKS, G. L.—Joined Lond. and Metropol. pol. force, 1885; inspr. Sierra Leone, Oct., 1894; supt., sheriff and provost-marshal, July, 1896; ag. pol. mag., coroner and judge of ct. of requests for Freetown, July to Dec., 1897.

BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clk. exec. coun. and sec. defence comtce., 1878; ch. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1879; advoc., sup. ct., 1882; sec. coun. of educn., 1885; R.M., Newcastle div., 1889; acted several times as asst. col. sec., 1886-88; J.P. for the col. (1889); mem. of coun. of educn., 1891; master and regir. sup. ct., 1889; offices of master and regir. separated 1897, retains that of master; mag. Durban, 1898; judge of spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as mag., Durban divn., 1902; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1904; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1904.
BROWN, SIR CHARLES GAGE M.D., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889), Hon. LL.D.—B. 1826; was for many years med. adviser to the Col. Office; retired 1897.
BROWN, H. Y. L.—Govt. geologist, S. Australia, Dec. 1882.
BROWN, JOHN FRANK.—Imperial service, 10th Sep., 1873; P.M.G., Natal, 14th Sep., 1900; lent to Transvaal govt., 1st Oct., 1902; P.M.G., Transvaal, 3rd April, 1903; M.L.C.

BROWN, JOHN JAMES.—Ed. Liverpool Collegiate Institn. and Royal Coll., Mauritius; volr., col. sec's. office, Mauritius, Apr., 1863; 2nd clk. to sen. stip. mag., Aug., 1863; clk. to the ch. judge, May, 1864; curator of vacant estates, Oct., 1874; govt. agent before the forest lands purchase comsn. in conjunction with his office of curator, Nov., 1881, till Dec., 1882, and alone from Dec., 1882, to Feb., 1884; is also marshal of the vice-admiralty ct. since Feb., 1873; asst. rec-gen., Dec., 1888; ag. collr. of customs, May to Nov., 1891; collr. of customs, Jan., 1895; rec-gen., June, 1897; rec-gen., Jan., 1897; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1901.

BROWN, JONATHAN RICHARD.—Prison off. B. Guiana, 1st Feb., 1878; promoted 1st cls. and served at penal settm., Massaruni, 18th Nov., 1884, to 30th Dec., 1887; ag. keeper, Fellowships pris., West Coast, Demerara, 3rd May to 3rd Nov., 1890; principal warden, penal settm., Massaruni, 19th June, 1894, confirmed; ag. chief ward. Georgetown pris., 4th July to 8th Dec., 1895, 23rd Dec., 1896, to 30th Mar., 1896, and 3rd Nov., 1896, to 12th July, 1898; ag. keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 9th to 21st Dec., 1895; ag. ch. warden and asst. sup't. at penal settm., Massaruni, 15th June to 14th Sep., 1897, and 12th Mar. to 16th June, 1900; keeper, Suddie pris., Essequibo, 1st Aug., 1900, to 31 Aug., 1903 (confirmed 19th ch. warden, Georgetown pris., June, 1903, confirmed, Mar., 1904); ag. sup't., Georgetown pris., 14th Nov., 1903, to 13th Apr., 1904, ditto, Dec., 1904, to Apr., 1905; ag. asst. sup't. and ch. warden (combined), Massaruni penal settm., May to Aug., 1905.

BROWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool Coll. Institn. and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st English scholarship); called to the bar, Mid. Term., June 1869; counsel to gov't., Seychelles, 1872 to 1874; practised before sup. ct., Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; mem. of comsn. on laws and rules affecting civil jurisdiction and procedure of dist. cts. in Mauritius, Apr., 1882; dist. judge, Seychelles, Nov., 1884; judge of Seychelles, and sen. mem. exec. and legal couns., 1889; adminstr. govt., Seychelles, Nov., 1891, to May, 1892; judge sup. ct., Mauritius, Aug., 1900.
BROWN, W.—Ck. of wks. for post and telegraph extension wks., Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1902.

BROWNE, ALBERT, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1876; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., Sept., 1877, to Oct., 1880; clk. in ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 21st Oct., 1880; returned to duty in C.O., Jan., 1883; apptd. to act for three years as asst. accntnt. to the high comsrr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1891; has acted also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; mem. of bd. of inquiry into postal and telegraph adminstr., Bechuanaland, Apr., 1892; ag. Imperial sec., Jan. to Mar., 1893, and (jointly with Capt. Dawkins) Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial sec., as accntnt., Aug., 1895; ag. Imp. sec., Apr. to June, 1900; finnr. advr., O.R.C., August, 1900; col. treasr., O.R.C., Dec., 1901.
BROWNE, ALFRED.—Apptd. asst. harbour-master, Barbados, 1890.

BROWNE, FRANK.—Student in Pharmacist Soc.'s Schl. in 1887; medallist in chemistry; elects research schlr. in 1888; apptd. a demonstrator in practical chemistry labortories., 1889 to 1893; apptd. asst. apothecary and analyst, Hong Kong, 1893; govt. analyst, 1898; sec. to liquor comsn., 1898; is Ph.C. and F.C.S.; J.P., 1903; author of several scientific papers.

BROWNE, GERALD.—Cadet, Fiji, July, 1879; sec. to high comsrr. for W. Pacific, Sept., 1879; also priv. sec. to gov. of N. Zealand, Nov., 1880; priv. sec. to gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1883; ditt. Natal, Jan., 1886; clk. of exec. coun., Natal, Feb., 1886; sec. for Zululand on annexation of the country, and J.P., May, 1887; entd. Line's Inn, 1889; priv. sec. to gov. of Ceylon, May, 1890; also sec. central irrigation bd., Ceylon, 1893; special mission to Sultan of Maldives Islds., May, 1894; sec. to gov't. Selangor, Malay State, July, 1894; ex-officio mem. of state coun., 1898.
BROWNE, ROBERT.—Gen. Post Office, Barbados, 1890; customs, 1894; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1900; ag. astt. treas., Gold Coast, July to Sept., 1900; 1st cls. supervisor, May, 1902.

BROWNING, JOHN GRANT, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Catford Sch.; res. engr. Eastwood and Greasley sewage scheme; exec. dist. engr., P.W.D., Selangor, Malay States, Feb., 1896; engaged upon Pahang Trunk rly. construction.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Damayanté" (Sanscrit text, published by the Imp. Acad. of St. Petersburg, 1862); poems, 1866, and other wks.; astt. libr., Br. Museum, 1863; prof. of Sanscrit, King's Coll., 1865; rector Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1868; dir. of pub. instruction, Ceylon, 1878; col. sec., Mauritius, 1882; adminstd. the govt. in 1883; lieut.-gov. and govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1885; gov., Windward Is., 1893; gov. of Mauritius, 1897; ret., 1903.

BRUCE, ROBERT R.—Clt., Saint Helena, Mar., 1885; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; dep. regisr. of births and deaths, Sept., 1886; J.L., Sept., 1896; dep. harb. mast., Oct., 1898; collr. of dues, 1887 to 1901; col. treas. and harb. mast., Jan., 1902.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Mid. Term., Oct., 1872; called to the bar, June, 1875; practised his profession in Br. Guiana until Apr., 1882, when he was apptd. a stip. justice of the peace, stip. justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as pub. prosecutor, on two occasions as judge of the inf. crim. ct.

BRUNKER, HOWARD M.—2nd lieut., Donegal Artillery (militia), 1890; lieut., 1891 (passed schl. of instrn., Woolwich, 1893); resign., 1892, to join Bechuanaland border pol.; served in ditto, Oct., 1892, to Nov., 1895; served through Matabele campaign, 1893-4 (medal); apptd. sub-inspr. of pol., B. Guiana, 12th Feb., 1896; promoted dist. inspr. 6th April, 1900; acted adjutant and musketry instr. to B. G. militia and police, April, 1901, to April, 1902; apptd. capt. in Donegal Artillery (militia), 18th June, 1902; passed schl. of musk., Hythe, 1902; promoted county inspr. of pol., B. G., 19th Nov., 1903.

BRYAN, HERBERT.—2nd lieut., Line regt., 18th June, 1892; lieut., 16th Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., 26th July, 1899; brev. major, 29th Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expdttn. to Basema (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expdttn., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., 16th Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., 8th May, 1902, to 14th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, 29th Nov., 1903; ag. govr., 9th Feb., 1904, to 3rd Mar., 1904; apptd. col. sec., G. Coast, 14th Feb., 1904.

BRYANT, ALFRED THOMAS.—Ed. Tonbridge schl. and Wad. Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1883; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1886; dist. offr., Malacca, 1887; ag. ditto, P.W., 1889; dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1890; ag. collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Mar., 1894; inspr. of schls., S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1905.

BUCHANAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. St. Peter's, Adelaide, S. Australia, and Hofryl and Glasgow; admitted to S. Australian bar, 1884; stip. mag. Port Adelaide, 1891; master sup. ct., regist. in admty., regist. of companies, industrial and provident societies, and trades unions, 1891; industrial regist. under Conciliation Act, 1895; also regist. of probates, Aug., 1903.

BUCHANAN, SIR E. J., KNT. BACH. (1901).—B. 1844; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1873; advoc. of sup. ct. of Cape Col., of high ct. of prov. of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty ct., Cape Col.; elected mem. for Worcester in house of assem., 1877; ag. atty.-gen. of Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; apptd. a puisne judge of the sup. ct. of the Cape Col., Apr., 1880; assigned to the ct. of the eastern dist., May, 1880, and to sup. ct., June, 1887.
BUCKELL, CHAS. PHILIP.—Govr.'s messenger and telegraph clk., Portland prison, 1st Mar., 1877; warder, Singapore pris., S. Sttlmts., 29th Mar., 1883; supt. of telegraphs, Penang, 23rd Dec., 1892.

BUCKNILL, JOHN ALEXANDER STRACHEY, M.A. Oxon.; J.P.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1896; mid. circuit; advocate of sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; comsnr. of patents, regist. of trade marks and regist. of companies, Transvaal, 5th June, 1902.
BUDGE, ALEX. CAMPBELL, J.P.—Entered the civ. ser. of N. S. Wales, Nov., 1858; clk., exec. coun., Oct., 1863.

BUKO, T. B.—Ed. at C.M.S. gram. school, Lagos; entd. civ. serv., Feb., 1888; clk. to ch. regist., sup. ct., Jan., 1890; transf. to customs dept., Apl., 1891; dep. regist. and interpreter, Leckie, east. dist., May, 1892; clk. to atty.-gen., Mar., 1901; 3rd cl. clk., col. sec.'s off., May, 1901.

BULLER, SIR WALTER LAWRY, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875), F.R.S.—B. 1838; author of "The Birds of New Zealand"; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1874; served in the Maori war, 1865 (medal); has served in various offices in the native affairs dept., and several times received the thanks of govt.; comsnr. for New Zealand at the Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886; is Hon. D.Sc., Camb.; offr. of legion of honour for services at Paris exhibn., 1889; Kt. Comdr. of the Crown of Italy.
BULMER, J. A.—I.S.O. (1903); Postmr., Cyprus, 27th July, 1878; postmr.-gen., Trinidad, 1883; ret., 1903.

BULWER, SIR HENRY ERNEST GASCOYNE, G.C.M.G. (1883), K.C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; was one of the offl. residents under the lord high comsnr. in Ionian Is., from Oct., 1860, until the withdrawal of the Br. protectorate in June, 1864; apptd. treas. and rec. gen. for the Is. of Trinidad, in Feb., 1866; adminstd. the govt. of Dominica, Leeward Is., from Mar., 1867, to Jan., 1869; apptd. gov. of Labuan, and H.M. consul-gen. for Borneo, in Aug., 1871; lieut.-gov. of Natal, Aug., 1875, to 1880; gov. of Natal and special comsnr. for Zulu affairs, Dec., 1881; high comsnr. Cyprus, 1885; ret. 1892.
BULYEA, GEORGE HEIDLEY VICARS.—Lieut.-gov. of prov. of Alberta, Canada, 1st Sept., 1905.

BUNDEY, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.T. BACH. (1904), K.C.—B. 1838; atty.-gen. of S. Australia, Sept., 1878; entered parlmt. in 1871; held office as min. of justice and educn., July, 1874, to Mar., 1875; 3rd judge of the sup. ct., 1884; also pres. of state bd. of conciliation, Jan., 1895, to Apl., 1896; ret. 1904.

BURBIDGE, HON. G. W., LL.D., K.C.—Called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1871; sec. to comsn. to consolidate statutes of New Brunswick, 1876-7; dep. min. of justice, Canada, and solr. for Indian affairs, May, 1882; one of the commrs. to revise the consolidated statutes of Canada, June, 1883, to June, 1885; judge, exchequer ct. of Canada, Oct., 1888.

BURCHELL, H. C.—Mem. Can. Soc. C.E.; mem. Am. Inst. M.E.; Mount Allison and McGill univs.; asst. engr. to prov. engr. of Nova Scotia, 1875-8; govt. engr., Newfndld., 1884; manager govt. telegraphs, 1893; man. Newfndld. rly., 1897; dep. min. and ch. engr., P.W., and chmn. St. John's municipal coun., 1898; supt., pub. wks., Br. Hond., 31st July, 1905, but did not take up apptnt.
BURGESS, PERCIVAL JAMES, M.A. Cantab., F.C.S.—Govt. analyst and science lecturer, S. Stltltns., 19th Apr., 190.

BURKE, HENRY LARDNER, K.C.—Ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 2nd cl. classical mods., 1871; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1901; LL.B., of Cape univ., 1880; admitted as an advocate of east. dist. et., 1880, and of sup. ct., 1886; examiner in literature at Cape univ., 1888-1890; ag. solicitor-gen., 25th June to 25th July, 1895, 22nd Dec., 1896, to 28th Mar., 1897, and from 8th May to 12th June, 1897; apptd. crown prosecutor, Griqualand West, 14th June, 1897; apptd. Q.C., 13th May, 1898.

BURLEY, E. B.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd divn. civ. ser., and assigned to registr. gen.'s office, 21st Aug., 1896; transfd. to C.O. 20th June, 1896; special service in office of high commr., South Africa, July, 1900, to Jan., 1902.

BURMESTER, CHARLES MANSEL.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; ent. serv. of Norddeutscher Lloyd SS. Co., Bremen, April, 1898; res. Dec., 1899; joined 58th Co. (Berks) Imp. Yeom., Jan. 29th, 1900; served in S. Afr. camp., 1900-1901; clk. to sec. O. R. C. Admin., Mar. 20th, 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902.
BURNS, ALAN C.—Revenue offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905.

BURNS, R. E.—2nd clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town commrs., Bassee-terre, June to Dec., 1899; clk., P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev.offr., treas., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treas., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. govt. clk., clk., ex. and leg. couns., clk. of health, Apl., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904; tarif clk., treas., St. K.-N., Oct., 1904; ag. audt. clk., May, 1905.

BURNSIDE, Sir BRUCE LOCKHART, KT. BACH. (1884).—B. 1833; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Apr., 1856; solr.-gen., Bahamas, May, 1864; was ag. atty.-gen. in 1865, 1866, 1869, and 1875; mem. of exec. coun. in 1866; and of legis. coun. in 1872; mem. of the local legislature from 1859; and was speaker of the House of Assem. in 1866; made Q.C. in 1874; atty.-gen., Sept., 1875; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1893.

BURNSIDE, Nigel BRUCE.—New Providence, Bahamas, 3rd clk., surv.-gen.'s dept., 1879 and 1881; ag. clk. hd. of pub. wks., 1890 to 1882; clk. legis. coun., 1881 to 1888; 2nd clk. surv.-gen.'s dept., 1882 to 1883; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1883 to 1887; priv. sec. to Admstr. E. B. A. Taylor, C.M.G., 1886; clk., col. sec.'s dept., and clk., bd. of pub. wks., 1887 (still holding same); ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1889, July to Oct., 1890, again in 1891.
BURNSIDE, R.B.—Called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1884; admtd. to W. Australian bar, July, 1884; usher of black roil, legis. coun., Dec., 1890; crown solr., July, 1894; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1902.

BURROWS, STEPHEN MONTAGU, M.A.—Scholar of Eton, scholar of Exeter Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1876; graduated in classical hon., 1879; writer, Ceylon service, Oct., 1880; priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gov. Douglas, June, 1881; J.P. and additional coroner for Colombo, Nov., 1881; attached to the Queen's advoc. dept., June, 1882; J.P. and additional pol. mag., Calle, Aug., 1882; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., N.W. prov., Apr., 1884; extra office asst. to the govt. agt., central prov., May, 1884; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., north-central prov., and additional dist. judge, commr. of requests and pol. mag., Anuradhapura, Aug., 1884; ag. office asst., Badulla, July, 1889; ag. ass't-agt., Matale, June, 1887; ag. dist. judge, Kegalle, 1892; ag. fiscal agt. C.P., 1892; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 1893; do., Nuwara Eliya, 1896; dir. of pub. instruction, 1899; govt. agent, N.W.P., 1902; author of "The Visitor's Guide to Kandy and Nuwara Eliya," and "The Buried Cities of Ceylon."

BURSLEM, W.—Ed. Manchester gram. schl. and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1882; asst. mast., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1880; 2nd mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1891; head master, Lodge schl., Barbados, 1892; principal, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1895.

BURT, ALFRED EARLE.—Cik. to ch. justice, W. Australia, June, 1871; clk. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1873; to gov., 1874; in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, marine survey dept., 1875; and in crown lands dept., Sept., 1877; acted as regis. of deeds and titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; regis. of titles and deeds, June, 1890.

BURT, OCTAVUS, I.S.O. (1903).—Cik. in gov.'s office, W. Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to ag. gov.; Apr., 1874 to Dec., 1874, priv. sec. to Gov. Weld; May, 1875, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to Gov. Sir Wm. Robinson, K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, ch. clk. and keeper of records in survey office; res. mag., Newcastle, Apr., 1880; ditto, York, Apr., 1887; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1887; ag. col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. and legis. couns., under sec. for W. Australia under responsible gov't., 1891; ag. sec. to agent-gen., Apl. to Oct., 1898; under sec., May, 1899; sheriff, W.A., 1901.

BURY, FRED W.—Junior clk., registr.'s off., B. Guiana, Feb. to Aug., 1887; librn., govt. secr-tariat, Sept., 1887, to Feb., 1888; asst. clk. recvr.gen.'s off., Aug., 1888, to Mar., 1889; 5th cls. clk., Apr., 1889, to May, 1892; 4th cls. clk., June, 1892, to May, 1897; 3rd cls. clk., June, 1897; ag. 1st cls. clk., Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; ag. ast. recvr.-gen. and senr. administrator., Berbice, Feb., 1904, to 23rd Dec., 1904.

BUSHE, ROBERT GERVAISE.—Ed. at King's Coll., Camb., where he obtained an exhibn. on entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the math. tripos, 1875; grad. B.A., 1875; 2nd master Queen's Roy. coll., Trinidad, 1878; inspr. of schools, 1890; auditor-general, 1903.

BUSHE, R. J. SCOTT.—A.M.I.C.E., Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Malvern Coll., and Lancing Coll.; ag. and special draftsman, P.W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1882-6; draftsman light rlv., Feb., 1880; survr. of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1889; ag. dir. pub. wks., Mar., 1891; engaged on loan wks., Trinidad, 1893, and in charge of S. div., P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1897; dist. offr., 1st grade, 1899; ast. col. civ. engnr., B. Guiana, 1902.

BUTLER, ERNEST ORMOND.—Served in Bech Border police and Bech. mounted pol., 1891-1897. Matabele war, 1893-4; served in B.S.A. police, Bech. Prot. div., 1898-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902 (medal and clasp, defence of Mafeking); ch. clk., headquarter off., Lt.-Gen. Baden Powell's force, 1900; customs off., 1902-1903; asst. commr.'s clk., Bech. Prot., 1902; J.P., ditto, 1903.

BUTLER, F. G. A.—Exhibitioner of Trin. Coll., Oxford, 1892; 1st class classical mods., 1894; 1st class final classical schools, 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Admiralty, Oct., 1896; 2nd class clk. in C.O., Apl., 1897; sec. to W. Africa currency comte., 1899.

BUTLER, CAPT. P. J. H.—Late 3rd Batt. E. Lancs. Regt.; entered Natal civ. ser., Jan., 1890, customs dept.; sergt.-at-arms, House of Assem., Natal, Nov., 1896; volunt. for spec. serv., and as lieut. proceeded, Oct., 1899, with Hotchkiss gun detach. to Ladysmith, present during siege, medal and clasp.
BUTLER, RICHARD, M.L.A., S. Aust. (1890).—Min. of ed. and agric., 1898-9; treas., 1901; treas. and commr. of crown lands and immigrn., 1902; also premier, 1st Mar. to 26th July, 1905.

BUTLER, CAPT. VERA ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I. and 7th Roy. Lanc. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of pol., Mauritius, June, 1880; pol. offr., with magisterial powers, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1886; reverted to former post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. 1st class inspr. of pol., Jan., 1892; inspr., Sept., 1894; ag. ch. offr. of pol. on several occasions for Port Louis.

BUTLER, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WM. FRANCIS, K.C.B. (1886), C.B. (1874); B. 1838; served in Canada in Red River expdn., 1870; in Ashanti, 1874; Zulu War, 1878-9; Egypt, 1882; with Gordon Relief expdn., 1884-5; comdg. troops S. Africa, 1898; admnstr. govt. Cape, Nov., 1898, to Feb., 1899; commanding troops W. Dist. England, 1899; do., Aldershot, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, CAPTAIN ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; headqrs. offr. armed constab., Feb., 1891; also port and harmr., and native mag., promoted commandant constab. and res. mag., Oct., 1894; active serv., Tugere expdn., 1896 (medal and clasp); volunt. for serv. in S. Afr., and joined 4th Q'land contng., Mar., 1900, as sen. lieut.; prom. capt. at Pretoria, Nov., 1900; ment. by Lord Roberts in cablegram to gov. of Q'land for splendid behaviour, 2nd Dec., 1900; resign. contng. and Br. N. Guinea govt. serv., Sept., 1901; 2nd lieut., Royal Lanc. Regt., Sept., 1901; capt. and company commr. Lagos batt. W.A.F.F., Sept., 1901; active serv., Aro expdt., S. Nigeria, 1901-2 (medal and clasp); capt. 4th batt. Royal Lanc. Regt., Aug., 1904; dist. commr., Lagos, Oct., 1904.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; was mem. of the London schl. bd. from 1876 to 1882; hon. sec. to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigrn. fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Questions," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the inp. parlmt. series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, 1896, and 1900, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; partly under-sec. of state for the cols., 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895; postm.-gen., Dec., 1905.

BUXTON, SIR THOMAS FOWELL, BART., (C.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1895).—3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; B. 1837; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; lieut.-col., 2nd Tower Hamlets rifle volr. brigade, 1864; hon. col. since 1884; gov. of S. Australia, 1895-9.

BYNOE, A. D.—Clk., G.P.O., Barbados, 1874; rev. offr., Jan., 1879; landing waiter, May, 1879; 2nd clk., customs, June, 1887; ch. clk., customs, Dec., 1890; ag. compt. of cust., Apl., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and on other occasions; admeasurer of shipping, Sept., 1904.
BYRNE, M.—Sec. to law dept., Victoria, Oct., 1896; formerly ch. clk. in same dept.

CADE, CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Capt. in the W. Cork artillery militia; served through the Zulu war, 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; asst.-inspr., G.C.C., Mar., 1880; inspr., 11th Dec., 1882; adjt. and musketry instructor of the G.C.C., Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local commd. of pol., Cyprus, Sept., 1883; also asst. to commr., Nicosia, and gov., central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commr.

CADOGAN, 5TH EARL (Great Britain), GEORGE HENRY CADOGAN.—B. 1840; eldest son of the 4th earl, succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. for Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; parl. under sec. for war, May, 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; partly under sec. for the col., 2nd Mar., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 28th June, 1896-1902.
CALDER, CHARLES MACLEAR.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; clk. of cts., parish of Portland, Apr., 1888; ditto, parish of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889; res. mag., Trelawney, Jan., 1897.

CALVERT, JOHN J., I.S.O. (1904).—Clk., col. sec.'s office, N. S. Wales, 1883; clk. of select comtees., legis. assem., May, 1886; 1st clk. legis. coun., Apr., 1889; clk. asst., Jan., 1890; clk. of the parlmts. Apr., 1871.

CAMERON, ALLAN DUNCAN.—Ed., Ireland; cler. ass't, inspr. of villages, and sec., bd. of health, B.G., 14th Apr., 1890; 6th clk., clk., inspr. of pris., Oct., 1893; 6th ditto, 9th Feb., 1896; gov't offr., dep. of mines, Bartika, 8th Dec., 1896; acted as navg. offr. for Michael McTurk, C.M.G., 2nd Mar., 1897, to 8th Dec., 1898; ag. magt.'s clk., Bartika, 1897-98; transf'd, same appt., to Koribae station, 19th Oct., 1899; J.P., 30th Oct., 1899; magt.'s clk., Corentyne jud. dist., 1st May, 1900; 3rd clk., pol. dep., 1st Nov., 1901; commanded B. Guiana militia as lieut., 21st Nov., 1901; recommended by H.E. Sir J. A. Swettenham for appt. as hon. A.D.C. with rank of capt., Aug., 1902.

CAMERON, MAJ.-GEN. DONALD RODERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1834; entered R.A. 1856; capt., 1866, maj. 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886; ret. 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1869-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Bhootan campaign, 1864-66, as adjt. and as staff offr. of offr. comdg. R.A., Doonar Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjt. 1st Bde. R.A., 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a mem. of the exec. coun., N.W. Territories, 1869; was commr. internat. bdry., comau., and suptd. the expdn. which marked the internat. bdry. from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the Br.-Alaskan bdry., 1874; sec. to Canadian delegation at the Paris internat. confce., 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the E. bdry. of Br. Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan bdry. in 1886; priv. sec. to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenip. to the fishery confce., 1887; principal, Roy. mil. coll., Kingston, Canada, 1888. Ret. 1896.

CAMERON, DONALD CHARLES.—Ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th dt., 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. ast. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec., 19th Dec., 1904.

CAMERON, EDWARD J., C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury schl., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lees, gov. Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1884; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Sttlmt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legia. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comnr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1895, and Apl. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901.

CAMERON, MAJOR MAURICE ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1900), R.E.—B.1855; first comnr. in Roy. engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engr., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted for about two years and a half as col. engrnr. and surrv.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, Julv., 1895.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD NEIL, F.R.G.S.—Capt. reserve of offrs., late R.A.; South Africa, 1897-1903; on spec. serv., 1st July, 1899, to 31st Dec., 1900, including spec. apptnt. at H.B.M. consulate-gen.'s, Delagoa Bay, May to Dec., 1900; afterwards on intell. staff, army headqrs.; operations in Natal, 1899-1900, including actions at Colenso and Spionkop, and relief of Ladysmith (twice ment. in desps.; Queen's medal, 4 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); Anglo-Portuguese boundary delimit. comnr., East Africa, 1904-5.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS GRAHAM.—2nd Surveyor, P.W.D., Selangor, S. Sttlmts., 1883; ditto, land office, 1885; ast. dist. offr., Rawang, 1887; sec. to res., 1888; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1890; ditto, Ulu Selangor, 1893; ditto, Klang, 1897; ditto and ast. Indian immigrtn. agt., 1898; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1899; sec. to res., 1901; res. of Negri Sembilan, 1904; has acted as res. on several occasions, and also as sec. to govt.

CAMPBELL, H. C., I.S.O. (1903).—Second clk. in the deeds registry office, Natal, Apr., 1861; 2nd clk. audit office, Jan., 1863; 1st clk. R.M. ct., co. Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1868; R.M., div. of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; ag. R.M. div. of Umgeni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Inanda, and J.P. for col., 1880; master and regiar., sup. ct., 1882; col. audr., Apr., 1889; acted as judge native high ct., and as 2nd puisne judge and atty.-gen.; audit-gen., 1894; chmn. civ. ser. bd.; judge president, native high ct., 1899; judge, spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as judge pres., nat. high ct., 1901.

CAPE TOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. WILLIAM WEST JONES, D.D.—Consec. 1874; metropolitan of the church of the prov. of S. Africa; formerly fellow of St. John's Coll., Oxon; preacher at Chapel Royal, Whitehall, and rural dean of Oxford.
CAPPER, THOMAS.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Sc. Triposes), 1879; impr. of schools, Jamaica, Oct., 1880; official mem. of legis. coun., 1882.

CARBONE, SIR JOSEPH, LL.D. (1862), C.M.G. (1867), K.C.M.G. (1891), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; received at the Malta Bar, 1863; reporter, ct. of commerce, 1868; crown advoc. and govt. legal adviser, 1880; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., ex officio; ch. justice, Malta, 1895; vice-pres. legis. coun., 1898.

CARDEN, J.—Shorthand writer in office of ast. sec. (for India) to Roy. comnr. Col. and Indian Exhib., 1886; diploma for services; entered service of Roy. Niger Company, Jan., 1887; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, Jan., 1887, to Dec., 1899; transferred to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.
CARDEW, CLAUDE AMBROSE.—Ast. agt., Chinde, B. Cent. Africa prot., June, 1893; collr., South Nyasa dist., 1894; 2nd class ast., Apr., 1902; dist. mag., Upper Shire, 1902.

CARDEW, COL. SR FRED., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1839; engaged in N.W. Frontier of India campaign, 1863, and Zulu and Transvaal campaigns, 1879-81; D.A.Q.M.G. in S. Africa, 1879-80; ast. mil. sec. China, 1882-3; sub-comsnr., Zululand, 1884-6; A.A.G., S. Africa, 1890; res. comsnr. Zululand, 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1894 to 1900.

CARLILE, EDWARD, K.C.—Gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868; clk., census office, Victoria, May, 1861; registr.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1862; crown law offices, Mar., 1865; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parly. and professional ast. to crown law offrs., 1873; parly. draftsman, July, 1879; clk.-ast., legis. assem., Apr., 1882; re-apptd. parly. draftsman with higher status, Apr., 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Constitution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

CARLING, THE HON. JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1828; mem. for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; rec.-gen. of Canada, 1862; min. of wks. and agricul., Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy coun. and made postmr.-gen. in Sir J. Macdonald's govt., 1882; min. of agricul., 1885 to 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; ret., 1894.

CARLSON, KNUT ALEXANDER.—Forest probationer in the Knysna conservancy, Cape Colony, 5th Feb., 1888; probationary ast. to conservator of forests, Knysna, Sep., 1889; dist. offr., Kokstad, Transean conservancy, May, 1892; passed through course of forestry at R.I.E. Coll., Cooper's Hill, during 1894-5, and was awarded a diploma; dist. forest offr., Butterworth, Aug., 1895; transf. to O.R.C. as chief of forestry div., 1st Dec., 1903.

CARMODY, P.—Entered Imp. service after open competn., 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1880; diploma (1st class) Roy. Sch. of Mines; ast. analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90; govt. analyst, Trinidad, and prof. of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll. and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; mem. central agricul. bd., 1890; mem. bd. of health, 1891; is F.I.C., F.C.S., mem. of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B.—Puisne judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.—B. 1842; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Q.C., 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits; min. of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1890; postmr.-gen., 1892 to 1896; one of representatives of Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.
CARPMAEL, ERNEST.—Ed. at Dulwich and Trinity Hall, Camb.; scholar, 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., cadet, Weihaiei, 1904.

CARRENA, ALBERT EMANUEL E.O.—Ed. at St. Gregory’s R.C. gram. schl., Lagos; asst. clk. har. dept., 1894; clk. and storekeeper, 1895; messenger and copyist, Queen’s advocate’s off., 1896; 2nd clk. gen. registry, 1897; Queen’s advoc.’s clk., 1897; assisted as clk. in the trade comsn., 1898; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the conduct of Dr. D. J. Jones, 1899; promoted 3rd class clk., gov’t’s office, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the railway accident at Owowo, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the customs dept., 1901; acted as regisr. of corresp., secretariat, 1902; on spec. serv. to Ekiti countries, 1903; ag. conf. clk., clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 1904.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), VISCOUNT WENDOVER (U.K.), BARON CARRINGTON (Great Britain, 1797, and Ireland, 1796), CHARLES R. W. CARRINGTON, P.C. (1881), G.C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1843; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1863; late capt. Roy. Horse Guards, lieut.-col. 3rd batt. Oxford Light Infantry; capt. Queen’s Body Guard, 1881 to 1885; gov. of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; lord chamberlain, 1892-95; joint hered. lord great chamberlain of England.

CARRINGTON, MAJOR-GEN. SIR FREDERICK, K.C.B. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1844; ed. Chelt. Coll.; 24th regt.; comdr. of the F. L. H. in the Transkei war, S. Africa, 1877-78; commanded Transvaal volks against Sikukuni, 1878-79; commanded left wing C.M.R., 1879 to 1883; commanded col. forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd mounted rifles, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; comdt. of pol., Br. Bechuanaland, 1885; comdt. native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland pol., Dec., 1888-93; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; comdg. brigade at Gibraltar, 1896; commanded forces in Matabele war, 1896; resumed command at Gibraltar, 1897; commanded Rhodesian Field Force during S. Afr. war, 1900.

CARRINGTON, SIR JOHN WORRELL, KNT. (1897), C.M.G. (1888), K.C., F.R.G.S., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1847; ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C.C., 1866), and Linc. Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln’s Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1874-5; J.P., 1874; mem. house of assem., 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solr.-gen., 1878; again acted as judge, asst. ct. of appeal, 1879; and as atty.-gen., 1880; hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; hon. LL.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was pres. of the educn. bd., and of the bd. of health, and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with educn.; ch. justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; admnstd. govt. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition Tobago laws; ag. ch. justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the laws of St. Lucia; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888; chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of Br. Guiana bills relating to the cts., criminal law and procedure, etc., of that col.; chmn. central bd. of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country comsn., 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance comsn., 1893-4; maj. in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised edition of the laws of Br. Guiana; ch. justice, Hong Kong, 1896; ret. 1902.

CARRUTHERS, J. B., F.L.S.—Ed. Dulwich Coll., Royal Schl. of Mines, London, and Griefswald Univ., Prussia; demonstrator of botany, Royal Vet. Coll., London, 1892; prof. of botany, Coll. of Agric., Doniton, Wilts., 1894; proceeded to Ceylon to investigate cacao disease under joint auspices of govt. and Ceylon planters’ assoc., 1898; mycologist to govt. of Ceylon and asst. dir., Royal Bot. Gardens, 1900; dir. of agric. and govt. botanist, F.M.S., 1905.

CARTER, SIR GILBERT T., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1848; entered navy 1864; paymr. of col. steamer “Sherbro.” S. Leone, Aug., 1870; served on the G. Coast during a part of the Ashanti campaign; was one of the commsrs. for valuing the ordnance and stores left behind by the Dutch at the transfer of Elmina; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., Sept., 1875; collr. of customs and treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1879; treas. and postmr., Gambia, Oct., 1882; admnstd. the govt. several times; admstr. of Gambia on separation from S. Leone, Nov., 1888; gov., Lagos, 1890-96; gov., Bahamas, 1898; gov., Barbados, 23rd July, 1904.

CARTER, WILLIAM STUART.—After compet. exam., apptd. copyist to the treasury, cust., and post office, Br. Honduras, Feb., 1894; ag. 5th clk. of same, Sep. to Dec., 1896; 4th clk. Mar. to June, 1898; ag. clk. to registr.-gen., June to Sep., 1898, Mar. to Oct., 1899, and Mar. to Oct., 1900; ag. clk. to col. engrnr., Oct. to Feb., 1900.

CARTWRIGHT, THE RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD JOHN, P.C. (1902), G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1835; entered Canadian parlmt., 1863; sworn of the privy coun., and was min. of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resign. of the Mackenzie cabinet, Oct., 1878; min. of trade and commerce in Sir W. Laurier’s govt., June, 1896.
CASAUT, SIR L. N., KT. BACH. (1894).—B. 1822; ch. justice super. ct., Quebec, 1894

CASEY, THE HON. JAS. JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1831; barrister-at-law; M.L.A., Victoria, 1861-1879; mem. exec. coun., 1868; min. of justice, 1868; solr.-gen., 1869; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and min. of agricult., 1872 to 1875; pres. of Victoria comsn. to Paris Exhbn., 1878; exec. vice-pres. of Melbourne internat. exhibn., 1880; pres. of fed. bk. of Australia; is now judge of insolvency ct. and chmn. gen. sessions; judge of cts. of mines and co. cts.; is knight offr. of the legion of honour and of the crown of Italy.

CASOLANI, EDWARD ROBERT.—Apptd. clk., Sep., 1878, and higher div. clk., Mar., 1883, on the high comsn.’s estab., Cyprus; transf. to the ch. sec.’s estab., Mar., 1884; ag. loc. commdt. of pol. and asst. comsn., Nicosia, July to Sep., 1898; ch. clk. and sub. loc. commdt. of pol., Jan., 1899; again ag. loc. comdt., Nicosia, May to Oct., 1902.

CASOLANI, WILLIAM.—Clk. port dept., Malta, May, 1863; cust. dept., Jan., 1865; 2nd class clk., ch. sec.’s office, June, 1880; 1st class clk. and asst. clk. to the coun., Jan., 1886; dep. asst. sec. to govt. and clk. to exec. coun. and coun. of govt., July, 1894; rec-gen. and director of contracts, 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and coun. of govt.
CASSERLY, JAMES MICHAEL.—3rd class clk., col. sec.’s office, Jamaica, 1878; 2nd class clk., Nov., 1885.

CASSON, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Priv. sec. to Sir H. H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., H.M.’s comsnr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1894-1896; clk. in ch. acctn.'s off., Feb., 1896; ast. sec., B.C.A. admtn., Apr., 1901; supt. of native affairs, Aug., 1903.
CASTOR, C. F., M.B., C.M., F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., Feb., 1879; med. offr., Br. Guiana, 1885; med. supt. leper asylum, 1887; is J.P. and coroner.

CATT, THE HON. A.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia, since 1881; comsnr. of crown lands, S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884; comsnr. of pub. wks., June, 1887, to June, 1889; chmn. of comtees, legis. assem., 1890-1895.

CAVENDISH, A., B.A. Oxon.—Cadet, S.S. Jan., 1901; ag. 3rd mag., Penang; ag. 4th mag., Singapore; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 23rd Jan., 1904, ag. supt. of educ., Penang, Apr., 1906.

CAYLEY, SIR RICHARD (Knt., 1882).—B. 1833; M.A. and scholar of St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1862; advoc. sup. ct. of Ceylon, June, 1863; dep. Queen's advoc., May, 1868; puisne judge of the sup. ct., 1873; Queen's advoc., 1876; ch. justice, 1879; resig., 1883; is a mag. for Rutland.

CELESTIN, LOUIS ALBERT.—Ed. govt. normal schl., Mauritius; teacher in edn. dept., 1874; ag. inspr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. cashier, Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; ch. inspr. of distilleries, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, Apr. to Oct., 1895; dist. cashier, Flaoc, June, 1898; Rose Hill, Jan., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., June, 1903.

CHADWICK, JOHN COURTENAY CHASMAN.—Cllk. and interp. to the R.M., Inanda div., Natal, Jan., 1868; clk. of the ct., Oct., 1870; acted as R.M., and admstr. of native law on various occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for the Inanda div., Aug., 1875; registr. of the native high ct., and sec. to native admstr. bd., Nov., 1876; admitted an atty. of the sup. ct., 1878; first class interp. on the staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War of 1879 (medal and clasp); admstr. of native law and border agt., Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., Lions River div., and J.P. for the col., 1882; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Ixopo, Sep., 1886; mag., Lions River div., 1897-8; Umgeni divn., 1898; judge, native high ct., July, 1906.

CHADWICK, OSBERT, C.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1834; has been employed by the C.O. to report on the drainage and water supply of various cols.; was consult. engr. for the Malta water wks. and Hong Kong water supply and drainage.

CHALMERS, A. J., M.D., C.H. (Victoria Univ., Eng.), F.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; ag. ch. med. offr., 1900, and during siege of Kumassi, 1900 (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); registr., med. coll., Ceylon, 22nd Feb., 1901.

CHAMBERLAIN, THE Rt. HON. JOSEPH, P.C. (1880), M.P., Hon. LL.D. (Camb.), Hon. D.C.L. (Oxford).—Eldest son of the late Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, 1836, and ed. at University Coll. schl., after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a mem. of the Birmingham town coun., and in the same year a mem. of the schl. bd., of which he became chmn. in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parlmt. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's admtn. he was apptd. pres. of the bd. of trade, with a seat in the cabinet. While at the bd. of trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through Parlmt. the Bankruptcy Act, which is now in force, the Patents Act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1886 he was returned as mem. for W. Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's 3rd admtn. in 1886, as pres. of the local govt. bd., but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for W. Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the U.S. States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s min. there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus vivendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's govt. in June, 1896, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's govt. as sec. of state for the col., and continued to hold that office in Mr. Balfour's cabinet, 1902. At the general elections of 1895 and 1900, in both of which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for W. Birmingham. At the end of 1902 he visited S. Africa, to deal with questions connected with the settlement of the country; Lord Rector, Glasgow Univ., 1896; Chancellor, Birmingham Univ., 1901; resigned office in connection with the question of a new fiscal policy, Oct., 1903.

CHAMBERLAYNE, MAJOR TANKERVILLE JAMES.—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; ensign, Ceylon rifles, June, 1868; transf'd. to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; fort adjt., Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; ag. brig.-maj., Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as asst. mil. sec., China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to gov. of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; priv. sec., Mar., 1882; also ag. clk. exec. coun., July, 1885; priv. sec. to high comsnr. of Cyprus, and clk. exec. coun., Mar., 1886; local comdt. of pol., 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to comsnr., Larnaca, 1894; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Oct., 1895; Kyrena, Nov., 1896-Apr., 1897; Nicosia, June-Nov., 1897; Kyrena, Apr., 1898; comsnr. and local comdt. of pol., Kyrena, 1899; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Nov., 1900; do. Nicosia, Jan. to May, 1900, Nov., 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Aug., 1902, to Nov., 1903.

CHAMBERS, CECIL H.—Cashier, St. John's savings bank, Antigua, 1893; accountant, ditto, 1894; 4th out-door offr., treasy. and cust. dept., 1896; 3rd in-door offr., treasy., Antigua, 1897; audit clk., Dominica, 1900; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901; ag. chief audit clk. and federal acctn., Antigua, May, 1902, to Mar., 1903, and May to Nov., 1905; ag. 1st treasy. offr., Montserrat; M.L.C.; comsnr. for oaths, Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904.

CHAMBERS, JOHN RATCLIFFE.—2nd clk. to pres. St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasy., Dec., 1878; 1st clk. to regist. and provost-marshal, Apr., 1881; sec. of the coman. to inquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., 1882 to 1883; clk. to mag. dist. C. St. Kitts, July, 1883; dep. coroner, May, 1885; qtrmr. with St. Kitts militia, June, 1885; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; ag. mag. dist. C, 1899, 1902, 1903 and 1904; escheator-gen., 1905.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St John's Coll., Camb. (1875-79); law tripus, 1879-80; called to the bar. Inner Temp., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. comsnr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of asst. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assem., Mar., 1881; represented parish of St Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR.—Assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. supt. fire brigade, 1891-92; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volunteer corps, 1893; capt., 1897; major, 2nd in command, 1899; ag. comdt. on several occasions; thanks and medal for services during plague, 1894; mem. of comttee. on volunteer corps, 1898; commanded volunteers during disturbances in New Territory, 1899; commanded Hong Kong and China Coronation contingent, 1902.
CHAPMAN, FRANK.—3rd class clk., audit dept., Natal, Feb., 1898; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1900; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1901; asst. inspr., audit dept., July, 1901.

CHATHAM, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E.—Ed. Roy. High Schl. and Univ., Edin.; asst. to Messrs. Thos. Meik and Son, C.E., Edin., 1880; res. engrn., Bo'ness drainage works, 1883; asst. engrn. dock engrn.'s office, Bristol, 1885; exec. engrn. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. dir. of pub. wks., 1893-94-97-1901; mem. legiu. coun., 1897; asst. dir. P.W., 1898; director, 1901; mem. exec. coun., 1901; pres. of san. bd., 1901.
CHELMSFORD, 3rd BARON (U.K.), creat. 1858.

CHERMSIDE, SIR HERBERT CHARLES, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1896), C.B.—B. 1850; Roy. Eng., 1870; during Russo-Turkish war, 1876-78, accompd. Turkish troops as mil. attache; served in Egyp. camp., 1882; and was attached to Egyp. army, 1883 to 1888; in 1896 became Brit. mil. comsnr. and commander of Brit. troops in Crete, and for services there was made K.C.M.G.; appointed to the Curragh district in 1899, and has since served in S. Afr. in commd. of 3rd infant. div.; gov. of Queensland, 1901; resigned 1904.

CHERRY, JAS. WM.—Ed. at Bedford Modern schl.; apptd. asst. ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., G. Coast, 9th July, 1898; ch. clk. and acctnt., 1st Jan., 1900; offi. mem. Accra town coun., 1898, 1899, 1900 and 1901; chrmn. streets and roads comttee.; apptd. 2nd lieut. G. C. rifle volunteers, 16th Nov., 1898; lieut. 17th Aug., 1900; acted adjutant, 3rd May to 30th June, 1899, and 26th June, 1900, to 1st June, 1901; apptd. capt., 12th Oct., 1901; ag. offr. commanding, Dec., 1901, to July, 1902; certificate, army form E 511, Chelsea barracks, Oct., 1901.

CHICHESTER, ATHOL AUGUSTUS.—Joined H.M.S. "Worcester" (training-ship), 1876; served in H.M. hospital ship, "Ganges," during Egyptian campaign, 1885; temp. trav. inptr. of bd. of agri. under cont. diseases acts, 1890 to 1892; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. Niger Coast Prot., May, 1897; dist. comsnr. Sapell, 1900-02; transf'd. to Asaba, Aug., 1902; trav. comsnr., 17th Jan., 1903; ag. divisional comsnr., Central Division, Apr. to Sept., 1903, and again in 1904.

CHICHESTER, CHARLES RENFREY.—Ed. Stonehurst Coll. and Dublin Univ.; B.A. Dub.; L.R.C.P. and L.R.C.S., Ireland; diplomate in public health; licentiate in midwifery, Rotunda, Dub.; dipl. in pub. vaccination; asst. col. surg., Gambia, Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Apr. to Nov., 1896, May to Dec., 1898; pres. of bd. of health whilst ag. col. surg.; mem. quarantine bd.; sen. med. offr., So. Nigeria, 1904.

CHILD, LIEUT. HERBERT A.—Entered H.M. navy as cadet, 15th July, 1883; specly. promoted to midshipman, 23rd July, 1885; served in H.M. ships, "Conquest," "Triumph," "Calypso," "Minotaur," "Duke of Wellington," "Pilot," and "Thrush"; specly. promoted to lieut., July, 1891; landed with nav. brig. in Gambia expd., 1891-92 (West African medal and clasp); resigned comsnr., Feb., 1894, to take up appt. as asst. supt. marine in N. C. P., 4th May, 1894; took part in naval operations against Chief Nana of Benin river, 1894 (mentioned in desps., 2nd clasp); took part in naval operations against Brass chiefs, 1895 (3rd clasp); sup. of marine, S. Nig., 15th Jan., 1897; took part in naval operations against King of Benin, 1897 (mentioned in desps., 4th clasp); took part in Aro exped., 1901-2, having organisation and charge of marine transport arrangements (mentioned in desps., African gen. serv. medal and clasp); comsnr. as lieut. R.N. restored 21st Aug., 1902, on joining emergency list of retired officers, R.N.

CHIPPENDALL, GEORGE HERBERT.—Sub-lieut. 3rd foot, 1874; lieut. East Kent regt., 1874; capt. Nov., 1883; maj. W. Riding regt., Dec., 1893; lieut.-col., Nov., 1898; comdt. defence force, W. Australia, Dec., 1898; transf'd. to commonwealth govt., 1901.

CHITTY, LOUIS OGILVY.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; asst. mr., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1882-91; headmnr. gram. schl., St. Vincent, Sept., 1891, to Dec., 1893; inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Jan., 1894, to Dec., 1896; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, Jan., 1897, to Mar., 1898; audr. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Apr., 1898; audr. only, 1902.
CHURCH, THK HON. C. E.—Mem. for Lunenburg in Nova Scotian legis. assem.; comsnr. for pub. wks. and mines.

CHURCHILL, JOHN SPENCER, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1835; Ed. at Winchester; ensign, 46th regt., 1854; served at siege of Sebastopol (medal and clasp); capt. 36th regt., 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Lisgar, when lord high comnr. of the Ionian Is., 1857; pres. of Virgin Is., 1879; ag. pres., Nevis, 1879, and of Dominica, 1882 to 1887; pres., Montserrat, 1887; comsnr. of St. Kitts-Nevis, 1888; col. sec., Bahamas, 1896; ret., 1905.
CHURCHILL, WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER.—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester,
CLARK, THOMAS.—App'td. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., Apl., 1902.
CLARK, WM. MORTIMER, K.C.—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1861; Q.C., 1887; senator of Toronto univ.; chrmn. of bd. of management of Knox Coll., Toronto, 1880; lieut.-gov., Ontario, Apl., 1903.

CLARKE, SIR CHAS. MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.B. (1901); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; entd. Army, 1856; served in N. Zealand, 1861-66 (medal); Zulu war (promoted brevet-col., C.B., medal and clasp), 1879; Basutoland, 1890-81; commdt.-gen. of col. forces at Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; A.A.G., War Office, 1884; D.A.G., Ireland, 1886-88; maj.-gen. commanding 3rd infantry brig., Aldershot, 1889-92; D.A.G. at headquarters, 1892-93; com.-in-chief and lieut.-gen. commanding the Forces, Madras, 1893-98; quartermaster-gen. to the Forces, 1899; gov., Malta, 2nd Mar., 1903.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep.-regisr., sup. ct., Trinidad.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, KT. BACH. (1894).—LL.B.—B. 1851; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Easter, 1876; N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; ag. ch. just. and ch. judicial comsrr. for the W. Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884; confirmed, 1885; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; ch. just., 1891; ch. just., Jamaica, 1896.
CLARKE, GEORGE H.—Asst. res. just., Nicoll's Town, Andros Is., Bahamas, Dec., 1902.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir George S., R.E. G.C.M.G. (1905), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expedit., 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comttee, 1886-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; app'td. as one of a comttee to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comttee., 1904.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir Marshal James, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880), (late R.A.).—B. 1841; res. mag., co. Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepstone, H.M.'s special comsrr., S. Africa, 1876; polit. offr. and special comsrr., Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign 1880-1, in command of Landdrost's office, Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; res. mag. Quithin, Basutoland, 1881; comsrr., Cape pol. King William's Town, 1882; col. comdgr. Turkish regt. Egyptian gendarmerie, 1882; 3rd class order of Medjidie; ret. from the army, with rank of lieut.-col., 1883; res. comsrr. Basutoland, 1884; res. comsrr. and ch. mag., Zululand, 1883; res. comsrr., Rhodesia, 1898.

CLARKE, WILLIAM JAMES.—Joined Natal Mounted Police, 1878; served through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp); accompd. ex-Empress Eugenie on her tour through Natal and Zululand, 1880; served in Basutoland war, 1880 (medal with clasp); served in Transvaal campaign, 1881, present at battle of Laing's Nek; accompd. mission to Pondoland, 1887; with mission to Tongoland, under Col. Sir R. Martin, in 1888, and with the Portuguese-Swazi bdry. coman., 1888; ag. res. mag. and admstr. of Native law for Ixopo div., 1889-90; app'td. crm. investign. offr., 1895; commanded Natal police field force on S. border during disturbances in E. Griqualand, 1897, and in Zululand, 1898; took part in Boer war, 1899-1902; commanded N.P. field force, in operations round Ladysmith, and during the siege; present at capture of Boer guns on Gun hill, and at battle of Caesar's Camp, on 6th Jan., 1900; with cav. brig. in attack on Botha's Pass, 1900; attached to the F.I.D.; was intell. offr. with Gen. Dartnell's column in the operations under Gen. French in E. Transvaal, and was sent by the latter gen. on spec. mis. to Zululand, 1901; joined Gen. Bullock's col. as intell. offr. in the E. Transvaal, 1901; ag. intell. offr. with General Dartnell's col. in O.R.C., 1901. In charge of sec. serv. dept. in Natal, 1902; sent by Gen. Bruce Hamilton on spec. mis. to Zululand, and was afterwards intell. offr. on that gen.'s staff, in the operations in S.E. Transvaal (medal with 4 clasps); accompd. the Prime Min. of Natal to England, in the capacity of sec., to attend the coronation, 1902; asst. commnr., Natal pol., 1st Dec., 1904; ag. ch. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1905.

CLAUSON, MAJOR J. E., R.E., C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1866; Ed. at Clifton and R.M.A., Woolwich; 1st cl. hons., B.A., Lond., 1887; admitted to Inner Temple, 1897; lieut., R.E., 1897; designed pontoon equipment of army, 1889; passed staff coll. (last) 1893; employed at army headqrs., 1895; sec. to army rly. council, 1897; sec. to mobilization comtee., 1898; sec. to col. def. comtee. (acting 1899) 1900.
CLAXTON, T. FOLKES, F.R.A.S.—Joined mag. and met. dept. R. Obser., Greenwich, Feb., 1890; appd. asst. dir. Royal Alfred obser., Mauritius, Jan., 1896; dir., Dec., 1896; is also sec. met. soc., Maur.
