DYER, SIR WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1882).—B., 1843; Chr. Ch., Oxon, 1873; astt. dir., Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; dir., 1885; ret., 1905; botan. adviser to S. of S. for Col.

DYETT, EDWARD F.—2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1886; admeasurer of vessels, 1889; 1st treasy. offr., June, 1891; comsnr. oath, Nov., 1895; clk. of waterworks comsnrs., 1896; sec. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; holds comsn. to act as treasur. of Montserrat in absence of the comsnr. and has so acted on numerous occasions since Dec., 1897; offl. receiver in bankruptcy, Aug., 1898; holds comsn., dated Feb., 1899, to admnr. oaths of office to ag. comsnr. in absence of comsnr. and govnr.; mem. legis. coun., Mar., 1900.
DYETT, HENRY.—Comsnr.'s and mag.'s clk., Montserrat, Feb., 1896; ag. 2nd treasy. offr. in portions of Apl., May, Sept., 1897; clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1898.

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KOBTRIGHT.—Served in pres.'s office, Virgin Is., 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clk., regist.'s office, Nevis, 1886; 2nd clk., regist.'s office, Antigua, 1887; clk. to trustee, supt. et. lib., July, 1888; comsnr. to admnr. oaths, 1889; marshal V.-A. ct., L.I., Dec., 1889; clk. to mag., dist. A., 1890; dep. cor., dist. A., 1892; ag. postmr., May to Sept., 1894; 1st clk. regist.'s off., Jan., 1896; dep. cor., dist. B., Oct., 1896; ag. mag., dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apl. to Nov., 1897; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comte., June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apl., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. mag., dist. B., May to Sept., 1899; and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (Jan. to Apl.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900, to Mar., 1901; and Apl. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comte., and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; regist. and prov.-marshal and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., June, 1903; regist. and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and ch. regist., Leeward Is., July, 1905; trustee, sup. ct. library, Aug., 1905.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; govt. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., South Naparima, and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.

EARDLEY-WILMOT, COLVILLE.—Writer, Ceylon service, Sept., 1879; in Colomba Kachcheri, Dec., 1879; pol. ct., Badulla, Aug., 1881; in Colomba Kachcheri, Aug., 1882; Kegalla, May, 1883; Anuradhapura, Aug., 1883; astt. collr. and landing survr., Trincomalee, June, 1886; ag. astt. collr. of customs and landing survr., Galle, Apl., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Nov., 1894; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1895; dist. judge, Jaffna, 6th Apl., 1898; ditto, Kalutara, 21st Oct., 1901; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 23rd Nov., 1903.
EARL, ROBERT STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab., 1891). Med. offr., Leogedist., Dominica, July, 1892; med. offr., No. 5 dist. Antigua, 1896; comsnr., Virgin Is.
EASTERBROOK, ARTHUR DOVE.—Asst. collr., B.Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1896; 2nd cls. asst., May, 1902; dist. mag. and collr., N. Nyass, May, 1904.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Ck. col. storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1863; transf'd to civil commissariat, Feb., 1864; acctnt., govt. stores, Feb., 1865; transf'd to treas'y., Mar., 1865; ag. acctnt., treas'y., 1869-70, 1876, 1886-7; acctnt. to treas'y., 1887; under treas., Sept., 1889—1895; capt. vol. artillery, 1876; is in the commission of the peace of the Colony.

EDWARDS, COL. ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apl., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apl., 1892, to Apl., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apl., 1897, to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899, to June, 1900; dist. comanr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers.

EDWARDS, CHAS. HENRY.—Ed. St. Bees gram. schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; exhibtr. of Queen's Coll., B.A., 1894; B.C.L., 1901; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1898; dist. comanr., G. Coast, Doc., 1899; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1904.
EGERTON, RICHARD.—Ed. Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A. 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1869; asst. res. mag., Kingston, May, 1892; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1893.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); ag. sen. dist. offr., Butterworth, P.W., Apl., 1890; a J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offr. assignee and reg. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., May, 1897; dep.-pres.of mun. comansrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apl., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1900; ag. res. counclr., Penang, Feb. to Apl., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; rea. Neg. Sem. Aug., 1902; high comanr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1902; gov. and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904.

EGGETT, W. H.—Apptd., after a comp. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Nov., 1894; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., Apl., 1896; ass. acctnt., 6th Apl., 1899.

EHRHARDT, ALBERT.—Ed. King Edward High Schl., B'ham, and Worc. Coll., Oxford graduated with honours in moda. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Tem. schlrshp., common law, 1886; called to bar, 1889; dist. comanr., Lagos, 1890; travelling comanr. on the E. bndry., Aug., 1891; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and legis. couns., June, 1900; ag. att.-gen., S. 1900; rlyw. comanr. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., May, June, 1902; apptd. res. Ibadan, Feb., 1903; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1904; apptd. as comanr. to produce revised ed. of ordinances of the colony, 1903; ag. ch. jus. May to Oct., 1904.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., OXON.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1894; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schls., S. Sttlmts., June, 1896; also ag. official assignee and reg. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas'y., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instrn., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. comanr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Ck., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and co. exec. coun., 1882; postmr. of Antigua, Apl., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896 and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nov., Apl., 1899, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-New legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899; ag. comanr. of Virgin Is., and ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1903.

ELGEE, CAPT. C. H.—Ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st comanr., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitral, 1899; (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, reliefl Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Ha. Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir Macgregor, gov. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and c. of ex. coun.; ag. insp.-gen., 1902; trav. comanr., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and legis. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903.

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE, 9th EARL (creat. 1833), VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.C. (creat. 1899); G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C., LL.D., D.C.L.; Baron Bruce, 1603; Earl of Kincardine and Baron Bruce of Torry, 1647; Baron Elgin (U.K.), 1849—B. 1849; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, M.A.; treas. of Household and comanr. of wks., 1886; Lord-Lieut. of Fifeshire since 1885; Viceroy of India, 1894-99; chrm. Roy. Comsn. on S. African War, 1902; chrmn., Roy. Comsn. on Free Churches Controversy in Scotland, 1904; chrmn. Roy. Comsn. to administer Scottish Churches Act, 1906; sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1906.

ELLIOT, EDWARD CARLTON.—Ed. Bradfield Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. 5th class clk., govt. secretariat, British Guiana, 1895; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.-W. dist., Br. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartermr. to Haute force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. senior ass't treas., Oct., 1902; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 5th May, 1905.

ELLIOT, LAURENCE S., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1845; clk. to mgs., W. Australia; landing and tide waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmr. and asst. dist. regiar.; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to central bd. of educn., and asst. clk. in legis. coun., 1873; regiar.-gen., regiar. of deeds, and regiar. of brands, 1876; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; 1st clk. and regiar.-gen., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; apptd. to take the census, Apl., 1881, and to superintend its compilation; ch. clk. and asctnt., treasy., Apl. 1881; ag. asst., col. sec., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treas., Jan., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clk. to asst. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to concessions coman., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir S. Ship-pard's boundy. coman., 1894; J.P., 1893; asst. mag., 1898; served in S. African war, including relief of Mafeking; spec. comsnr. for oaths of allegiance in So. dist. of Bech. Prot. to burghers of late S.A.R. and O.F.S.; asst. comsnr., So. dist., Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.
ELLERY, R. L. J., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1827; astronomer, Victoria, 1853.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1826; ch. mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope; in command of Tembu forces during Basuto war; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES BLETTENHAM, L.I.B., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1841; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1859; clk. to Justice Water-meyer, 1863; sec. to examrs. in literature and science; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1867; ch. clk. dept. of lands and wks., Dec., 1872; advoc. sup. ct., 1875; asst. comsnr., crown lands and pub. wks., 1876; gen. manager, rlys., Dec., 1880; is mem. of coun. of Univ., Cape; examr. and moderator of examrs. in science; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, COL. JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1824; entered R.N. in 1838; 2nd lieut., June, 1846; col., 1869; served in the China expdn., 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1852 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; commd. the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in gov.-gen.'s despatch; medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at the bombardment and surrender of the forts of Bomarsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expdn. to Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with clasp, 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as maj. of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., Devonshire Rifle Volr. Corps, 1870; in 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Mid. Term., and joined the western circuit; in 1876 inspr. of prisons and stip. J.P., Br. Guiana; inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1881; adminstd. govt. of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1889; acted as col. sec., Barbados, 1886 and 1894; ret. 1895.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G.—B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. asst. agt., Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apl., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo, 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen. pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; govt. agt., S. prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; govt. agt., W. prov., Sept., 1897; aud.-gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903.
ELLIS, FRANK THOS.—Cadet, S. Sttlmta., 27th Nov., 1903.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—Ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxon; scholar, 1890-1895; prox. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical mods., 1892; Univ. jun. Greek testament prize, 1893; 1st class literae humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's prize, Latin essay, 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clk., 1899.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D., M.R.C.S.—Med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, again 1894; ag. col. res. surg. in 1901, 1902 and 1903.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—Clk. in dept. of agt.-gen. for Tasmania; ch. clk., 1896; sec., 1899; acted as priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.

EMERSON, GEO. HY. K.C.—Speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1896; asst. judge, sup. ct., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Word. Coll., Oxon, 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd clas. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; asst. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-1902; ditto, gram. school, St. Lucia, 1894-95; ditto, Grenada, 1897-98; head master, The Lodge school, 1899.

EMTAGE, WM. THOS. ALLDER.—M.A., Oxford; Fell. of Phys. Soc., Lond.; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; scholar, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, 1880; 1st cls. math. mods., 1881; 1st cls. final maths., 1883; 1st cls. nat. sci., 1885; University Coll., Nottingham, math. lecturer, 1888; asst. prof. math. and phys., 1889; prof. maths. and phys., 1891; principal, tech. inst., Wandsworth (L.C.C.), 1895; director of pub. instr., Mauritius, 1900; examnr., Oxford Locals, maths., 1888, 1889; examnr., final hon. school nat. sci., Oxford, 1890, 1891; author of "Mathematics of Electricity" (Oxford Press), and other works.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1860; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam.before the civ. ser. comsnr., clk. in the accts. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1868; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and sen. asst. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.

ENNIS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACDANIEL.—Ed. at King's Coll. school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. to gov., B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; session judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of general ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of chief ct., B.N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province Alcock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resign., Oct., 1897; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 18th May to 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; reglar., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town mag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of Uganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa.

ENRAGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basutoland police, Mar., 1884; acted as govt. sec., Basutoland, 1893-1895; commanded Bech. Prot. native police with rank of capt., 1896; aast. comsnr., Basutoland, 1897; acted as govt. sec., 1900-1901; mentioned in com-in-chief's despa., 1901; native comsnr., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special comsnr., Swaziland, June, 1902.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—Dist. mag., Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878; dist. mag., Moka, 1883; jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. master sup. ct., May, 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. sen. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1896; ag. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Feb., 1898; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Riv. du Rempart, Mar., 1898; dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Mar., 1899; ag. substitute mast., sup. ct., Aug., 1901.
EVANS, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; Lord Mayor of London, 1891-2; raised funds for relief of distress caused by the Mauritius hurricane and the fire at St. John's, Newfoundland.

EVANS, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transf. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; aast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; aast. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1893; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—Cadet, S. Sttlmtns., Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. protector of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1885; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, May, 1886; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, May, 1887; ag. asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; passed exams. in Chinese and Malay; J.P., ag. dist. offr., Dindinga, 1888; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S. Sttlmtns., Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S. Sttlmtns., Apr., 1895; ag. audr. gen., S.S., May, 1901, to July, 1902; pres., municipal comsrs., Singapore, Sept., 1903; seconded for 6 mos. spec. serv. in Transvaal as prot. of Chinese, 25th Dec., 1903.
EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to registr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apl., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; asst. clk., govt. savings bk., 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, Jan., 1885, to Nov., 1890; acnt., P.W.D., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Mar., 1894; ch. clk., govt. offr., and clk. of coun., Apl., 1894; asst. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900.
EVELYN, E. F.—Acctnt. of post office, Barbados, Sept., 1880.

EVELYN, MARRIT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis, treasy., 1883; prot. of imigrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsnr., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6; water comsnr., Nevis, 1901; mem. quarantine bd., Nevis, 1903.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of inl. rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1885; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EVERETT, COL. SIR W., K.C.M.G.—B. 1844; apptd. one of Brit. comsrs. in Paris for settlement of W. African affairs, 1895-1898.
EVERY, THE REV. EDWARD FRANCIS, M.A.—Bishop of the Falklands, 1902.
EYLES, C. H.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., 1883; col. surg., Br. Honduras, 1887.

EZECHIEL, P. H.—B.A., Bombay, 1889 (at 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhibn. at matric., 1892); major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th (or.) 1898; passed final exam. for Bar, 1902; sec. to Crown agts. for the Colonies, May, 1905.
FABRE, HECTOR, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1834; formerly senator, Canada; hon. comsnr. for Canada Ind. and Col. exhibn.; now Canadian agt., Paris.
FACEY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. clk., June, 1879; 1st cl. clk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892.

FAGAN, JOSEPH PATRICK.—L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.M., Ireland; dist. med. offr., Niger Coast Prot., 13th Apl., 1894; senior med. offr., 29th May, 1903 (medal and clasp); Benin River, 1904 (medal and clasp); Aro Field Force, 1901-2.

FAILLE, GEO. CUNNINGHAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; ag. 2nd clk. to gov., Leeward Is., May, 1889; 2nd clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1890; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, June, 1890; ch. clk. col. sec. and clk. exec. coun., Aug., 1891.

FAIRBAIRN, JOHN.—Temporary clk. in leg. coun. off., Cape Town, Apr., 1881 to 30th Apr., 1889; crown lands off., 31st May, 1890, to 31st July, 1891; clk. to register of mines, Prince Albert, 1st Aug., 1891, to 31st May, 1892; leg. coun. off., 1st June, 1892, to 30th Sept., 1893, and 1st Jan. to 31st May, 1899; clk. of the papers and comte., 1st July, 1899; clk. asst. and taxing offr., 1st Jan., 1904; hon. sec., Central Good Hope Red Cross comte., Victoria League for concentration camp relief, etc., etc.; is an esquire of the order of St. John of Jerusalem, in England, 1901; S. African War medal and clasp, 1899-1901.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT.—Second master, boys' schl., Perth, W. Australia, 1859; clk. to bench of mage., landing waiter, and postmr., Vasse, 1862; res. mag., mag. of local ct., and dist. regiar., 1875; Newcastle, 1875; Vasse, 1880; Kimberley, 1883; Fremantle, 1886.

FALCK, DAVID GEORGE ANOSI.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Nov., 1881; postmr. and telegraphist, Winburg, Nov., 1881; landdrost's elk., Bethlehem, Aug., 1889; ag. landdrost, Heilbron, Apl., 1890; sec., G.P.O., O.F.S., Aug., 1892; landdrost, Ficksburg, 1894 (not assumed); postmr.-gen., O.F.S., 1st Jan., 1895; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.

FALCONBRIDGE, HON. WILLIAM GLENHOLME.—Ed. Toronto Univ.; B.A. (gold medalist), 1866; M.A., 1870; barrister, 1871; regiar., Univ. of Toronto, 1872 to 1881; elected mem. of senate of Univ., 1881; Q.C., 1885; bencher of law society of Upper Canada, 1885; judge of sup. ct., Ontario, Q.B. div., Nov., 1887; pres. high ct. and ch. just., Q.B., 1900.
FALSHAW, PERCY SCOTT.—M.R.C.V.S., Govt. vet. surgeon, Singapore, S. Sttlnts., 15th June, 1893.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Supernum., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 3rd cls. clk., G.P.O., 1893; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1900; postmr.-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Jan., 1905.
FARRELL, WILLIAM OWEN.—Ast. guard, Mauritius rly., 1882; goods elk., 1884.

FARRER, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sep., 1898, Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1900; ag. supt. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd asts. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proscr., July, 1904.
FAULKNER, E.—Ed. at the gram. schl., Free-town; brigade office clk., S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; ch. clk. col. secretariat, 1883; ast. col. sec., 1889.

FAURE, HON. Sir P. H., K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1848; sec. for native affairs, Cape, July, 1890; col. sec., May, 1893; sec. for agricult., Jan., 1896, to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1902-04; M.L.A. for Namaqualand, 1904.
FAWCETT, WILLIAM, B.SC. (LOND.), F.L.S.—Ast. botanical dept., Br. Museum, 1880; dir. pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, 1886; mem. bd. of govs., Jamaica institute, 1887.

FAWKES, ARCHIBALD WALTER, K.C.—Ed. Repton schl.; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1879; regiar., sup. ct., comnrr. of ct. of requests and of stamps, Malacca, 1884; ag. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, 1885; pol. mag. and coroner, Gibraltar, 1886; acted as atty.-gen., 1890 and 1891; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1892; puisne judge, O.R.C., 1901.
FEILDEN, CAPT. R. B.—Capt., R.A.; ed. Wellington Coll.; lieut. R.A., July, 1884; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, May, 1890; to high comnrr., Cyprus, 1892; and to gov. Br. Guiana, 1898.

FELL, T. E., B.A., Cantab., 1897—Apptd. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., G. Coast, 2nd Oct, 1897; ag. asst. col. sec., 14th Sep., 1899; inspr., preventive services, 4th Dec., 1899; ag. trav. comnrr., 1900-1901; trav. comnrr., 16th June, 1902; ag. comnrr. for nat. affairs, 26th June, 1902.

FERGUSON, DONALD.—Elected to provincial legislature, Pr. Ed. Is., 1878; apptd. to provl. cabinet in 1879, with the portfolio of pub. wks.; resig. the latter office in 1880, when he was apptd. provl. sec. and comnrr. of pub. lands; served as deleg. to Downing st. in 1886, regarding communication between P. E. Is. and mainland.

FERGUSSON, RIGHT HON. Sir James, Bart., P.C., M.P., G.C.S.I. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1874), C.I.E. (1880).—B. 1832; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; was lieut. and capt. of the Grenadier Guards; served in the Crimean campaign, including the battles of Alma and Inkerman (wounded) and siege of Sebastopol; was M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57 and 1859-68; unsuccessfully contested Sandwich, 1859, Frome, 1875, Greenock, 1878; was under-sec. of state for India and the home depts. in Lord Derby's 3rd, and Mr. Disraeli's 1st admtns.; gov., S. Australia, 1868; and gov. of N. Zealand, 1873; resig. 1874; gov. of Bombay, Mar., 1880, to Mar., 1888; M.P. for Manchester, 1885 and 1886; under-sec. of state foreign affairs 3rd Aug., 1886; postmr.-gen., 1891-92.
FERNANDEZ, HENRY.—Clerical astt. treas., Br. Guiana, 1887; 5th class clk., 1889; 4th class, 1891.

FERNANDO, MARCUS H.—Ed. Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. and Gilchrist scholar, 1883; B.Sc., 1886; M.B., 1888; gold medallist in med. and forensic med.; M.D., 1889; fellow of Univ. Coll., Lond., 1890; col. surg., civil med. dept., Ceylon; regiar. of Ceylon Med. Coll., lecturer on pathology, 1890, and analyst to gov't. of Ceylon; reported on epidemics of malarial fever, 1895; also in Bombay, 1897, on epidemics of plague; physician, gen. hosp., Colombo, 1897.
FERREIRA, P. J., C.M.G. (1880).—Was comdnt. of local cavalry in the Transvaal against Sikukuni.
FFOLOTTI, LIEUT.-COL. O. HUMAN.—Served in Imp. army, 1858-81; mil. sec. col. forces, Cape, June, 1882.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDES, GEORGE VANDELEUR, C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and ass'tnt. to Sir A. Milner, high comnrr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as priv. clk.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1895; Goldsmiths co. exhib., 1895; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apl., 1906.

FIELDING, HON. WILLIAM STEVENS.—Mem. for Halifax, Nova Scotia assem., 1882; declined premiership, but entered gov't. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he resig.; premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., 1884; min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's admtn., June, 1896.

FIRMSTONE, H. W.—Ed. at Rugby and Hertford Coll., Oxon (scholar); cadet, S. Sttlnts., 1890; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, May, 1894; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1896; dist. offr. P.W., Oct., 1896; asst. protec. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1897; ag. asst. protec. Chinese, supt. Indian imigr., and mun. comsrr. for Penang, Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1902, and from Aug., 1902; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Feb. to July, 1902; ag. asst. ditto, Penang, Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and from Aug., 1903; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of trees, Malacca, Jan., 1905.

FISHER, HON. SIDNEY ARTHUR.—B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb. He first contested Brome unsuccessfully in 1890. In 1897 he was elected, but was defeated in 1891. Re-elected, in 1896, and became min. of agricul. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FISHER, STANLEY.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrenia, Cyprus, May, 1902; acted as King's advocate, May to Oct., 1902.

FISHER, WILLIAM WOODHOUSE.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; ag. crown counsel, N. circuit, Ceylon, Jan., 1880, to Feb., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Matara, June, 1886, to Feb., 1887; ag. crown counsel, Kandy, Feb., 1887; crown counsel, N.W. prov., Ceylon, Mar., 1887; sec. to comte. for drafting code of civ. procedure, June, 1887, to May, 1888; additional crown counsel, N. circuit, Jan., 1890; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1891; ag. puisne judge, 1894; mag. Jamaica, 1895; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Apl., 1906.
FITZGERALD, THE HON. G. P. — Mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92.

FITZPATRICK, HON. CHARLES.—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; first returned to the Quebec legis. assem. at the gen. election of 1894, and again in 1896; solr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FLEISCHER, THEODORE T. C.—Out-door offr. of customs and treasy. dept., 1st Aug., 1878; 4th clk., customs and treasy. dept., 25th Oct., 1878; 3rd clk., 22nd June, 1879; 2nd clk., 16th May, 1882; apptd. 1st class clk. in the treas. dept. in 1885, on separation of the customs from treasy.; ag. chief clk. and book-keeper, treasy., 9th March, 1899, to 10th Jan., 1900; ag. ch. clk. and cashier, 5th June, 1900, to 31st May, 1901.

FLEMING, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1842, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1866; crown solr. for Mauritius, 1869; dist. and stip. mag., 1872; acted as dist. judge, Seychelles, 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1876; atty-gen., Barbados, 1878; acted as ch. just., Sept., 1878, to Mar., 1879; acted as ch. just., St. Lucia, July, 1879; priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Strahan, administering the govt. of the Cape, in 1880; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1881; acted as atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1883; atty-gen., 1884; acted as ch. just., Mar. to Dec., 1885; col. sec., Natal, July, 1886, but did not take up apptmt., being apptd. col. sec. of Mauritius, Dec., 1886; administd. govt., July, 1887, to Dec., 1888; col. sec. Hong Kong, 1889; administd. the govt., Feb. to Dec., 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1892; ditto, Leeward Is., 1895; ret. 1901.

FLEMING, SIR SANDFORD, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.E., C.M.G. (1877), LL.D. (St. Andrews), 1884.—B. 1827; engrnr.-in-ch., Northern Railway, Canada, 1856 to 1863; deleg. to Canadian and Br. govt. from Red River settlers, 1863, in reference to Pacific Rly.; nominated by provs. of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia; govt. engrnr.-in-ch. inter-co. rly., 1863; engrnr.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; elected chancellor, Queen's Univ. Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1885; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. York, to internat. geograph. congress at Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at internat. prime meridians confce., Washington, 1884; dir. Hudson Bay Co. Canadian Pacific rly., etc.; author of "The Interco., an Historical Sketch," 1876, "Old to New Westminster," 1884, memoirs on "A Prime Meridian for all Nations," "Universal Time," and various other wks.; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; representative of Canada, col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer of the C.O., negotiating for landing place for Pacifico cable.
FLETCHER, A. G. M.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; ag. asst. regar. gen., 1903-4; ag. asst. col. sec., 1903.
FLETCHER, GEO. WM.—Temporarily employed in Imp. commissariat, Hobart, 1861; clk. col. treasy., Hobart, July, 1862; receiver and paymr., Jan., 1866.

FLETCHER, STANLEY HWITT.—Mem. Inst. Chartd. Acctnts., 1892; 2nd acctnt.. Br. C. Africs Prot., June, 1893; collr., Zomba dist., Apl., 1900; British vice-consul and admnstr. agt., Chinde, Jan., 1901 (Cent. Africa medal and clasp).

FLETCHER, WM., D.S.O., Surgeon, cap. militia med. staff corps.; 6 mos. in 1898 attached to regulars at R.A.M.C. depot, Aldershot; med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1898; served with Ashanti expnd., 1900 (medal and clasp); Aro expnd., 1901-2 (medal and clasp).

FLYNN, EDMUND JAMES, K.C., LL.D.—Born 1847, at Perce, Gaspe'; ed. Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.L. with honours, 1873, LL.D. (Lav., 1878); Q.C. 1887; called to the bar 1873; prof. of Roman law, Laval Univ., since 1874; mem. legis. assem., Quebec, Apr., 1878; mem. exec. coun. and comsrr. crown lands in Chapleau govt., Oct., 1879, to July, 1882; comm. of rlys. in Ross govt., Feb., 1884, and solr.-gen. May, 1885.

FORD, HENRY FRANCIS.—Entered G.P.C. Cape, 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; apptd. to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, and served as clk. to ch. mag. Transkei; in charge of deeds office, King William's Town 1884; ch. clk., insolvency branch, master's office, Capetown, Jan., 1888; registr. and mstr. high ct., Griqualand, July, 1890.

FORD, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. in France (Academie de Douai), and by private tuition by Dr. Langley at Wolverhampton; sec. inst. Jamaica, 1889; one of the comsrs. of Jamaica internat. exhib., 1891, and spec. comsrr. for Bahamas at that exhib.; apptd. superint. of govt. printing off., Feb., 1891, co-ed. and compiler of "Handbook of Jamaica."

FORD, SIR THEODORE THOMAS, KT. BARC (1888).—B. 1829; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1866; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1874; ag. judge of Penang, 1874, to Apr., 1876; resig. and returned to England; re-apptd. senior puisne judge 1876; ch. justice, 1886; ret., 1889.

FORDE, ROBERT MICHAEL.—Asst. col. surg. Gold Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. dist. comsrr., Axum, 1892; on special service on Anglo-French bndy. comsrr., Feb. to July, 1892; med. offr. special mission to Kumasi, Dec., 1894, to Jan., 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1896.

FOREMAN, JOHN.—Mont., France, L.K.Q.C.P.I. and L.M.; med. offr., Virgin Is., 1878; ag. mag., Virgin Is., 1880; M.L.C., 1880, and mem. ex. coun., 1885; ag. med. offr., Dominica, 1882-1884; med. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1889-1893; med. supt. leper asylum and Poggon hosp., and med. offr., dist. 5, Sandy Point, St. Kitts, 1893; surg.-capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. forces.

FORESTIER-WALKER, GEN. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM EDWARD FORESTIER, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886), Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots. Guards, 1862; capt. and lieut.-col., 1873; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafir war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in desps.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar 1st Aug., 1905.

FORGET, AMÉDÉE EMMANUEL.—Ed. Marieville Coll., Canada; called to the bar, 1871; served as sec. to council of the bar, Quebec; clk. of coun. and priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., N.W. Territ., 1876; asst. comnr. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instr., 1893; Indian comnr., 1896; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898; lieut.-gov. of Saskatchewan, 1st Sept., 1905.

FORREST, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Camb.), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. socy.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geog. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1865; commanded exploring expds. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. Coast; to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. coun., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Socy. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883, reported on the Kimberley dist.; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1865, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901, which he only held a few days, and then took the portfolio of defence.

FORTH, J. OGILVIE.—Audit clk., Fiji, Aug., 1883; acctnt. native taxes and clk. native accts., June, 1889; ch. clk. audit office, Oct., 1890; col. auditor, Nov., 1891; Oct., 1890, proceeded to Tonga to audit accts. of Tongan govt., for which he received thanks of King George Tubou's cabinet.

FOSBERY, WIDENHAM FRANCIS WIDENHAM, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-couns., 1894-5; dist. comnr., 1896; ag. div. comnr., 1897-8; pol. offr., central division expdn., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Bonin Terr. expdn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comnr., 1900; pol. offr., Ijahan expdn., 1901 (medal with clasp); div. comnr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint. expdn., 1902; senior div. comnr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expdn., 1903; ag. high comnr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminstr., 1904; dep. high comnr., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and again in Sept., 1905.

FOSTER, E. ALEXANDER.—I.S.O. (1903); ed. at Kingwood Coll., Bath, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; clk., audit office, Jamaica, Mar., 1866; 2nd clk., May, 1868; ch. clk., island med. office, June, 1882; sec. of cent. bds. of health and quarantine; auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1897; mem. of gen. legis. coun., fed. exec. coun. and exec. and legis. couns., Antigua; J.P., ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., Sept., 1897, Sept. to Oct., 1899, Nov., 1899, to Apr., 1900, and Aug. to Oct., 1901; admnstr., Antigua, in Jan., 1898, Dec., 1899, and in Jan., Feb., Mar., 1900, also in June, July, Aug. and Sept., 1905, under dormant comnr.; holds dormant comnr. to administer govt. of Leeward Is. in certain events.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Ed. common and superior schls., King's County, New Bruns.; Douglas gold medallist, Univ. N.B., 1866; B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-9; returned to parlmt., 1882; min. of marine and fisheries for Dominion, Dec., 1885; min. of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.
FOSTER, RICHARD WITTY, M.L.A., S. Aust., 1893.—Comnr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1905.
FOSTON, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glasa.); col. surg., prov. Wellesley (South), S. Sttlmts., 2oth Aug., 1891; supt. S. S. emigr. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1900.
FOWLER, CHAS. W., C.M.G. (1899).—Late R.N.; asst. collr., Uganda Prot., 7th July, 1896; supt. of marine, 1st Apr., 1898; sub-comnr., 1st Apr., 1904.

FOWLER, FRANK.—Asst. clk., pub. works dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apr., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, govt. ld. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd govt. survr., May, 1886; senr. govt. survr., Apr., 1896; apptd. J.P., Nov. 1900; asst. comnr. of lands and mines, Apr., 1902; comnr. of lands and mines, 1st Apr., 1903; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEO. MERRICK, C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service, Jan., 1874; pol. mag., Galagedara, Sept., 1876; landing survr., Galle, Feb., 1878; pol. mag., Navalapitiya, Jan., 1880; asst. collr. customs, Galle, June, 1880; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1886; asst. govt. agt., Matara, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1888; ditto, N’Eliya, 1891; ditto, Trincomalee, 1895; apptmnt. confirmed, Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897; Negombo, Oct., 1897; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., July, 1897; govt. agt., Ratnapura, June, 1898; ag. govt. agt., W. prov., June, 1899; govt. agt., S. prov., Feb., 1900; govt. agt., and fiscal, W. prov., Mar., 1902.

FOX, HENRY WILSON, B.A.—Ed. Charterhouse, Marlborough Coll., Univ. Coll., Lond., Trin. Coll., Cam.; exhibtnr., Trin. Coll., 1882; foundation scholar, 1883; natural sci. trip.; exhibtnr., Lincoln’s Inn, 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln’s Inn, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir C. Mills, K.C.M.G., 1887 to 1889; admitted to practise in sup. ct., Cape Colony, and high ct., So. Rhodesia, 1894; pub. prosecutor, So. Rhodesia, 1894-1897; manager B.S.A. Co., head office, London, 1898; served during 1896-97 in the Matabeleland and Mashonaland rebellion (medal and clasp and ment. in desps.).
FOX, WALTER.—Asst. supt., botanic gardens, Singapore, 16th June, 1879; supt., forests and gardens, Penang, 7th Dec., 1903.

FOXON, CUTHBERT COLENSO.—Ck., and Zulu interp. to asst. comsnr. and res. mag. Ndwandwe dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Entonjaneni dist., Apl., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z’land. pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apl., 1893; transf. as ck. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist., Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z’land, pol., Apl., 1893; commanded detachment Z’land, pol. with the special comsn. annexation of Sambasas and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy comsnr. Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z’land, Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umlalazi, 1905.

FOXON, FRANK ERNEST.—3rd class ck. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd ck. and Zulu interp., Umgeni div., Nov., 1880; ag. ck. of ct., regisr. circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; ck. of ct., dep. ck. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, UpperUmkonangi div., May, 1887; admstr. native law, Impendlele, May, 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag. R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for col., and mag., Ndwedwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for col., 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in imp. transport dept., afterwards as lieut. Natal N. pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibepu’s contingent and Yamela’s mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal carbiners, Apr., 1889, capt., Dec., 1897; mag. Ixopo div., 1897; served through the Boer war, 1899-1900, including the siege of Ladysmith.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff ck., treas., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit ck., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904.

FRANCIS, COL. CLAUDE AUGUSTUS.—Ed. Craven Coll. and Vienna; entd. Impl. Yeo, 1875; C.M.R., 1876; S. African War (medal with clasp) 1877-8; inspr. constab., and armed pol. and J.P., Br. Guiana, 1880; higher grade, 1883; county inspr., 1891; acted as dep. inspr.gen., 1892, and from Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. inspr.gen., May to Oct., 1896; supt. fire brig., 1895-6; mem. excise, quarantine, and health bds.; author of “A Manual for the Use of Police and Constabulary,” “A Criminal Code,” 1895, and “A Police Catechism,” 1898; sheriff-comdt., armed constab., supt. civ. pol., and J.P., Fiji, 1897; marshal, V.-A. ct.; comdt., European vol. force, which he raised; local col., 1898; M.L.C., 1905.

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—Ck., G.P.O., Fiji, 1898; ditto, treas., 1899; lieut. armed constab., 1901; in command detachment Nadarivatu, 192; ag. adjut., A.N.C., and inspr. pol., 1903; P.S. certif., schl. instruc., Chelsea bks.; passed course musketry and Maxim gun, Hythe, 1905.

FRANKLIN, WILLIAM.—Asst. regisr., natives, Kimberley and De Beers, Mar., 1877; regisr. deaths, Kimberley and De Beers, 1878; clk. to civil comsnr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Jan., 1879; issuer of passes, Mar., 1879; inspr. of claims, Barkly West, June, 1879; inspr. of Sivonelli’s diggings, May-June, 1882; from Oct., 1882 to Aug., 1883, offi. valuator of all diamonds exported from the dist. of Barkly West; apptd. regisr. of claims, Barkly West, Sept., 1883; inspr. and regisr. mines and diggings, Barkly West.

FRASER, CHARLES A.—Ed. at Wellington Coll.; entd. 54th regt., Jan., 1875; resigned, 1877; in colonial forces in S. Africa, 1878 (medal and clasp); asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1882; dist. comsnr., 1889; treas., etc., Falkland Is., 1891; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1893; comdt. and prov.-marshal, Bahamas, 1894.

FRASER, F. BLACHE.—2nd ck. atty.-gen.’s office, Trinidad, Jan., 1865; promoted to col. sec.’s dept. as 3rd ck., Sept., 1867; 3rd landing waiter, customs dept., Jan., 1875; principal landing waiter, Jan., 1883.
FRASER, J. J., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and premier, N. Brunswick; ret., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1883.

FRASER, MALCOLM A. C.—Ed. at King Edward’s schl., Bromsgrove; probation clk. in col. sec.’s office, W. Australia, Apr., 1876; 2nd ck., land and survey dept., June, 1876; transf. to col. sec.’s office, Aug., 1877; clk. and assist. priv. sec. to gov., July, 1878; also ass’t. clk. to the exec. comm.; clk. to gov., Sir W. C. F. Robinson, K.C.M.G., Apr., 1880; corrpdg. clk., col. sec.’s office, Jan., 1881; also meteorological reporter; regisr.-gen., 1891; also regisr. of patents, trade mks., etc.; represented the gov’t. of W. Aust. at the conference of govt. statisticians apptd. to arrange for a uniform census of Australia, Sydney, Jan., 1900; supt. of census, Western Australia, 1900; govt. statistician, 1901; represented W.A. at conference of statisticians convened to arrange for uniformity in the collection and compilation of statistics throughout the Commonwealth of Aust.

FRASER, SIMON JAMES.—Barrister-at-law: a stip. mag. of Tobago and J.P., June, 1868; coroner for the Is., July, 1868; has acted on several occasions as atty.-gen. from 1868; changed to Windward magistracy, Apr., 1874; acted as inspr. of imigrts., 1875 and 1876; inspr. of schls., 1876 to 1884; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1883; comsnr. of roads, dist. No. I, Dec., 1883; pol. mag., Leeward dist., Nov., 1884; chmn. Metayer comshn., Dec., 1884; mem. bd. of educn. and chmn. bd. of health Aug., 1885, and of quarantine bd., Sept., 1887; judge of petty debt court of Bridgetown, Barbados, Jan., 1894.

FRASER, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS, K.C.B. (1900), R.E., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; apptd. C.M.G. for civ. service in connection with the settlement of the Transvaal question; served in expeditry. force in Egypt, 1892, and in the Nile expeditionary force, 1884, with brevet rank 4th class Osmanlieh, and 3rd class of col.; Medjidie.

FREER, GERALD DUDLEY, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1888, M.B. (Lond.), 1898.—House surg. gen. hosp., Birmingham; house surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, July, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893; col. surg. res. Penang, Jan., 1897; ag. col. surg., Penang, June, 1900; col. surg., Singapore, 1st Mar., 1901; principal, med. schl., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1905; mem. S.S. med. coun., July, 1906.

FREMANTLE, ADMIRAL THE HON. EDMUND ROBERT, R.N., SIR, G.C.B., C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; apptd. to the Order of St. M. and St. G. for service during the Ashanti war, 1873-74, having been some time sen. naval offr. on the station.

FRENCH, MAJ.-GEN., SIR GEORGE ARTHUR, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1841; ed. at Sandhurst and Woolwich; joined R.A. as lieut. in 1860; proceeded to North America in Dec., 1861, with expeditionary force sent out in consequence of the "Trent Affair"; adjt. R.A., Kingston, from 1862 to 1866; qualified as 1st class gunnery instructor in 1867; 1st class inspr. of warlike stores in 1868; apptd. I.S.W. at Quebec in 1869 on the withdrawal of the Imp. troops; inspr. of artillery, with rank of lieut.-col., in 1870; organised the permanent batteries of artillery in 1871; comsnr. N.W.M.P., and stip. mag. for the territories, in Dec., 1873; raised, organised, and equipped the force; commanded the expdn. sent from the Red River to the base of the Rocky Mountains in 1874; inspr. of warlike stores, Devonport, 1878 to 1883; comdt. Queensland forces, with rank of col., 1883-91; comdt., N. S. Wales forces, 1896.

FRENCH, JOHN K.—Ck., poor law bd., Sandy Point, Mar., 1894; ck., P.O., St. Kitts, Nov., 1897; ck. to registr. and prov.-marshal, Nevis, Sept., 1901; govt. offr., sub-treasy., Sandy Point, St. Kitts, June, 1905.
FRENCH, SIR S. R., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1849; sec. and acctnt., G.P.O., Cape, Aug., 1880; controller P.O. savings bk., 1884; postmtr.-gen., 1892.
FRERE, AUBREY TEMPLE.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1886; astt. res., May, 1890; res., 2nd class, 4th div., Jan., 1894.

FRERE, BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse school and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902.

FRERE, HAROLD ARTHUR.—Cadet, Br. North Borneo Co.'s serv., Nov., 1897; astt. supt. of gaols, 2nd May, 1898; ag. supt. of pub. wks., 2nd May, 1898; ag. P.M.G., 16th Sept., 1898; sec. to the comsnrs., 2nd Aug., 1899; ag. supt. of gaols, 22nd Jan., 1900; editor "British North Borneo Herald," and manager, govt. printing off., 8th May, 1900; inspr. of prisons, 1st Dec., 1901; resig., to take up apptnt. as supt., Georgetown prison, Br. Guiana, June, 1905.

FRETZ, W. H.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.; med. offr., dist. 2, Nevis, Feb., 1882; dist. 3, S. Kitts, May, 1886; dist. 1, S. Kitts, Aug., 1896; official mem. leg.isl. coun., S. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1896; offl. mem. leg. coun., St. K.-N., Nov., 1896, to Dec., 1900; senior med. offr., health offr., and analyser of vital statistics, Feb., 1903; mem., Basseterre town bd., 1903; offl. M.L.C., 1903.

FROST, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; M.L.A., C. of G. H., field comdt. of Cape vols., and late of C.M.R.; sec. for agricult., Cape Col., 1893-6; ditto, 1902-1904; M.L.A. for Queenstown, 1904.

FROUDE, ASHLEY A., B.A., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1863; ed. Westminster and Oriel Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. (unpaid) to Sir Robert Herbert at the C.O., Nov., 1886; sec. to roy. coman. for the division of Malta into electoral dists., Jan., 1888; sec. to the Behring Sea coman., June, 1891, and to the joint Behring Sea coman. at Washington, Jan., 1892; on the staff of the Br. agt., Behring Sea arbitration, 1892-3.

FRY, R. S.—Trigonometrical ast., survey dept., S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1881; res. Aug., 1884; rejoined July, 1885; dist. survr., Sept., 1885; ch. survr., Dec., 1888; observer for time balls and dep. regist. of shipping, Jan., 1894.

FRY, WM. HERBERT, L.R.C.P. (Loud.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—House surgeon, S. Sttlmts., 13th Aug., 1896; supernumerary col. surg., Sing., 1st Apr., 1900; col. surg., prov. Wellesley, South, 17th Nov. 1900; ditto, prov. Wellesley, North, 1st Mar., 1901; state surg., Pahang, F.M.S., June, 1905.
FURLINGTON, R.—Harbourmr., Melbourne, 1877; ch. harbourmr., pres. pilot bd., and chmn. steam navig. bd., 1882; capt. comdg. Victorian naval brigade, 1871; mem. of coun. of defence, 1884.
FULLER, A. J.—M.L.A. for Tembuland, Cape Colony, since 1898; sec. for agric., Feb., 1904.
FULLER, F. C.—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; res. comsnr., Rotumah, 1889; dis. comsnr., Lagos, 1892; res. of Ibadan, 1897; col. treas., Lagos, 1901; astt. sec. to govt., Malta, 1902; mem. exec. coun.

FULLER, SIR THOS. EKINS, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1903).—Emigtn. agt. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1872; resigned, 1876; served as mem. of house of ass. for 23 years; agent-gen. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1st Jan., 1902; J.P., Cape Town; has served on various councils and comtees. in Cape Colony.
FULLONG, L. O'B.—M.H.A., St. John's, E. Newtdld., 1893; mem. exec. coun. and chmn. bd. of wks., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., St. John's, E., 1897; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1905.

FYSHE, THE HON. SIR PHILIP OAKLEY, K.C.M.G., (1896).—B. 1835; treas. in Mr. Kennsley's ministry, Tasmania, 1873-5, and without portfolio to July, 1876; premier without office, Aug., 1877, to Mar., 1878, and member without office of Mr. Giblin's 1st administration; maj. comdg. Tasmanian volr. rifle regt. 1880-4; again premier, Mar., 1887, to Aug., 1892; treas. in the Braddon Ministry, 1894-9; agt.-gen. for the col. in London, 1899 to 1901.
FYSON, PERCIVAL WILLIFRID.—Astt. govt. printer, Perak, 1896-99; head printer, Br. Cent. Africa Prot., July, 1899.
GABRIEL, J. S., M.R.C.S.E.—Med. offr., Antigua, dist. 6, 1881.

GAGNON, CHARLES ANTOINE ERNEST.—Ed. St. Ann's Coll.; notary public, 1869; since 1885 pres. of Quebec board of notaries; elected to Quebec legislature, 1878-81, and since 1883; prov. sec., 1887; sheriff of Quebec, 1890.

GALE, CHARLES HENRY.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; articled pupil, 1882-5; astt. and resident engrnr., 1890; ch. draughtsman P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890; astt. engnr., Jan., 1900, and ag. exec. engrnr., May, 1900; exec. engrnr., Feb., 1901; J.P., 1904.

GALE, WALTER A.—Ed. high schl., Perth, St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; 3rd master, high schl., Perth, 1886; astt. registr., col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, 1886; sec. to cent. bd. of educ., 1888; regiar.-gen.; regiar. of patents, etc., Sept., 1890; in charge of census for 1891; clk. of legis. assem., 1891; transf. to Commonwealth govt., 1901.
GALEA, HENRI.—Master, sup. ct., and regiar., Mauritius, 4th Feb., 1899.

GALLWEY, LT.-COL. HENRY LIONEL, C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1887; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsnr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Jan., 1891; dep. comsnr. and consul, Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comsnr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (deeps., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (deeps., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (deeps.); gov., St. Helena, 1902.

GALLWEY, SIR MICHAEL HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1826; ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A.; barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trin. term, 1853; Munster circuit; atty.-gen., Natal, 1857; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Transvaal and Zululand bndry. comsn., 1878; received the thanks of the sec. of state; ch. just., Natal, 1890; ret. 1902.

GALLWEY, Lieut.-Gen. Sir THOMAS LIONEL J., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1821; 2nd lieut., R.E., Mar., 1839; served in the West Indies from Jan., 1842, to June, 1845; employed in Ireland during the famine, 1846-47, as inspecting officer, under the bd. of works; served in Canada, 1849-58; employed 1858-62 in the construction of fortified position, Gosport; mem. of the ordnance select comttee., 1862-65; mem. of special mil. comsn. to the United States during the war of 1864; employed as C.R.E. in building wks. of defence at Quebec, 1865-68; comdt., schl. of mil. engineering at Chatham, 1868-75; comdg. roy. engnr., and col. on staff, Gibraltar, 1877-79; inspr.-gen. of fortifications, 1880-82; gov. and comdr.-in-ch. of Bermuda, 1882-88.

GARDINER, JOHN.—Entd. govt. ser., S. Austral., in ch. sec.'s off., 1866; treasy., 1871; clk., engnr.-in-chief's dept., 1874; acctnt., 1876; ch. acctnt., 1888; sec. comsnr. P.W., and chrmn. supply and tender bd., 1899; transf. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust. as pub. serv. inspr., 1902.

GARDNER, HARRY.—Major (ret.), Roy. Scots; served in Bechuanaland expdn., 1884, and Zululand, 1888; ag. col. sec. and rec.-gen., St. Helena, 1886-7; ag. treas., Zululand, 1890; confirmed, 1891; acting. offr. for prov. of Zululand, 1897; impr., audit dept., 1899.

GARLAND, PATRICK JOSEPH.—Apptd. a sen. med. offr., Northern Territories, 1899; served on Fra Fra expdn., under Captain Donald Stewart, June and July, 1899; specially mentioned in despatches; served on Neutral Zone expdn., Feb., 1900; mentioned in despatches; served on Fra Fra expdn., Mar., 1900; served during Ashanti rebellion and siege of Kumasi; acted as P.M.O. to column during retirement of Sir F. M. Hodgson, governor; specially mentioned in despatches; apptd. dist. comsnr. of Axim Dist., 1901.
GARLING, HENRY CHAS.—Supt.'s asst., Skerrett's farm and school, Antigua, 18th Apr., 1894; ag. asst. supt. of agric., Feb., 1904; 2nd outdoor offr., treasy., Feb., 1905.

GARNEAU, PIERRE.—Merchant and mem. of bd. of trade, Quebec; late govt. dir. of North Shore railway; mayor, 1870-73; M.L.A., Quebec, 1873-78 and 1881-86; mem. of prov. exec. coun. and ministry as comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands, 1874-78; M.L.C., Quebec, 1887, and again comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands to 1891.

GARRARD, C. G.—Ed. Haileybury; dep. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, Jan., 1892; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., Singapore, Feb. to Oct., 1893; ag. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, July, 1894; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1896; also ag. collr., land rev., and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Aug., 1895; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1896; ast. regiar. and mag., Malacca, 1896.

GARRAWAY, DAVID G.—Entered the provost-marshal's office, Grenada, in Jan., 1867; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1868; clk. of couns., 1868 to 1873; dep.-sec., regiar., and clk. of the crown, and sec. bd. of educ., 1871 to 1878; comsnr. of census, 1871; ag. auditor, 1871 to 1872, and 1875; sec. to exec. comttee., 1875-6; ag. treas., 1876-7, 1880-1-2; and ex officio a mem. of the legislature; auditor, 1878; notary public, 1880; compiler of census, 1881; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1883-4; treas., St. Lucia, July, 1884; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; performed duties of col. sec. in conjunction with his own during visit of gov.-in-ch., 1889 and 1896; adminstrd. govt., part of Feb., 1899; comp. of customs.; Br. Guiana, Sept., 1900; mem. of excise bd.; cent. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., div. pub. offr. guar. fund.

GARRAWAY, D. JULIAN.—Entd. pub. serv., St. Lucia, Mar., 1892, as supernum. clk., treasy.; ag. 4th clk., Nov., 1892; clk., P.O., June, 1893; ag. clk. to admistr.-in-chief, Windward Is., Aug., 1893; 2nd clk. regiar.'s off., June, 1896; ch. clk., Jan., 1898; ag. regiar., sheriff and administr.-gen., on 3 occasions, 1901-2-3; rev. offr., Grenada, June, 1903; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., Feb., 1904; additional coroner, West Dist., Mar., 1904; ag. pol. mag., North Dist., for spec. purpose of hearing appeals agt. town bd. assessmnts for 1904; ast. tax offr. in connectn. with hearing appeals agt. assessmnts. for land and house taxes for 1904.

GARRAWAY, E. C. F., L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. surg., Millwood, Knysna, Cape Colony, 1888; ditto, Kuruman, Br. Bech., 1891; surg., Bech. Bord. pol., 1892; divanl. med. offr., S.A.C., with rank of major, 1901; served in Matabele wars and Boer war (1899-1902).
GARRAWAY, R. F.—Postmr., Dominica, June, 1881; sec. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., Sept., 1881; ag. dist. mag., dist. G., Apr., 1893.

GARRETT, MARK.—In F.A.M.P., Cape, July, 1876, to Nov., 1879; clk. to C.C. and R.M. King William's Town, Sept., 1880; clk. col. sec.'s office, Capetown, May, 1884; sec. civ. ser. comsnr., Mar., 1886; prin. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1886; sub-comsnr., war losses compensation comsnr., Mar. to Sept., 1903; census staff, col. sec.'s office, prin. clk., pol. branch, atty.-gen.'s office, Aug., 1904.

GARRICK, THE HON. SIR J.F., K.C., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1836; sec. for public lands and mines, Queensland, 6th Feb., 1878; atty.-gen., Dec., 1878, to Jan., 1879; col. treas. and postmr.-gen., 13th Nov. to Dec., 1883, when he resig. the former office; agt.-gen. for that col. in London, June, 1884, to June, 1888; was deleg. to the col. confe., 1887; again agt.-gen., 1890-5.

GATT, C.—Supt. govt. printing office, Malta, Feb., 1882; was priv. sec. to Sir Richard Wood, H.M.'s diplomatic agt. and consul-gen., Tunis, Sept., 1868, to May, 1871; asst. sec., Gozo, 1892; rec-gen., 1899; audr.-gen., 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

GATT, L., C.M.G. (1901), C.E.—Entered the Malta ser., Mar., 1883, as land survr.; rly. inspr., 1884; asst. engr., waterwks., 1885; survr. P.W.D., 1888; manager and engr., Malta rly., 1896; ch. engr., waterwks., and elect. lighting dept., 1896; supt. of P.W., with a seat in exec. coun., and a seat in coun. of govt., 1897.

GATTY, SIR STEPHEN HERBERT, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—Scholar Winchester schl. and New Coll., Oxon; called to bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1874; went the N.E. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, June, 1883; ag. ch. justice and local comsnr., W. Ind. incumbered estates ct. for Antigua, June to Oct., 1884; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, July, 1884; local comsnr. incumbered estates ct. for St. Kitts, Oct., 1884; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Dec., 1885; chancellor of diocese and adm'tg. advoc., 1887; chmn. roy. comsn. on franchise and electoral dists., 1888, and of royal comsn. on Metayer system in Tobago, 1890; puisne judge, S. Stilmts., 1892; chief just., Gibraltar, 1895; resig., Mar., 1905.
GAVIN, J. C.—Sec. to treasy., rec-gen., and paymr.-gen., New Zealand, Mar., 1860; asst. comptroller and audr.-gen., 1890.
GEAR, HON. HENRY.—M.H.A. for dist. of Burin, Newfoundland, 1900; again elected, 1904; mem. exec. coun., 1903.

GEIKIE, R.—Ed. at Harrow and Cambridge; undergraduate scholarship, King's Coll., 1895; Winchester prizeman, 1896; 1st class hist. tripos, 1896; elected fellow King's Coll., 1900; apptd., after open compet. exam., 2nd class clk. Local Govt. Bd., Nov., 1897; 2nd class clk., C.O., Mar., 1899.
GELLIBRAND, HON. W. A. B.—Mem. legis. coun., Tasmania, since 1871; formerly pres. legis. coun.
GEMMEL, GEORGE.—Clk. treasy. dept., Seychelles, Feb., 1887; clk. to admr., Feb., 1889; clk. of councils, Jan., 1897; 1st customs offr., May, 1900; postmtr., June, 1905.

GENIS, MICHAEL CHRISTIAN.—Clk. to state atty., Transvaal, 1874; clk. to R.M., public prosecutor, and postmtr., Christiana, 1876; C.C. and R.M., Christiana, 1880; ditto, Rustenburg, July, 1880; pensioned upon retrocession in 1881; registr. of deeds, Stellaland, Sept., 1884; also treas. and distributor of stamps; clk. to land comsnr., Bruchuanaland, Nov., 1885; registry clk. and J.P., Vryburg, July, 1886.
GENNADIUS, P.—Dir. of agricul., Cyprus, 1896.
GEORGE, WM. JAMES, J.P.—Ed. Midland Institute, Birmingham; M.L.A., W. Aust., 1895 to 1902; comsnr. of rlyws., July, 1902.

GIBBES, REGINALD PRESCOTT.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1889; passed final exam., Tamil, Jan., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Aug. to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P. W., Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Malay, 1894; 2nd asst. prot. of imigts., Penang, May, 1896; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1897; transf'd. to Calcutta as (temp.) asst. emigtn. agt. for Trinidad, Mauritius, Fiji, Jamaica, &c., Aug., 1900; appt. confirmed, Aug., 1901; passed exam. in Hindustani, May, 1901; ag. emigtn. agt., 19th Feb. to 12th Oct., 1902; emigtn. agt. 14th Sept., 1903.
GIBBS, LAWRENCE.—A.M.I.C.E., grad. assoc., and county engr.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. exec. engr., 1896.

GIBRALTAR, LORD BISHOP OF, Rt. REV. WM. ED. COLLINS.—Late Exhib. of Selw. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Jun. Opt.), 1887; Lightfoot schl., 1889; Prince Consort pri., 1890; M.A., 1891; B.D., 1902; D.D., 1903; F.C. of All Hallow's, Barking, Lond., 1890-1; lectr. of Selw. Coll., 1891-3; lectr. of St. John's Coll., Camb., 1891-4; exam. hist. trip., 1894-5; select pr., Camb., 1899-1902; prof. of eccles. hist., King's Coll., Lond., 1893-1904; C. of All Hallows, Barking, 1894; exam. chap. to Bp. of St. Albans (Festing), 1897-1903; Bishop of Gibraltar, 25th Jan., 1904.

GIBSON, FRED. ALLAN, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1860; clk. Mauritius civ. ser., after exam., Oct., 1875; elk. despatch branch, Sept., 1878; ag. précis writer, Nov., 1882, to June, 1883; ag. oh. clk. and sec. to exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1890; elk. exec. and legis. couns., Sept., 1892; ag. jun. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1892; ch. elk., Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., June, 1894, to Jan., 1896; June to Dec., 1895, Mar. to Sept., 1896, Jan. to Nov., 1897; ag. regist.-gen., Jan. to Sept., 1899; nom. mem. coun. of govt., Apl. to Sept., 1899; ag. asst. col. sec., July, 1900; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1901; collr. of cust., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903, to 19th Aug., 1904; ditto, 19th Sept. to 18th Dec., 1904.

GIBSON, HENRY JAMES, C.B. (1902), B.A.—B. 1860; ed. Rossall and St. John's Coll., Oxford; Casberd scholar; 1st cl. class. mods.; elk. War Office, 1885; princ. elk., 1897; asst. acctnt.-gen., 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1903; asst. dir. of Army finance, 1904; priv. sec. to Mr. Woodall, 1894-95, to Hon. St. John Brodrick, 1895-6; asst. comptr. and audr., 1905; is hon. ast. audr. for certain Crown Colonics and Prots., ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for the Colonies.

GIBSON, JAS. YOUNG.—Clk. and Zulu interp. to ct. of R.M.; sub-distr. of stamps, and regisr. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, Apr., 1882; also dep. clk. of peace, Mar., 1887; asst. comar. and R.M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, Ndawande dist., May, 1889; mag. for colony; mag., Umvoti div., 1900; J.P. for colony, 1902; ag. mast., sup. ct., 1904; mag., Up. Umkomanzi, 1905.

GILES, MORTIMER.—Entd. survey dept., S. Australia, 1864; draughtsman, 1875; clk. and asst. draughtsman, lands titles off., 1876; draughtsman, 1889; draughtsman and 3rd dep. regisr.-gen. of deeds, 1901; regisr.-gen. of deeds and regisr. of buildg. socs., 1904.
GILL, C. I.—Puisne judge, super. ct., Montreal prov. of Quebec, 1879.
GILL, THOMAS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered col. service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; acctnt., treasy., Jan., 1883; under treas., 1894.
GILL, WALTER, F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Conservator of forests, S. Australia, July, 1890.

GILLIES, THE HON. DUNCAN.—Premier and treas. of Victoria, also min. of rlys., and min. of mines; resig., Nov., 1890; pres. of the bd. of land and wks., and comsnr. of crown lands and surveys, May, 1868; held the office of comsnr. of rlys. and roads, June, 1872, to Aug., 1875; was comsnr. of crown lands, &c., Oct., 1875, to May, 1877; comsnr. of rlys., &c., Mar. to Aug., 1880; also Mar., 1883, to Feb., 1886, when he took office as premier and treas. on the retirement of Mr. Service; resig. 1891; was one of the representatives of Victoria at the Federation Convention, 1891; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1894 to 1897.

GILMAN, E.W.F., B.A., Oxon.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th magis, Sing., Nov., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, June, 1902; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigrants, Penang, Apl., 1902; confirmed, July, 1904.
GIROUARD, DESIRÉ, K.C.—Ed. Montreal Coll.; called to the bar, Canada, 1860; K.C., 1876; represented Jacques Cartier co., 1878 to 1895; judge sup. ct., Canada, 1895.

GIROUARD, LT.-COL. SIR EDWARD PERCY CRANWELL, K.C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O., R.E.—Served with Dongola expeditionary force, 1896 (desp., brevet-major); British medal, Khedive's medal, 2 clasps); Nile expedn., 1897 (desp., clasp); rlyw. traff. man., Woolwich Arsenal, 1890-5; dir. of Soudan rlyws., 1896-8; pres. Egyptian rlyw. bd., 1898-9 (2nd cls. Medjidie); S. African war, 1899-1902 (desp.); comsnr. of rlyws., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902; mem. I.C.C.
GISBORNE, HARTLEY, A.M., Camb., C.E., M.A.I.E.E.—Ed. at Mercers' schl. and Windsor Coll., Canada; asst. geol. survr., Canada, 1876; tel. engrnr. 1880; dist. tel. supt. Manitoba, 1882.

GLASGOW, 7TH EARL OF, THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE, G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1833; capt. (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Avonshire, and chmn. of co. coun.; gov. of N.Z., 1892-97.

GLASIER, F. BEDFORD.—Asst. div. engrnr., Bermuda harbr. wks., June, 1894; engrnr.-in-charge, Coatracoalos harbr. wks., Mexico, May, 1896; dist. engrnr., S. Leone govt. rly., June, 1897; dist. engrnr., Tarkwa rly., Gold Coast, Oct. 1898; and gen. manager, S. Leone govt. rly., May, 1899; promoted gen. manager, Lagos govt. rly., Apr., 1900.

GLASS, DAVID, K.C.—Ed. gram. schl., London (Ont.); called to the bar; Q.C. for Ontario, 1875; elected alderman in 1855; and mayor in 1858-64-65; mem. of commons for E. Middlesex, Canada, 1872; has been pol. mag., recorder, and also dep. judge of Middlesex, and bencher of law soc., Ont.; removed to Manitoba, 1882; solr. of Winnipeg, 1884; mem. legis. assem., Winnipeg, 1886; unanimously elected speaker, 1887 to 1888.

GLYDE, ADOLPHUS YEOVIL.—Apptd. clk. in land titles dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1882; dep. regis. of titles, ag. regis. of deeds, sub. collr. of internal rev., asst. regis. of titles, 1893; ag. regis. of deeds, Nov. 1896, to Jan., 1897.
GOBEIL, ANTOINE, I.S.O. (1904)—Law clk., dept. of pub. wks., Canada, 1879 to 1885; then sec., dep. min.of pub. wks., Jan., 1891.

GODBOLT, THOMAS.—Entd. N. S. Wales public serv., July, 1871, as jun. clk., govt. printing off.; asst. paymaster of imperial pensions, 1877; hd. of trade clk. and cashier, govt. shipping office, 1881; emergency offr., col. treasy., 1891; paymaster of imperial pensions and allowances, 1895; paymaster, N. S. Wales Imperial Bushmen's contingent (local pay and allowances), 1900, and chief paymaster (for local pay and allowances), N. S. Wales Naval Contingent to China, 1900.

GODFREY, JOSEPH ED.—M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regis. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., registr.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., registr.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, July, 1904.
GODFREY, LEOPOLD HENRY.—Active serv. volunteer in 1st batt., Royal Sussex regt., Feb., 1901, to May, 1902; architectural draughtsman, pub. wks. dept., O.R.C., June, 1902.

GOLDIE, CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., an examg. offr. H.M. customs, Greenock, Sept., 1863; inspr. of invoices and ch. landing survr. H.M. customs, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; one of the commrs. to inquire into the pilot service of Jamaica, Apr., 1870; ag. collr. of customs, Kingston, 1873, and 1875 to 1876; acted in 1874 as island treas., and as collr.-gen. of customs, 1875 and 1885; collr. of customs and shipping master, Kingston, 1883.

GOLDNEY, SIR JOHN TANKERVILLE, KT. BACH. (1893).—B. 1846; ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1869, N. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1880; ag. ch. just., May, 1881; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, June, 1883; judge sup., st. Sttlmts., Mar., 1887; ch. just., Trinidad, 1892; retired, 1902.

GOLLAN, H. C.—Ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, comsnr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., 1st Jan.-21st Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admnstr., 1st June-31st Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., 21st Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., 16th Nov., 1900-4th Apr., 1901; ch. just., 4th Nov., 1901; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904.

GOMPERTZ, H. H. J.—Ed. at Bedford schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxon (scholar); 2nd cl. lit. hum., B.A. (1890); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1890; passed final exam. Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Aug., 1894, to Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1896; actg. deputy-regist., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Hokkien, 1897; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, 1st Jan., 1897; transf'd. to Hong Kong as asst. regist.-gen., 1897; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1899; actg. sen. mag., Hong Kong, June, 1899, to June, 1900; asst. col. sec., Hong Kong, Jan. 1st, 1900; mem. land ct., Hong Kong, 1st June, 1900; pres. land ct., 10th Jan., 1902; ag. pol. mag., 1904-5.
GOODMAN, CLIFFORD E.—Entered pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1890; 5th clk., audr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1891; ch. clk., inspr.-gen.'s office, July, 1892; also regis. of births, dist. A.

GOODMAN, GERALD AUBREY.—Ed. at Lodge schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; 1st com. law schlr., Mid. Temp. Trinity term, 1885; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1885; ag. judge of petty debt ct., and of ass't. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1889; J.P., 1889; ag. solr.-gen. in 1890-1-2; M.H.A. since Oct., 1889; mem. bd. of educ. since Oct., 1891; solr.-gen., Mar., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., 1891, 1898, 1900-1-2-3-4; K.C. for Barbados, 1903; mem. of quar. bd. and gen. bd. of health, 1902.

GOODMAN, SIR WILLIAM MEIGH, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comsnr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st April, 1902; ret., 1905.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Mem. of house of assem. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1885; for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resign. of the Whiteway ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col., which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsrs. under the Thorburn admstr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.
GOOLD, ALBERT WM. FISHER.—Supernum. inspr. of pol. Mauritius, 19th Dec., 1881; inspr., 16th Sept., 1894; ch. offr., 1st Nov., 1898; inspr.-gen., 17th Aug., 1902.

GOOLD-ADAMS, MAJOR SIR H. J., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.B. (1898), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1858; Roy. Scots fus.; comdg. a troop Bechuanaland border police, Aug., 1885; comdt., 1888; major, 1889; again comdt., 1893-5; commanded field force against Matabele, 1893; res. comsrr. for Bechuanaland Protectorate; dep. admnr., Orange River Col., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., 7th Aug., 1901.
GORDON, ARTHUR HENRY WYNHAMD.—Supt. of police, Mauritius, 28th Nov., 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., 2nd Aug., 1892; supt. of prisons and reformatory, 25th Oct., 1892.

GORDON, ARTHUR JOHN LEWIS, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; priv. sec. to the gov. of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1870; priv. sec. to gov. of Mauritius, 1870; priv. sec. to the gov. of Fiji, 1875-80; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Canada (Earl of Aberdeen), 1893.

GORDON, BRIG.-GEN., J.M., C.B. (1901).—Lieut. R.A., from the R.M. Acad., Woolwich, 1875; lieut. staff instr. R.V. force, S. Australia, Dec., 1881; on the formation of a permanent artillery force in S. Australia, apptd. lieut.-comdt., Sept., 1882; capt., Aug., 1883; maj., May, 1885; ag. D.A.A.G., Dec., 1885; and hon. A.D.C., May, 1886; comdt. S. Australia mil. forces, 1893; lieut.-col., 1892; col., 1895; brig.-gen., 1901; inspr. mil. stores for Australian cols., 1898-9; transf., with mil. dep. dept., to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901.
GORDON, JAMES.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1881; stip. mag., Adelaide, 1892; pol. mag., Adelaide, 1902; mem. of Adelaide licensing bench, 1892; chrmn. ditto, 1901; mem. central bd. of health, 1901.

GORDON, THE HON JOHN HANNAH, K.C.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1876; M.L.C., 1888; min. of ed., 1889-90 and 1892; ch. sec., 1893-6; atty.-gen., 1890-92; atty.-gen. and min. of ed., 1902-3; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1903.

GORDON, WILLIAM MONTGOMERIE.—Ed. Edin. Acad.; clk. to consul-gen. of W. Pacific, Aug., 1879; clk. of exec. coun. and ch. clk. of crown lands in col. of Fiji, May, 1880; also acted as sec. to landa comsrs.; res. comsrr. and stip. mag. for the island of Rotumah, May, 1882; comsrr. to inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stip. mag., Fiji, Apr., 1886; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, June, 1887; ch. clk. col. secy.'s off., Leewards, and clk. of fedl., exec. and legis. couns., and of the local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1889; ag. comsrr., Montserrat, Dec., 1891, to Mar., 1892, Mar., 1894, to June, 1896, and July to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Leeward Islds., Aug. to Nov., 1900; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, July, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Aug.-Sept., 1903, Mar.-Aug., 1904, Apr.-Sept., 1905; admstd. govt., 25th-28th Aug., 1906.

GORE, LIEUT.-COL. JAMES C.—Audit.-gen. W. Africa Stlmnts., from 1882 to 1887; apptd. to admstr. govt. of the Gambia, June to Nov., 1894; col. sec. S. Leone, 1894; held a dormant coman. to admstr. govt. of the col. of S. Leone under certain circumstances; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1901.

GOTTLIBE, F. H. VALENTINE.—Entered govt. service, S. Stlmnts., Oct., 1880; ch. clk. G.P.O., Singapore, 1888; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1892; dep. regisr. sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., 1896; ag. ast. regisr. sup. ct. and mag., Malacca, Mar., 1898; also ag. sheriff, Sep., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ast. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, July, 1904.
GOUGE, HERBERT DILLON.—Assessor, land tax dept., S. Australia, Apl., 1885; pub. actuary, Mar., 1894.
