GOUGH, FREDERICK.—Messenger in office of Woods and Forests, 12th Apl., 1869; apptd., after exam., mess. C.O., 20th Jan., 1871; Queen's mess., Mar., 1880; sent three times on foreign service; acted as Queen's page-in-waiting on col. premiers during diam. jub., 1897; read. address of thanks, and jub. medal, 1897; res. off. keeper, C.O., Mar., 1898; apptd. by the S. of S. to be in attendance on col. premiers during their Coronation visit, 1902; (received joint letter of thanks, and Coronation medal).

GOULBURN, 1ST BISHOP OF—RIGHT REV. MESAC THOMAS, D.D. Ed. at Trin. Coll., Camb.; graduated B.A. 1840; M.A. 1843; ordained deacon, 1840; priest, 1841; vicar of Tuddenham St. Martin, Suffolk; sec. to the col. and continental church society. Goulburn formed part of the diocese of N. S. Wales until its separation in 1863.
GOULD, JULIAN BARING.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Mar. 1897; asst. res., Nov., 1900; res., 2nd cls., Jan., 1902.

GOURDEAU DE BEAULIEU, MAJOR FRANZOIS FREDERIC.—Priv. sec. to Hon. J. Cauchon; mem. of Mackenzie admin., 1870; ch. clk. and acctnt. dept. of marine and fisheries; dep. min. of marine and fisheries of Canada, May, 1896.

GOWAN, SIR JAMES ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1893), LL.D.—B. 1815; lieut., 4th regt., North York, 1838; called to Canadian bar, 1839; judge of Simcoe, 1842-83; chairman of bd. of pub. instruction, 1844 to 1871; comsrr. for consolidating statute law of Canada, 1868-69; chmn. of bd. of judges, 1869-86; engaged in consolidation of the crim. law for Dominion, 1869; mem. of "The Law Reform Coman," 1871; roy. comsrr. to inquire into charges against the ministry in reference to the Pacific rly. contract, 1873; comsrr. for consolidating the statutes of Ontario, 1876-77; apptd. to high ct. of just., 1882; a senator of the Dominion, 1885; chmn. of Barrie Collegiate Inst., by annual election, 1881-87; called to Irish bar, 1889.

GOWDEY, WM. WALLACE.—Solr., Barbados, 1881; mem. house of assem., 1883-93; mem. of comittee of inquiry into Dodd's reformatory and industrial schl., 1884; mem. bd. of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; mem. of financial inquiry comsrr., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge petty debt ct., Dist. E. Barbados, 1892; receiver of wrecks, Dist. E., July, 1897; pol. mag., Dist. A., Oct., 1901; visiting just. Lazaretto, Jan., 1902; receiver of wrecks, Dist. A., May, 1902; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., May and June, 1902; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal. July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; comsnr. of probates, Aug., 1902; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Dec., 1902; ag. coroner, Dist. A, March to May, 1903; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, April, 1903; ag. inspr. of prisons, May, 1903.

GRABHAM, MICHAEL. M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. Lon., M.A., M.B., B.C., Camb., 1891.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St. Thomas's hosp., Lon.; B.A. Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences, tripods and exhibitioner, St. John's Coll.; asst. med offr., city asylum, Birmingham, 1891; surg. to the lying-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1892; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bd. of governors, Institute of Jamaica, 1902; joint author, with F. V. Theobald, S.E. Agric. Coll., "A Monograph on the Mosquitoes of Jamaica," 1905.

GRAHAM, FREDK., C.B. (1899).—B. 1848; apptd. clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., after compet. exam., 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 6th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1883; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; 1st class clk., Dec., 1887; ag. prin. clk., Feb., 1892; prin. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897.

GRAHAM, SIR JOHN JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1847; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clk. to regis. E. dists. ct., May, 1865; asst. regis., Jan., 1868; asst. regis., sup. ct., Mar., 1872; regis. and master, E. dists. ct., Feb., 1875; ch. clk. to atty.-gen. and clk. of peace, Capetown, Jan., 1878; sec. law dept., July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing offr., sup. ct., May, 1884; mem. of the col. tender bd.; a J.P. for the col.; sec. to law dept., 1889.
GRAINGER, HENRY ALLERDALE.—Mem. H. of Ass. S. Austr., 1884, 1890 and 1901; state agent in London for S. Austr., 1901; ag.-gen., 1902-1905.

GRANDJEAN, REV. J. F. J.—Ed. at Bastogne and Floreffe (Belgium); student in theology at Paris, 1864 to 1870; prof. at Nice, 1870 to 1872; priest in 1871; missionary in France, 1872 to 1874; preacher at St. Martin's-le-Grand, London, 1874 to 1876; ag. curate of Grouville and St. Heliers (Jersey), 1876 to 1878; curate of Melton Mowbray, 1879; civil chaplain of the Seychelles, 1880; senior curate, St. Andrew and St. Philip's, N. Kensington, 1891; returned to chaplaincy, Seychelles, July, 1892.
GRANGE, HERBERT CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to bd. of educ., Aug., 1901; transfd. to C.O., Feb., 1902.
GRANNUM, ALLEN.—Asst. auditor of Sierra Leone and Gambia, Aug., 1897.

GRANNUM, CLIFTON.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; 2nd clk. to gov. Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto, St. Vincent, Apr. 1892; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leone, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast, Apr., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903-June, 1904.

GRANNUM, EDWARD ALLAN.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. registrar's off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; clk. master in chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1888; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 25th Sept., 1897, to 15th Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898 asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to March, 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901.

GRANNUM, EDWARD T.—Representative of Bridgetown, Barbados Assem., 1883; J.P., 1886; mem. exec. comtee., 1885-89, and 1890-94; mem. gen. bd. of health, 1885; deleg. to Washington as to tariff arrangements under McKinley act, 1891; audr.-gen., Barbados, 1894; ag. col. sec. and mem. exec. coun., Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; ag. col. sec., mem. of exec. coun., May to Sept., 1897.

GRANT, H. E. W.—Sec. to the educn. bd., Barbados, 1882; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1883; and at Trinidad, 1885 to 1891; went on mission to Venezuela, 1887; sec. to special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Trinidad, 1890-1; priv. sec. to Sir W. B. Griffith, gov. of G. Coast, 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1896; res. just., Harbour Is., Bahamas, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, and ag. clk. of councils, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1899; appt. confirmed, 1901; ag.-atty.-gen., B. Hond., Mar., 1902 to Oct., 1903; ag. col. sec., 14th Dec., 1904, to 21st July, 1905; admstr. govt., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905; ag. col. sec., 7th Aug., 1905.
GRAVES, F. HELY-HUTCHINSON.—Cadet. H.M.S. "Conway," 1889; clk., Imperial Ottoman Bank, Alexandria, 1894; clk., crown agent's off., 1899; acctnt., P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 1905.
GRAVES, THE HON. J. H.—M.L.A. for Delaite, Victoria; comsnr. of trade and customs, Victoria, 9th July, 1881, to Mar., 1883.
GRAVES, SILAS.—Clk., procureur-genrl.'s dept., Mauritius, 2nd May, 1869; deputy collr. of cust., 2nd Mar., 1896; ag. collr. in 1899, 1900, 1903 and 1905.
GRAY, E. A. S.—Demy, Magd. Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1892; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1896; asst. dist. comsnr., Niger Coast prot., Sept., 1899; dist. comsnr., Feb., 1902.

GRAY, GEORGE SMITH BOOTH ERSKINE.—Conway, 1879-90; midshipman, R.N.R., Dec., 1880; mercantile marine offr., 1884 to 1894; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1887-1894; Lieut., R.N.R., 1894; H.M.S. "Anson" and naval employment, 1894-897; joined So. Nigeria marine, Jan., 1900; served Ubium expdn. and Aro campaign (medal); gov. of gaols, 1902.

GRAY, REGINALD.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled Bermuda laws, 1890-1883, in 1884; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; counsel to statute law consolidation comtee., Bermuda, 1898-1900; atty.-gen., 1900.
GRAY, ROBERT J.—Under-col. sec., Queensland, Jan., 1880; asst. comsnr. of rly., 1889; comsnr., 1898.

GRAY, ST. GEORGE.—B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Univ. Dub.), L.M.(Rotunda); ag. col. asst. surg., St. Lucia, 1892; supernumerary ditto, 1894; col. asst. surg. and med. offr. in charge of Yaws hosp., 1898; med. offr. in charge of agric. schl., 1901; med. offr., W. Africa med. staff, Sierra Leone, 1903.

GRAY, SIR S. BROWNLOW, K.T. BAGH (1901), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1823; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847; inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1848; atty.-gen. and advoo.-gen., 1861; ch. justice, May, 1900, ret., 1904.

GRAY, THOS. ALEXANDER.—Served in R.I.C., June, 1882, to Jan., 1899; srgt.-maj. Leeward Is. pol. and jailer, Montserrat, Jan., 1899; mem. of hurricane relief distress comte., and survr. of roads, Northern dist., 1900; inspr. of pol., Dominica, Feb., 1902; J.P.; and mem. of telephone bd., Dominica, May, 1902; lieut., Roosevelt vol. fire brigade, July, 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, June to Nov., 1903; mil. instr., Dominica defence reserve, last April, 1904.
GREAVES, DUDLEY, A., M.R.C.S., Edin., L.R.C.P., Edin.—Asst. med. offr. Virgin Is., March, 1901; deputy cornr.

GREAVES, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—B. 1831; has held several important posts; ch. sec. to govt. in Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjt.-gen. in India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; ch. of the staff at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut div. of Indian army to 1892.

GREAVES, KENNETH J.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Mid. Temple, 26th Jan., 1900; ag. deputy clk., house of assm., Barbados, May-Sept., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ditto, April, 1901; ditto, St. Thomas and St. James, Nov., 1901, to Aug. 1902; apptd. pol. mag., 1st Sept., 1902.

GREAVES, WILLIAM HERBERT, K.C.—B.A., Oxon, honours, juris, 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1886, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1886; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assm. since 1882, and of bd. of educn.; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; now ch. just.
GREEN, A. PIERCE.—Surg., A.M.D., 1878; med. offr., col. forces in Basuto war, 1881; regtl. surg., left wing, C.M.R., 1881-4; med. offr., Cape rly., 1885; ditto, Bechuanaland border pol., 1885.

GREEN, CHARLES FRANCIS J.—Cadet, Sta. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., June, 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Feb., 1902; confirmed, July, 1903.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901.

GREEN, JOHN FREDERICK NORMAN, B.A.—Scholar, Emman. Coll., Camb., 1890; bracketed 15th wrangler, 1893; B.A., 1893; 1st class, Part II., nat. science tripos, 1894; 2nd class clk. C.O. after compet. exam., Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Dec., 1898; to Sir M. Ommanney, 1900; 1st class clk., 16th June, 1902.
GREENE, GEOFFREY PHILIP.—Gen. mag. Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GREENE, GEORGE BALL.—Ed. in England; jun. clk., ld. dept., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1887; 5th class clk., Feb., 1888; 4th cl., secretariat, 1891; sec. to comte. for inquiring into post-office, 1893; sec. pilotage comsn., and 3rd class, 1895; 2nd class, and despatch clk., 1899; 1st class clk., 1901; ag. prin. clk., 1902-3, Aug.-Sept., 1904; ag. ast. govt. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Sept., 1904.

GREENE, JOHN LE SUEUR.—Ed. Roy. Coll.; entered col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan., 1886; jun. copyist, procur.-gen.'s office, Mar., 1887; sen. ditto, Feb., 1888; govt. translator of laws, Nov., 1888; 5th class clk., July, 1890; 4th class clk., Mar., 1896.

GREENE, SIR WILLIAM CONYNGHAM, K.C.B. (1900), C.B. (1897).—B. 1854; ed. Harrow, and Pemb. Coll., Ox.; M.A. 1880; entrd. F.O. 1877; ag. 3rd sec. legation at Athens, 1880; ditto Stuttgart, 1883; ag. chargé d'affaires Stuttgart and Darmstadt at various times, 1883-9; 2nd sec. diplomatic service, 1887; 2nd sec. Hague, 1889-91; Brussels, 1891-3; sec. of legation and chargé d'affaires, Teheran, 1893-6; H.M. agt. at Pretoria, S.A. Republic, with rank of chargé d'affaires, 25th Aug., 1896, to 1899.
GREENE, W. H., M.I.C.E. (London.)—Asst. engrnr., Victorian rly., 1885; dist. engrnr., 1858; res. engrnr., 1868; ch. engrnr. of existing lines, 1882; Victorian rly. comsnr., 1887-92.

GREENER, HERBERT.—D.S.O., clk., sec.'s dept., G.P.O., Cape, 1894; in charge of money order dept., 1886; 1st clk., admstr.'s office, B. Bechuanaalnd, 1888; recvr. of revenue, Vryburg, and P.M.G., Bechuanaalnd, 1889; examnr. of accts., acctnt. to high comsnr., Cape-town, 1890; paymr. (rank captain), Bech. Br. pol., 1891, combined with prin. cust. offr., for Bech. Prot., 1895; specially employed in charge of finances, Mafeking siege, and financial asst. to mil.-govrn., Bloemfontein, 1899-1900; transf. to S.A.C. as ch. paymr. and acctnt.-gen. (rank major), Oct., 1900; promoted lt.-col., 1901.

GREENLEES, THOMAS DUNCAN, M.B. Edin.—Surg.-capt., Graham's Town 1st city volrs.; late asst. med. offr., counties asyl., Carlisle, and City of London asyl.; prizeman medico-psychological assn.; med. supt., Graham's Town asyl., and visiting surg. to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.
GREENWAY, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. of legis. assem., Manitoba; formerly pres. of exec. coun.; premier; min. of agric. and immigr. and rly. comsnr.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Exec. staff, inventions exhibn., 1886, and Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886, and Royal Jubilee exhibn., Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable institns., 1887 to 1890; priv. sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov. Bahamas, and clk. to coun., 1891-4; treas. of Gibraltar, 1897; J.P. and mem. of sanitary comsn., chrmn. at various times, 1898-1904, also contractg. offr., 1897; ag. capt. of port, July to Oct., 1901-1903; hosps. comsnr., 1903; chrmn. of comte. on wharfage tolls and storage waterport wharf, 1902; chrmn. of comte. on management, working and revenue of new comml. mole, 1904.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS J.—Inspr. Cyprus pol., Feb., 1887; ag. local comdt., Limasol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to July, 1895, bronze medal, roy. hum. soc. for saving and attempting to save life during Limasol floods, Nov. 12th, 1894; local comdt., 1896.

GRENFELL, GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WAL- LACE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Malta, 1898; A.D.C. to Sir A. Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. asst. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; asst. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakin against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903.

GRENIER, GEORGE.—Employed in Jaffra Kachcheri and registrn. dept., 1861-1872; in Queen's advocate's dept., April, 1872, to 1878; second dep. regisr. sup. ct., 9th April, 1878; dep. regisr., 15th June, 1885; regisr., 1st May, 1894.
GRENIER, JOSEPH.—Dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, 23rd April, 1903; has acted on various occasions since 1897 as sol.-gen., commr. of assize, and puisne justice.

GREY, 4TH EARL OF (great. 1806), VISOOUNT HOWICK, BARON GREY, BART.—G.C.M.G. (1904), Ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll. Camb.; M.P., 1880-6; dir. B.S.A. Co.; admstr. of Rhodesia, 1896 to 1898; gov.-gen. of Canada, 26th Sept., 1904.

GREY, ROBT. CAMPBELL.—Junior offr. treasry., Selangor, 1st Aug., 1888; asst. in land offr., Krian, 14th June, 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, 6th Dec., 1890; asst. mag., Kinta, 24th Dec., 1890; ag. supt. of Penghulus, Lower Perak, 14th June, 1891; supt. ditto, 1st Nov., 1891; asst. mag. and collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1st Apr., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Kualas Kangar, 1st Jan., 1894; asst. sec. to govt., Selangor, 28th Feb., 1895; ag. dist. mag., Krian, 9th Apr., 1896; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1st May, 1897; ditto, Kuala Selangor, 1st Jan., 1899; ditto, Ulu Pahang, 1st Nov., 1899; dist. mag., Kualas Kangar, 19th Jan., 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Selangor, 1st Oct. to 30th Dec., 1900; ag. dist. mag., Kinta, 10th Oct., 1901; resumed duties as dist. mag., Kuala Kangar, 21st Nov., 1901; sec. to res., Selangor, 1903.

GREY-WILSON, SIR WM., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; adt. Chelt. Coll.; priv. sec. to Sir William Grey, gov. of Jamaica, 1874; to Lieut.-Gov. Edw. E. Rushworth, Mar., 1877; admstr., Major-Gen. J. R. Mann, R.E., Aug., 1877; to Lieut.-Gov. F. P. Barlee, Br. Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clk. of the exec. and legis. couns., June, 1878; ag. mag., Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to Apr., 1880, and June, 1880, to Mar., 1881; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, 1883; special commr., Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; 4th asst. col. sec., G. Coast Col., 1884; col. sec., St. Helena, July, 1886; ag. gov., 1887; gov., 1890; gov., Falklands, 1897; gov., Bahamas, 5th May, 1904.
GRIEVE, R., M.D., C.M.G. (1894).—Med. supt. lun. asyl., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Sept., 1875; ag. med. offr., immigr. dept., Aug., 1885; surg. gen. of col., Oct., 1885; ret. 1894.

GRIFFIN, CHAS. JAMES.—B.A. (Hons.) Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st schlr. in mod. liter.; triple exhibnr.; chancellor's gold medallist; called to the bar, Ireland, June, 1898; apptd. crm. proser., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Aug., 1901; ag. chief judicial offr., and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901 to June, 1902; ag. judge of high ct., Feb. to Oct., 1904; apptd. regisr.-gen.of births, deaths, marriages, and banking, under various local ordinances, and is also regisr. of deeds and admstr. of deceased estates under the high ct.

GRIFFIN, C. T.—M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.S.A., Lond.; supt.dg. med. offr., Dikoya, Ceylon, 1st Jan., 1883; dist. med. offr., 17th Jan., 1888; govt. med. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; col. surg., prov. of Uva, 21st Sept., 1898; asst. prin. civ. med. offr. and insp.-gen. of hospitals, 17th Jan., 1901.

GRIFFIN, EUGENE PATRICK.—Third clk., treas., Gibraltar (after compet. exam.), Feb., 1883; pol. clk., 1883; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s office, 1886; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1890; 1st class clk., Jan., 1893; ch. clk. and cashier, P.O., Nov., 1893; transf'd to col. sec.'s office, July, 1896.

GRIFFIN, MARTIN J.—Called to the bar in 1868 from offices of Hon. James McDonald (now ch. just. of Nova Scotia) and of Hon. Wm. Miller (speaker of Senate of Canada); edited Halifax "Express" till 1874; contested Halifax co. for local legislature in 1874; edited Halifax "Herald" till 1878; apptd. asst. on fishery commn. under Washington Treaty in 1873; priv. sec. to min. of just. in 1878; sec. of civ. serv. commn. in 1891; edited Toronto "Mail" till Aug. 6, 1885; when apptd. joint partly. librarian.
GRIFFIN, REGINALD HERBERT.—Ed. Marlborough and C. C. Coll., Oxford; 1st mods., 1899; 2nd lit. hum., 1901; clk., higher div. W. O., Oct., 1901; 2nd clas. clk., C. O., Feb., 1903.

GRIFFITH, CHARLES DUNCAN, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1830; was lieut. Ittenhage Hottentot levy in Kaffir war of 1846-47; lieut. Kaffir pol., Jan., 1848, to Dec., 1860; capt. commanding the Albany Hottentot levy in Kaffir war of 1851-52 (medal); several times mentioned in gen. orders; comdt. of the 2nd div. of the armed mounted pol. force, Nov., 1852; 2nd in command during the operations in 1857-58 against the Tembu chief Fadana, and the Galeka chief Kreli; captured the chief Fadana; C.C. and R.M., Albert, 1858; Queenstown, June, 1859; Albany, Nov., 1868; King William's Town, July, 1869; gov.'s agt. and ch. mag., Br. Basuto-land, July, 1871; comdt. of the F.A.M.P. upon the outbreak of the Galeka tribe under the chief Kreli, Sept., 1877; Jan., 1878, comdt.-gen. of col. forces, and from that date in command of the col. forces in the col. until the end of the Galeka war and Gaika rebellion; in July, 1878, received thanks of houses of parlmt. for his services; Aug., 1878, re-apptd. gov.'s agt. in, and ch. mag. for, Br. Basuto-land; ret. 1882.

GRIFFITH, F.W.—Matric. Lond. Univ., 1885; supernum. P.O. St. Vincent, Sept., 1885; clk. P.O., Jan., 1886; clk. to col. regisr., June, 1886; ag. clk. to regisr. sup. ct., Jan., 1887; marshal V.-A. ct., Nov., 1887; ag. regisr. sup. ct., 1888; 2nd clk. govt. office, Apr., 1889; ag. col. postmr., Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1890, Dec., 1891, to Jan., 1892, and May to June, 1892; escheator-gen. and casual receiver, Nov., 1890; inspr. of schls., Dec., 1890; ch. clk. govt. office and clk. of couns., June, 1893; J.P., Apr., 1894; filled combined offices of inspr. of schls. and local audit clk., Jan.-Dec., 1897; med. regisr., Jan., 1898; spec. ment. in deeps. and commended by S. of S. for services rendered in connection with hurricane relief work, 1894; sec. of hurricane loan bd., June, 1899; mag. 3rd dist. and mag. 1st dist. (criml. jurisdn.), Oct.-Nov., 1899; has acted as mag. and coroner, 1st dist., on several occasions; offl. visitor and visiting just., Kingstown prison, June, 1900; nominated offl. mem., Kingstown bd., 30th June, 1900; acted as regisr., sup. ct., and regisr.-gen. for short period; sec. to hosp. and asylums inquiry commn., 1900; performed duties of warden, Leeward dist., Nov., 1900, to Feb., 1901, and July-Aug., 1901; supvr. of cust. and port offr., Apr.-May, 1901; supt. of Crown lands, land commn., and in charge of survey dept., May, 1903, to Jan., 1904; chmn. of Kingstown bd., Jan., 1904; chief of pol., chief of excise, supt. of pris., port offr., supvr. of cust., and in charge of fire brig., May to Oct., 1904.

HADDON-SMITH, GEO. BASIL, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; asst. inspr., Lagos constab., 1886; adjt., 1887; priv. sec. to ag. gov. and clk. of couns., 1887, 1889, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. commr., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jeju expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political offr. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; asst. col. sec., Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expdn., 1896; ch. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast col.; political offr. to Col. Sir J. Willoocks in the Ashanti expdn., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in desps. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec. S. Leone, Oct., 1901.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl., and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. ch. olk., col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade comm., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confce., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on introduction of responsible gov.
HAGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Postmtr.-gen., Canada, 1888; min. of rly. and canals, 1892-6.

HAHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; apprenticeship under J. A. Biernatzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the technological instrn., Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, pub. wks., Jan., 1888; ag. asst. egr., N. Div. in 1889, 1890-1; asst. engr., N. Div., 1891; asst. engr. of works and buildings, Feb. 1894; asst. engr. office and wks. Nov., 1899.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmnr., High schl., Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901; (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—Parly. librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., asst. and serg.-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk., legis. coun., 1901.

HALE, ABRAHAM.—Ag. mag., Matang, 13th Dec., 1884; insp. of mines and asst. mag., Kinta, Feb., 1885; collector and mag., Tampin, 17th Oct., 1887; ag. sup. of Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1888; clk. of coun., 20th Oct., 1889; asst. Indian immigr. agent, Dec., 1889; ag. for resident, Feb. to Mar., 1900; offr. in charge, Mar. to Nov., 1894; dist. offr., Tampin, 1st Jan., 1896; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, 26th Jan., 1897; passed higher standard in Malay, Apl., 1897; examiner in Malay, 1898; dist. offr., Klang, 9th Mar., 1899; collector of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regir. of titles, Selangor; chmn., pub. gardens comtee., Kuala Lumpur, and of museum comtee., 21st Nov., 1899; vice-chmn., Malay agric. sttlmt., 23rd Sep., 1900; dir. of W. and O. fund, 19th Apl., 1901.

HALL, CECIL G.—Volr. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Nov., 1866; clk., gen. bd. of health, June, 1867; clk. med. dept., Nov., 1868; asct., immigr. dept., June, 1872; senior clk. Oct., 1880; and ag. ch. olk. several times from 1880 to 1886; inspr. of immigr., 1886; ag. storekr.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1888; do., Sept., 1901; ditto, 12th Aug.-1903 to 19th Aug., 1904.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. regir., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. ld. rev. Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. ld. rev., Penang till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy, Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 1904; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1906.

HALL, THE HON. SR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1824; emigrated to N.Z., 1852; mem. legis. assem., 1856; col. sec., 1856; mem. legis. coun., 1862-6; postmtr.-gen., 1866-9; col. sec., 1872-3; premier, 1879-82.
HALLETT, W. CARDY.—Clk. to col. survr., Bermuda, Aug., 1881; asst. supt. pub. wks., Jan., 1887; supt., July, 1887; col. survr., Oct., 1887.
HALLEY, ROBT. HENRY.—Ed. Dollar Academy, Scotland; clk., central rep. dep. bloemfontein, Apl., 1902, to May, 1903; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., May, 1903.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD.—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; asst. sup. of pol., and pol. mag., N.T., 1901; ag. registr.-genl., May-June, 1902; ag. deputy sup. of police, 1903-4.

HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. offr. Penang, Apr., 1896, and Malacca, Nov., 1896; supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1897; dist. offr., June, 1898; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1901; ag. coll. of land revenue, Penang, Apr., 1902; also ag. registr. of deeds and registr. of bills of sale, Penang, May, 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1902, but continued to act as coll. of land rev.; ag. collr. of land rev., and offr. in charge of Treasury, Malacca, Apr., 1903; 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1905.
HAMBLIN, R.A.—Aptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1850; entered the service, 1865; 2nd clk. rec. gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1873, 1877; col. book-keeper, 1879; ag. rec.-gen. 1881; and ag. audr.-gen., 1883 to 1886; received thanks of coun. on leaving for post of rec. gen., &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; ag. audr.-gen., M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; mem. of bd. of educn., 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chmn. of comtee. for widows fund, 1889; ag. col. sec., 1890; chmn. hbr. improvement comtee., and ag. audr.-gen. and col. sec., 1891; mem. coll. coun., 1891; rec.-gen., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. audr.-gen. and mem. ct. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1895, and again Oct. 1895; sen. commr., Vlisingen estate; chmn. spirits comtee., "Trotman," trust comtee.; dir. widows' and orphans' fund, &c.; mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1895; off. mem. ct. of policy, July, 1898; chrmn., excise bd., 1901; chrmn., educn. comtan., 1902; vote of thanks of comb. ct. for spec. serv. gratuitously rendered in public roads and bridges adminstr., 1894 to 1906.
HAMILTON, CHARLES EDWARD—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1865; elected mayor of Winnipeg, 1885; elected to Manitoba legislature, 1885; atty.-gen., Manitoba, Feb., 1887-8.
HAMILTON, R. W.—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1889; M.A., 1892; sec. comm. of enquiry, Dominica, 1893-4; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1896; dist. commr., Lagos, 1896.

HAMILTON, SIR W. A. B., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.B. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1844; ed. at Harrow; apptd. a jun. clk. in the C.O., May, 1864, after compet. exam.; served on secret service to N. America, 1867; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1st class clk., July, 1879; and priv. sec. to Mr. Knatchbull-Hugessen (now Lord Brabourne), M.P., 9th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 18th Nov., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. James Lowther, M.P., under sec. of state for the cols., 25th Feb., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. James Lowther, M.P., ch. sec. for Ireland, 15th Feb., 1878; capt in the E. Lothian yeomanry cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th June, 1883; hon. lieut.-col. and comdg. regt., 1894; comdg. 12th yeomanry brigade, 1895; author of "Mr. Montenello: a Romance of the Civil Service"; priv. sec. to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; and to Sir H. Holland (now Lord Knutsford), Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; sec. to col. confece., 1887; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1894; ch. clk., Mar., 1896; apptd. off. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1901.

HAMBLEY, WILLIAM WYMOND, M.A., F.R.M.S.—Clare Coll., Camb. (hon. classics, 1873); 1st class prof., Roy. Coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1879; sen. prof., May, 1887; ag. rector, May, 1891, to July, 1892, and Apr. to Nov., 1894, again May, 1900; rector, 1901; ag. director of pub. instn. in 1902, 1903 and 1904.
HAMMOND, E. J.—Govt. med. offr., Savanna Grande district, Trinidad, 1 Jan., 1876.

HAMPDEN, 2ND VISCT. (creat. 1884), BARON DAGRE (creat. 1807), G.C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1841; dep.-lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; J.P. Herts, was M.P. for Hertfordshire, 1868 to 1874; for Stroud, July, 1874 (unseated Dec. 1874); and again 1880-5; and for Mid-Gloucester, 1880-6; survr. gen. of ordce., 1883-5; Gov. N.S. Wales, 1896-9.

HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A.—Scholar St. John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods., 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1883; asst. master Cheltenham Coll., 1879; 2nd master Bosworth schl., 1880; prof. classics and moral philos., Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883; ag. prin., 1889-91; 2nd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. prinl., Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. to coll. coun., 1892; lieut. Trinidad field artillery.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM J., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Glasgow Univ.; supt. of telephones, W. Australia, 1885; supt. of telegraphs and telephones, 1890; hon. local sec. and treas. for W. Australia of the institn. of electrical engineers of London; govt. electrician, 1894.

HAND, JOHN ST. VINCENT.—1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Oct., 1898; ch. clk., gen. man.'s off., Lagos govt. rlyw., Apr., 1901; asst. sec., sec.'s off., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1904; priv. sec. to J. J. Thorburn, esq., ag. gov. of Lagos, July, 1905; and clk. to exec. coun.
HANITSCH, KARL RICHARD, PH.D. (Jena).—Demonstrator of Zoology, Univ. Coll., Liverpool, 1887; curator and librarian, Raffles library and museum, Singapore, 1895.

HANLEY, ALLAN HASTINGS, C.M.G. (1903); F.R.C.S.—Med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now S. Nigeria), May, 1893; sen. med. offr., Apl., 1901; dep. prin. med. offr., May, 1903; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1903 to Apl., 1904.

HANLEY, GEORGE A.—Joined R.I.C., June, 1886; Leeward Is. pol. force, Dec., 1898; lieut. and adjt., Sept., 1899; Hythe musk. certif., including machine guns, June, 1898; inspr. of pol., Antigua, Apr., 1902; lieut. and adjt., Antigua def. force, 1902; capt., Mar., 1904; visiting just., cent. prison, Dec., 1903, to July, 1905; inspr. of pol., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; capt. and adjt., St. Kitts def. force, July, 1905; J.P. for Antigua and St. Kitts-Nevis.

HANNAM, WILLOUGHBY, M.I.C.E.—Apprentice to M. Dr. Bays, C.E., Reading; asst. survr., Melb. and River Murray rly., Victoria, 1860; engr. Moreton Bay tramway co., Brisbane, 1861-63; dist. engineer (S. dist.) Queensland govt. rlys., 1863-8; contractor's engr. on same, 1868-72; dist. engr. and in charge of surveys, cent. div., Queensland rlys., 1872-85; ch. engr., N. div., 1885; has been engaged on survey of 1,060 miles of rly., and directed survey of 250 miles more.

HANNYNGTON, JOHN ARTHUR.—Capt., Indian army; temp. maj., King's African Rifles; ed. at Unit. Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; passed in Hindustani, higher standard, Pushu, higher standard, Kiswahili, govt. test. exam. for promotion to field offr. and staff employ; gazetted 8th June, 1889; apptd. comdt., Jubaland Camel Corps, 15th Dec., 1901; comdt., 6th King's African Rifles, Somaliland Prot., 28th Feb., 1905.

HANSON, CHAS. RAISTRICK, L.S.O. (1903).—B. 1850; M.I.C.E.; Cape govt. rly., staff, 1874; Ceylon, ditto, 1879; res. engr. and man., Perak state rly., 1887; res. engr. for rly., Perak, 1890, and Prov. Wellesley, 1903.

HARBIN, JOHN ALPHONSO.—Ch. clk. to registr., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to registr. sup. ct., July, 1886; marshal V.-A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. registr. sup. ct., and ag. registr. in V.-A. ct., May, 1888, to Apr., 1890; acctnt., treasy., July, 1890; sec. to comm. pub. wks. dept. inquiry, Dec., 1890; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1892, to Feb., 1893; ag. inspr. of schools Feb., 1893, to Mar., 1894; inspr. of schools Mar., 1894; ag. col. postmaster, Aug.-Sept., 1894; mem. bd. of ed. Apr., 1895; ag. registr. sup. ct., Aug.-Sept., 1901; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. and assts. treas., Carriacou, Dec., 1901, to Jan., 1902.
HARBOUR, E. W. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to hd. of trade, 22nd Jan., 1901; transf. to C.O., 22nd July, 1901.

HARCOURT, ALGERNON BERNARD.—Apptd. cons. agnt. in Oil R. Protect., Oct., 1892; ag. v.-cons., Bonny dist., June, 1894, to July, 1895; dist. commr., Bonny, 1st Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. cent. div. on various occasions; transferred to Cross Riv. div., Dec., 1899; travlling commr., S. Nig., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. divil. commr., Cross Riv. div., May-Dec., 1900; ag. res. Benin City Territories, 1901 and 1902; divl. commr., Cross River, 17th Jan., 1903; ag. sec. and dep. high commr., 24th Feb. to 3rd Apr., 1905.

HARDING, ALFRED J.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's, Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901.

HARDING, EDWARD J.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904.

HARDY, WENTWORTH MARMAIDUKE.—Entered survey dept., S. Australia, Oct., 1865; accompanied the survr.-gen. as survr. and draughtsman to Port Darwin, Dec., 1868; rose by step, till apptd. Jan., 1878, trigonometrical survr. In 1894, at the recommendation of the survr.-gen. of Victoria, and to carry out his suggested reformation, appointed inspr. of surveys and prof. adviser, survey dept., Tasmania.

HARE, FREDERICK ARTHUR.—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Wicklow; clk. to mags., Vasse, W. Australia, 1880; clk. and asst. priv. sec. to Sir William Robinson, during various periods from 1880 to 1882; inspr. of pol. for the S. dista. of the col., 1882; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, Cambridge Gulf, 1886-7; ag. govt. res., Albany, 1887-8; res. mag. York, 1888-97; warden and res. mag. East Coolgardie Goldfield, 1897-1900; comsnr. of police, 1900.

HARE, GEO. THOMPSON, C.M.G. (1902); I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1863; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1884; passed in Chinese, 1888; dist. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, June, 1890; 2nd asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, Oct., 1891; ag. asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apr., 1895; sec. for Chinese affairs, Fed. Malay States, Feb., 1897; on spec. serv. in Weihaimei, 1901-2; ag. prot. of Chinese, 20th Nov. 1903; ret., 1904

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES.—Clk. treasry., W. Australia, Apl., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, Mar., 1888; ditto, Roebourne, Junc., 1889; sec., W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1890.

HARGROVE, HERBERT.—Ed. at Harrow, 1888-1891; admitted a solicitor of sup. ct., England, Jan., 1897; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. (probationary), G. Coast, Apl., 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Axim, May, 1902.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apl., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY.—Transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900.

HARRIOTT, ALEXIS WYNNS.—Ed. at Yale; B.A. 1856; M.A. and B.Ph., 1859; ag. col. survr. and inspr., P.W., Turks and Caicos Islds., Feb. to Apl., 1864; J.P., 1879; unoffl. mem. legis. bd., 1881 to 1888, when apptd. asst. comsnr. (Grand Turk; ag. comsnr., Apl. to July, 1888; manager savings bk., 1889; mem. bd. of educn., 1891; coroner, 1893; ag. auditor on various occasions, 1888-1902.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—Clk. to comsnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and '902; ag. regis. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. comsnr. G. Turk, May to July, 1896; and Aug. to Nov., 1901; ag. auditor, Aug. and Sept., 1901; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr. and cashier), comsnr.'s office, Apl. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; is a J.P.

HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancered student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd class clk., C.O. after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Financo Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Confco., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bindry. arbitn., 1896-8; priv. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bindry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundy. arbitn., 1901-4; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govrs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRISON, C. W.—Ed. at Sherborne schl., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren, Esq.; 3rd cl. classical mods., 1894; apptd. cadet, Selangor, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; mag. 2nd cl., Kuala Langat, Feb., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1899; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT E.—Apptd. consular agt., Oil Rivers Prot., June, 1891; ag. vice-consul, 1892 to 1895; made dist. comsnr., and served through Brass punitive expedition (medal and clasp), 1895; apptd. political offr., Niger column, Aro Field Force, 1902 (medal and clasp); ag. div. comsnr., Cross River division, Apr.-Sept., 1904.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT HUGH.—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College (Lond.), and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag. and dist. surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dist. surg., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. comsnr. and dist. surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892.

HARRISON, JOHN B., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1836; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1887; M.A., 1887; prof. of chemistry and agricul. science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of chem., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; is F.C.S., F.G.S., and F.L.C., and author of several papers relating to the agriculture and geology of the W. Indies.

HARRISONSON, SYDNEY THIRLWALL.—Ed. Blackheath; apptd. asst. acct., P.W.D., G. C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ch. acct., 2nd June, 1897; asst. acct., W.A.F.F., 26th Feb., 1898; ch. acct., 8th Nov., 1899; treas. N. Nig., 1st Jan., 1900.

HART, ANTHONY DE LLANO.—Storekeeper, Trinidad, Oct., 1866; office of the ct. of intendant, 1868; office of stip. mag., Port of Spain, 1868; marshal's office, June, 1869; clk. at govt. house, Sept., 1869; promoted to the customs dept., 1871; landing waiter, 1872, and inspr. of Venezuelan vessels in addition to other duties, 1876.

HART, JOHN HINCHLEY.—Supt. King's house, gardens and grounds, Jamaica, during their formation, Nov., 1875; supt. govt. cinchona plantations, Oct., 1881; ag. dir. pub. gardens and plantations, July, 1886; ch. botanical dept., Trinidad, Mar., 1887; is a fellow Linn. Soc.; author of a "Botanist's Ramble in Cent. America," a work on cacao culture, curing, and fermenting (1891), a report on forest conservation (1891), and pamphlets relating to the cultivation of W. Indian products; engaged by the govt. of the Windward Is. to inspect and report on the working of the botanical stations, St. Vincent and Grenada (1890); lieut. Trinidad rifle volvs., Mar., 1889; capt., 1897; capt. reserve, 1898; govt. member, Trinidad public library; ex. off. mem. agri. soc. Trinidad; supt. botanic station, Tobago; author of paper on rubber-cultivation in West Indies.
HART, W. EDWARDS.—Librarian, public library and museum, Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1903.

HARTE, LOVELACE FRED, EDWARD RALPH.—First class certif. at I.C. Coll. in Dec., 1875; articled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; ag. clk. of the peace the same year; Jan., 1877, clk. in customs dept., Trinidad; clk. in audit office, Sept., 1878; 7th clk., Jan., 1883, June, 1887; 2nd clk., regar., sup. ct., 1887; ch. clk., 1897; also comsrr. of oaths for whole isld.; ag. dep. regar. on occasions in 1896-7-8.
HARTLEY, CHAR.—M.A., Cantab.; lecturer in English and modern languages, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 11th Dec., 1896; principal, Royal Coll., 23rd June, 1903.

HARTLEY, E. B., C.M.G. (1901).—Surg.-Lieut.-Col., C.M.R.; created V.C. for gallantry in the operations against the stronghold of the Basuto chief Morosi; prin. med. offr., Cape col. forces, 1878; served through the Gaika, Morosi, and Basuto wars, 1878-81; gov't. med. offr., Basutoland, 1874-7; ret., 1904.

HARVEY, GEORGE LEONARD, C.E.—Asst. dir. of wks., So. Nigeria, June, 1900; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1901; deputy dir. of pub. wks., 1901; asst. dir. of pub. wks., Mar. to Nov., 1902, and Apl. to Nov., 1904.
HARWARD, JOHN.—Principal, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 1st Apl., 1892; dir. of pub. instrn., 23rd June, 1903.

HASTINGS, W. C. HOLLAND.—H.M.'s navy, 1865-81; retired with rank of commdr.; inspr. of imigrts., Jamaica, Nov., 1881, to May, 1889; coroner, Nov., 1886, to May, 1888; ch. of pol. and ag. town warden, St. Lucia, May to Dec., 1889; also for two months harbrmr. and pilot; asst. harbrmr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1889, to Feb., 1899; ag. harbrmr., Feb., 1891, to Apr., 1892; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1892, to May, 1896; ag. capt. supt. of pol. and supt. fire brigade, May, 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. harbrmr., June to Dec., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1896, to Apr., 1897, and Feb., 1898, to Feb., 1899; postmrgen., Feb., 1899; seconded to bury the dead in plague epidemic, 1894, June to Aug., inclusive; mem. legis. coun., Nov., 1891, to Apr., 1892, and again from June, 1895, to Oct., 1896; mem. of various comsrs. and comtees.
HATCHER, C. M.—Apptd., after compet exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11 Sept., 1893; transf'd. to C.O., 18 Mar., 1896; asst. to chief regar., Jan., 1900.
HATHERLEY, GEORGE MOOR.—2nd lieut. roy. artily., Nov., 1890; lieut., Nov., 1893; apptd. with local rank of capt. to commd. of permanent force at Albany, W. Australia, July, 1899.
HATHERTON, BARON.—Col. the Hon. Edward George Percy Littleton, C.M.G. (1880); b. 1842; late mil. sec. to the gov't-gen. of Canada.

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIBAN, G.C.M.G. (1895), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1844; entered the army, 32nd lt. infy., Jan., 1862; capt., 1873; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the offr. admnstrg. the gov't. of Mauritius from July, 1873, to Jan., 1874; ag. ch. civ. comsrr., Seychelles Is., from Apr., 1874, until Feb., 1875; mem. of the exec. and legis. couns. of Fiji, May, 1875; apptd. (provisionally) col. sec. and rec. gen., Fiji, 1st Sept., 1875; pres. of Nevis, 1877; admnstrg. the gov't. of St. Lucia from June, 1878, to Aug., 1879; ch. civ. comsrr., Seychelles, Oct., 1879; gov'w. Africa Sttlnts., 1881; Apr., 1881, sent on a mission to Paris in conjunction with Mr. (now Sir A.) Hemming, of the C.O., to assist in negotiating the settlement of certain questions at issue between Great Britain and France, with regard to territory on the W. Coast of Africa; apptd. H.M.'s consul for Liberia, Dec., 1881; negotiated the stltm. of a conterminous boundary between S. Leone and Liberia, and inquire into the claims of Br. subjects against the Liberian Gov't., Mar., 1882; gov'w. of Trinidad, Dec., 1884; gov'w. of Natal and special comsrr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also gov'w. of Zululand, 1887; attended internat. confce. at Brussels on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; gov'w. of Ceylon, 1890; of Madras, 1895 to 1900; of Tasmania, 1901 to 1904.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt., at Dum Dum in 1869; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volks. in 1868 at their formation; asst. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. comsrr. of Accra, Oct., 1875; insprn., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; asst. col. sec. G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; insprn.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; admistr. of the Gambia, 1885; administd. the gov't. of the W. Africa Sttlnts., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov', Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie expdn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admistr., Barbados, 1891; gov', Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, THOS. WAGSTAFFE.—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lon. chain of arbitration, 1897; pros. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; ag. Queen's advocate, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYNES, THOR. BENJAMIN.—Apptd. dispenser, penal settlement, Massaruni, B. Guiana, 6th June, 1870; 1st cls. prison warder, 12th July, 1877; schlinstr. to penal settmtns., 1st Sept., 1878; dispenser in charge of leper asylum, Kaow Is., 27th Oct., 1879; clk. to penal settmtns., 27th Oct., 1883; keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 5th July, 1887; supt., Georgetown prison, 19th June, 1894.

HAYNES, W. LINDSAY H.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1874-8; clk. to comsrrs. apptd. to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; incl. rev. offr., dist. B, 1878; now inspr. of incl. rev. offrs. regist.

HAZELAND, FRANCIS ARTHUR.—Cllk. to puisme judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. dep. regist., Mar. to Nov., 1882; clk. to ch. just., Sept., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. office, Mar. to Oct., 1890; ag. clk. of coun., Mar. to Dec., 1890; dep. regist. and acctnt., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; ag. regist., sup. ct., offl. trustee, regist. of companies and land offr., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; has passed in Chinese (higher standard); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Hilary, 1900; ag. pol. mag., June, 1900, to June, 1901; 2nd pol. mag., 7th June, 1901.
HEALY, P. J.—Crown prosecutor, S. dist., N.S. Wales, Jan., 1878; ditto, S.W. dist., 1879; crown prosecutor, Sydney, Jan., 1882.
HEARSEY, HERBERT HYDE YOUNG.—Apptd. med. offr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 6th June, 1896; principal officer, April, 1902.
HECTOR, SIR JAMES, M.D., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1834; dir. of geological surveys and curator of the col. museum, N.Zealand.
HEIDENSTAM, DR. F. C., C.M.G. (1884).—Ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1882.

HELY-HUTCHINSON, THE HON. SIR WALTER FRANCIS, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G.(1883).—B.1849; B.A. of Trin.Coll.,Camb., called to the bar by the soc. of the Inner Tem.; accompanied Sir Hercules Robinson to Fiji as special attache on the occasion of the cession of the islands to Gr. Britain, in Sept.-Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, 10th Oct., 1874, for Fiji affairs; priv. sec. for N.S. Wales affairs, Jan., 1875; col. sec. of Barbados, Nov., 1877; ch. sec. to govt. of Malta, 1883; and lieutenant-gov. and ch. sec. to govt., 1884; gov. of Windward Is., 1889; gov. of Natal, 1893; gov. Cape, 1901.

HEMERY, PERCY.—lst. clk., govt. savings bank, Br. Guiana, July, 1880; 3rd clk., treas., Jan., 1881; cashier, Aug., 1881; ag. ch. clk., 1884 and 1885; acctnt., govt. savings bank, Apr., 1888; ag. col. book-keeper, July, 1888, and Aug., 1890; ag. asst. rec-gen., and ag. sub-admstr., Berbice, Feb., 1891; ag. dep. rec-gen., Apr., 1891, May to Dec., 1892, May, 1893-1894, twice in 1895-6; sec. cattle diseases comnr., Oct., 1891; asst. rec-gen., Berbice, June, 1897; J.P., and comnr. of oaths and affid., June, 1897; sub-admstr.-gen., Berbice, Nov., 1897 (additional); ag. compt. of cust., 22nd Feb., 1904.

HEMMING, SIR A. W. L., G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G.(1890), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1841; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Feb., 1860; promoted Oct., 1864, 1st junior class; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers (now Lord Blachford), 20th May, 1867, till 20th May, 1871; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 21st May, 1871, till 29th Sept., 1872; apptd. a 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; 1st class clk., Nov., 1874; priv. sec. to the Earl of Cadogan, 2nd March, 1878, to 30th June, 1879; sent on a special mission to Paris, Apr., 1879; prin. clk., 1st July, 1879; again proceeded on special service to Paris, May, 1881; Br. deleg. to the W. African confce. at Berlin, 1884; on special service at Paris, 1889-90 and 1894, in connection with the delimitation of French and English possessions on the W. coast of Africa; gov. of Br. Guiana, Mar., 1896; gov. of Jamaica, 1898 to 1904.
HENDERSON, A.—Apptd.clk., refugee camps, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1901; clk. attached to audit dept., April, 1902; clk., treasy., 20th Aug., 1902.
HENDERSON, J. T.—Shorthand reporter of debates, legis. coun., Natal, 1883.

HENDERSON, W. R., C.M.G. (1901), M.D., and M.Ch., Dublin (1878).—B. 1855; entered army med. staff, Mar., 1880; served in India, and in Egypt during Soudan and Nile campaigns; senr. med. offr., imperial troops, Cape Coast Castle, 1886, and at S. Leone, 1888; ret. from army as capt., Feb., 1889; now on reserve of officers; entered Lagos med. serv., Oct., 1889; prin. med. offr. of expeditn. against the Jebus, 1892, ment. in despatches; name brought to the notice of S. of S., received medal and clasp; during serv. in Lagos was in charge of secretariat, ag. trav. comnr., and polit. offr. at Jebu Ode; prin. med. offr. of col. troops, Ashanti expeditn., 1895-6 (ment. in despatches and received star); apptd. prin. med. offr., G. coast, June, 1897; accompanied late Sir W. Maxwell on expeditn. to N. Territories, July to Nov., 1897, and received thanks of H.M.'s govt. for services; mem. of comte. at C.O. for re-organisation of W. African med. staff, Oct., 1901, and received thanks of S. of S.

HENDY, CHARLES LOWICK.—Sen. clerical asst. inspr., prisons office, Br. Guiana, 1881; ag. clk. of supplies and sec. to the bd. of prisons, July to Oct., 1885; July to Sept., 1888; and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; issuer to convict prison, Rio Cassaruni, Nov., 1890; clk. of supplies and sec. to bd. of prisons, June, 1892; ag. supt. orphan asyl., Nov. to Dec., 1895, and Feb. to July, 1896; ag. man. dir. orphan asyl., in addition to duties as clk. of supplies, from July to Nov., 1898, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900, Nov. to Dec., 1900; ag. supt., orphan asyl., July, 1901, to Jan., 1903; ag. supt., almshouse, and sec., poor law comsnrs., June, 1903.
HENKEL, C. C. H.—Conservator of forests, Transkeian territories, Cape Col., July, 1889.
HERBERT, CHAS. EDWARD.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1900-05; govt. res. and judge of N. Territory, 1906.

HERCHENRODER, FURCY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros. conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge Seychelles, 1900; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1905.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A.—Cadet, S. Sttlmnts, Nov., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malav., Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904.

HERVEY, DUDLEY FRANCIS AMELIUS, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; ed. at Marlborough; cadet, S. Sttlmnts, May, 1867; passed in Malav. in Nov., 1868; ag. priv. sec. to govt. in Mar., 1869; ch. clk. and interp. to the lieut.-gov., Penang, Jan., 1870, in which year he accompanied H.M.S. "Algerine" to inquire into various cases of piracy on the coast of Acehien; sen. sworn clk., sup. ct., Singapore, Jan., 1871, also clk. in bankruptcy, and J.P. and ag. mag., Sept., 1871; Aug., 1878, pol. mag. Malacca; has accomplished various expedns. to the different native states of the peninsula; J.P.; res. councillor, Malacca, 1882; also supt. of Negri Sembilan, 1883-6; ret., Dec., 1893.
HESKETH, GILBERT WOOD.—Ed. Birkenhead, and Caughton, Ches.; engineer, corporation, City of Cape Town engine works; joined service of P.W.D., O.R.C., 2nd July, 1903.

HEWICK, JOHN EDWIN.—Ed. Highgate Schl.; asst. supt. of pol., P.W., 1872; during the Perak war commanded an expeditn. across the country in pursuit of Sultan Ismail. Called to the bar of the Mid. Tem., 1880; stip. mag. Br. Guiana, 1883; sheriff of Essequibo, 1884; sheriff of Berbice, 1889; stip. mag. east and west bank, New Amsterdam, 1892; Berbice, 1893; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1st Oct., 1900.
HEYWOOD, A. W.—Dist. forest offr., Cape, 1888; conservator, Knyasa, Oct., 1895.
HEYWOOD, J. B.—Sec. to treas., N. Zealand, 1890.

HICKSON, ROBERT R. PURDON, M.I.C.E.—Res. engr., Carlingford harbour wks., Ireland, 1886 to 1872; res. engr., Barrow harbour wks., England, 1872-6; engr.-in-ch., harbours and jetties, S. Australia, 1876 to 1891; asst. engr., harbours and rivers, N.S. Wales, 1881-9; comnr. and engr.-in-ch., roads, bridges, and sewerage, 1889 to 1896; under sec. for pub. wks., and comnr. for roads, 1896; vice-pres. of pub. wks. tender bd., and chmn. of deptltn. bd. of reference, N.S.W.; J.P. for S.A. and N.S.W.
HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramagate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1906.

HILL, CHARLES W., F.R.G.S.—Clk., lower div., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Jan., 1886; passed in Greek, May, 1888; acted as clk., exec. coun., Aug., 1889, to Mar., 1890; and as priv. sec. to admstr., Oct., 1889, to Mar., 1890; govt. clk., shipping master, clk. of couns., and J.P., Falkland Is., 1892; also priv. sec. to gov.; ag. col. treas., collr. of customs, postmtr., &c., 1893; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., &c., 1894; special commr. on land question, W. Falkland, 1895; ag. inspr. of schools, 1895; special mission to S. Chili, 1897; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1897; col. treas., collr. of cust., and postmtr., Falkland Islds., Oct., 1897; ag. col. sec., May, 1899, to June, 1900; senr. astt. treas., G. Coast, 1901.

HILL, DAVID YOUNG CAMPBELL.—As 6th clk., treasy., Br. Guiana, July, 1874; ag. 4th clk., Apr., 1875, to Feb., 1876; ag. 1st clk., Oct., 1877, to May, 1878; provisional 3rd clk., audit office, May, 1878; 2nd clk., treasy., 1880; ch. clk., 1881; col. book-keeper, 1886; ag. receiver-gen., 1887; again 1888; dep. rec-gen., Apr., 1891; acted as rec-gen. at various periods from 1891 to 1898; dir. pub. offrs. widows and orphans fund, 1896; dir. of pub. offrs. guarantee fund; dir. of "Trotman" trust fund; ag. revcr.-gen. in 1899, 1900 and 1903; ag. comptroller of cust. in 1904 and 1905.

HILL, E. C. H.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1875; ag. treas., Malaca, Mar. to July, 1876; ag. inspr. of schls., June to Oct., 1877; ag. collr. of land revenue, Penang and Province Wellesley, Oct., 1877; ag. inspector of schools, Feb., 1879; supt. of educn., Penang, and ag. 2nd mag., June, 1880; ag. mag., P. W., 1881; inspr. of schls., S. S., 1883; ag. res. coun. Malaca, Apr., 1895, to Feb., 1896; 1st mag. Penang, 1897; auditor-gen., S. S., Apr., 1897.

HILL, J. K. D.—Entered treasy., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1879; 6th clk., Feb., 1881; 2nd book-keeper, aud. office, Apr., 1881; 1st ditto, Mar., 1882; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1889; 1st ditto, July, 1889; sec. P.W. dept. comm. of inquiry, 1889; ag. ch. clk., 1890, 1894, and 1895; ag. ch. clk., audit office, 1894-5; apptd., Sept., 1896, acctnt. sup. ct. of civ. justice, and acctnt. regist.'s office; student Linc.'s Inn, July, 1898; passed final exam., Mar., 1899; stip. mag., J.P., and coroner, July, 1899.

HILL, P. H. R.—Clk. and book-keeper, pol. estabmt., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1867; sen. inspr. of pol., July, 1868; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., July, 1872, to Apr., 1873; ag. stip. mag., Apr., 1873, to Nov., 1876, and on other occasions; astt. pol. mag. for Georgetown, Aug., 1880; ag. sheriff of Essequibo and stip. mag., Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1881; ag. pol. mag. of Georgetown and sheriff of Demerara, 1882; chmn. poor law comm., Apr., 1885; ag. recr.-gen. at various times, 1893-8; dir. of public offrs. widows and orphans fund, 1896.
HILLMAN, GEORGE FRANCIS.—Ed. Highbury New Park Coll., Lond.; admitted to the bar of supreme ct., W. Aust., 1898; apptd. clk. of legis. coun. and clk., of parlts., June, 1901.
HILLYER, H. K.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900.

HIME, RT. HON. LIEUT.-COL. SIR A.H., P.C. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1876), (late R.E.).—B. 1842; entered roy. engnr. in 1861; became capt. in 1874; employed in Bermuda under col. govt. from 1869 to 1871 in construction of a causeway and iron swing bridge connecting the island of St. George with the main island, for which he received the thanks of the legislature and the acknowledgments of the S. of S. for the cols.; col. engrn. of Natal, May, 1875; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1876; employed on survey of boundary between Orange Free State and Natal, 1894-5; ret., 1893; mem. legis. assem., 1897; min. of canals and works, 1897; premier, Natal, 1899; resig., 18th Aug., 1903.
HISLOP, THE HON. T. W. THOMAS.—Col. sec., N. Zealand, 1889-90; apptd. offr. of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhibn., 1889.

HIXSON, FRANCIS, late master R.N.—Served 15 years on Australian station, principally in surveying and exploring service; supt. of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours, N. S. Wales, Jan., 1863; organised N. S. W. Naval Bde., 1863, and still commands it; pres. marine board, Apr., 1872.

HOAD, COLONEL JOHN CHARLES, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1856; dep. adj.-gen. and chief staff offr., commonwealth forces of Australia. Lieut. 1884; adjt., mounted rifles, 1886; capt., 1887; maj., 1891; 2nd in command mtd. rif., 1892; astt.-adj.-gen. and lieut.-col., 1895; col., 1899; reprd. headqrtrs. staff, Viet., at Queen's Jubilee, London, 1897; attached to personal staff of Field-Marshal Lord Roberts; S. Afr. War, 1899-1900, comd. the first Austr. regt., converted regt. into mtd. inf., Bloemfontein, Johannesburg, Pretoria; A.A.G. mtd. inf.; mentd. in desps., C.M.G., three clasps; apptd. A.D.C. to H.E. the govr.-gen. of the commonw.; apptd. ch. of staff for all mil. arrangements for reception in Vict. of H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, and opening of first commonw. parl.
HOARE, GEORGE EDWARD.—Employed in operations against Arab slave traders, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1891-1894; medal, 1898; 2nd cls. astt., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1898.
HOBBS, STEPHEN BARTON.—Lower mechanical clk., survt.-gen.'s dept., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1866; govt. survt., 25th Apr., 1892; on spec. duty at Seychelles, 1897-1900.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the commrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1886; transf. to the C.O., April, 1891; astt. regist., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900.

HOCKING, SIR HENRY HICKS, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1842; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1864, and B.C.L. in 1867, 2nd class mods., 1st class law and history; Vinerian scholar, 1866; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Nov., 1867, and joined the home circuit; atty.-gen. for W. Australia, 1872; ag. ch. just., 1873; ag. ch. just., Gibraltar, 1879; atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1880; ret., 1896; acted as legal adviser to C.O. for some months in 1897.

HODGE, LEONARD PERCIVAL, F.S.I., Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Taunton, and King's Coll., Lond.; honors construc., 1886; 2nd cl. supt., P.W., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cl. ditto, and attached to head off., 1892; dir. of roads, G. Coast, 1894, but did not take up apptnt.; col. engrn. and surv.-gen., Dominica, 1898; draughtsman, P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 20th Mar., 1901.

HODGSON, SIR FREDERICK MITCHELL, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1851; entered Imp. P.O. and apptd., after compet. exam., Feb., 1869; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the state, and apptd. clk. in that office, 1870; apptd., after further exam., to the 3rd class (grade I) of the sec.'s office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was sen. capt. of the 24th Middlesex (post office), R.V.; postmstr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec., G. Coast, Sept., 1888; adminstd. the govt., June, 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894; Apr. to Oct., 1896; and Dec., 1897, to March, 1898; major comdg. the G. Coast R.V., which he raised in 1892; gov., G. Coast, 1898; gov. Barbados, Oct., 1900; gov., B. Guiana, 16th July, 1904.
HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Formerly mem. for Stellenbosch in Cape house of assembly; mem. of exec. coun., 1881; deleg. to col. confce., 1887 and 1894.

HOLDER, THE HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem. S. Australia, 1887; treasr. 1889 to Aug., 1890; premier and treasr., June to Oct., 1892; comsnr. of public works, June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; treasr., April, 1894, to Dec., 1899; premier and treasr., Dec., 1899, to May, 1901; mem., hse. of represent., Austrln. parl., 1901; speaker, 1901.

HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B. (1904).—Ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comsn., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comsn. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Ld. Rothschild's comttee. on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comsn., 1900-1901; to royal comsn. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comsn. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, 1905; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.
HOLLAND, J.—Apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904.

HOLLINGSWORTH, A. H.—A.M. Inst. C.E.; ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engrn., Croydon; ast. borough and water engrn., Croydon; ast. engrn., P.W.D., Hong-Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engrn., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engrn., 1st Jan., 1902.
HOLMES, JOHN RICHARD.—S.S.C., 1879; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1889; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1889; commanded detachment in Awoonah expdn., 1889-91; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1899.
HOLROYD, SIR E. D., KT. BACH. (1903).—Puise judge, Victoria.
HOLTZEE, MAURICE WM., F.L.S., etc.—Govt. gardener, Port Darwin, 1878; botanic gardener, 1880; dir. botanic garden, Adelaide, 1891.

HOMAGEE, JAMES FRANCIS.—Court messenger, St. Helena, 1859; clk. to ch. just., 1862; man. govt. savings bk., 1865; clk. of the peace, taxing master, and registr. sup. ct., 1867; legal adviser, crown prosecutor, and registr. V.-A. ct., 1870; also supervisor of customs and registr. of shipping, in 1883; admly. advoc., 1890.
HOMBURG, ROBERT.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., since 1884; atty.-gen., 1890-92 and 1892-3; ditto and min. of educn., 1904-5; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1905.

HONEY, JOHN WM.—Served under Cape govt., 1880 to 1901; transf'd. to Transvaal when collr. and prin. controller of cust. and prin. registr. of shipping; served in Gaika-Galeka and Basutoland campaigns, holding comsn. in latter as lieut. Capetown Rifles; dir. of cust., Transvaal, and cust. adviser to high comsnr., 16th May, 1901; M.L.C., Mem. I.C.C.; chrmn., tender and post office investment boards.

HONIBALL, OSCAR DUNSCOMBE, M.D., M.Ch., Queen's Univ. Ireland (scholar in mathematics and honourman in nat. phil.).—Govt. med. offr., Antigua, 1868; jun. and sen. med. offr., public hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, 1869-1870; gov't. med. offr., 1870-1872; med. supt., Imigrtn. Union hosp., St. Thomas-in-the-E., Jamaica, 1872; dist. med. offr., imigrtn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1879; is a fellow of the Obstetrical Soc. of Gt. Britain and Ireland; and J.P. and coroner for Br. Guiana.

HOOD, SAMUEL J.—Ast. warehouse keeper. Br. Hond., Oct., 1887; 4th clk., treas. and cust. Mar., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; clk., reg-gen., June, 1893; superv. of cust., Gold Coast, Feb., 1894; inspgr. superv., Nov., 1898; ast. col. of cust., S. Leone, Jan., 1901; ag. collr., Feb. to Aug., 1901; ast. comp. of cust., Gold Coast, Oct., 1901; ag. compt., Feb. to June, and Aug. to Dec., 1902, and Aug., 1903, to Feb., 1904; collr. of cust., Lagos, June, 1904.

HOOD, THO.—Ed. City of Lond. schl., Univ. Coll., and St. Bart.'s hosp., London; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lond.; ast. col. surg., S. Leone, 1897; served on staff during native rising, 1898 (W. African medal and clasp, 1898); comsnr. of Bandajuma dist., 1899; senior med. offr., 1902; ag. prin. med. offr., Oct. 1903, to Mar., 1904; J.P. for the colony.

HOPE, J. W.—Med. offr., convict estabmt. Fremantle; med. supt., invalid depot; med. offr., native penal settlement, Rottnest Is.; and health offr., Port of Fremantle, W. Australia, July, 1884; dist. med. offr. and quarantine offr., Fremantle, 1895.

HOPKINS, EDWARD FRANCIS.—Ed. at Portora roy. schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dub.; roy. scholarship, 1869; 1st honors in classics; honors in mathematics; B.A. in 1873; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1874; pol. mag., 1877; dist. judge, Matara, 1887; ast. govt. agr., Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1891; ditto, Hambantota, 1896; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1897; Negombo, Sept., 1898; Batticaloa, Dec., 1899; Negombo, June, 1900; registr.-gen., 23rd Nov., 1903.

HOPELEY, WILLIAM MUSGROVE.—B.A. (Camab.), 1878; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1878; joined Cape sup. ct. bar, 1878; ag. crown prosecutor (Griqualand W.), July, 1883; crown prosecutor (Griqualand W.), Feb., 1886; Q.C., 1890; a judge of the Cape sup. ct. and assigned to the high court of Griqualand Mar., 1892.

HORE, ERNEST HENRY.—Entered Imp. postal ser., July, 1871; transf'd. to Cyprus for special service, Oct., 1878; entered col. ser., Aug., 1882; as sub. postmtr., Limasol; acted several times as ch. clk., Feb., 1890; acted several times as Istd. postmtr.; apptd. Istd. postmtr., Apr., 1893.

HORNBY, MONTAGUE LYLAND.—D.S.O., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; interp. in Russian and Swahili; passed in Hindustani, Persian, Pushtri, and Arabic; passed entrance exam. for staff coll.; holds certif. as instr. in signalling, and army transport cert.; apptd. 2nd lieut. East Lancs. Regt., 29th Jan., 1889; lieut., 18th Mar., 1892; joined Indian Army, 27th Mar., 1892; statn. staff offr., Situbaldi, Cent. Prov., 12th Dec., 1892; orderly offr. to brig.-gen. comndg. Waziristan delimit. escort and 1st brig. Waziristan field force, 1894-95 (severely wounded at Wano, ment. in desp., D.S.O., medal with clasp); apptd. to Punjab frontier force 19th Sept., 1896; served in Tirah expdn., 1897-8, including operations in Bara Valley, 7th to 14th Dec., 1897 (medal with two clasps); apptd. adjt. and qrtmr., 1st Batt. (Indian contingent) Uganda Rifles, 9th Mar., 1898; served in Ogaden expdn., Jubaland, 1898 (medal and clasp); staff. offr., Unyoro mil. dist. (Uganda), 5th Feb., 1899; served in operations against mutineers and rebels in Unyoro, 1899, including capture of Kabarega and Medanga, as transport and signalling offr. to the force (clasp); promoted capt., 29th Jan., 1900; commanded Bukkedi expdn. and reconnaissnse towards Mount Elgone and Lake Salisbury (E. Africa), 1900; served in Nandi punitive expdn., 1900, in command of No. 1 column (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); raised and was apptd. comdt. of 5th (Indian) Batt. K.A.R., 1901; chief transport offr. to Somaliland field force of 1902-3, under Brig.-Gen. Sir W. H. Manning, and as comdt. 2nd (Cent. African) Batt. K.A.R., in subsequent operations of Gen. Sir C. Egerton in 1904 (ment. in desp. of Brig.-Gen. Manning, brevet of major, clasp); staff offr. to comsnr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 21st June, 1904, with temp. rank of lieut.-col.; ag. command of troops, Somaliland Prot., 22nd June, 1905.

HORNBY-PORTER, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1901; senior asst. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1902-1903; ag. col. sec., 1904; holds dormant comsn. to sit as pol. mag., and sat as such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1903; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1904, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. comsn., 1901; mem. of rlyw. comsn., 1901; pres. of cust. comsn., 1904.
HORNE, C. A.—C.C. and R.M., Bredasdorp, Cape Col., June, 1886; res. mag. and sub-collr. of cust., July, 1890; civ. comsnr. and res. mag., Ceres, Sept., 1902.

HORWOOD, SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1914).—Called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confederation with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., Sept., 1902, and again in 1904.
HOSE, CHARLES, D.S.C.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; res. 2nd cl., 4th div., Jan., 1891; res. 3rd Div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

HOWARD, E. C. C.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; cadet, S. Sttlmtns., 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Mar., 1894; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, Aug., 1894; collr. of land rev. Singapore, 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Sept. to Dec., 1901; 2nd mag., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. comsnr., ct. of req., Sing., Nov., 1903; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1904.

HOWARD, HON. OLIVER.—Honorary attache to legatn., at Tangier, June, 1899; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, 1901; 2nd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1903; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st July, 1905; 2nd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1905.
HOWELL, A. G.—5th clk. corrpdng. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. comsnr. of agriculture, 1899.
HOWELL, C. G.—Sec. and actuary, Barbados savings bank, 1891.
HOWELL, E. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898.

HOWELL, J. B.—3rd clk., treasy., Barbados, Nov., 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; ag. ch. clk., record branch, C.S.O., Sept., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., provost-marshal's office, Dec., 1897, to Apr., 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comttee., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, May to October, 1900; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. tc the gov. of Barbados, 1st Apr. to 6th July, 1902.
HOWES, SEYMOUR WYLDE.—Ag. 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Nov., 1899; temp. offr., treasy. dept., May, 1900.

HOWLAND, SIR WM. PEARCE, K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B.—B. 1812; min. of finance for the prov. of Canada, May, 1862; rec-gen., May, 1863; postmr.-gen., Nov., 1864; min. of inland rev., 1867; heut.-gov. of Ontario, 1868 to 1873.

HUDSON, ARTHUR.—Balliol Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1900; called to the bar (Inner Temp.), 1892; of S.E. circuit and W. Ham sessions; dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone protectorate, Oct., 1896; solr.-gen. of col. of S. L., Dec., 1897; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1897-1901; apptd. atty.-gen., 11th Aug., 1901.
HUDSON, C.—Dist. traffic man., Auckland rly., N. Zealand, 1884; asst. gen. man., N.Z. rly., Jan., 1895; now comsnr. of rlyws., Victoria.

HUDSON, HERBERT HENRY.—Dep. registr. sup. ct., Singapore, 1888; ag. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar. to Dec., 1892, and from Feb., 1893; ag. registr. sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1894; dep. registr. sup. ct. and mag., Malacca, Nov., 1894; asst. registr. sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1896; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May to Oct., 1900; sol.-gen., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., S. Sttlmtns., Oct.-Nov., 1903.

HUGGINS, HENRY DANIEL.—4th clerk audit office, Trinidad, Nov., 1873; asst. clk. of peace, St. Joseph's, Jan., 1876; clk., Aug., 1877; ditto, and sub-registr. sup. ct., San Fernando, Sept., 1879; clk. of peace, Port of Spain, Nov., 1880; special J.P., co. St. George, Feb., 1885; asst. mag., Port of Spain, Jan., 1887; stip. J.P. and warden, counties Niagara and Nariva, Sept., 1887; stip. mag., Cedros, Feb., 1894.
HUGHES, D. B. B., M.B.C.M. (Edin.).—1st prizeman, senior surgery; medallist, anatomy and pathology; ag. surg. in charge, col. hosp., Grenada, 1900; dist. med. offr., Saint Vincent, Dec., 1900.

HULL, H.M., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Charterhouse; priv. sec. to gov., G. Coast, and clk. of legis. and exec. couns., May, 1888, to Mar., 1891; travelling comsnr., G. Coast, Mar., 1891; one of the Br. comsners. for the delimitation of bndry. between the G. Coast Col. and the German Protectorate of Togo, Jan., 1892; ag. dir. of telegraphs, July to November, 1892; apptd. Br. comsnr. to delimit the bndry. between the G. Coast and the Ivory Coast, 1894, the comsn., however, did not meet; ag. dir. of telegraphs, Aug., 1895, to Feb., 1896; and mentioned in despatches for services during Ashanti expedition, 1895-96; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; ag. compt. of customs, Feb. to June, 1900; on sp. service to E. Akim during Ashanti rising, 1900, raised 3,500 native levies (medal); ag. compt. of customs, Nov., 1901; sec. for native affairs, Nov., 1902.
HULLETT, RICHMOND WM.—M.A. (Cantab); principal of Raffles Inst., S. Sttlmtns., Feb., 1871; ag. insp. of schls., S.S., 1874-76; ag. dir. of pub. inst., 11th Apr., 1903.

HULSE, LEIGH R.—Ed. Belize, Br. Honduras; appren. in gen. registry, Belize, Apr., 1893; copyist, Apl., 1894; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Nov., 1896; 4th clk. treasy. and customs dept. and clk. to the commrs. of currency, June, 1898; 3rd clk., Sept., 1900; 2nd clk., and clk. to quarantine bd., Dec., 1901; ag. auditor, Apl., 1903; ag. ch. clk., treasy. and cust., 30th May, 1905.
HUMPHREYS, JOHN.—Mag.'s clk., Br. Guiana; stip. mag., May, 1897.

HUMPHREYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treasy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevia, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treasy., Antigua, Apr., 1904.

HUNT, WALTER R.—Ed. at Charterhouse and King's Coll., Lond.; 2nd and audit clk. col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Mar., 1887; auditor Bahamas, 1890; M.L.C., Apl., 1899; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1901, and May, 1904; recvr.-gen. and treas. and mem. ex. coun., Mar., 1904.

HUNTER, C. H. P., I.S.O. (1903).—Clerk to auditor, St. Lucia, Nov., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., govt. office, Mar. to Nov., 1884; ag. ch. clk., treasy., Nov., 1884, to Mar., 1885, and May, 1889, to Mar., 1890; 3rd clk., treasy., Jan., 1885; 2nd clk. May, 1887; ch. clk., regir.'s office, Grenada, Nov., 1890; clk. of couns., Sept., 1890; ag. regir., sup. ct., Nov., 1890; marshal V.-A. ct., Feb., 1891; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Windward Is., Apr., 1891; 1st-class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1891; ch. clk., Aug., 1892; asst. treas., Jan., 1896; asst. col. sec., S. Leone, Oct., 1896; J.P. for the col., 1896; asst. col. sec., G. Coast Col., Oct., 1897; mentioned in despatches for services during Ashanti rebellion, 1900; ch. asst., col. sec., July, 1901; ag. col. sec., April to Dec., 1901, 27th Dec., 1902, to 27th May, 1903, 9th Feb. to 2nd Mar., 1904, and 20th June to 12th Nov., 1905; ag. deputy gov., 3rd Mar. to 30th Apr., 1903.

HUNTER, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1841; in service of N. Br. Rly. Co. 1853 to 1879; apptd. by S. of S. gen. man., Natal govt. rlys., Sept., 1879; mentioned in despatches, 1881; comrn., Natal harbour bd., 1881; received thanks of lieut.-gen. commanding troops for services of his dept., 1882; mem. exec. coun., 1890; deleg. to Harrismith rly. confce., 1890; deleg. to S.A. Republic on rly. extension from Natal terminus at Charlestown to Johannesburg, 1892, when survey of line by Natal engineers was arranged; again in 1893-4, when agreements were completed for construction of Charlestown extension; member Capetown rly. confce., 1895.

HUNTER, HAMILTON.—Ed. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. mag. for Taviuni, Fiji, Oct., 1874; stip. mag., Cakandrovi, Sept., 1875; comnr. of sup. ct., Apr., 1876; ch. pol. mag. for Fiji, Mar., 1877; mem. of land comnr., Sept., 1877; dep. comnr. for the W.
