Pacific, Nov., 1881; ag. regir.-gen., dep. regir. of titles, and dep. comnr. of stamps, Jan., 1892; also dep. judge V.-A. ct.; off. recr. in bankruptcy, 1894; regir. sup. ct. and curator, intes. estates, 1895; special judi. comnr. to visit islds. in H.M.S. "Goldfinch," 1896; visited Cook Islda. and Pitcairn in H.M.S. "Rovalista," 1898; ag. consul, Samoa, 1899.
HUNTER, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., April, 1890.

HUNTER, WILLIAM, M.B., C.M., Aberdeen, 1896 (Honours) (Univ. Aberdeen, Leipzig, Berlin, King's Coll. and London Hospital); James Anderson medallist and scholar, 1896; John Murray medallist and scholar, 1896; George Thompson travelling fellow, 1897-99; mem. of brit. med. assoc.; mem. of physiological and neurological societies; late astt. director of pathological inst., and astt. bacteriologist, London hospital; govt. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1902.

HUTCHINS, DAVID ERNEST.—Passed Indian forest service exam., Nov., 1869; three years at school of forests, Nancy, France, and special course of forestry instruction in Scotland; astt. conservator of forests, India, 3rd grade, 1872; 2nd grade, 1875; 1st grade, 1878; selected for special duty with the inspr.-gen. of forests and dep. conservator of forests, 2nd grade, 1882; transfd. temporarily to Cape Col., June, 1883; awarded diploma at the internat. forestry exhibtn., Edinburgh, 1884; conservator of forests, E. division, Cape, Jan., 1886; Knysna, Sept., 1888; selected to visit and report on forests of W. Virginia, 1889; accompanied Cooper's Hill students on official tour through S. German forests, 1890; conservator of forests, W. div., and consulting forest offr., Mar., 1892.

HUTCHINSON, SIR JOSEPH TURNER, K.T. BACH. (1895).—B. 1850; M.A., Christ's Coll. Camb.; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1879; Queen's advoc., G. Coast, Apr., 1888; ch. just., Jan., 1889; ditto, Windward Is., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1898.

HUTSON, BYRE, B.A.—2nd clk. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1885; astt. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1885; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and regir.-gen. Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comnr., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour comnr., 1903.

HUTSON, JOHN, B.A., Durham. 1879 (2nd cls. honours in class.); M.B., C.M., Edin., 1883; D.P.H., Cantab., 1906.—Res. surg., gen. hosp., Barbados, 1885; sen. res. surg., 1886-88; mem. of gen. bd. of health, Barbados, 1895; poor law inspr., 1901; mem. of quarantine comnr., 1903; chmn. of Glendairy prison comnr., 1903; delegate for Barbados at W. Indian quarantine confce., 1904.

HUTTON, EDWARD MALIN, M.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl.; scholar Queen's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods., 3rd class final classics; admitted solr., 1870; regir. sup. ct. and ct. of bankruptcy, Gibraltar, 1891; J.P. for Gibraltar, 1892.

HYSLOP, DR. J., D.S.O. (1901); M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—Res. surg., lunatic asylum, Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 4th July, 1882; and surg., Natal carabiniers; formerly astt. physician, Morningside Asyl., Edinburgh, and of Border Counties Asyl., Melrose; prin. med. offr., volrs., 1899; S. African war, 1899-1901 (ment. in despa.); chrmn., Natal bd. of health, 1903.

IM THURN, SIR EVERARD F., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxon; special mag., Pomeroon Dist. Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1890; govt. agt., N.W. dists., 1890; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Dec., 1899; col. sec. Ceylon, 1901; admstd. govt., Ceylon, 1902; gov. Fiji, and high comnr., W. Pacific, 21st June, 1904; editor of the Guiana scientific journal "Ti-mehri," 1882-7; author of "Among the Indians of Guiana," 1883, and papers on the anthropology, natural history, geography, and history of Guiana; in 1884 made first ascent of Mount Roraima in the interior of Br. Guiana.

INGLIS, JAMES.—Ed. Edinburgh Univ.; in India for twelve years; well known as an author under nom de plume of "Maori"; famine comnr. in Bhangulpore, 1875; exec. comsnr. for the govt. of India at Melbourne Exhibn., 1881; omsnr. at Sydney, Calcutta, and other exhibns.; entered parliament of N.S.W., 1885; min. of educn. in 1887; author of "Sport and Work on the Nepaul Frontier," "Our Australian Cousins," "Our New Zealand Cousins," "Tent Life in Tiger Land," and other works.

INNES, SIR MAMES ROSE, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1824; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reprtd. Cape div.; atty.-gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, 17th July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comtn. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; dele. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, 29th Mar., 1902.

INNES, JAMES ROSE, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1824; was teacher in govt. schls., C. of Good Hope, 1842; clk. to supt.-gen. of educn., 1845; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1847-56; mag. of Riversdale, 1856-63; ditto of Uitenhage, 1863-67; C.C. and R.M., Bedford div., Oct., 1867; also inspr. of roads; ag. admstr. of Griqualand W., 1880; under sec. for native affairs, 1881; ret. 1902.

INNES, JOHN ROBT., barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—Ed. Edin. and Brussels Univa.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1886; passed in Malay, 1889; dist. offr., S. Malacca, June, 1890; ditto, N. Malacca, June, 1890; ast. Indian immigr. agt., Malacca, June, 1889; ag. collr. land rev. and mag., Malacca, June to Nov., 1892; passed exam. in Dutch, 1893; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Feb., 1894, acted also as mag., Oct., 1894, to July, 1895, and as off. assignee and reg. of deeds, July to Oct., 1895; ag. ast. col. sec. Oct. to Dec., 1895; ag. collr. land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., also ag. mag. and dep. regist., sup. ct., Malacca, Mar., 1896; collr. of land rev., Penang, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr. prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1898; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Aug., 1898, to Apr., 1899; ag. inspr. prisons, S. S., June, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and from May to Dec., 1901; superint. census, 1901; deputy public prosecutor, Sing., Jan., 1902; sec. to govt., Perak, Feb., 1904.

INNISS, W. E.—Machinist, survey dept., S. Leone, Dec., 1866; under gaoler, Freetown, June, 1871; foreman and machinist, harbour works, Dec., 1872; keeper, Freetown gaol, Aug., 1874; inspr. of pub. wks., Dec., 1882; served in Yonnie expedn., 1887; offr. in charge, Sulymah, 1888.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY.—Clk., admstr.'s off., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to admstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion, 1896 (medal), and Rhodesia field force, 1900 (medal and 2 clasps).

IRELAND, ARCHIBALD EDWARD.—M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., D.P.H. Oxford; St. Bart's hosp., Lond.; late clinical asst., Birmingham and Midland skin and urinary hospital; res. med. offr., The Infirmary, B'ham.; ditto, City Hosp., B'ham.; asst. comsnr. and govt. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1903.

IRVING, CHARLES JOHN, C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1831; in col. land and immigr. office from June, 1852; clk., audit office, 1853; special clk., audit office, Mauritius, 1864; auditor-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1867; rea. councilr, Malacca, 1879; ditto, Penang, 1882; ag. lieut.-gov., Malacca, 1870 and 1871; and of Penang, 1879; ag. col. sec., 1871, 1875, 1878, and 1888; ret., 1887.

IRVING, EDWARD A.—Joined Perak, F.M.S. service, 25th Mar., 1891; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese (Hakka dialect), Feb., 1895; ag. collr. land rev., Kinta dist., June, 1895; prot. of Chinese, Lerut dist., July, 1896; warden of mines, Selangor, Feb., 1896; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1899; passed final exam. in law, July, 1900; inspr. of schools, Hong Kong, Apl., 1901; mem. of educn. comtee., 1901-2; passed final exam. in Cantonese, May, 1905; ag. registr.-gen. and M.L.C., July to Sept., 1905.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD.—Ed. at Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1890-1902; served in S. African campaign; oh. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902.

IRVING, SIR HENRY T., C.G.M.G. (1888), K.C.M.G. (1878), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1833; clk. in C.O., Nov., 1864; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers, now Lord Blachford, 1862; promoted to 2nd class, Apr., 1863; col. sec. of Jamaica, June, 1866; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1869; gov. Leeward Is., 1873; gov. Trinidad, July, 1874; gov. Br. Guiana, 1882; ret. 1888.

IRVING, WILLIAM HOWE.—Apptd. stock inspr., Queensland, 1866; junr. offr., customsdept., Brisbane, 1867; 2nd offr., shipping master, etc., Maryborough, 1870; ch. clk., Brisbane, 1883; landing survr. on amalgamn. of offices, Apr., 1886; collr. of customs and ch. inspr. of distilleries, Queensland, 1893; mem. of immigr. bd., 1894.

ISAAC, CHARLES POWYS.—Clk., col. branch, ex. and audit dep., Aug., 1890; clk. in charge of acta., Jan., 1894; detached for service as loc. auditor, Uganda Prot., June, 1895, to Sept., 1897; and again as loc. audtr., N. Nig., May, 1900, to Aug., 1901; prin. clk., Transvaal aud. off., Apr., 1902; ast. and-gen., Transvaal, Feb., 1903; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1903, to May, 1904; aud. to I.C.C., S. Africa, Oct., 1904.

ISAACS, HENRY BABER.—Clk. of petty sessions and sub-clk. of taxes, dist. No. 3, Tobago, July, 1878; ag. inland rev. offr., Leeward Dist., Oct., 1878, to Dec., 1878; ag. 2nd rev. offr., Mar., 1880; 1st rev. offr., May, 1882; rev. offr., and regist. of births and deaths, Carriacou, Nov., 1884; is a J.P.; pol. mag. and coroner, 2nd dist., St. Vincent, 1898.
ISRAEL, JOHN W.—Ent. govt. service, 1872, Tasmania; auditor-general, 1896.
IZARD, REV. HERBERT CRAWFORD.—B.A. (Oxon); col. chaplain, Malacca, S. Sttlmts., 19th Oct., 1901; ag. col. chap., Sing., Oct., 1904; confirmed, May, 1905.
JACKMAN, HON. E. M.—M.H.A. for Placentia and St. Mary's, Newfoundland, 1900; min. of finance and cust., 1900; re-elected to Assembly, 1904; gov., sav. bank, 1900.

JACKMAN, HENRY THOS.—Crystal Palace engnl. schl., 1890-3; pupil and ast. on water and drainage wks. to Messrs. McLandborough and Preston, civ. engrs., 1893-6; in charge of new sewerage wks., Ripon, 1896-7; ast. dist. engrn. on electric tramways, drainage wks., etc., at Sheffield, 1897-1903; exec. engrn. on water and drainage wks., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1903; ag. sanitary survr., 1904-5.
JACKSON, BEDVER BLAINE LOWTHER.—Ed. Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., July, 1901.
JACKSON, CECIL GOWER.—Ag. clk. and student interpreter, sec. native affairs off., and as

JAMESON, ADAM.—M.B. (1883), M.D. (1897), Edin.; M.L.C., metropolitan suburban province, W. Aust., 1901; hon. minister, 1901; min. for lands, 1901-2; ditto, 1902; comnr. of crown lands, Transvaal, 1903; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.
JAMESON, CHAR.—Called to the bar, Queensland, Feb., 1883; sec. to crown law offrs., Apr., 1886.

JAMESON, HENRY B. L.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; clk. to col. sec., Falkland Is., Nov., 1882; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., May, 1883; ag. col. sec., treas., etc., Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1888; J.P. for the Islands; asst. col. treas., West Coast, 1892; warehouse keeper and examining offr., Bahamas, 1894.

JAMESON, DR. LEANDER STARR, C.B. (1894).—B. 1853; M.B., B.S., M.D., Lond., M.R.C.S., Eng.; chief mag. and admstr. of Mashonaland, Sept., 1891; admstr. of Matabeleland, Sept., 1894; res. comnr. of territories along western border of late S.A.R. north of Bechuanaland, Oct. to Dec., 1895; M.L.A. for Kimberley, 1900; ditto for Grahamstown, 1904; premier and sec. for native affairs, Cape Colony, Feb., 1904.

JANISCH, NOEL.—Served under the St. Helena govt., Aug., 1870, to Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., office of comnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., Capetown, Apr., 1878; on active service with Cape Volra, in the Transkei in 1879, and during Basuto war, 1881; 2nd class clk., July, 1881; 1st class, Apr., 1887; ch. clk., 1892; sec. for pub. wks., Sept., 1892; additional mem. of tender bd., May, 1894; under col. sec., July, 1898; dir. of census, 1904.
JARRETT, MICHAEL LEWIS, M.R.C.S. (Lond.), L.R.C.P. (Edin.).—Asst. col. surg., Sherbro, W. Africa, 1882; is a J.P.

JARVIS, LIEUT.-COL. ARTHUR LEONARD FITZGERALD, I.S.O. (1905).—B. 1852; entd. Canadian govt. service, 1868; priv. sec. to P.M.G., 1882 to 1885; and to min. of agric., 1885 to 1892; sec. of dept. of agric., 1896; commanded Gov.-General's Foot Guards, 1899 to 1904.

JARVIS, EDWARD B.—2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., 1890; clk. of gov.'s office, 1891; ag. 1st clk., 1891; ag. 1st clk. col. sec.'s office, Mar. to June, 1896. On reorganisation of office, apptd. 2nd clk.; ag. ch. clk., and clk. to fed., exec., and legis. couns. and local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1897, to Jan., 1898, and July to Dec., 1899; P.S. to ag. gov., June to Oct., 1899; ch. clk. col. sec.'s off., and clk. to fed., exec., and leg. couns. and local leg. coun., 3rd July, 1901; asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., Feb., 1903; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1905; ag. fed. treas., Aug. to Oct., 1905.

JARVIS, J. LESLIE H. —Dep. regisr. of marriages, births and deaths, 1890-2, and census offr. for parish of St. George, Antigua; clk. to admstr., and to exec. coun., Dominica, Oct., 1895; ag. clk. bd. of health, 1896.

JEFFERY, GEORGE.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archeology of Russia, 1897; instr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903.

JEKYLL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1846; lieut. R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comte., 1878; sec. to the roy. comn. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comte., 1885; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comn. for Melbourne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comn. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JENKINS, Sir GEORGE HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1843; clk. in Victorian govt. rlys., 1861-5; clk. of priv. bills and priv. sec. to speaker in legis. assem., 1865-70; clk. of comtees., 1870-78; clk.-asst., 1878-82; and clk. of legis. assem., 1882-91; since when he has been clk. of parlmt. of Victoria; sec. to royal comn. on question of payment of members of legislature, 1868; sec. to royal comn. for completion of houses of parlmt., 1877; clk. of Australasian Federation conf., Melbourne, 1890; special vote of thanks for services; presented by members of legis. assem. with service of silver plate on completion of his twenty-fifth year as an offr. of their house, and a unanimous resolution of legis. assem. was placed on its records in recognition of his prolonged and singularly valuable services rendered to the house and its members, on his retiring from the position of clk. of the assem. to fill the office of clk. of parlmt.; clk. to fed. coun. of Australasia during its eighth and last session, Melbourne, Jan., 1899; presented by members of legis. coun. with a gold watch and chain on leaving their services temporarily for the Commonwealth parlmt.; apptd. by Prime Minister of Australia to act as sec. to Commonwealth govt. and to undertake management of ceremony connected with opening of Commonwealth parlmt., and also of the reception given to their Royal Highnesses the Duke and Duchess of York, on 9th May, 1901; specially thanked for services by the Duke of York, the Gov.-General, and the Prime Minister; offered by the Prime Minister the position of clk. of the House of Representatives of Commonwealth parlmt., and accepted it temporarily; on retiring from clerkship of H. of R. to return to his office of clk. of parlmt. of Victoria the members unanimously accorded him a vote of thanks for his able services to the Commonwealth in respect of the inauguration of the parlmt.; elected pres. of the Society of Old Melburnians for the year 1894-5; is an hon. J.P. for Victoria, N.S. Wales and S. Australia.

JENKINS, THE HON. JOHN GREELEY.—Mem. H. of A., S. Aust., 1887; min. of ed. and N. territory, 1891-2; comsnr. of pub. wks., 1892; ditto, 1894-9; chief sec., 1899-1901; premier and ch. sec., 1901; agent-gen. in London, July, 1905.

JERNINGHAM, Sir Hubert Edward Henry, F.R.G.S., K.C.M.G. (Jan., 1893), C.M.G. (1889), B. ès Lettres, Paris, Officier d'Académie, France (1904).—B. 1842; attaché, 1866; passed an exam. and obtained an hon. certif., 1886; attaché at Paris, 1867, and was transf. to Constantinople, 1870; 3rd sec., 1870; on temporary duty at Athens, 1870; and was transf. to Carlsruhe and Darmstadt, 1872; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1873; 2ndsec., 1873; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1874, 1875, 1876, and 1877; was transf. to Vienna, 1877; ag. agrt. and consul-gen. at Belgrade, 1878; was unemployed from 1879 till 1881, when he resig.; M.P. for Berwick, 1881-5; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1887; received Queen's Jubilee Medal, 1887; administered the govt. for three months in 1888; col. sec., Mauritius, 1889; administered the govt., Mar., 1892; lieut.-gov., Aug., 1892; gov. and commr.-in-ch., July, 1893; gov., Trinidad, Jan., 1897, to 1900; is a J.P. and D.L. for Northumberland.
JETTE, Sir L. A., K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1836; puisne judge, Quebec, 1878; lieut.-gov., Quebec, 1897.
JEWELL, M.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899.

JEX, ROBERT A. N.—Ed. St. John Berchman's Coll., Belize, Br. Hond.; apptd., after compet. exam., copyist in col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1897; ag. 4th clk., Aug. to Nov., 1897; ag. 3rd clk., May, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., Feb., 1899, to Feb., 1900; ag. clk. to reg. genl., Oct. to Dec., 1900; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to Sept., 1901; 3rd clk., May, 1901; clk. to auditor, 1st Dec., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., C.S.O., 24th Apr., 1903, to 26th July, 1904; ag. clk. to col. engrnr., 5th Jan. to 18th Apr., 1905; ag. 1st cls. clk., C.S.O., 21st Apr. to 31st Aug., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., gen. regist., 1st Sept., 1905.

JOHNSON, DUDLEY.—Dist. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1891; registr-gen. births, deaths, and marriages for dist. A., Oct., 1893; road survr. for dists. 2 and 3 north and 3 south, Jan., 1900; local agricultural instructor, July, 1900.

JOHNSON, EDWARD RICHARD.—Joined post. and tel. dept., O.F.S., Oct., 1890; asst. postmr. and telegraphist, Jagersfontein, Oct., 1890; ditto, Fauresmith, Nov., 1892; postmr. and telegraphist, Thaba' Nohu, June, 1894; ditto, Koffyfontein, Sept., 1894; ch. clk. to P.M.G., O.F.S., Dec., 1896; relieving offr., Feb., 1897; ch. clk., secretarial branch, dep. of posts and tels., O.R.C., Mar., 1900; prin. clk. and asst. sec., ditto, July, 1902; asst. sec., ditto, July, 1903.
JOHNSON, EDWIN.—Apptd. to educn. dept., N.S. Wales, 1855; inspr. of schls., 1862; ch. inspr., 1889; under-sec., educn. dept., 1884.

JOHNSON, E. O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Harr. Coll., Barbados; 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, 1884; 1st treasy. offr., 1886; 1st clk. treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1891; 1st rev. offr., 1894; ag. treasr., Dec., 1896, to Sept., 1897; asst. treasr., S. Leone, Nov., 1897; col. treasr., Mar., 1899.
JOHNSON, F. E. G.—B.A., Cantab.; asst. dist. comsrr., So. Nigeria, Apl., 1900; dist. comsrr., July, 1902; asst. sec. (temporarily), Feb. to Aug., 1904; ag. pol. mag., Calabar, Aug., 1904.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAM.—2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; ag. extra rev. offr., May, 1895; 2nd clk. admstr.'s office, clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; ag. acctnt. treasy., Jan., 1897; audit clk. and clk. legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1897; clk. to admstr., exec., and legis. counc., and clk. to bd. of health, Jan., 1901; asst. priv. sec. (unpaid) to H.E. Chas. T. Cox, C.M.G., ag. gov., Leeward Is., 1903, 1904; ag. asst. treas. and controller of cust., offl. mem., legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis; chrmn., Basseterre poor-law bd., Apl., 1903, to Mar., 1904; mem. quarantine bd., 1903; compiled index to the laws of Leeward Is. and St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901.

JOHNSON, GEORGE WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1905), M.A.—Scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 8th wrangler and 3rd class classics, 1880; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Mar., 1881; sec. to Eastern currency comtce., 1893; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, May, 1896; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, Sept., 1896; 1st class clk., Mar., 1897; ag. prin. clk., July, 1900; mem. of S. S. currency comtce., 1902.
JOHNSON, HENRY STAFFORD BROWNLOW.—Cadet, Sarawak, 1897; astt. resid., 1901; 2nd cls. res. in charge of Upper Rejang, 1903.
JOHNSON, JOHN RICHARD.—Pharmaceutical chemist, mem. of phar. soc. of Gt. Britain; med. storekeeper, govt. med. dept., Jamaica; apptd. to act as govt. analytical chem., May, 1899.

JOHNSTON, HARRY F.—Contract survr., W. Australia, Jan., 1874; ag. staff survr., Nov., 1883; confirmed, Feb., 1885; surveyed and triangulated Kimberley dist., and discovered Elvire and Mary rivers, where goldfields have been found; in charge of constrn. of telegraph, Roebourne to Derby, 1886-9; survr.-gen., July, 1896.

JOHNSTON, LEWIS AUDLEY MARSH.—Ed. Derby schl.; B.A. Trin. Coll., Dub.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam., Malay, Oct., 1890; attached to G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; ag. dist. offr., N. Malacca, June, 1892; dep. regis. deeds and official assignee, Singapore, June, 1894; astt. postmr.-gen., June, 1895; ag. postmr.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1896; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Aug., 1896; ag. postmr.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1902; P.M.G., Hong Kong, 1902; ag. treas. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1903-5.

JOHNSTON, R. FLEMING.—M.A., Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901); Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; proc. acc. Lord Rector's essay; 1st cl. certs. Eng. lit., mod. history and constitutional law, Edin.; exhibitioner, Magdalen Coll., 1894; mentioned hon. causa Stanhope essay, 1898; B.A. (hons.), 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of councils, 1899; priv. sec. to H.E. the administrator; ag. astt. col. sec., 1900; priv. sec. to the governor, 1900-1901; passed cadet, Oct., 1901; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of councils, Nov., 1901; ag. sec. to govt., Weihaiwei, 1904.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT MACKENZIE, I.S.O.(1903), F.S.S.(1879).—Regis.-gen. and govt. statistician of Tasmania; ed. Andersonian Univ., Glasgow; apptd. to organise and superintend the working of the acctt.'s and traffic audit branch of the first rly. opened in Tasmania (1870); transf. to service of Tasmanian Govt. (1872); ch. clk. in auditor-gen.'s office (1880); apptd. to reorganise and conduct the then combined offices of regis.-gen. and govt. statistician (1882); roy. comsnr. to inquire and report on the fisheries of the col. of Tasmania (1882); apptd. salmon fisheries comsnr. (1882); mem. of the fisheries bd. (1889); mem. of technical educn. bd. (1888-90); chmn. of boundaries bd. of advice (1890); author of "Systematic Account of the Geology of Tasmania" and other scientific wks.; fellow of the Roy. Statis. Soc. of Lond.; mem. of the coun. of the Roy. Soc. of Tasmania and of the Australasian Assoc. for Advcmt. of Science; fellow of the Roy. Geog. Soc. of Australasia.

JOHNSTONE, EDWARD.—Ed. Cheltenham and Westward Ho! colleges; lieut., 4th Batt. P.A., Somerset L.I., 1885-1887; astt. supt. of pol., Madras presidency, 1887-88; served in 1st Batt. Durham L.I. (83rd), 1889-1895; astt. inspr., Lagos Hausa force, 1896; on spec. serv. to G. Coast hinterland, 1897 (Samoury expedn., W. Africa, medal and clasp); priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Denton, lt.-gov. of Lagos, 1900; comnr. of police, Lagos, 1900.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Edinburgh; temporary clk., audit office, Jamaica, 1878; 3rd-class clk., col. sec.'s office, 1879; sec. to bd. of supervision, poor relief, Sept., 1886; is also sec. schools commn. from 1883; and astt. govt. meteorologist from 1890 to 1899; sec. to customs defalcations, 1889; conduct of two public offrs., 1890; Milk River, 1892; ed. in Jamaica, 1898; apptd. mem. and man. dir. of local bd. of Mans. Ditchfield (school) trust property, 1899; author of "Nelson and the West Indies," and of "Poverty and the Poor Law in Jamaica," &c.; F.R. Met. Soc., 1896.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and City of London Schl.; astt. warden, Naparima, Trinidad, 1879; of St. Ann's and Diego Martin, 1885; warden, Naparima, Nov., 1889; stip. J.P. of Naparima, Croupouche, and La Brea, Mar., 1891; is major Trinidad light volra.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT STEWART.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. commr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comman. 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. places, Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902.
JOLICEUR, PHILIPPE J., K.C.—Asst. sec. of the prov. of Quebec.
JOLLIVET, ARTHUR B. YVES.—L.R.C.P., Glas.; L.R.C.S., Edin.; health offr., Mauritius, 20th Mar., 1885; med. supt., Barkly asylum, 15th Nov., 1895.

JOLY DE LOTBINIERE, SIR HENRY GUSTAVE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1829; ed. in Paris, and called to the bar of Quebec in 1855; elected to parlmt. of old Canada for Lotbiniere in 1861, and continued to represent the county till 1867, when he was returned both to the parlmt. of the Dominion and to the legislature of Quebec; in 1874 he ret. from parlmt. to devote his attention to provincial affairs; in 1878, on the dismissal of the De Boucherville cabinet, he was called on to form a ministry, which he did, holding power for one session by virtue of the vote of the speaker; resig. 1879, after defeat in the house of assen.; later he ret. from the leadership of the party; and in 1886, as a protest against the Riel agitation carried on by the provincial liberal party, ret. from public life; in 1877 was offered the portfolio of agricul. in the Mackenzie govt., with a seat in the senate; controller of inland rev., without a seat in the cabinet, in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 1900.

JONES, HON. ALFRED GILPIN.—Ed. Yarmouth Acad., Nova Scotia; W. Indian merchant; gov. Dalhousie Coll.; pres. N. S. Marine Insurance Co., and dir. Acadia Fire Insurance Co.; comdr. Halifax garrison artillery; for some years mem. of Dominion house of commons for Halifax, 1867-72, 1874-78, and again in 1877, sworn of privy coun. and min. of militia in Mackenzie admin., 1878; mem. Pacific Cable comtce., 1896; lieut.-gov. of Prov. of Nova Scotia, Aug., 1900.

JONES, EDWARD RUTTER.—Acted aidwaiter, customs, Antigua, 1875 to 1880; apptd. cashier, treasy., Antigua, Oct., 1880; landing waiter, customs, Antigua, 1881; acted 1st clk., audit offr., Leeward Is., 1887 and 1892; apptd. 1st indoor offr.. treasy., Antigua, 1895; ag. treas., Antigua, Sept., 1899, Oct., 1900, July, 1901, May, 1902, and Mar. to Dec., 1903; astt. treas. and compt. of cust., St. Kitts-Nevia, Mar., 1904; offl. M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevia, 1904; chrmn., P.L. board and mem. quarantine board., J.P., St. Kitts-Nevia, 1904.
JONES, JAMES W.M.—Survr., survey dept., S. Aust., 1865; chief survr., 1877; dep. survr.-gen., 1881; conservator of water, 1883; sec. to commr. of pub.wks., and chrmn. of supply and tender bd., 1902.

JONES, PATRICK N. H., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond., applied sciences; student I.C.E., 1884; res. engrnr., Barbados Water Supply Co., 1889 to 1895; ag. D.P.W., Barbados, Sept. to Oct., 1894; thanked by leg. coun. for services in drafting waterworks bill, 1894; apptd. engrnr. in charge of waterworks surveys, Trinidad, 1895; ag. res. engrnr. of water and sewerage works, Feb. to Nov., 1898; and Jan. to Oct., 1900; in charge of construction of water and sewerage works, 1900 to 1903; mem. of comte. on tech. educn. in the colony, 1902; astt. D.P.W., Hong Kong, 3rd April, 1903; ag. D.P.W. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1904-1905.
JONES, S. SALTUS.—Asst. book-keeper, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 4th clk., rec-gen.'s office, Jan., 1884; 3rd clk., May, 1884; 1st-class clk., Apr., 1888; cashier, July, 1888.

JONES, S. T.—Ed. Wes. high schl., S. Leone; spec. prize for "marked proficiency" in studies, 1895; R. coll. of preceptors and Pitman's shorthand certificates; appointed 6th cls. clk., P.O., Niger Coast Prot., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., high comsmr.'s off., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., 1902; Aro medal and clasp, 1902; 1st cls. clk., secretariat, 1903; sen. clk., secretariat, S. Nigeria, 1904, chief olk., ditto, 1906.

JONES, W. H. HYNDMAN.—Ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; LL.B. honours, 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1878; ag. judge of astt. ct. of appeal, Apr., 1880; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Aug., 1880; one of the comsmrs. to inquire into the condition of the pol. force in Barbados, Oct., 1880; stip. mag., St. Lucia, Oct., 1881; mem. of legis. coun., May, 1881; has acted several times as ch. just., St. Lucia and of Tobago; pol. mag., Grenada, 1887; and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1888; puisne judge, S.S., 1896; acted as judicial comanr., Fed. Malay States, April, 1903.

JONES, SIR W. H. QUAYLE, KT. BACH.(1892).—B. 1854; temporary puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1882 and 1883; mem. of Lond. exec. and chmn. of local comte. for the Col. and Indian Exhibn., 1886; Queen's advoc., 1883; ch. just., W. Africa Sttlmtns., 1887; ret. 1895; chmn. of sessions.

JONES, WM. SOUTHERN MALCOLM.—Tempry. employed as clk. in sup. ct. at Shanghai from 19th Apr., 1874, till Jan., 1878, when he passed an exam., and was confirmed in that appt.; retired on a pension, 1st Apr., 1891; apptd. dist. offr. at Bulhar, Somali Coast Prot., 1st Oct., 1898; promoted to be H.M.'s vice-consul at Zaila, 15th Nov., 1902; style altered to "sub-comsmr." on the withdrawal of the consular attributes of Protectorate officials, 27th July, 1904.
JORDAN, ALBERT JOHN.—Late of Kew Gardens; agricultural instructor, Montserrat, 1899.
JOSCELYNE, R. H.—Transferred from Royal Niger Company's service to C.O. as supplementary clerk, Jan., 1900.
JOSEPH, GERARD A.—Priv. sec. to Mr. Just. Clarence, Ceylon, 1891; librarian and sec. Colombo museum, Oct., 1893; is hon. sec. Ceylon branch Roy. Asiatic Soc.

JOSKE, ADOLPH B.—Inspr. native taxes, Fiji, June, 1884; stip. mag. and astt. res. comsmr. for Colo. E., and inspr. of native taxes, Oct., 1884; comsmr., Colo. N., S.M. Ra., and dep. comdt. armed native constab., 1892; comsmr., Colo. E., 1897.

JOYNT, HENRY NOBLE H.—Ed. at Portora Roy. Schl. and Univ. of Dub.; prizeman, royal sohrl., 1st honour-man, 1st senr. modr. and gold medallist in nat. sc.; B.A., M.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O., diploma in state med.; L.M., Rotunda Hosp.; astt. med. offr., fever hosp., Dub.; sen. astt. med. hosp., City Hosp., Birmingham; supt., City Hosp., Bradford; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1892; J.P. of col.

JULLI, MARTIN EDWARD.—Ed. Brighton Gram. Schl.; articled 3 yrs. and afterwards astt. survr. with a firm of survs. of city of London; clk. P.W.D., W. Australia, Jan., 1887; ch. clk., Jan., 1892; under sec. P.W., July, 1896.

JULYAN, SIR PENROSE G., K.C.M.G.(1874), C.B.(1869).—B. 1816; served as a volr. during Canadian rebellion in 1837-8; spcl. comsmr. of roads and bridges in Lower Canada in 1839; apptd. to the commissariat in 1845; promoted to rank of dep. astt. comary.-gen., and received the thanks of col. govt.; astt. financial sec. to bd. of wks. in Ireland, 1848; dir. of a branch of the royal mint in Australia in 1852, and was employed on mint service until the war broke out with Russia, when he was recalled to the commissariat; employed as an engrn. during early part of the war, and then designed and superintended the construction of a marine steam flour mill and a floating bakery for the supply of the army in the Crimea, which was of much service throughout the war. Received the special thanks of the min. for war, and promoted astt. comary.-gen., Jan., 1856; and created a C.B. in May, 1869; crown agt. for col. in 1858; sent on special comsn. of inquiry into the civil establishments and general expenditure of Mauritius, 1873; and again on a similar mission to Malta, in 1878; ret., Nov., 1879.

JUST, ALBERT WOLFGANG, B.A.(1891).—Jun. officer, State of Perak, July, 1892; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1893; astt. to dist. mag. and collr. of land rev., Krian, Jan., 1894; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1903.

JUST, HARTMANN WOLFGANG, C.B.(1902), C.M.G.(1900), B.A.(1877).—B. 1854; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in office of sec. of state for the col., 3rd June, 1878; ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; was an open scholar on the foundation; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; Taylorian exhibtioner for German, 1876; 2nd class in final classical school, 1877; astt. priv. sec. to the late Earl of Derby, 6th Feb., 1883, to Col. F. A. Stanley (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, and to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; priv. sec. to Mr. Osborne Morgan, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; astt. priv. sec. to Sir H. Holland (now Viscount Knutsford), 12th Jan., 1887, and to the Marquess of Ripon, 17th Aug., 1892; astt. sec. to the col. confce., 1887; 1st class clk., Apr., 1895; prin. clk., Mar., 1897; accompanied Rt. Hon. Joseph Chamberlain on his visit to S. Africa, 1902-3.

JUSTICE, MAJ.-GEN. WILLIAM CLIVE, C.M.G.(1881).—B. 1836; entered 75th regt. in 1862, col., 1881; has held the following apptmts.; adjt., 75th regt.; adjt., depot batt.; town major, W. dist., England; mil. sec. to comdr-in-ch., Bombay; astt. adjt.-gen., Bombay; astt. adjt. and qrtmr-gen., W. Indies; col. on the staff, W. coast of Africa; col. on the staff, Jamaica; served in E. Indies; during the mutiny, 1857-8, and was present during the siege, assault, and capture of Delhi (medal and clasp); in command of an expdn. sent in anticipation of war with Ashanti in 1881; now in command of the troops in Ceylon; was pres. of commn. on Barbados pol. force, 1880; sen. mem. exec. and legis. couns., G. Coast, 1881; sen. mem. exec. couns., Barbados, 1883; sen. mem. privy and legis. couns., Jamaica, 1886; and administered the govt., Feb. to Mar., 1887, May to Aug., 1888, Jan. to Mar., 1889; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Ceylon, 1893.

KAYE, A. B. R.—Lieut. 3rd batt., Roy. Ir. Fus., 1879; capt., 1887; hon. major, 1894, and local lieut.-col. in the army, Feb., 1903; served during Indian rebellion, N.W. Territory of Canada, 1885, with Boulton's M.I., was present at the Fish Creek engagement and the taking of Batoche (medal and clasp); sub-insp. of constab., Br. Hond., 1887; ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, Br. Hond., 1889; J.P., 1889; inspr., 1889; ag. inspr. comdt., Br. Hond. constab., and ex-officio mem. of exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1889, to May, 1890; again ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, Nov., 1890, to Apr., 1892; recd. gratuity and thanks of govt for serv. during small-pox epidemic, 1891-2; ag. inspr. comdt., Apr., 1892; inspr. comdt. and mem. exec. coun., Aug., 1892; inspr. of pol. and supt. of fire brig., Barbados, Feb., 1899; inspr.-gen. of pol., Aug., 1901.
KEAN, HON. ABRAHAM.—M.H.A., Bonavista, Newfild., 1885-89, Bay de Verde, 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; ag. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.
KEEBLE, JOHN BRIGHT.—Ed. Queen Elizabeth's gram. schl.; 1st clk., sec.'s off., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Feb., 1902; ag. ass't. sec., Apr. to Aug., 1903; asst. sec. to admtn., Aug., 1903.
KEEFER, THOMAS COLTRIN, C.M.G. (1878), C.E.—B. 1821; engnr. of several rlyws. and other wks. in Canada; was exec. commr. for the Dominion of Canada at the Paris Exhib., 1878.

KEITH, A. BERRIEDALE.—Ed. at Royal High Schl. and Univ., Edinburgh; 7 class medals, Van Dunlop scholarship in classics, 1895; M.A., 1st class hon. classics, 1897; Furguson schl., Glasgow, 1897; Guthrie Fellowship in Class. Lit., 1899; open scholarship, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1895; Boden scholarship, 1898; 1st class hon. moderations, 1899; 1st class Lit. Ind. (Sanskrit and Pali), 1900; 1st class Lit. Hum., 1901; B.A., 1900; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 21st Oct., 1901; sec. to crown agta., July, 1903; called to the bar (1st cls. honours) 1904; B.C.L., 1905; re-transfd. to C.O., 1st May, 1905.

KELK, REV. W. HASTINGS.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; deacon, 1892; priest, 1893; curate of Morley, York, 1892-4; missionary at Ainanoob, Syria, 1894-7; Haifa, Syria, 1897-8; col. chaplain and regar. of deaths, Accra, 5th Aug., 1899; chrmn. of census comte, and compiler of census, 10th May, 1901; ag. director of educn., July to Oct., 1901.

KELLY, C. C.—Supernum. marshal, Essequibo, Dec., 1873; ag. clk., Suddie treasy., Oct., 1883; ordinary marshal, provost marshal's office, Georgetown, June, 1884; mag.'s clk., Essequibo, Aug., 1887; ag. ch. clk., pol. mag.'s office, Georgetown, June, 1883.

KELLY, HENRY GREENE.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the Bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as Chief Justice; Chief Justice, 1899; Chief Justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900.
KELSON, JACOB WALTER.—Ck., survr.'s dept., G. Coast, 1885; ch. warder and med. dresser, lun. asyl., Accra, 1887.

KEMP, J. H.—B.A., Cape Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land offr., New Territory, 1899; registr., land court, July, 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; asst. sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-regar. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1904.

KEMPE, JOHN ARROW, C.B. (1900), B.A.—B. 1846; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; clk., treasy., 1867; priv. sec. to chancellor of excheqr., 1874-80, to Sir R. Lingen, 1880-81, to Lord F. Cavendish, 1881; mem. of commn. on agric. and dairy schls., 1887-88; priv. clk., treasy., 1888; dep. chmn., bd. of cust., 1894; mem. of royal commn. on elec. commun. with lighthouses and light vessels, 1892-97; asst. comptlr. and auditor, 1904; comptlr. and auditor-gen., 1905; is hon. auditor of certain crown colonies and protectorates, ag. on behalf of S. of the cols.
KEMPSFORD, GEORGE HENRY.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 22nd Feb., 1904.

KENNAN, THOMAS PONSONBY K.—Served in F.A.M.P. and C.M.R., 1877 to 1882, during Griqua and Basuto wars (medal); temporary clk. to R.M. Leribe, Basutoland, 1882; sub-inspr., Basuto pol., 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. commr., Quilting, 1889.

KENNAWAY, WALTER, C.M.G. (1891).—Mem. prov. coun., Canterbury, N.Z., 1868-74; prov. sec. and sec. pub. wks., Cant., 1870-74, mem. bd. of educn., also bd. of govt., Cant., Coll.; 1873-4; sec., N.Z. govt. offic., London, 1874; commr., Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; apptd. offr. of French Acad. for services at Paris Exhibn., 1889; ag. agt.-gen., 1891; stock and loan agt. for N.Z. govt.; commr. under N.Z. Pub. Rev. Acts.; custod. Pub. Trust Securities, London.

KENNY, W. EYRE.—A.M.I.C.E.; jun. asst. engr., Cook county, N. Zealand, Jan., 1883; 2nd asst. engr., Gisborne har. bd., N.Z., July, 1885; ch. asst., Nov. 1887; ag. ch. asst. to col. marine engr., N.Z., Dec., 1890; offr. in charge, special survey dept., Sarawak, Sept., 1891; consulting engr. to Sarawak govt., Nov., 1892; in addition engr. for water wks., Jan., 1893; asst. supt. of wks., P.W., S. Stltmts., Jan., 1895; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1897; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, June, 1898, to Feb., 1901; ag. dep. col. engrn. on three occasions; ag. state engrn., Selangor, Apl., 1903.
KENT, R. G.—Accnt., rly. dept., Victoria, 1891.

KERNAHAN, J. B.—Ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad, 1876 to 1883; superv., survey office, Trinidad, 1885 to 1886; acted asst. draughtsman, S.O. Trinidad, 1886; passed survey exam., Univ. Coll., London, 1886 to 1887; priv. survr., Grenada, 1888 to 1890; engaged by St. Vincent govt. on surveys, June, 1890, to Dec., 1894; crown and col. survr., 23rd June, 1890; staff survr., Jan., 1895; apptd. to carry out duties assigned to col. engrn. under provisions of road ordce. of 1896, Jan., 1896; staff survr., supt., crown lands, and land commr., Feb., 1899; dir. of surveys, still holding other offices above, Aug., 1899 J.P., Dec., 1901; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1902.
KERR, C. M. MACLEOD.—3rd class clk. constab., office, Jamaica, Feb., 1868; 2nd class Aug., 1869; 1st class, Oct., 1873; ch. clk., July 1879.

KERR, LOUIS.—Clk. imigrn. dept. Br. Guiana, Sept., 1887; clk. pol. dept., Jan., 1888; ag. inspr. pol., 1891-2; ag. paymr., 1892; dist. inspr. and J.P., 1893; co. inspr. pol., Jan., 1897; in charge Aruan frontier station, June to Oct., 1895.

KERR, MARK.—Priv. sec. to gov., Falklands, Sept., 1884, to Apr., 1888; acted also as govt. clk., clk. of couns., shipping master, regiar-gen. and receiver of wrecks from Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1888; on special service to S. Leone, Nov., 1888, to July, 1889; prin. clk. col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, Oct., 1889; chief clerk, July, 1891; asst. col. sec., July, 1892; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; clk. of couns., Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Apr. to Sept., 1895; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1896; asst. col. sec., Lagos, Aug., 1896; mem. exec. and legis. couns. G. Coast, Apl., to Aug., 1896; mem. exec. and legis. couns. Lagos, at intervals from Aug., 1896, to Dec., 1899; col. sec., Northern Nigeria, Jan., 1900; treas., St. Lucia, 1903.

KERR, THOMAS, C.M.G.(1893).—B. 1818; was employed in the col. sec.'s dept., Barbados, in 1856; pol. mag., 1860 to 1869; judge of the petty debt ct., Bridgetown, to 1874; judge of the asst. ct. of appeal, to 1880; represented the parish of St. Joseph in the house of assem. from 1862 to 1866; mem. of the legis. coun., 1876; major 11th regt. of militia, 1854; comdg. offr. since 1868; a mem. of the militia board. Author of "Kerr on the Cultivation of the Sugarcane and the Manufacture of Sugar"; administered the govt. of Grenada, Aug., 1878, to Apr., 1879; gov't., Falkland Is., 1880-91.

KERSHAW, CAPT. A. E., 4th Brigade, S. I. div. Roy. Artillery.—Gov't. ser., S. Australia, 1887; Canada (N.-W. territory), 1881; Col. Engrs. Survey Depta., Br. Honduras, 1884; priv. sec. to Gov. Sir R. T. Goldsworthy, K.C.M.G.; clk. of couns., 1884; ag. registr., sup. ct., 1885; A.D.C., 1886; passed school of instruction, Woolwich, 1886; inspr., constab., 1886; dist. mag., Orange Walk, 1887; comdt. and ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1888; ch. comdt. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, Cyprus, Sept., 1892; inspr.-gen. of pol., Jamaica, 1905.

KERSHAW, THOS. HERBERT, C.M.G.(1903).—B.A., Trin. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. classics, 1872; 2nd class finals, 1874; Inner Temple equity scholar, 1876; called to the bar, Apr., 1877; regis. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1887; official assignee, Nov., 1-90; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1894; legal adviser, Federated Malay States, June, 1896; ag. atty.-gen., S.S., March, 1901, to March, 1902; ret., 31st Dec., 1902.
KEUN, ALFRED HAVELOCK, M.B., B.Ch.(Edin.).—House surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st Apl., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Settlements, 17th Nov., 1900; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, South, 1st Mar., 1901.

KEYT, FREDERIC T.—Ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.D. (honours), D.P.H., 1902; M.B.C.M., 1888; mem. Br. med. assoc.; asst. col. surg., Br. Hond., 1892; ag. inspr. of schla., Oct., 1892, to June, 1893; dist. comsrr. and J.P., 1893 to 1902; port health offr., Hong Kong, 1902.

KIDD, JOHN, C.M.G.(1882).—B. 1821; priv. sec. to the late Lord Dudley Coutts Stuart, M.P. for Marylebone, 1847; sec. on the staff of Gen. Count L. Zamozski in H.M.'s Imperial service in Turkey, 1855-56; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, 1857; entered the service of the Canadian Gov't., 1858; clk., 1858; ch. clk., govern.'s office, 1875; ag. gov.-gen.'s sec., 1876, 1877, and 18-0; ret. on pension.
KIDD, J.—Mem. legis. assm., N.S. Wales; postmnr.-gen. in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4.

KILPIN, ERNEST FULLER, C.M.G.(1901).—B. 1854; clk. of house of assem., Cape; clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1876; priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and col. sec., Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; clk. assist. of the house of assem., June, 1880; clk. 16th July, 1897; has served as sec. on various Cape govt. commissions, and acted as priv. sec. to premier, the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; sec. to the Anglo-German comm. on Angra Pequena and W. Coast claims, 1885; is a J.P. for the col.

KING, CHARLES KEASLEY BERRY.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1855; 1st clk. and 3rd sub-imigrn. agt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-imigrn. agt., June, 1872; sen. ditto, Apr., 1881; acted as imigrn. agt.-gen., 1881-2, 1888-9, 1892-3-4-7; mem. of ct. of policy on last three occasions; also mem. poor law bd., and ag. chmn., 1889 and 1894; res. 1896. In 1895, one of the comsrs. to report on the best scheme for commutation by E. Indian immigts. of the right of return passages to India; and in 1896, senior mem. of special comte. to deal with allotment of ld. to E. I. immigts. in lieu of return passage.

KING, F. EUSTACE.—Clk. in col. sec's office, Barbados, from May, 1866; transf'd. to govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, in June, 1868; inspr. of pol. and just. of peace, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1871; comary. of taxation, 1873; emigrn. agt. at Barbados for Br. Guiana, 1875; dist. comary. of taxation, 1884; treasy. departmental inspr., Mar., 1896.
KING, GEORGE HOWARD.—Ag. clk. registr.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1906.
KING, HENRY SANDFORD.—Survr., temporary staff, W. Aust., 1884; staff survr., 1887; inspr. of mining surveys, Coolgardie goldfields, 1894; undersec. for mines, 1899.

KING, JOHN.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Feb., 1866; transf'd. to col. sec.'s office, June, 1870; 2nd clk., Nov., 1877; assisted in audit duties, 1875-83; ch. clk. and sec. to the bd. of health, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1891; has acted as col. sec. on various occasions.

KING, JOHN HAMPDEN.—Entd. secretariat, Br. Guiana, 1880; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov'r., 187-88; ag. supt. reformatory schl., 1894 and 1896; ch. clk. secretariat, 1895; ag. supt. penal sttlmt., 1898; ag. inspr. of prisons, 1898, 1899 and 1900; asst. gov't. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., ct. of policy and combined ct., Jan., 1900; ag. auditor-gen., 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; J.P. for colony, 1904; served on the following comsrs.: manufacture and storage of bitters and cordials (chmn.), administn. of the funds of the De Saffron Trust, supplies for the lunatic asylum (sole comnrr.), reformy. and indust. schl., 1906.

KING, JOSEPH ARTHUR.—Copyist, registr.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1878; ag. 3rd asst. sworn clk., Mar., 1882; 4th asst. sworn clk., Oct., 1882; 2nd ditto, July, 1883; passed exam. for certif. as sworn clk. and notary public, June, 1885.
KING, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; supernum. clk. imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1888; 6th cl. clk., Mai., 1893; 4th class elk., Jan., 1894; 3rd-cl. elk., Apr., 1895.
KING, JOSEPH LEEMAN.—European master, govt. English schl., S. Stilimte, 4th Oct., 1889; ast. treas., Singapore, 17th Apl., 1899.

KING, M.—Ast. comsnr., Limasol, Cyprus, Nov., 1878; regis., high ct., Mar., 1879; ast. comsnr., Paphos dist., Mar., 1881; local comdnt., mil. pol., and ast. comsnr., Nicosia, Mar., 1883; pres. of municipal and water comsrs. of Nicosia, in addition to other duties, 1884-9, and 1891-4; mem. legis. coun., 1893; Br. deleg. of Evcaf, 1894; ag. rec-gen and mem. exec. coun., May to July, 1896, June, 1896, to Feb., 1897; sec. Western Pacific coms., 1897.

KING, N. W.—Cler. ast. surg.-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th cl. elk., off. of insp. of prns., 1896; 5th ditto, June, 1897; ag. clk. of supplies, May, 1900, to Jan., 1903; ditto, June, 1903.

KING, T. M., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1842; entered Queensland civ. ser., customs dept., as elk., Sept., 1863; passed through various grades of the dept.; apptd. collr. of customs, Sept., 1882; mem. of the immigr. bd., Aug., 1884; under-sec. of the treasury, Nov., 1893; aud.-gen., Sept., 1901.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chel. Coll.; B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. to the ch. sec., Cyprua, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec. Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsnr., Cyprus, 15th Apl., 1904.

KINGSTON, RT. HON. C. C., P.C. (1897), M.P., K.C.—B. 1850; atty.-gen. and premier of S. Australia, 1893-9; pres. of the fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee, 1897; one of the delegates sent to England in connection with passing of Federation Act; held office in 1st Commonwealth Ministry as Min. of State for Trade and Customs, Jan., 1901, to Aug., 1903.
KINTORE, RT. HON. EARL OF, G.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1852; lord in waiting, 1885; capt. of yeomen of the guard, 1886; privy councillor, 1886; gov. S. Australia, 1889-95; a lord in waiting, 1895.
KIRBY, AUSTIN H., B.A. (Cantab.).—Agric. and science master attached, on behalf of imperial dept. of agric., to the Antigua gramr. schl., 1903.
KIRK, SIR JOHN, M.D., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1832; formerly H.M.'s polit. agt. and consul-gen. at Zanzibar.
KIRK, JOHN WM.—Chief engr., Niger Coast Prot. steam yacht "Ivy," July, 1895; supt. engr., So. Nigeria, Oct., 1900.
KIRKHAM, JOHN KARKERK.—Ag. harbrmr., Barbados, Sept., 1877, to June, 1878; ast. harbrmr., Dec., 1878; shipping master and survr. of shipping, Jan., 1881; ag. harbrmr., Oct., 1887.
KIRKPATRICK, ANDREW ALEXANDER, M.L.C., S. Australia, 1891-97 and 1900; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 1905.

KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ. ser., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simangang, Jan., 1893; res. 2nd cls., Mar., 1893, of Upper Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd cls., Sadong, Feb., 1901; Kapit, 1905.
KIRKWOODE, RICHARD H.—Clk. col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1884-91; 3rd cl., 1891-2; ch. clk. audit dept., 1892-7.

KITCHENER, LT.-COL. ADAM.—Statistical Eastern sys. of rly., Cape Town, Feb., 1881; stats examiner, July, 1882; construction bookkpr., Aug., 1883; transf. to Western System, Dec., 1884; head bookkpr., 1888; ast. acctnt., 1892; acctnt. dep. of agricult., Nov., 1896; ch. acctnt., May, 1898; acctntg. offr., May, 1899; served as lient with the P.A.O.C. artillery, in Bechuanaland, 1897; prin. paymaster, col. def. force, Apr., 1901, with rank of major; lient.-col., Oct., 1902; acctntg. offr. in comsnr. of pub. wks. off., July, 1904.

KITSON, A. W.—2nd lieut Militia, Aug., 1879; lieut., 1881; capt., 1890; served with Roy Niger Constabulary as sub-comdnt., 1890-91; and as ag. comdnt., Sept., 1891, to Apr., 1892; comsnr. of civil justice, Gold Coast, June, 1893.

KNAGGS, H. L.—Clk., San Fernando Hosp., Trinidad, Apr., 1885; 4th elk. col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1885; 3rd elk., July, 1892; 2nd elk., Nov., 1900; 3rd inspr. of immigrts., Aug., 1897, to Nov., 1900; ag. ast. col. sec., 1895, 1899-1900; sec. to civ. ser. coms. and to rly. and roads coms., 1894.

KNAGGS, SAMUEL WILLIAM.—Entered Trinidad service, Aug., 1875; ch. elk. col. sec.'s office, July, 1892, and elk. of legia. coun., Jan., 1893; sec. to coms. to inquire into road system, 1886; ag. priv. sec. to Gov. Sir W. Robinson, 1886, 1889 and 1891; sec. to bd. of educn. and coll. coun., 1889-91; priv. sec. to Sir F. N. Broomes, Aug. to Sept., 1891, and to the gov. of Hong Kong, Oct., 1891, to July, 1892; ag. comsnr., Tobago, 1897; ag. audr.-gen., Trinidad, 1898; ast. col. sec., 1900; rec.-gen. and M.L.C., 1901; ag. col. sec., 1902; ch. coms. Port of Spain, 1899 to 1903; col. sec., Barbados, 1903; ag. gov., 1903.

KNIGHT, F.G.—Jun. elk. prothonotary's office, Barbados, 1874; 4th elk. correspondence branch col. sec.'s office, 1879; 2nd elk. audit office, 1882; ag. ch. elk. on several occasions, and as gov. acctnt. to Barbados rly. co.; ch. clk. record branch col. sec.'s office, 1892; ag. elk. ast. ct. of appeal, 1894 and 1895.

KNIGHT, J. G.—Ed. Lodge gram. schl., and Codrington College, Barbados; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1885; prizeman in jurisprudence, 1884, and in common law, 1885; ag. judge, ast. ct. appeal, Barbados, 1889-9; judge, ast. ct. ap., July, 1889; is regis. of friendly societies, and visiting just. of Glendairy prison.

KNIGHT, JOHN LOCKE.—Clk. and Zulu inter. to the Upper Tugela magistracy, Feb., 1876; May, 1878; ditto, Umsinga magistracy, May, 1878; to Aug., 1881, during which time he acted for R.M. and admstr. three times; Aug., 1881, ch. and Zulu interp., and regis. to the circuit in Newcastle dist.; Apr., 1882, ag. res. mag. and admstr. of native law, Newcastle; Feb., 1883, ditto, Umsinga div.; Oct., 1883, admstr. of nat. law, and Zulu border agt. to Lower Tugela div.; has held the hon. appts. of crown prosec. and offr. for the sale of stamps at the magistracy named; commanded a native levy of over 1,000 strong during the Zulu war; acted for two months in 1884 as R.M., Lower Tugela div.; sub-coms. Zululand, Apr., 1887; ast. comsnr. and R.M., Entojaneni dist., June, 1887; R.M., Nkandla dist., Jan., 1893; mag., Inanda div., 1900; m.e. Camperdown, 1905.

KNOCKER, FRED. WM.—Ed. Haberdashers' schl., Lond.; studied nat. sci. at Univ. Coll., Bristol; asst. curator, town museum, Leicester, Oct., 1892; sub-curator, civic museum, Bristol, Jan., 1898; settmt. offr., Negri Sembilan, Fed. Malay States, Feb., 1901; curator, state museum, Perak, May, 1904.

KNOLLYS, MAJOR LOUIS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; Marlborough school; ensign, 32nd regt., 1866; capt., May, 1880; major, 26th Cameroons, 1881; A.D.C. to the gov. of Mauritius, 1872-74, to gov. of Fiji, 1875; commanded force sent to reduce mountain tribes, 1876 (promoted to company and C.M.G.); comdt. armed native constab., and comntr. in mountains, 1877; A.D.C. to gov. N. Zealand, 1880, and to gov. of Ceylon, 1883; inspr.-gen. of constab., and dir. of prisons, Jamaica, 1886; inspr.-gen. of pol., Ceylon, Oct., 1891; inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Sept., 1898; retired, 1902.
KNOWLES, JOSEPH.—Tide waiter, recvr.-gen.'s dept., Bahamas, 1890; res. just. and school-teacher, Ragged Island, 1897; clk., pol. off., Nassau, Sept., 1903.
KNOWLING, HON. GEORGE.—M.L.C., Newfoundland, 1897; mem. of Cabinet, 1900; mem. of treasy. bd. and gov. of savings bank, 1900.

KNUTSFORD, 1ST VISCOUNT (creat. 1895, created BARON 1888), THE RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY THURSTAN HOLLAND, BART., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1877), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1829; succeeded to the baronetcy on the death of his father in Oct., 1873; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Nov., 1849, and went to the N. Circuit; legal adviser to the sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1867, and asst. under-sec. of state, Mar., 1870; resign., 3rd Aug., 1874, and elected mem. for Midhurst, 22nd Sept., 1874; mem. of the roy. comm. on the treatment of fugitive slaves, 1876; one of the roy. commrs. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, Sept., 1879; sec. to the treasy., June, 1885; and vice-pres. of the comm., Sept., 1885, and again in Aug., 1886; returned to Parlim. as mem. for the Hampstead div. of Middlesex, 1886, and again 1886 and 1887; sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; pres. col. confce., 1887.

KOCH, REV. C. A.—Asst. missionary to Bishop of Lebanon, bishop's chaplain, regis. of the diocese, 1886; diocesan chaplain, Ratnapura, Ceylon, Mar., 1886; col. chaplain, Jaffna, Aug., 1889; col. chaplain, Colombo, 1880.

KOCH, HENRY C.—Admitted to Natal bar as solr., 1884, as advoc., 1887; crown prosecutor and legal adviser to Zululand Govt., Apr., 1889; is also regis. of deeds, regis.-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1900; spec. comntr. for Natal to administer oaths to surrendered burghers; resumed duty as mast., sup. ct., 1902.

KOCH, WILFRED VINCENT MILLER, M.B.,C.M. (Edin., 1884), M.D. (Edin., 1895, highest honours, gold medal, thesis).—Late asst. in eye dept. of Edin. roy. infirmary; and asst. to the lecturer on diseases of the eye and mental diseases, Edin. Univ.; and asst. to the gynaecological dept., Edin. schl. of medicine; clin. asst., Royal London Ophthalmic hosp., 1901; clin. asst., hosp. for diseases of ear, nose, and throat, Golden Sq., 1901; ag. asst. med. offr., W. Riding Asyl., Sheffield, 1884; asst. med. offr., Hull Borough Asyl., 1884-88; late surg. to Monckton and Carlton Main Collieries, Barnsley; asst. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1889; ag. med. supt., Leper Asyl., Trinidad, 1890-92; dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1890-92; ag. dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, 1892-93; ag. med. supt. Lepre Asyl., 1893; ag. dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1893; ag. med. supt. lun. asyl., June to Oct., 1894; in charge of col. hosp., San Fernando, June to Dec., 1899; and of col. hosp., Port of Spain, for various periods; ag. dist. med. offr., Couva, 1900; St. Joseph, 1902; lieut., 1891; capt., 1892; comdg. Port of Spain art. volks.; surg. to permanent staff, Trinidad local forces; major, local force reserve, 1903; asst. surgeon, Hong Kong, 1903; J.P.; ag. med. offr. in charge of gaol and infectious disease hosp., 1903-4; asst. supt., govt. civ. hosp., 1905.

KENIG, ETIENNE.—Crown prosecutor, Mauritius, Nov., 1904; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1903; asst. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. substitute procureur and advocate-general, 2nd Feb., 1904; substitute ditto, 15th Apr., 1904.

KENIG, LEON.—Senior clk., judicial dept., Seychelles, 2nd June, 1892; ag. 5th cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, in 1897, 1898 and 1899; 5th cls. clk. ditto, 1st July, 1899; 4th cls. ditto, 7th July, 1902; clk. to ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 9th Nov., 1903.

KOLLER, T. W.—Clk., acctns. dept., G.P.O., O.R.C., 1st July, 1900, to 31st Oct., 1902; clk., pub. health dept., 1st to 30th Nov., 1902; examr. of acct., audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902; is also sec. to tender bd.

KORTRIGHT, L. M.—A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E.; surv. of roads and supt. of pub. wks., St. Kitts, 1879; official mem. legis. coun., St. Kitts and Nevis, 1886; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; mem. ex. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901.
KROHN, A. W.—Served in 33rd Co. Army Service Corps, as spec. clk., 1st March to 31st Aug., 1902; stationery dept., O.R.C., 1902.
KYNSLEY, SIR W. R., F.K.Q.C.P., KNT. BACH., C.M.G. 1888.—B. 1840; prin. civ. med. offr., and inspr.-gen. of hospitals, Ceylon, Feb., 1875; ret. 1897.

LABORDE, ARTHUR LIONEL CRICHTON.—Supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 3rd Jan., 1893; acted as clk. to gov. in exec. comte., and to the exec. coun., May to Oct., 1896; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. registr., July to Aug., 1901; ag. col. postmtr., 29th Aug., 1901, to 31st Mar., 1902, confirmed in the appt.; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1905.

LABORDE, E.D., I.S.O. (1903).—1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. registr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1888; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; commended in despatches for relief wk. during hurricane, 1898; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905.
LACOSTE, THE HON. SIR ALEXE, K.C., KT. BACH. (1892).—B. 1842; ch. just., Court of King's Bench, Quebec; mem. of the Dominion privy coun., 1892.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1851; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admnstr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-coms. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3, present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basutoland, 1884; asst. comsnr., 1885; established magistracy in Maseru's country, 1886; acted as res. comsnr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. comsnr., Swaziland govt. comtee. of adminstr., Apr., 1892; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; comsnr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chrmn. of S. African inter-col. native affairs comn., 1903-4.
LAIDLAW, ROBERT, M.D. (Aberdeen).—Asst. med. offr., Seychelles, 21st Jan., 1902; J.P.; and chairman, local bd. of health, Praslin and La Digue Is., Seychelles.

LAING, J. E.—A.M.I.C.E., formerly ch. asst. engnr. in municipalities of Manchester and Leicester; dist. survr., Sheffield; col. survr., S. Leone, Sept., 1894; title changed to dir. of P.W., Nov., 1896.

LAIRD, THE HON. DAVID.—Mem. educn. coun., P.E. Is., 1872-3; one of the delegs. who negotiated the admission of the island into the Dominion; sworn of the Canadian priv. coun. and min. of interior, 1873-6; lieut.-gov., N.W. Territories, 1876-8; was ch. comsnr. in negotiating treaty with Blackfeet Indians, 1877.
LAMB, BENJAMIN.—Lieut., R.F.A.; served in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1902; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903.

LAMB, SIR JOHN CAMERON, KT. BACH. (1905), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1845; entered the P.O. in 1864; prin. clk., 1877; was one of the Br. delegs. to the submarine cables protection confce. at Paris, May and Dec., 1886; asst. sec. to the P.O., 1889; 1st Br. deleg. to the internat. telegraph confce. at Paris, 1890, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape and Natal; roy. comsnr. on electrical communication with lighthouses and lightships, June, 1892; 3rd sec. to the P.O., Apr., 1896; sen. Br. deleg. and "president de la commission des tarifs" at internat. telegraph confce. at Budapest, 1896, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape, Natal and N. Zealand; 3rd sec. to P.O., Apr., 1896; 2nd sec., Jan., 1897; pres., internat. telegraph confce., London, 1903; and sen. deleg. of U.K., Cape and Natal; also sen. Br. deleg. at internat. confce. on wireless telegraphy, Berlin, 1903; holds Queen Victoria's Jubilee medal, and the Decoration of Nat. Lifeboat Instit.; ret., 1905.

LAMBERT, HENRY CHARLES MILLER.—Ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; entered as student of Lincoln's Inn; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtees. emigrts. information office, 1894; chrmn. of the comtees., 1897; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtees. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; visited Australia, on behalf of the Emigrts. Inf. Off., in connection with emigration questions, 1905.
LAMBERTON, STEPHEN HARBORD.—Entered postal service, N.S.W., Sept., 1852; sec., Sept., 1860; dep. postm-r-gen.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903.

LAMONT, WILLIAM J.—Grad. Moray House Coll., Edin.; entd. imp. custs. by comp. exam., 1st place in U.K., 1894; superv. of custs., G. Coast, 1896; travellg. and inspectg. superv., G. Coast, 1901; asst. collr. cust., S. Leone, 1901; ag. collr. cust., 10th May to 17th Oct., 1902, 8th Dec., 1903, to 14th Aug., 1904, and 19th June to 21st Nov., 1905; J.P. for Colony.
LAMPSON, P. E.—Ed. Wesleyan mission schl., Anamaboe, G. Coast; asst. master, govt. schl., Accra, 1893.

LANGEVIN, EDOUARD JOSEPH, N.P.—Admitted as a notary public, Lower Canada, 3rd Dec., 1858; a mem. of bd. of notaries of Lower Canada for several years; clk. of the Crown in Chancery, Canada, Jan., 1865; to same office for Dominion, July, 1867; dep. registr.-gen., July, 1868; under-sec. of state of Canada, July, 1873; elected sec. to civ. ser. bd. of Canada, 1876; clk. of the Senate, 1883.

LANGEVIN, THE HON. SIR HECTOR LOUIS, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1868), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1826; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1850; created a Q.C., 1864; mayor of Quebec from 1858 to 1861, and as such received H. R. H. the Prince of Wales on his landing in Canada, 1860; entered Canadian parlmt., 1857, and held a seat, first in the old legis. assem. and, since confederation, in the house of commons of the Dominion till 1892; mem. of the exec. coun., Canada, and solr.-gen. for Lower Canada, Mar., 1864; postm-r-gen., Nov., 1866; at confederation, July, 1867, sworn of the Queen's priv. coun. and was apptd. sec. of state of Canada until Dec., 1879, when apptd. min. of pub. wks., and whilst holding that office, was elected as the successor of the late Sir G. E. Cartier, Bart., as leader of the liberal-conservative party of Lower Canada; resig. office, Nov., 1873; postm-r-gen. in Sir John A. Macdonald's 2nd administration, Oct., 1878; then min. of pub. wks.; resig., 1891; was one of the framers of the present Canadian constitution, and in furtherance of the cause of confederation attended the col. confces. at Charlottetown and Quebec in 1864, and in London in 1866, when the Act of Union was carried through the imp. parlmt.; created a Knight Commander of the Roman Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1870; is the author of a prize essay on Canada, and of several other works.

LANGFORD, CHARLES WM.—Issuer, commissariat dept., Barbados, 1868; asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869; clk., G.P.O., Trinidad, June, 1871; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1871; 6th clk. rec-gen.'s office, Jan., 1875; 5th ditto, Feb., 1877; 4th ditto, May, 1878; 3rd ditto, May, 1879; excise officer, Apr., 1884.
LANGFORD, JOHN.—Clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, Oct., 1874; ch. clk. and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1878; registr., sup.ct., and curator of intestate estates, June, 1884.

LANGHAM-CARTER, W.—Ed. at Bradfield Coll., Berks.; cadet, S. Stltms., 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1896; dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; continued to act at Malacca; ag. collr. of ld. rev. and registr. of deeds, Singapore, Dec., 1898; inspr. prisons, S.S., Jan. to May, 1901; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Dec., 1901; 2nd magis., Sing., Aug., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev. and registr. of deeds, Sing., Sept., 1902; app'td.

LANSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (Gt. Brit., creat. 1784).—K.G., G.C.M.G. (1884), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.: Henry Charles Keith FitzMaurice, eldest son of the 4th Marquis.—B. 1845; succeeded his father in 1866; was under-secr. for war from Apr., 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under-secr. India Office, May to Aug., 1880; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov., 1888-94; sec. of state for foreign affairs, July, 1895; sec. of state for foreign affairs, Nov., 1900.
LAPRIMAUDAYE, C., M.V.O. (1903).—Commander R.N.; capt. of port Malta, Oct., 1888; supt. of pol., 1890; collr. of cust. with seat in ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 1903.

LAYMORE, CAPT. H.D., C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1867; ed. Westminster; entered Artill. Militia, 1886; lieut., R.A., Dec., 1886; capt. R.A., Sept., 1897; apptd. G. Coast constab., Feb., 1891; adjt., July, 1891; served in Jebu expdn. as staff offr. and sec. to Sir F. Scott; mentioned in despatches, and received thanks of gov. and exec. and legis. couns. of Lagos (medal and clasp); served in Kumasi expdn., 1896, as A.D.C. and headquarters camp commdt. (ment. in desps. and recd. spec. thanks for services; bronze star), ag. res., Kumasi, 1896-97; R.A. India, 1897-1901; R.A., S. Leone, 1901-02; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1902; 2nd cls. ditto, Oct., 1902; Haussa scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., 1903.

LASCELLES, ALFRED GEORGE, B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Paphos, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1896; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902.
LASH, JAS. MILLER.—Ed. upper and middle schls., Peckham; schlmr., Kirkdale prison, Liverpool, 1876; gaoler, Grenada, 1886; supt. of prisons, 1890; gov., Glendary prison, Barbados, 1894.
LAUDER, CHARLES R.—Cllk., pres.'s office, Dominica, Dec., 1872; 4th revenue and quarantine offr., Nov., 1874; 3rd ditto, Feb., 1880; 2nd ditto, rum dept., keeper, 1883.
LAUDER, JOHN J.—Ag. 2nd clk., regr. and prov. marshal's off., Dominica, Feb., 1900; 2nd clk., Jan., 1901.
LAURENCE, P.M.—Puisne judge, Grigualand W., Cape Col., 1882; judge pres., 1888.

LAURIER, THE RIGHT HON. SIR WILFRID, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1897).—B. at St. Lin, Quebec, Nov. 20th, 1841; became a B.C.L. of McGill in 1864, and a Q.C. in 1880; entered parlmt., 1871, being elected for Drummond and Athabasca, and was re-elected in 1874 for the same constituency; min. of inland rev. in the Mackenzie ministry, Sept., 1877, which office he held until the resignation of that govt. in 1878. He was defeated at the gen. election of 1878, but was immediately afterwards elected for Quebec E. He was re-elected at the gen. elections in 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1891. Became leader of the Opposition in Canada in 1890, and premier in June, 1896, on the resignation of the govt. of Sir C. Tupper; was present as the representative of Canada in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; mem. joint internat. high commn., Quebec and Washington, 1898-9.

LAW, ARCHIBALD FITZGERALD.—Ed. at Oriel Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1879; asst. comsnr., Cyprus, 1880; pres. dist. ct., 'amagusta, 1883; passed higher standard modern Greek, 1885; dir. of survey, prin. offr. of land regian. and prin. forest offr., 1886; mem. legis. coun., 1887; ag. Queen's advoc., 1889, and again in 1890; ag. ch. sec., 1890-91; chrmn. of retrenchment comm., 1891; Queen's advoc., 1892; puisne judge, S. Stltlms., 1893; conducted spec. inquiry into the State of Perak, 1894, and received thanks of S. of S.; ag. ch. just., S.S., Apr.-Dec., 1901; reverted to post of senior puisne judge, Penang, Dec., 1901.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; deputy-administr., Matabeloland, Nov., 1896; administr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. administrator, Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commr., 1905; chrmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C., gov. of Madras, 1905.

LAWRANCE, W. T.—Asst. priv. sec. to Baron Henry de Worms, partly sec. to the Bd. of Trade, 1886-6; in same capacity at the C.O., Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; attached to the internat. confere. on sugar bounties, 1887-88.

LAWRENCE, JOHN DUNCAN.—Ed. at Clepington schl., Dundee, and civ. serv. coll., Aberdeen; prelim. in arts, (higher mathematics and German) Aberdeen Univ.; govt. trav. schl. master, Falkland Is., Sept., 1896, to Jan., 1902; 2nd asst. insp. of schl., B. Guiana, Oct., 1903.
LAWRENCE, W. C.—Apptd. after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 15th Apr., 1904.

LAWRIE, JAS. WM.—Asst. govt. engrnr. survr., Singapore, 12th June, 1883; resigned, 10th Oct., 1884; re-appointed asst. govt. marine survr., 26th Sept., 1887; govt. marine survr., Penang, 9th Mar., 1901.
LAZZARINI, MAJ.-GEN. C. C. JAMES, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1819; of the Royal Malta Fencible Artillery, decorated for services in connection with the expdn. to Egypt, 1882.

LAYARD, SIR CHARLES PETER, KT. BACH (1903).—B. 1849; ed. Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1873; advoc. sup. ct., Ceylon, Aug., 1873; solr.-gen., July, 1878; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1891, to July, 1892; puisne judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1892; atty.-gen., Nov., 1892; ch. just., 26th Apr., 1902.
LEACH, JOHN VINCENT.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., England; admitted solr. sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; res. mag., 1888; has frequently been specially apptd. to conduct important inquiries and to report thereon.
LEARMONT, A. E.—Ch. comdt. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, Cyprus, 1905.

LEARMONT, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, late capt. 12th Lancers.—Ed. at Eton; staff adjt., S. Australia mounted forces, 1886-89; A.D.C. to Earl of Kintore, 1889; officer's extra musketry certif., Hythe, 1889; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir C. A. Moloney, gov., Lagos, and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Feb., 1890; inspr. Bahamas constab. (which force he raised in Barbados), Aug., 1891; inspr.-gen. Leeward Is. pol., 1895, and inspecting offr. of defence forces; J.P., Antigua, St. Kitts and Montserrat.

LEASK, J. T., M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.) 1879.—Col. surg., Malacca, Jan., 1891; ditto, Penang, Apr., 1893; ag. col. surg. res., Singapore, May, 1895, to Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Penang, Mar., 1896; col. surg. res., Singapore, Jan., 1897; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., S. S., Aug., 1902.

LE CAMP, CHARLES LOUIS THEODORE ADLER.—Ed. South African Coll.; passed Cape civ. ser. exam., Dec., 1888; apptd. to gov.'s staff on probation, Jan., 1889; on fixed estab. as 3rd cl. clk., July, 1889; res. Cape serv. and apptd. to high comsnr.'s staff, Oct., 1890; 1st clk. to high comsnr., 1895; transfd. to Bloemfontein as ch. clk. and regnr. to sec., O.R.C., Admintrn., Feb., 1901; ch. clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., June, 1902; clk. to ex. coun., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., April to Aug., 1903.

LEE-JONES, JOHN WM.—Called to the Bar, Gray's Inn, 1903; asst. master, Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1885; ag. ch. clk. at the magistracy and clk. to the justices, Mar., 1894; ag. dep. regist. and acctnt., sup. ct., Hong Kong, Sept., 1897; comsnr. for oaths, 1897; ag. dep. regist. and appraiser, May, 1900; J.P., 1900; dep.-regist. and acctnt., sup. ct., June, 1902; ag. appraiser, Mar., 1903; comsnr. for taking acknowledgments of married women, Aug., 1903.

LEFEBVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLIN.—Ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1898; clk., sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901.

LEFROY, GEORGE ANTHONY, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. survr., W. Australia, 1879-80; priv. sec. to Sir J. H. Lefroy as admnstr. of Tasmania; joined pub. wks. dept., Perak, 1882; ag. sup't., pub. wks. and res. engrnr., Larut, 1885-7; ag. res. engrnr., Perak state rly., 1886-7; ch. survr. and in charge survey dept., 1887.

LEFROY, HON. HENRY B., C.M.G. (1903).—Elected M.L.A., W. Australia, 1892; re-elected, 1894 and 1897; min. of educn., May, 1897; min. of mines, May, 1898; ag.-gen. for W. Australia in London, 1901; resigned, 1904.

LEGGE, C. A. S.—Clerical cert., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905.

LEGGE, CHAS. HENRY GLADMAN.—Clk. to the atty.-gen., Trinidad, Sept., 1867; 3rd clk. in the audit office, Apr.; 1872; was clk. of the col. hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1873; 6th clk., rec-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1874; 2nd clk., audit office, Jan., 1875; ch. clk., patent office, 1880; 2nd clk., audit office, Demerara, May, 1880 (designation altered to "Inspr. of Accounts," Jan., 1881); ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. auditor-gen., 1883-6-8-9.

LE HUNTE, SIR GEORGE RUTHVEN, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1852; Trin. Coll., Camb., 1873; M.A., 1880; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1881; 2nd priv. sec. to gov., Fiji, 1875; stip. mag., 1875; res. comsnr., Colo Viti Levu, 1877; ag. comdt., A.N.C., 1878-9; ag. col. sec., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1882; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1882; judicial comsnr. for the high comsnr. to W. Pacific Is., May, 1883; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1884; ag. rec-gen., Jan., 1886; pres. of Dominica, 1887; col. sec., Barbados, with dormant comsnr. as admnstr., 1894; col. sec. of Mauritius, 1897; lieut. govnr. of Br. New Guinea, 1898; gov. of S. Australia, 1903.

LEISK, JAMES RANKINE.—Chartered acctnt., Glasgow; asst. recvt. of rev., Johannesburg, 22nd Sep., 1900; ch. clk. to cont. of treasy., Transvaal, 21st May, 1901; asst. sec. treasy., 1st Apr., 1902; sec. to treasy., 1st July, 1903; (Queen's S. African war medal with 3 clasps).
LE JUGE DE SEGRAIS, PAUL.—Assoc. M.I.C.E., govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, June, 1891; govt. engr. and archt., Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896; ag. survr.-gen., 1896-97.

LE MESSURIER, H. W.—3rd landed waiter and asst. tide survr. of cust., Newfild., 1884 to 1897; asst. collr. and sec. to bd. of rev., 1897; dep. min. of cust., with title of asst. collr., 1898 (commissariat clk. under asst. commissaries prior to the removal of the Imperial troops).

LEMON, ARTHUR HENRY.—Ed. at Merchant Taylors' Schol. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (scholar); B.A., 1887; cadet, Straits, 1888; priv. sec. to gov., Sept., 1889, to Jan., 1890; attached to Chinese protectorate, Penang, Aug., 1891; sec. to currency comte., June, 1893; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, Dec., 1893; passed in Malay, 1894; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., June, 2nd asst. col. sec., Aug., 1895; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1895; dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, June, 1896, but continued to act at Malacca; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev. and regist. of deeds, Penang, April, 1903.

LENNOX, ARKYLL NAPIER OLIVER.—Sixth clk., immgr. dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 5th clk., 1884; 4th clk., 1885; awarded 2nd class certif. in Hindustani, 1885; acted as 1st clk., 1887, and July, 1889, to Jan., 1890; 3rd class clk. land dept., Jan., 1891; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1891.

LEVEY, GEORGE COLLINS, C.M.G. (1878)—B.1835; mem. leg. coun., Victoria, 1861-8; was sec. to the Victorian comsnr., and ag. comsnr. in charge of Victorian exhibits at Paris Exhibtn., 1878; sec.
