Melbourne Exhibtn., 1880-1; exec. comsnr., Victoria Amsterdam Exhibtn., 1883; sec., Adelaide Jublie Exhibtn., 1887, and Tasmanian Exhibtn., 1894.
LE VIEUX, LÉON.—Asst. survr., pub. wks. dep., Seychelles, 24th May, 1902; ag. inspr. pr. and supt. prisons, July, 1905.

LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY.—Ed. at Wesley high schl. and C.M.S. grammar schl., S. Leon med. dresser attached to Anglo-French boundary comsnr., Dec., 1891 to Apr. 1892 (recl. thanks gov't.); clk. to D.C.; clk. of ct. of request-registr. of births and deaths, W. dist., 1888-1895; 3rd clk. regiar. gen.'s dept., 1895 to 1897; 2nd clk., atty.-gen.'s off., 1898; travelled wt. and clk. to atty.-gen. during trial of insurgent by Judge G. A. Bonner, 1898; transport elk N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P. W. D., Jan., 1900; July, 1901; offr. in charge of transport on govr.'s tours, 1899-1901; regian. offr., G.P.O., Freetown, Aug., 1901 to Jan., 1903; ass't postmr., Gambia, Feb., 1903.

LEWIS, E. CORNEWALL.—Entered Govt. office, St. Vincent, as supernumerary, May, 1877; clk. to Kingstown pol. mag., 1880; ag. col. regis., 1883; acted as provost-marshal, 1884; ag. col. postmtr., 1886; ag. regis., 1886; dep. coroner, Kingstown, 1886; acctnt. of pub. wks. dept., Grenada, 1887; col. postmtr., St. Vincent, 1889; ag. col. postmtr., Grenada, Jan. to Mar., 1889; ag. regis. sup. court, St. Vincent, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; ex officio inspr. of prisons, regis. of the vice-adm. court, ag. col. postmtr., Grenada, Aug., 1891; resumed col. postmtr., St. Vincent, Jan., 1892; J.P., 1892; ass't postmtr.-gen., Hong Kong, 1899; J.P., 1899; ag. postmtr.-gen., 1900, 1901, 1902, and 1903.

LEWIS, CAPT. E. H.—Joined 21st Lancers in India, 1892; present with that regt. at battle of Omdurman, 1898 (medal and Khedive's medal); served with W.A.F.F. from July, 1899, to May, 1903; served with expedit. for relief of Kumasi, 1900 (Ashanti medal); present at capture of Kontagaro, 1901 (ment. in desps., medal with clasp for N. Nigeria); present at capture of Kano (class for N. Nigeria) 1903; apptd. 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

LEWIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Ent. Trinidad service, 1861; postmtr., San Fernando, Sept., 1869; 5th clk., G.P.O., 1868; ag. 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1863; ag. P.M.G., 1886, 1891, and 1899; recd. thanks of inhabitants of San Fernando and the Naparimas for his zeal in the discharge of his duties, 1882; recd. thanks of Sir Wm. Robinson for the efficient and satisfactory manner in which he had performed the duties of P.M.G.; mem. of mun. coun., San Fernando, 1903; mayor, 1904; mgr. of govt. cent. boys' and girls' schl. since 1890; J.P. and comnrs. of affidavits.

LEWIS, JOHN E. A.—B.A., Caius Coll., Camb., 1883; extra offr., Sarawak civ. ser., Oct., 1886; mag., and manager Sarawak gov't printing off., Aug., 1888; ag. prir. inap. of pol. and prisons, Nov., 1889, to May, 1890; Sept., 1894, Dec., 1895; clk. to gen. coun., July, 1897; res., 2nd class, Sept., 1898.

LEWIS, JOHN PENNY.—Ed. at Mill Hill Schl. and Queen's Univ.; B.A., 1876; M.A., 1882; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1877; ag. pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1881; ditto, Pet. Pedro, 1883; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Jaffna, N.P., 1883; pol. mag., Gam-pula, 1884; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., cent. prov., Kandy, 1886; ag. comnrs. of requests, Colombo, 1885-6; office asst. to govt. agent, C.P., 1887; ag. dist. judge, Matara, 1888; ass't agt., Negombo, 1891; ditto Vavuniya Vilan Kulam, Apr., 1896; ag. ass't govt. agt., Chilan, Apr., 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June; Negombo, Sept., 1896; special offr. under waste lands ordnec. No. 1. of 1897; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., 19th May, 1902; ditto Cent. prov., 7th Nov., 1902; ditto N. prov., 13th June, 1903.

LEWIS, HON. SIR NEIL ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901.

LEWIS, SIR WALTER LLEWELLYN, KT. BACH. (1904), M.A.—Gold Medallist, Queen's Univ. (Ireland), 1869 and 1871; called to the bar. Mid. Tem., Jan., 1876; South Wales and Chester circuits; stip. justice, Trinidad, Jan., 1885; chmn. of coman. on agricultural contracts; chmn. of directors, public service widows' and orphans' fund, 1890-1900; puisne judge, 1893; ch. justice, British Honduras, 1900.

LEY, ANDREW K.—Priv. sec. to gov. Labuan, 1882; ag. col. treas. and auditor, 1882 to 1885, and judge gen. ct., 1884-5; mag., ct. of requests, and P.M.G. Sarawak, Oct., 1886; also shipping master and coroner, 1st div.

LEY, PETER, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1851; ed. at Univs. of Glasgow and Edinburgh, of which latter he is a graduate in medicine; entered col. service, Labuan, Feb., 1876; adminstrd. the govt. 1881-7; held a coman. as H.B.M.'s consul-gen. for Borneo; ret., 1889.

LIGHTBOURN, JAMES THOMAS.—Ed. at Bahamas collegiate institute; supernumerary, treasy. and cust. dept., Bahamas, Jan., 1885; 3rd clk., treasy., Dec., 1885; 2nd clk. and bookkeeper, treasy., 1895; has held several acting appointments.
LINCOLN, GABRIEL.—Copyist, col. sec.'s off., Mauritius, 24th June, 1884; clk. to ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 1st June, 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 9th Nov., 1903.
LINLITHGOW, 1ST MARQUIS OF (U.K. creat. 1902), 7TH EARL OF HOPETOUN (Scoot.), P.C., K.T. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1889), G.C.V.O. (1900).

LISTER, JOSEPH STORR, I.S.O. (1903).—Asst. to man. of Tukva tea and cinchona estate, Darjeeling, May, 1870; passed lower standard exam. in Hindustani and an exam. in land surveying and levelling; apptd. to the forest dept., Punjab, in charge of Changa Manga, Lahore, and Umballa plantations, 1871; transf'd. to Hazara, to assist dep. conservator in exploring and reporting on hill forests of Khasgan; on special duty to Kulu and Kangra in charge of Nupur sub-division; left India owing to ill-health, 1874; supt. of plantations, Cape, Jan., 1875; J.P. and road mag., Aug., 1875; in conjunction with other duties, custodian of ex-chief Langalibalele and ex-king Cetewayo during their detention at Uitvlugt and Oude Molen; established plantations at Worcester, Beaufort West, Tokai, and Kluitjes Kraal; sec. to sub-comtee. for wood and vegetable products, Indian and Col. Exhib., 1886; conservator of forests, E. diva., Sept., 1888; ag. ch. conservator of forests, Oct., 1906.

LLEWELYN, SIR ROBERT, B., K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1845; extra clk., C.O., London, from 1868; registr. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Nov., 1869; was nominated as one of a coman. apptd. to inquire into prison discipline in Jamaica, 1873; priv. sec. to Sir J. P. Grant, July, 1873; clk. of priv. coun., Jamaica, 1877; comanr. for Turka Is., 1878; admstr. of Tobago, 1885, admstr., St. Vincent, 1888; ag. admstr., Grenada, 1888; admstr., St. Lucia, 1889, with dormant coman. as admstr., Windward Is.; admstr. Gambia, 1891; African war medal with two clasps, 1891-2, and 1893-4; gov. Windward Islands, Oct. 1900.

LLOYD, AUGUSTUS GEORGE.—Ch. clk. and cashier, col. treasy., S. Leone, Dec., 1896; ag. col. treas., Apr. to July, 1899; ag. asst. col. treas., July, 1899; asst. acctnt., African field force, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1899; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1900; ditto, G. Coast, Sept., 1900; asst. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1902; asst. col. sec., Mar., 1904.
LLOYD, JAMES S.—Ed. at Codrington Coll., Barbados; apptd. to 3rd clerkship, customs, Barbados, Nov., 1876; 2nd indoor offr., customs, Dec., 1890.

LOCKE, R. F.—Apptd. cons. agt. in Oil Rivers (now S. Nigeria) Protec., July, 1892; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Benin river, May to Nov., 1894; Brohemie expdn., 1894, mentd. in Adml. Bedford's desps., med. and clasp; apptd. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Warri, Sapele, and Benin Riv. Dist., May to Dec., 1896; one of two survivors Benin massacre, Jan., 1897; apptd. dep. comsnr. and v.-cons., Apr., 1897; now divisi. comsnr., West Div., S. Niger. Protec.

LOCKHART, J. H. STEWART, C.M.G. (1898); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—B. 1885; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; apptd., after compet. exam., to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; asst. col. sec. and asst. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. registr. gen., 1884 and 1885; registr.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chairmn. bd. of examiners in Chinese, 1891; ditto govern. body Queen's Coll., 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and registr.-gen., Mar., 1895; special comsnr. to report on the extension of the bndry. of H. Kong, 1898; representative of Gt. Br. to delimitate bndrys., for both of which recd. thanks of S. S., 1899; rector of coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1896-1902; comsnr. of Weihiwei, 1902.

LOCKHART, NORMAN.—Fourth revenue offr., Dominica, Feb., 1880; ag. postmr., 1881; ag. excise offr., May, 1882; 3rd revenue offr., 1883; quarantine offr., 1886; ag. mag., Nevis, 1891; ditto, Dist. E., Dominica, 1892, 1895, 1896; mag., Anguilla, 1895; ag. mag., Dominica, 1897; asst. treas. and registr. of shipping, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1897; ag. treas., 1899, 1900, 1901 and 1902; treas., Grenada, 1903.
LOCKYER, E.—Examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 26th June, 1903.
LONG, THOMAS.—Topographical survr. in the survr.-gen.'s office, Newfild., Mar., 1872; 1st clk., Sept., 1882; dep. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.

LONGLEY, Hon. J. WILBERFORCE.—B.A., Acadia Coll., N. Scotia, 1871; M.A., 1875; called to the bar, N.S., Sept., 1875; comsnr. sup. ct., 1876; elected to Prov. Parlnt., June, 1882; sworn in mem. of exec., July, 1884; attv.-gen., May, 1886; comsnr. for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act, Oct., 1882; editorial writer "Acadian Recorder" since 1873.
LORANGER, L. O., K.C.—Atty.-gen., prov. of Quebec, Canada; apptd. a judge of sup. ct. of Quebec, 1882.

LORANS, HENRI, M.B.C.M., and D.P.H., Edin.—Police and prison surg., Mauritius, 1884; poor law med. offr., Port Louis, 10th Jan., 1884; med. inspr., 15th Nov., 1895; ag. diretr., med. and health dept., 1900 and 1902; nom. mem. of coun. of govt.

LORNIE, J., M.A., B.Sc. Edin.—Cadet, S. S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1901; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. asst. registr., sup. ct., and mag. and sheriff, Malacca, Apl., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1904.

LOVEGROVE, CHARLES ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Charterhouse; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., Westminster, 1879; contractor's engrnr., Coleford and Monmouth rly., 1881; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., 1884; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; ag. prov. engr., W. Prov., 1897; ditto, N.W. Prov., 1897; ditto, S. Prov., and mem. mun.coun., Galle, 1898; ag. prov. engrnr., E. Prov., 1898; ag. asst. dir. of pub. wks., Ceylon, 1900; prov. engrnr., Uva, 1900; asst. dir., P.W., 1903.

LOVELL, Sir FRANCIS HENRY, KT. BACH. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1844; col. surg. S. Leone, Dec., 1873; med. offr., imgrn. dept., Mauritius, 1878; ch. med. offr. and pres. gen. bd. of health, 1879; mem. coun. of govt., 1885; surg.-gen. and med. offr. of health, and mem. legis. coun., Trinidad, Sept., 1893-1901; also mem. exec. coun., 1894; ret., 1902.

LOW, ALFRED M.—Ed. at Aldenham; open scholarship at Pem. Coll., Camb., 1890; B.A. class. tripos, 1893; ag. prof. of classics, Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1886; 3rd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, June, 1897.

LUCAS, CHARLES PRESTWOOD, C.B. (1901), B.A.—B.1853; ed. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner; 1st class in moda., 1873, and 1st class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1877; priv. sec. to Sir R. Herbert, Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1886, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of comtee. of emigrants information office till 31st Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., Feb., 1892; author of "Historical Geography of the British Colonies"; prin. clk., Sept., 1896; asst. under-secr. of state, June, 1897.

LUCIE-SMITH, A. VAN W.—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge. coma., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. justice, 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900.

LUCKIE, D.M., F.S.S.—Trained in a lawyer's office in Montrose, Scotland; in 1862 emigrated to N. Zealand, as editor and part proprietor of the "Nelson Colonist," which he largely popularised; in 1872 was elected mem. of parl. for Nelson City; in 1873 became editor of the "Southern Cross," and when it was amalgamated with the "New Zealand Herald," the leading journal in the col., became ch. editor of the combined papers; asst. comsnr. of govt. assurance, 1878.

LUGARD, BRIG.-GEN. SIR F. D., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O.—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, May, 1878; capt., Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; temp. col., 1898; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879-80; medal, Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star; despatches, Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889; exploration of Sabakha; adminstd. Uganda; wrote "Our East African Empire." July, 1894, went to Borgu in comb. expedition, and made treaties; hit by arrow, returned, Apr., 1895; Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Br. Charterland Co.; apped. comsnr., and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; went out, Mar., 1898; high comsnr. for Northern Nigeria, 29th Dec., 1899.

LUMB, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Ed. at Liverpool Inst. (Yates exhibitioner), and Downing Coll., Camb. (scholar and prizeman in maths, nat. sci., and law); B.A., math. tripos, 1870; M.A., and LL.M., 1877; LL.D., 1892; barrister of Linc's Inn, 1874, and practised on N. circuit; vice-pres. of Liverpool inst., and mem. of Liverpool schl. bd.; prosecutor for the mint, Feb., 1886; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, 1887; mem. educn. comsnr., 1887; of bd. of educn., 1887; of Roy. Coll. coun., 1888; of library comteee., 1889; drafted dist. cts. and educn. ordnaces. and rules thereunder; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1892; mem. of bd. of educn., 1893-4; ag. ch. justice, 1895-6; chmn. of the education commn., 1897-8.

LUPTON, HARRY, M.J.R.I.E.—Ed. Epsom Coll.; articled pupil, City engrnr., Canterbury, 1892-6; asst. engrnr., Malvern dist. coun., 1896-1900; asst. engrnr., Ibadan-Jeoba survey, Lagos gov't. rly., 1900-1; asst. supt. of wks., Malacca, S. Sttlmtns., 1902-4; ag. supt. wks. and surveys, Malacca, 1904; J.P. for Malacca, 1905.
LUSCOMBE, CAPT. G. A.—Local comdt. of pol., Cyprus, 1898.

LUSHINGTON, CHARLES MORANT.—Ceylon writer, 1876; mag., 1878; asst. to govt. agt., N.W. prov., 1883; dist. judge, Kegalla, Mar., 1889; ditto, Ratnapura, July, 1889; ag. asst. govt. agt., Negombo, Oct., 1890; at N'Eliva, 1892; at Mataia, 1894; at N'Eliva, 1895; at Trincomalee, 1896; ag. govt. agt., E. prov., 13th June, 1903.

LYNCH, G. W. A.—Ed. at Westminster, Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb. (B.A., 1883), and St. Thomas's hospital, Lond.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond., 1885; M.B., B.C., Camb., 1887; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1890; sen. med. offr., 1898; ag. ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899, to Jan., 1900; ditto, and ag. agt.-gen. for immigrn., Mar., 1903, to Jan., 1904.

LYNE, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B.1844; elected to N. S. Wales parlmt., 1880; sec. pub. wks., 1885-7, and again 1891; mem. exec. coun.; prim. and col. treas., Sept., 1899; joined first Commonwealth ministry as min. of state for home aff., Jan., 1901.

LYONS, CAPT. F. W.—Ed. at Portarlington school, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 80th regt. as 2nd lieut., 15th Feb., 1878; capt., 1884; adjutant, 1885; adjut. of volunteers, 1887-90; capt. reserve of officers, 1890; ag. asst. supt. of pol., S.S., 5th Mar., 1891, to 31st Mar., 1893; apptd. asst. comsnr., Perak Sikha, 1st Apr., 1893; acted as capt., supt. of pol., and prisons, Selangor, Aug., 1893, to Feb., 1895; served in S. Africa, 1878-80, medal with clasp; served in Sudan, 1885-86, medal and Khedive's Star; served in Pahang during disturbances, and recd. thanks of S. of S., 1894; acted as chief pol. offr., Perak, 1900-2; apptd. deputy sup. of pol., Hong Kong, 18th Sept., 1902; ag. capt. supt. of pol., etc., 1903-4.

LYTTELTON, Rt. Hon. ALFRED, P.C., M.A., Camb.; Hon. M.A., Oxford; K.C.—B. 1857; ed. at Eton, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; barrister; bencher, Inner Tem., 1899; legal priv. sec. to Sir H. James, attorney-gen., 1882 to 1886; recorder of Hereford, 1894; recorder of Oxford, 1895; dep. high steward of Univ. of Cam., 1899; M.P. for Leamington, Warwick, 1895; chmn. of Transvaal concessions comsnr., S. Africa, 1900-1; apptd. by Newfd. govt. as arbitrator in case of the Reid railway contract, 1902; S. of S. for the colonies, Oct., 1903, to Dec., 1905.
MAASDORP, CHRISTIAN GEORGE.—Atty.-gen., Transvaal, Sept., 1878; resig., Jan., 1880; puisne judge, sup. ct., Cape, Nov., 1885; Superior ct., Cape Town, 1896.

MCCALLISTER, ANDREW.—Asst. survr., Oil Rivers Prot. (now So. Nigeria), Sept., 1892; ag. survr.-gen., Feb. to Oct., 1894, and Aug., 1897, to Dec., 1898; survr.-gen., Jan., 1899; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1900.

MCARTHUR, MALCOLM STEWART H.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet S. Sttlmtns., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. registr. of deeds, Penang, 1896; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov't., S.S., Jan., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, Aug., 1897; and 3rd mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to gov't. Selangor, Apr., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan. to May, 1901; ag. collector of land rev. and registr. of deeds, Sing.; also ag. sec. to high comsnr. of F.M.S., May, 1902; ag. comsnr. of ct. of requests, Sing., Aug., 1903; asst. supt. of Indian immigrts. in addition to other duties, 13th Nov., 1903; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1903; confirmed, July, 1904; seconded for spec. serv. under F.O. in Brunei, Apr., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1905.
MACARTHUR, WM. HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—Govt. vet. surgeon, Penang, S. Sttlmtns., 21st May, 1892.
MACASKIE, JOHN COCHRANE.—Pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1895.
MACAULAY, JOHN DAVIDSON.—Extra clk., col. sec.'s office, S. Leone, 1874; in customs, 1875; tide waiter, 1877; landing waiter, 1879; clk. of customs, 1883.

McCALL, GEO. H.—Ed. at Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; ag. sub-collr., No. 4 dist., Tobago, Aug., 1895; clk. to comsnr. financial bd. and bd. of educn., Jan., 1896; asst. clk. to warden during expendre. of loan, Oct., 1896; ag. postmr., Dec., 1896; clk. to comsnr. financial and educn. bds. and asst. clk. to warden, Jan., 1897; twice ag. harbourmr., excise offr., and landing waiter, Tobago, 1898; landing waiter and clk. treasy., Jan., 1899; transf'd to cust., Trinidad, Feb., 1899; cust. offr., July, 1899.

McCALLUM, COLONEL SIR HENRY EDWARD, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1904), K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1887), A.D.C. to the Queen (1900).—B. 1852; entered the Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich, July, 1869; passed first out of 52 cadets, 1871; after two years' practical training at Chatham, proceeded to Portsmouth (1874), and apptd. supt. of telegraphy, S. dist.; transf'd to office of inspr.-gen. of fortifications; priv. sec. to gov. S. Settlements, Sir William Jervois, and attended him on various missions to the native states of the Malay Peninsula; mentioned in despatches for the assistance given in preparing a project for the defence of Singapore, and again in Dec., 1876, for services in connection with Perak comsnr. of inquiry; suptldg. engrnr. admiralty works, Hong Kong, Apr., 1877; in Apr., 1878, selected to proceed to Singapore to adopt measures for the defence of that port; attached to the office of inspr. of wks., roy. arsenal, July, 1879, to July, 1880, when apptd. dep. col. engrnr., S. Sttlmtns.; is a Pollock medalist of 1871; the annual Fowke medallist for 1874; is an assoc. of instit. of civ. engrnr., etc.; col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., S. Sttlmtns., 1884; men. of the exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Singapore municipality, Feb., 1881, to July, 1886; apptd. to construct new fortifications of Singapore,
MACDONALD, HUGH CAMPBELL.—Apptd. clk. in acctnt.'s off., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Sept., 1894; aast. collr., 1896; judl. offr., 1898; 1st cls. astt., May, 1897; dist. mag., Members, 1904.

MCDONALD, HON. JAMES, K.C.—Called to the bar of Nova Scotia, 1851; created a Q.C., 1867; chief rly. commr. of N.S., from June, 1863, to Dec., 1864; financial sec. until the union of 1867; one of the commrs. (representing N.S.) to open trade relations between W. Indies, Mexico, Brazil, and the B.N.A. provs., 1865-66; sworn of the privy coun. of Canada, Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of just. of Canada; sat in N.S. assem. from 1859 until 1867, and from 1871 until July, 1872, when returned to the house of commons; ch. just. of N.S.; joint commr. to inquire into charges agst. atty.-gen. of Jamaica, Dec., 1893.

MACDONALD, JOHN ALEX.—Ed. High School, Inverness; law clk., 1896-1900; joined service co. Cameron Hdr., 1900, and served in S. African campaign; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1900; clk. to crown prosecutor, O.R.C., July, 1901; clk. sec. O.R.C. admn., Oct., 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902.

MACDONALD, RONALD.—Apptd. astt. collr. and clk. to armed forces, Br. Cent. Africa Prot., May, 1897 (Cent. Africa medal, 1898); judl. offr., 1899 (S. Africa medal and two clasps, 1900); 2nd cls. astt. and dir. of cust., Apr., 1901.

MACDONALD, W.—Ag. 4th indoor offr., treasry., Antigua, Sept. to Oct., 1898, July to Dec., 1900, and Aug. to Sept., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., P.O., Jan. to July, 1900; 2nd clk., P.O., 1st Jan., 1901; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and Aug. to Oct., 1905; ag. postmtr., Jan. to Feb., 1905; 3rd outdoor offr., treasry., Feb., 1905; graduated Ontario business coll., Belleville, Canada, 1904.

MACDONALD, W. M., M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ag. med. offr., Montserrat, Mar., 1897; ag. med. offr., hosp., Antigua, Apr., 1899; med. offr., Montserrat, 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp Transvaal, Feb. to Dec., 1902; med. offr., Antigua, Sept., 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, Jan., 1905.

MACDONNELL, JOHN.—Clk. in the imigrn. office of N.S. Wales in 1854; promoted in 1856 sen. clk. to the regist.-gen.; placed in charge of the compilation of the gen. statistics of the col. in 1858; sec. of the pol. dept. of Queensland on its separation from N.S.W., Feb., 1860; imigrn. agt., Jan., 1866, inspr. of benevolent asylum and orphanages, also visiting justice to H.M.'s gaol and lun. asyl.; as well as ch. inspr. of distilleries; under-sec. and permanent head of the post and telegraph dept. in 1870; major-commd. of the Queensland volr. forces, 1877; lieut.-col. comdt. in 1878; ret., Nov., 1879, and placed on the staff with his rank as lieut.-col.; ag. comdt. of the defence forces in Feb., 1883, to 1884; mem. of imigrn bd. since 1865.
MACDOUGALL, JOHN LORNE, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1838; Auditor-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1878.

MACDOUGALL, JOSEPH EASTON.—Son of Wm. McDougall, C.B.; ed. U.C. Coll.; called to bar, Feb., 1870; lecturer on criminal law and torts to law society of Ontario, 1879-83; Q.C. 1883; jun. judge ety., York, Mar., 1883; sen. judge, Sept., 1885; judge maritime ct., Ontario, Sept., 1885; chmn. of pol. bd. for Toronto, 1886.

MACDOUGALL, DONALD KEITH, C. M. G. (1901).—Prin. med. offr. of W.A.F.F.; accompanied Ashanti expdn., 1900, in that capacity; prin. civ. med. offr., Straits Settlements, Mar., 1903, and inspr. of hospitals, F.M.S., 1905.

McEWAN, THOS. SMITH.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; asst. construction engr., Cape govt. rlyws., 1874; maintenance engr., 1877; dist. engr., 1880; res. engr., Bethulie junction, Jan., 1891; East London, 1st Jan., 1893; Port Elizabeth, 1st May, 1894; res. engr., western system, and dist. engr., Dist. No. 1, 13th July, 1896; res. engr., Cape Town, 1st July, 1897; ch. res. engr., Port Elizabeth, 1st Apr., 1899; asst. engr.-in-chief, 15th Mar., 1901; asst. gen. man. of rlyws., 16th May, 1901; gen. man., 12th Aug., 1902.
MACFARLANE, WM. HOULDSWORTH, M.B., Ch. B.—Supt. and med. offr., hosp. for insane, New Norfolk, Tasmania, Oct., 1880.

MCGEE, JOHN J.—Ed. at St. Peter's Coll., Wexford, affiliated with the Catholic Univ., Dublin, was medallist in physical science; employed by the govt. of Canada on special service in dist. of Algoma, and on various surveys; subsequently professionally engaged under the Dominion govt. in the construction of the inter-colonial rly., and in connection with the rly. comm. of the house of commons; 1st class clk. in charge of surveys branch of Dominion lands, Dept. of the Interior, 1879; clk. of the privy coun., Canada, May, 1882.
MCGOWAN, JAMES.—B.A., Lond., and fellow of the Inst. of Actuaries, Lond.; acted as examr. to that inst., and engaged for many years in life assurance business; govt. actuary, Cape, 1890.

MACGREGOR, D. S.—Fellow of soc. of accnts. and auditors; ch. audit clk., Leewards, Apr., 1895; and federal accnt., Jan., 1896; ag. treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1896; ag. aud.-gen. and mem. of Leewards and Antigua leg. couns. and Antigua exec. coun., Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1898; on special mission to investigate and adjust accnts. of Trinidad, Dec., 1899, to May, 1900; auditor, British Honduras, June, 1900; ag. reg.-gen. and reg. of sup. ct. on two occasions in 1904-5; ag. dist. commr. of Belize, Mar.-Apr., 1906; aud.-gen., Mauritius, 11th Oct., 1905.
MOGREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th battn. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub-inspr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1884; asst. commr., 1893.

MACGREGOR, Sir WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1897).—B. 1847; ed. at Aberdeen, Glasgow, Berlin, Paris, and Florence; M.B., Abdn., 1872; M.D., 1874; F.F.P.S.G., 1906; L.L.D.; D.Sc.; Watson gold medallist, 1872; formerly res. surg. and res. physician, Glasgow Roy. Infirmary; res. physician, Roy. Lun. Asyl., Aberdeen; asst. govt. med. offr., Seychelles, 1873; supt. lun. asyl., and res. surg., Civil Hosp., Port Louis, Mauritius, 1874; ch. med. offr., Fiji, Mar., 1875; also receiver-gen., and mem. of the exec. and legis. couns., Jan., 1877; has acted as regisr.-gen., agt.-gen. of inigrn., and commr. of lands; engaged, 1876, in the suppression of the disturbances in the mountains of Viti Levu, for which he was voted a gratuity of 200l.; joint commr., 1877, for the settlement of debts due from natives and Europeans, and for the settlement of all pecuniary claims against the late govt. of Fiji; mem. of the Native Regulation Board, 1877; proceeded to Tonga, 1879, to report on the financial condition of that country; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1874, to Jan., 1875; admstr. of the govt., and ag. high commr. and consul-gen. for the W. Pacific, Jan. to Aug., 1875; representative of Fiji at the first session of the Fed. Coun. of Australasia, at Hobart, Jan., 1885; Albert medal of the 2nd class (1884), with the Clarke gold medal of the Roy. Hum. Soc. of Australasia in 1886, for saving life at sea; dep. admstr. of Fiji, Sept., 1885, and Aug., 1886; declared the Queen's sovereignty over Br. New Guinea, Sept., 1888; admstr., Br. New Guinea, 1888; lieut.-gov., 1895; gov. Lagos, 1899; represented the W. African colonies and protectorates at Coronation, 1902; gov., Newfoundland, 23rd July, 1904.

MCGUIRE, WM.—Apptd., after open compet., clk. in the lower div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the audit office, Nov., 1884; transfld. to valuation office, Ireland, Nov., 1890, and to C.O., June, 1891; temp. staff officer, 1903.
MACINTYRE, D. C.—Harbour-master, Penang, S. Settmts., Sep., 1903.
MCINTYRE, JOHN.—Appd. puisane judge, Tasmania, 1899.
MCINTYRE, PETER ADOLPHUS, M.D.—Lieut.-gov., Prince Edward Island, Canada, 1899-1905.

MCINNIS, Lieut.—Col. EDWARD BOWATER (late 9th Lancers), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1847; entd. army, 1865; adjt. 9th Lancers for several years, including campaigns of 1878-9-80; Khyber Pass, under Lt.-Gen. Sir F. Maude, K.C.B., 1878; actions of Dei Khazi Killa, Dec., 1879; Siah Sung, Dec., 1879; others at Kabul; siege of Sherpur under Lt.-Gen. Sir F. Roberts, K.C.B.; with the 9th Lancers on the march from Kabul to Kandahar; at battle of Mazra (Kandahar), Sept., 1880; twice mentd. in despatches; bronze star, med. and two clasps; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Mar., 1887; mentd. in despatches by Sir R. B. Griffith, K.C.M.G.; comdt. local forces, Trinidad, June, 1890; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1890; inspr.-gen. of Br. Guiana pol., May, 1891; comdt. Br. Guiana militia, Nov., 1892, with rank of col.; on special duty to Uruan on Venezuelan frontier, Jan., 1896; commanded cavalry of the colonial contingent at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee, June, 1897; ret., 1902.
MACKAY, G.—M.A., Aberdeen, asst. master, Victoria Sch., Seychelles, 23rd Aug., 1903; head master and inspr. of schls., Dec., 1904.

MCKEAN, Lieut.—Col. ALEXANDER CHALMERS, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; cornet, Inniskilling Dragoons, Oct., 1871; A.D.C., Bengal, 1880; served in Transvaal war, 1881; staff offr., Zululand war (Eshowe column), 1883; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., S. Africa, 1884; ag. D.A.A.G., Natal, 1886; survr. to Zululand and New Republic boundary commr., 1886; asst. commr., Zululand, 1887; commr. of Basuto levies in Zulu disturbances, 1888; was honourably mentioned and made brevet lieut.-col. for services; assist. mil. sec. to gov., Malta, 1889.
MACKENZIE, DONALD.—Ch. inspr. of pol., Penang, Dec., 1887; ag. supt. pol., Sungei Ujong, Apr., 1890.

MACKENZIE RIVER, Bishop of, THE RIGHT REV. WM. D. REEVES.—C.M., Coll., Isl., 1865; consec. 1891; hon. D.D., St. John's Coll., Manitoba, 1891; missiony. (C.M.S.) at Fort Simpson, Mackenzie River, &c.; chapl. to Bishop of Athabasca, 1874-84; archd. Chipewyan, 1883-98.
MACKINNON, DONALD ALEXANDER.—Lieut.-gov. of Prince Edward Isl., 3rd Oct., 1904.

MCKINSTRY, W. L.—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; passed civil ser. test exam. 1882; supernum. col. sec.'s office, 1883; ag. clk. audit office, exec. comtee.'s office, col. sec.'s office, and as sec. to poor law bd., 1886 and 1888; clk. in petty debt ct., Jan., 1889; clk. to inspr.-gen. of pol., June, 1889; ag. inspr. of pol. and asst. of fire brig., 1889-91; int. rev. offr., 1892; transfld. to customs, 1896; ag. gov., Glenalry prison, May to Nov., 1904.

MCLEAN, THE HON. ALLAN.—M.L.A., Victoria; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and commr. of crown lands and survey, Nov., 1890, to Feb., 1892; min. of agric., Nov., 1890, to Apl., 1891; chief sec., Apl., 1891, to Feb., 1892; ch. sec., pres., bd. of land and wks., comsnr. of lands and survey, Feb., 1892, to Jan., 1893; premier and ch. sec., Dec., 1899, to Nov., 1900; min. for trade and customs, commonwealth parl., Oct., 1904.
McLELLAN, DAVID.—Mem. of house of assem. and exec. coun., New Brunswick; prov. sec. and rec.-gen.; pres. of exec. coun., 1890.
MACLEOD, NEIL.—Called to the bar, P.E.I., 1872; mem. of house of assem. since 1879; prov. sec. and treas., 1879 to 1882; atty.-gen. and premier, 1889.
MACLEOD, NORMAN MAGNUS, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1839; late capt. 74th Highlanders; was comsnr. of Swazi contingent against Sikukuni, 1879.

MACLURE, WILLIAM GRANT.—Admtd. Bahamas Bar, 1889; asst. clk., House of Assem., 1890; J.P. for col. and prov. res. just., Gov.'s Harbour, 1894; acted as stip. and circuit mag. and coroner, Is. of New Providence; regist., sup. ct., Bahamas, 1898.

MCMILLAN, SIR DANIEL HUNTER, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; Ed. Canada; served with Canadian Volunteers on Niagara frontier, 1864; during Fenian raid, 1866; Red River expdn., 1870; N.W. rebellion (medal), 1885; mem. for Winnipeg in Manitoba legislature, 1880; mem. of Manitoba govt., 1889; lt.-gov. of Manitoba 6th Sept., 1904.

MACMILLAN, EWEN JUDSON.—Ed. Charlottetown Business Coll., Guelph Agric. Coll.; received degree of B.S.A.; June, 1900; supt. of govt. experiment farm and lecturer in agric. at Prince of Wales' Coll., Prince Edward Is., Canada, 1901-4; chief of experimental farms and live stock division, O.R.C., June, 1904.

MCMILLAN, ROBERT FURSE.—Queen's scholar, Westminster school; scholar, Trin. Hall, Camb.; senior in law tripos, 1879; holder of law studentship at Trin. Hall; Inns of Court studentship and Inner Temple common law scholarship; called to the bar, 1881; joined western circuit and Devon and Exeter sessions; puisne judge, W. Australia, 1903.

MOIRAIR, J. F. A., C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; major, Roy. (late Madras) Artill. (retd.); ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; and student at the Schl. of Mines; entered the Madras Artill. in 1849; served in India, the S. Sttlmts., and Labuan; priv. sec. to the gov. S. S., 1857; and subsequently exec. engrn. and supt. of convicts, Singapore; ch. engrn., S. S., on their transfer to the crown in 1867; accompanied the gov. to Siam, Aug., 1867; survr. gen. S. S., 1872; ch. comsnr. of Perak throughout the disturbances in that country in 1875-6, and engaged in the affair at Kotah Lamah, on the Perak river; special comsnr. to Selangore to inquire into piracy, and to Perak in connection with the Pangkor treaty, 1874; again to Siam, in Aug. of that year, on special mission; again, in 1875, with Sir A. Clarke, in connection with unsettled state of affairs there; special mission to the State of Sungei Ujong, 1878; sec. to Sir W. Robinson's mission to Siam in Nov., 1878, to present the G.C.M.G. to the king; author of "Perak and the Malays," &c.; ag. lieut.-gov. and res. councillor of Penang from Feb., 1881, to Aug., 1884, when he ret.

MACNAGHTEN, T. C.—Ed. Charterhouse and Oxford; scholar of Hertford Coll., 1892; 1st class classical moda., 1894; B.A., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., Local Govt. Bd., Feb., 1896; 2nd class clk., C.O., Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, Dec., 1899, to Earl of Onslow, Nov., 1900, to Duke of Marlborough, July, 1903; 1st cl. clk., 24th Moh., 1904.

MCNEIL, MAJ.-GEN. SIR JOHN CARSTAIRS, G.C.B. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1880), C.M.G. (1870), V.C.—B. 1831; served during campaign of 1857-58 in India as A.D.C. to Sir E. Lugar during the siege and capture of Lucknow; in 1861-5 A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron in N. Zealand; commanded Tipperary flying column during Fenian disturbances, 1866-67; served on the staff of Red River expdn. under Sir G. Wolseley in 1870, and in the Ashanti war, 1873-74; A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Duke of Cambridge, Jan., 1874, and an equerry to Her Majesty in Aug. following; served in the Egyptian expdn., 1882, personally attached to the Duke of Connaught; served in the Soudan expdn., 1885; commanded troops at the action of Tobrek, for which clasps was granted.

MOWAN, ISLAY.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Fiji, Sept., 1892; stip. mag., ast. to native comsnr., and tax inspr., Lau, May, 1896; stip. mag. and tax inspr., Navua, and comsnr. for Namasi, Feb., 1900; stip. mag., Lomaiviti and Tailevu; comsnr. and tax inspr., Lomaiviti; offr. in charge, Levuka gaol, Jan., 1904.

MACPHERSON, A.—Served during S. A. war in Lord Dunraven's sharpshooters; civilian warrant offr. and paymaster, Imperial remount farm, 1st to 14th Dec., 1902; ch. clk. and sen. examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 15th Dec., 1902.
MACPHERSON, LT.-COL. JOHN.—Acctnt. and ch. clk. store branch dept. of militia and defence, Canada, Sept., 1872; dir. of stores and keeper of militia properties, Nov., 1880.

MCTURK, MICHAEL, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1843; revenue offr. and ast. survr., Essequibo, 1872; comsnr. of taxation, Essequibo dist., 1873; special mag. and ast. crown lands and forests, Essequibo, 1878; govt. gold mining offr., Br. Guiana, 1887; comsnr., Essequibo and Pomeroon districts; trav. mag., 1905.
MACWILLIAM, DONALD KINTON.—2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1894; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Apl., 1894.
MADDEN, SIR JOHN, K.C., K.C.M.G. (1899), KT. BACH. (1893).—B. 1844; ch. justice of Victoria, 1893; administered the govt., 1893, 1895, 1897, and 1898, and again, as lieut.-gov., Jan., 1900-1.
MADDEN, W.—Pres. bd. of land and wks., and comsnr., crown lands and survey, Victoria, Aug., 1881, to Mar., 1883.
MADLEY, LEWIS GEORGE.—COL. S. Australian Militia Infantry; principal of training coll., educn. dept., S.A., 1876; also D.A.A.G. of S.A. mil. forces, 1896; comsnr. of pol., 1896.
MAGRATH, THOS. HENRY.—Jun. clk., P.O., Tasmania, 1853; ch. clk., 1869; comptroller of money orders, 1879, and of savings bank, 1882; sec. to P.O., 1888.

MAGRO, PROF. E., M.D.—Grad. Malta Univ., 1877; examr. in arts and sciences, 1882; examr. in med. and surgery, 1884; prof. of math. and physics, Oct., 1885; examr. in land surveying and archi., Oct., 1885; ag. princ. and sec. to the univ., 1887; dir. of educn. and sec. to the senate, 1888-97; ag. inspr. in charge of elemen. schls., Jan., 1898; sec. to Oxford local exams. in Malta, 1898; mem. ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1902.

MAHAFFY, ARTHUR W.—Ed. at Marlborough, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, demy, 1889; B.A., Dublin Univ. (Trin. Coll.); 2nd lieut., 1st Batt. Royal Munster Fusiliers, 1892-1896; gov. agt. Gilbert Is. Prot., Oct., 1896; ast. to res. comsnr., Solomon Is., 1897; res. mag., W. division, Solomon Is., and dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1899; col. sec. and recvr.-gen., Fiji, Oct., 1904.
MAHONY, Lieut.-Col. John, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1824; paymr., army pay dept., in the Zulu war; ret. with the hon. rank of lieut.-col., Mar., 1894.

MAITLAND, Andrew Gilbert.—Reed his educn. as a geologist and civil engnr. at Yorkshire Coll. of Science, Leeds; graduated, 1886; asst. govt. geologist, Queensland, 1888; his official duties taking him as far afield as Br. N. Guinea; govt. geol. of W. Australia, 1896; has written several works on the geology, features, and mineral resources of Queensland and W. Australia.

MAJOR, Charles.—Called to the Bar (Mid. Temp.), June, 1887; mem. of Leeward Is. bar, Sept., 1887; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, Oct., 1889; nominated mem. of legis. coun., Antigua, 1896 to 1899; nominated mem. of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, Nov., 1896, and pres. of same, Dec., 1896, to 1899; mem. fed. ex. couns., Leewards, Mar., 1897, to June, 1901; vice-pres. legis. coun., Antigua, May, 1897; ag. sol. gen. and atty.-gen., Leewards, 1897, 1898, 1899; capt. commanding Antigua volunteer defence force on its establishment, 1897; J.P., 1897; ag. 2nd puisane judge, Leewards, June, 1899; atty.-gen. of Grenada, 1901; ch. just. of Fiji, and chief judicial commr. of Western Pacific, 1902; admnstd. govt., Mar.-Oct., 1904.

MAJOR, F. W.—5th clk., treasy., Trinidad, Oct., 1885; 2nd clk. to supt. of prisons, Jan., 1886; 9th clk., audit office, Aug., 1887; 8th ditto, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1888; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Feb., 1891; asst. comptroller of customs, 1891; asst. treas., Aug., 1891; ag. comptroller of customs, various dates from 1891 to 1896; comptroller of customs, Nov., 1896; rec.-gen., Bermuda, 1890.

MALCOLM, Dougal Orme.—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; Fellow of All Souls, 1899; M.A., 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O. Oct., 1900; priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, high commr. for S. Africa, Mar., 1905.

MALCOLM, Harcourt Gladstone.—Pri. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson when admnstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; mem. of house of ass., Bahamas, 1900; deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1905.

MALCOLM, Sir Ormond Drimmie, Kt. Bach. (1898).—B. 1839; called to the bar of the Bahamas, July, 1861; ag. pol. mag. in 1864 and 1866; elected mem. of house of assem. in 1865; speaker, 1868; ag. judge, court of common pleas, 1875; Q.C. in 1876; ag. atty.-gen., Mar., 1882; ag. adminar., June, 1895; ch. just. of Bahama Is., 1897.

MALING, Capt. Irwin Chas., C.M.G. (1-92).—B. 1841; ed. at Wimbledon schl. and Roy. Mil. Acad.; late 23rd Roy. Welsh Fus.; dep. mag. and collr., Nuddea dist., Bengal, 1-57; served throughout the Indian mutiny, 1857-59; present in many engagements (medal); ensign, 1861, 89th Princess Victoria's Regt.; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov., W. Coast of Africa, 1868; lieut. 35th Roy. Sussex Regt., 1870; priv. sec. to the Marquis of Normanby, gov. of Queensland, 1871, and New Zealand, 1874; col. sec. and regiar.-gen. of Grenada, 1879; adminstd. the govt. in 1880, 1882, 184-7; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1888-9; and represtve. at quarantine confe. at Demerara, 1888; ag. adminstr. 1889; adminstr. of St. Vincent, 1889; ret. 1893.

MALONE, Clement.—Ag. jnr. audit clk., Antigua, July and Aug., 1901, and Mar. to Nov., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., regisr.'s office, Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. cashier, St. John's savings bank, Jan. to June, 1903; ag. acctnt., treasy., Dec., 1902, and July to Dec., 1903; acctnt. of treasy., Antigua, Dec., 1903; clk. to income and trade tax assessors, Jan., 1906.
MALONEY, Henry.—Curator, Botanic Station, Montserrat; sanitary offr., Jan., 1897.

MALSCH, Charles Christian, A.M.I.C.E.—Draughtsman, works dept., admiralty, 1866; asst. engnr., German admiralty wks., Kiel, 1873; survr. state engnr.s dept., California; surveying Sacramento and Fraser Rivers, etc., California and Br. Columbia, 1875; exploring Formosa for Chinese govt., 1881 to 1883; asst. engnr., P.W. dept., Hong Kong, 1884; supt. of crown lands, 1892; ag. assessor of rates, 1894; J.P., 1894.
MANCHESTER, James.—2nd clk., registr., St. Kitts, Apr., 1903; clk. to supt. pub. wks., St. Kitts, Apr., 1905.

MANN, Maj.-Gen. J. R., R.E., C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1823; entered R.E., 1840; survr.-gen. of Mauritius, 1856 to 1861; dir. of roads and survr.-gen. of Jamaica, 1867; resig. 1873, and re-apptd. in Feb., 1874; official M.L.C., 1874; ret., 1886.
MANNING, Albert Edward.—Copyist in registrar's office, Br. Guiana; asst. sworn clk., 1887; sworn clk. and notary public, 1898; comsnr. to administer oaths and affidavits, 1900.

MANSFIELD, George, C.M.G. (1891).—Served in Weenen Yeomanry through the Langalibalele rebellion in 1873; served as sub.-inspr. and inspr. in Natal mounted pol. through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp), and Boer war, 1880 and 1881; raised and commanded the Reserve Territory Carabiners from 1883 to 1887, when the force was changed into the Zululand pol.; served in this force through all disturbances in Zululand from 1883 to 1888; twice mentioned in despatches as having shown conspicuous coolness and gallantry in action; asst. comsnr., Natal pol., 1897; ag. commtdt., 1902; ch. comsnr., pol., Jan., 1903.

MANSERGH, Cornwall Lewis Warwickshire.—Clk., crown lands office, Capetown, May, 1880; 2nd cl. clk. and priv. sec. to comsnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., May, 1884; 1st cl. clk., Apr., 1892; ch. clk. to the asst. comsnr. of rlys. and pub. wks., Oct., 1892; sec. pub. wks., July, 1898.

MANSON, Sir Patrick, K.C.M.G. (1903); C.M.G. (1900); F.R.S., M.D., F.R.C.P., LL.D.—B. 1844; late dean and lect. on med. coll. of med. for Chinese, Hong Kong; phys. Seamen's Hospital Soc. at Albert Docks branch; lect. on trop. diseases, Charing Cross Hosp., and senior lect., London schl. of trop. med.; apptd. med. adviser to C.O., 1897.

MARKS, Oliver.—Supt. govt. planta, Perak, Mar., 1891; 2nd cls. mag., Apl., 1894; ag. asst. mag., Kinta, July, 1894; asst. mag. Kinta, July, 1896; ag. asst. sec. to govt., Perak, Sep., 1896; asst. sec. to govt., Perak, May, 1897; passed Malay, Mar., 1898; 1st cls. mag., June, 1898; asst. sec. to res. gen., Fed. Malay States, Nov., 1898; ag. sec. to res. gen., F. M. S., Mar., 1899; sec. to high comsnr., F. M. S., Aug., 1903.

MARLBOROUGH, 9th Duke of (created, 1702).—Charles Richard John Spencer-Churchill, P.C., K.G.—B. 1871; Chancellor of Primrose League, 1897 and 1898; served with yeomanry cavalry, S. Africa, 1900, and A.D.C. to Lieut.-Gen. Sir Ian Hamilton, K.C.B.; pay-master-general, 1899; under-sec. of state for the colonies, July, 1903, to Dec., 1905.

MARRIOTT, H., B.A., 1st cls. math. tripos, Cantab.—Cadet S. Sttlmtns., Nov., 1896; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, May, 1898; dist. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1899; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Feb., 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1904; ag. off. assignee, Sing., Feb., 1905; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1906.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD.—Ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st cl. classical tripos, 1893; sen. chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st cl. with distinction classical tripos, Part II., and B.A., 1895; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. elk., C.O., Sept. 29, 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, July, 1900; to Mr. Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; 1st cls. elk., 1st Apr., 1905.
MARSH, T. R., C.M.G. (1901), on retirement from post as head of the contract branch, crown agents office; B. 1847.

MARSH, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1827; Clk. to procureur-gen., Mauritius, Feb., 1848; crown solr., Apl., 1854; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1859; special immigr. comsnr. to India, May, 1865; jun. asst. col. sec., Apl., 1866; sen. asst. col. sec., 1867; sole asst., 1870; ag. col. sec., 1875-6; audr.-gen., Apl., 1876; col. sec. and audr.-gen., Hong Kong, 1879; ag. audr. and acctnt.-gen., Cyprus, 1881; admnstr. govt. Hong Kong on six occasions; ret. 1887.

MARSHALL, COLONEL ROBERT SETON.—Lieut. 3rd brig. Scottish div. R.A., 1882; (lieut. reserve of offrs., 1885); capt., 1885; instructor of artillery, 1887; (capt., res. of offrs., 1896); major Edin. artill. militia, 1896 (hon. major in the army, 1900); hon. lt.-col., 1903; lt.-col., 1903; retired with hon. rank of col., 1903; served with the 1st brig. W. div. R.A., 1885; staff offr. local forces, Trinidad, Sept., 1888, to Dec., 1891; sec. local defence comttee., 1888; command of Trinidad artill. corps for purpose of organising the corps, 1888; A.D.C. to offr. adminstr. govt. of Trin. and Tobago, May to Dec., 1889; ag. commdt., Trinidad local forces, 6th-19th Nov., 1890; adjt. and musketry instr., Br. Guiana pol., Dec., 1891; ag. adjt. Br. G. militia, May to Oct., 1894; and Aug. to Sept., 1897; ag. inspr. of Br. G. pol. during period of special employment at Uruan frontier station, Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1896; adjt. of Br. G. militia and police, Dec., 1897; ag. commdt., Br. G. militia, 9th May to 22nd Aug., 1900; sent to frontier to hand over Amacura and Barima Sands to Venezuela, Oct., 1900; sent to Trinidad for special duty with police, after the riot, 2nd to 27th May, 1903.

MARTIN, GEORGE GOZZARD.—Clk., audit off., South Australia, Mar., 1873; clk., marine bd., Jan., 1874; clk. ch. sec.'s off., Sept., 1874; elk., lands titles offr., Aug., 1877; elk., law officers dept., July, 1880; ch. elk. and acctnt., law officers' dept., Nov., 1888; sec. to atty.-gen. and min. of educn., Jan., 1905; sec. to atty.-gen., July, 1905.

MARTIN, H. A.—Ag. elk. to auditor, Grenada, Aug., 1884, to June, 1885; elk. to promarshal, June to July, 1885; ag. ch. to audr., May to Nov., 1887; asst. elk., treasy., Jan., 1889; ag. wks. acctnt., Jan. to Oct., 1890; ch. elk., registr., Nov., 1891; treasy. acctnt., St. Lucia and Grenada, Apr., 1894; supt. prisons, Feb. to May, 1897; 2nd financial asst., P.W.D., Ceylon, June, 1897.

MARTIN, JAMES.—Royal Geographical Society's expeds., 1882-1884; Zanzibar govt. serv., 1884-1886; joined Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co., 1885; joined Zanzibar govt. serv., 1892; transport offr. and collr., Uganda Prot., 1894; collr., Ravine, 1894; collr., Entebbe, 1899.
MARTIN, JAMES H.—Apptd., after exam., messenger Admty., Aug., 1875; transf. C.O., Sept., 1877, Queen's mess., June, 1897.
MARTIN, JOSEPH.—Mem. of legis. assem., and exec. com., Manitoba; atty.-gen. and comsnr. of rlys. and lands.

MARTIN, LOUIS.—Voir. in survr.-gen.'s dept. at Mauritius, Dec., 1859; elk. in audit office, Jan., 1860; jun. examr., Mar., 1862; sen. examr., Sept., 1869; ch. elk., immigrn. dept., Mar., 1880; col. postmr. and supt. of telegraphs, May, 1890.

MARTIN, COL. SIR RICHARD EDWARD ROWLEY, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1888), Inniskilling Dragoons.—B. 1847; commanded mounted infantry at Cetywayo's installation, Zululand, 1883; commt. of gen. depôt, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; major, Bechuanaland border pol., 1885 to 1887; was Br. comsnr.-pres., Portuguese-Swazilandry, comsnr., and Br. comsnr. on special mission to Tongaland, 1888; proceeded on visit of inquiry to Swaziland, 1889; sec. to Br. comm. to Swaziland, 1889; Br. mem. of temporary joint comsn. of adminstrn., 1889-95; comdt. of pol. and dep. coma., Rhodesia, Mar., 1896.
MARTIN, S. H.—Ed. Wesleyan Mission Schl., C. Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; elk. to dir. of telegraphs and storekeeper, Aug., 1891; elk. and acctnt., July, 1893.
MARTINS, FRED. G.—Ed. C.M.S. Coll. Inst., Lagos; jun. bailiff, sup. ct., June, 1884; elk. to Queen's advoc., Aug., 1886; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Jan., 1891; audit elk., Jan., 1895.

MARTINS, G. N.—Ed. C.M.S. Grammar Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs outdoor offr., 1890 to 1893 (during which time attached to revenue office); ag. elk., P.O., 1893; 4th elk. of customs, 1893; 3rd elk. and statistician, Mar., 1894; transf. to the revenue dept., 1900; deputy for cashier and ch. elk. on various occasions; 2nd cls. elk., customs, 1901; chief examining offr. of cust., Lagos, 1st Mar., 1905.

MARWOOD, HORACE RICHARD, A.M.I.C.E.—Jnr. asst. engrn., Taff Vale rlyw., 1877; supernumerary 2nd cl. asst. engrn. Cape Govt. rlyws. (Eastern system), 1882; 1st cl. asst. W. system, 1883, also in charge of district, 1884; asst. engrn. rlyw. service of H.H. the Sultan of Selangor, S.S. 1885; chef-de-dvision, Pyraeus Larissa rlyw., Greece, 1890; engrn. of wks. and maintenance Trinidad govt. rlyws., 1894.
MASKELL, THOS. AUGUSTUS CORNELIUS.—Cler. asst. to inspr. of schls., B. Guiana, June, 1878; elk., Apl., 1891; 3rd cls. elk., Apl., 1893; sec. to educn. comsnr., 1902.
MASON, ARTHUR WIER, B.A.—Puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, 1896; ag. ch. jst., 1900-01; comsnr. of spec. treasun., 1900-01; pres. of ditto, 1901-02; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902.

MASON, CLAYTON T., M.I.C.E., J.P.—Ed. King Edward's Schl., Birningham; from 1874 to 1877 was engaged in the rly. branch of the dept. of pub. wks., N. S. Wales; res. engrn. of the Geraldton and Northampton rly., W. Australia, 1877; suptd. the construction of Point Moore Lighthouse and other pub. wks. in the Victoria dist.; afterwards res. engrn. for four years of the E. rly. in the same col.; ag. comsnr. of rlys. on two occasions during 1882 and 1883; was provisionally apptd. in 1884 dir. of pub. wks. and comsnr. of rlys.; mem. of the exec. and legis. couns., gen. man. and maintenance engrnr., June, 1-86; comsnr. of rlys. and mem. of exec. coun., Jan., 1890; after the introduction of responsible govt. was apptd. collr. of customs, June, 1891.

MASON, GERALD BOYKILL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; jun. asst. med. offr., St. Marylebone infirmary, 1896; clin. asst., aural and ophthalmic depts., Lond. hosp., 1897; house physician, Brompton hosp., 1898; priv. sec. to Sir C. Quilter, Bart., M.P., on voyage to W. Indies, 1899; ag. med. offr., Three Counties asylum, 1899; civ. surg. in charge, Station hosp., Woking, 1902; apptd. med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905.

MASSON, HON. LOUIS FRANCOIS RODERIQUE.—Called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1869; brigade-major, 8th military dist. of Lower Canada, from 1863 to 1868, and holds the rank of lieut.-col. in the militia; entered Canadian parlmt., 1867; sworn of the privy coun. of Canada, and apptd. min. of militia and defence, 19th Oct., 1878; ret., 1880; called to the senate, 1882, and to the legis. coun. of the province, 1889; Quebec, 1884; gov. of Quebec, 1884-7; senator, 1890.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS.—Passed through gov't. training instn., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schmstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 94 yrs.; educntl. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd asst. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

MATHEWS, THOMAS VINCENT, I.S.O.(1903).—Outdooroff., receiver-gen. and treas.'dept., Bahamas, Oct., 1874; 3rd clk., Mar., 1875; ag. priv. sec. to admstr., E. B. A. Taylor, from Sept. to Dec., 1879; ag. collr. of customs, 1883; 2nd clerk and book-keeper, 1885.
MATHIEU, M.—Puise judge, sup. ct., Quebec, 1831.

MATTHEWS, MAYNARD R. NELSON.—Jun. clk. telegraph office, Durban, Natal, 1878; tel. offr. and postmr., Bothas Hill, Verulam, and Stanger, 1879; 3rd cl. clk., R.M.'s office, Durban, 1880; field cornet, Durban, and sec. wreck comsnr.'s ct., 1886; 2nd class clk., 1886; ag. clk., mag.'s ct. and regis. circuit ct., Durban and Umlazi, 1886-7; clk., mag.'s ct., regis., circuit ct., sub-distributor of stamps, and dep. clk. of police, Klip River div., Dec., 1887; J.P., Ladysmith, 1894; has frequently acted as R.M., Klip River, 1889-90; ag. mag., Inauda, Feb., 1890; J.P. for col., 1890; mag., Weenen, June, 1895; asst. mag., Durban, Nov., 1896; mag., Weenen, Apr., 1897; rec. thanks of Gen. Sir Redvers Buller for services to Intelligence Dep. during the Boer war, 1899-1900; ag. mag., Newcastle, 23rd Sept., 1901; mag., Dundee, 9th Dec., 1901.
MATTHEWS, WM. HENRY.—Inspector of mines, S. Aust., 1599; warden of mines, 1900.

MAVROGORDATO, ALEXANDER STEPHEN.—Dispenser, civ. hosp., Nicosia, Cyprus, June, 1879; clk. to prin. forest offr., June, 1881; acted as forest offr., May, 1883, to Dec., 1884; was engaged on the locust destruction as paymr., 1883 to 1886; and as asst. to comsnr., Famagusta, 1889-92; examr. of accts., audit dept., May, 1886; transfd. to rec. gen.'s dept., Apr., 1891; asst. in survey and forest depts., Dec., 1892; pres. surv. delimitation coman.; supt. locust destruction operations, 1895-6; asst. in forest and agricultural depts., June, 1896; financial asst., P.W. Dept., October, 1896.

MAVROGORDATO, THEODORE E.—Ed. at Berlin; clk. to comsnr., Famagusta, 1881; clk. and interp. to Nicosia detachment of pol., 1882; asst. inspr., pol., Limassol, 1883; inspr. pol., Kyrenia, Sept., 1884; joined metrpr. pol., London, for six months' course of instrn., 1894; in charge special pol. force, 1895; local comdt. of pol., asst. comsnr., gov. of prison and dep. corrn., 1895; ag. comsnr., Papho, May to Nov., 1897; ag. comsnr., Limassol, May to Dec., 1900; asst. comsnr. of pol. and J.P., Johannesburg, Dec., 1901; comand. to draw up scheme for dest. of locusts in Transvaal, and had charge of locust dest. dept., in addition to other duties, Oct., 1902, to June, 1904; in charge of crimi. invstgn. dept. from June, 1903.

MAXWELL, F. M.—Ed. Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. honours jurisprudence, 1898; M.A., 1891; called to bar Linc.'s Inn, 1884; 1st cl. studentship, 1882; 2nd cl. intrmdt. exam. in laws, Lond. univ., 1885; ag. atty. gen., Br. Honduras, Feb., to Dec., 1890, and May to July, 1896; atty.-gen., Br. H., July, 1896; ag. ch. just., Br. H., 21st June to 29th Dec., 1899; ditto, 15th Mar. to 17th Oct., 1902; ditto, Apr. to Nov., 1904; lieut. commdg. mntnl. section B.H.V.F., Feb., 1905; K.C., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; attd. schl. of M.I., Bulford Camp, and certified as qualified to command Company of M.I., Sept., 1906; qualified at Schl. of Musketry, Hythe, Dec., 1906.

MAXWELL, GERALD VERNER.—Ed. Cheltenham and Camb. Univ.; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1898; ag. reg. genl., ag. reg. of titles and ag. dep. comsnr. of stamps, Dec., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. stip. mag., Macuata, Dec., 1900; ag. comsnr., Naitasiri, and stip. mag., Rewa, July, 1901; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1902; ag. gov't. storekeeper and recvr. nat. taxes, Sept., 1902; ag. P.I., Nadroga, Feb., 1903; asst. nat. comsnr., Jan., 1904.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P., M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., N. Zealand, 1874; gen. man., N. Z. rly., 1880; rly. comsnr. under Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

MAXWELL, THOMAS DOVETON.—Ed. Tiverton, Blundell scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1892; B.A. (honours, jurisprudence), 1896; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1903; asst. dist. comsnr., So. Nigeria, 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Degema, 1903; ditto, Calabar, Mar., 1904.

MAXWELL, WM. GEORGE.—Junior offr., Perak, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1891; asst. mag., Kinta, 25th Apr., 1892; ag. collr. and mag., Kuantan, Pahang, 16th Aug., 1895; ag. sup. of Ulu Pahang, 9th Aug., 1896; regis. of cts. Kinta, Perak, 1st Feb., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to gov't., Perak, 6th Feb., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Larut, regis. of titles, Perak, North, and warden of mines, Perak, North, 8th Mar., 1901; ag. sen. mag., Selangor and Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1903; ag. sen. mag., Perak, 1st Aug., 1903; ag. sec. to res., Perak, 6th Apr., 1904; ag. comsnr. of the ct. of requesta, Singapore, 3rd Nov., 1904; dist. offr., Dindinga, 3rd Aug., 1905.

MAY, ALFRED JOHN, M.R.A.S., F.E.I.S.—Ed. St. Saviour's Gram. Schl., New Shoreham, and Training Schl., St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint; headmr., St. Paul's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1875, to Jan., 1879; asst. master, govt. cent. schl., Jan., 1879, to Sept., 1881; prin., govt. normal schl., Sept., 1881; 3rd master, govt. central schl. (now Queen's Coll.), Sept., 1883; passed 11. stand. higher grade Chinese, 1886; 2nd master, 1889; ag. headmr., Apl., 1890, to Oct., 1891, and May, 1900, to Nov., 1901; J.P., 1890.
MAY, A. W. J.—Entered col. office after exam., May, 1882; asst. clk., May, 1892; asst. to supervisor of copying, Mar., 1900.

MAY, BARRY.—Cik. to res. mag., Vryburg, Brit. Bech., 1st June, 1888; clk. to res. mag., Kuruman, 1889; clk. to cvr. comsnr., Vryburg, 1890; acctnt. to recvr.-gen., 1894; acctnt. to res. comsnr., Behr. Prot., 1895, and in addition, govt. sec., 1902; is also ch. asst. offr. and mast. of res. comsnr.'s court.

MAY, C. G.—Apptd. asst. supt. of works, Straits Settlements, May, 1899; ag. supt. of works and surveys, Malacca, June, 1899, to Sept., 1901, and Oct., 1902, to Apl., 1903; supt. of works and surveys, Sing., July, 1903.
MAY, CECIL.—Clerical asst., treasy., Br. Guiana; 4th class clk., Apl., 1889; 3rd class, June, 1892.

MAY, FRANCIS H., C.M.G. (1896).—H.860; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Dub.; 1st honour-man and prizeman in classics and modern languages; apptd., after compet. exam., to Hong Kong cadetship, 1881; ag.-asst. regisr.-gen., Apl., 1886; ag. astt. col. sec., Jan., 1887; sec. to bd. of examrs., Jan., 1889; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1891; priv. sec. to Gov. Sir W. Des Voeux, to Admstr. Sir F. Fleming, and Maj.-Gen. Barker, Apl., 1889, to Dec., 1891; ag. col. treas., Apl. to Oct., 1892; mem. legis. comn., May, 1895; capt. supt. of pol. and fire brigade, 1893, and of Victoria gaol, 1897; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Sept., 1900; col. sec., 1902; admntd. the govt., Nov., 1903, to July, 1904.

MAY, GEORGE HERBERT.—Ag. apptnt. in regisr.'s off., Br. Guiana, 1889; admstr.-gen.'s off., on staff, June, 1892; pol. dept., Apl., 1893; 2nd lieut., Br. Guiana militia, Sept., 1894, and obtained certif. for drill and musketry from schl. of instruc., Chelsea Bks., Lon. and Hythe; ret. from mil. as lieut., and apptd. sub-inspr. of Br. Guiana pol., May, 1897; adjt. and musketry instr. of local forces, Fiji, with rank of capt., Oct., 1898; supt. of pol., ag. sheriff, 12th Dec., 1900; dep. comdt., armed constab., 24th Apl., 1901, also commanded volunteers; comdt., armed constab., supt. of pol., ag. sheriff, 24th June, 1902; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Fiji, July, 1901, to Sept., 1902; A.D.C. to Sir Henry M. Jackson, K.C.M.G., gov. of Fiji, 4th Nov., 1902; inspr. of constaby., Trinidad, 1906.

MAY, HENRY FREDERIC P.—Extra clk., treasy., Br. Guiana, 1877; clk., savings bank, 1880; 3rd cl. clk., 1881; 2nd ol. clk., 1884; clk. in charge of Fellowship branch, 1888; 1st cl. clk., 1893; ag. acctnt., G. S. bank, 1893-4; cashier of the treasy., 1896; acted as sub-acctnt. of Sudill branch, and clk. in charge of Belfield sub-treasy.; Hindustani interpreter for the treasy. and S. bank.
MAZERE, LOUIS JOSEPH.—Cik., survr.-genl.'s dept., Mauritius, 4th Jan., 1873; pay clk., ditto, 26th Mar., 1884; ch. clk. and acctnt., 3rd Dec., 1902.

MEADEN, C. W.—14th Hussars and 5th Dragoon Guards, 1870-6; conv. service, England, 1877; supt. of Chaguanas convict depot, Trinidad, 1885; J.P. for co. Caroni, 1887; astt. supt. prisons, 1890; mem. cent. agricult. bd.; carried out special agricul. experiments by prison labour, 1887 to 1892; twice ag. supt. of govt. farm and pastures, and sec., cent. agricult. bd.; warden of Chaguanas: lieut. and adjt., Trinidad Light Horse, Dec., 1894; man. govt. stock farm, and supt. of pastures, Mar., 1895; and adjt., Trinidad Light Horse, Dec., 1894.
MEANEY, S. J.—Entered col. office, after exam., Apl., 1881; astt. olk., May, 1892; supt. of copying dept., 1898.
MEIRING, E. J.—Apptd. acctnt., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902; clk. treasy., 1st Nov., 1903.

MELBOURNE, CHARLES ALEX. DICK.—Ed. privately and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1891; admitted to practice in Brisbane, Queensland, Nov., 1892; and in Hong Kong, Nov., 1895; apptd. 1st clk., magistracy, Hong Kong, Sept., 1900; J.P., 1900; ag. astt. supt., Victoria gaol, 1903 and 1904; ag. astt. land offr., 1905; comsnr. for oaths, Aug., 1906.

MELVILLE, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1900). C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1842; late lieut., Kent Artil. militia; ed. Edinburg Acad.; clk. in the solr.'s dept., inland rev., after compet. exam., June, 1862; afterwards in the legacy and succession duty dept.; ch. clk., treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1874; acted as col. treas., Dec., 1874, to Feb., 1876, during which period also acted as col. sec., July to Sept., 1876; acted also as collr. of customs and col. survr., and held comsnr. as dep.-coroner and J.P.; 1st clk., govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1877; astt. govt. sec., 1879; acted as govt. sec., 1881, 1882, 1887, and 1888, and also as audr.-gen.; col. sec. audr.-gen., and regisr.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1889; admstr. of govt., 1890 and 1891; admstr. of govt. of Falklands, 1893; col. sec., Bahamas, 1894; adminstr. govt., 1894 and 1895; col. sec., Leeward, 1895; adminstr. govt. under dormant commission, 1895, 1896, 1897, 1899, 1900; admstr. and col. sec., Saint Lucia, Sept., 1902; ret., 1905.

MENENDEZ, M. RAYMOND.—Ed. Emman. Coll., Camb.; LL.B., Camb., 1890; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; ag. stip. and circuit mag., and judge of com. pleas, Bahamas, 1892-4; dist. comsnr., Lagos, Aug., 1894; ag. Queen's advocate, and mem. exec. and legis. coun., Lagos, Dec., 1896, to Aug., 1897; ch. judicial offr., Niger Coast Protectorate, Aug., 1897; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Jan., 1, 1899; also puisne judge, N. Nigeria; ag. ch. just., 8 Mar. to 27 Oct., 1900, 28 Dec., 1901, to 26 Sept., 1902; reed. thanks of gov., for spec. services in reorganising native courts of prot., Jan., 1903.

MERCER, WILLIAM HEPWORTH, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1855; open scholar and Hody exhibnr., Wadham Coll., Oxford; 2nd cl. classical mods.; 2nd cl. final classical schl.; 2nd cl. clk. in the C.O. after open compet., 30th June, 1879; awarded 1st prize in common law offered by council of legal education, 1883, and a pupil scholarship of 100 guineas, in real property law, by the soc. of the Inner Temple, 1886; called to the bar, 1886; sec. to the Earl of Jersey, when ag. as Br. delegate at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii with Mr. (now Sir) Sandford Fleming, Sept. to Nov., 1894, negotiating for lease of landing station of proposed Pacific cable; astt. priv. sec. to Lord Ripon, Apr., 1896; to Mr. Chamberlain, June, 1896; 1st class clk., May, 1896; sec. to Pacific cable comteee, 1896; prin. clk., Oct., 1898; sec. to Pacific cable comtce., 1899; crown agt. for the colonies, June, 1900; mem. of Pacific cable bd., 1901; apptd. director of scholars sent to England from certain crown colonies, 1902.

MERCIER, GUY HENRY VIÉNOT.—2nd clk., regisr. and provost-marshal's office, Antigua, 1895; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, 1897; clk. to regisr. and provost-marshal, Nevis, 1898; dep. coroner, Nevis, 1901; rev. offr., Nevis, 1901; clk., B.C. Africa Prot., Apr., 1905.
MEREDITH, O. R.—For many years leader of opposition in Ontario legis. assem.; ch. just. ct. of com. pleas, Ontario, 1894.

MEREWETHER, EDWARD MARSH, C.V.O. (1903); C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1858; ed. Harrow; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1880; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1882; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, and J.P., S.S., May, 1882; confirmed, Jan., 1883; Malay interp. with H.M.S. "Pegasus" to Tenom in connection with the "Nisero" case, June, 1884; ag. mag. and offr. in charge treasy., Malacca, 1886; dist. offr., Dindings, 1886; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, 1888; ag. 1st mag., Penang, 1888; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1890; supt. of census, 1891; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1891; collr. of land rev., and in charge of treasy., Malacca, July, 1892; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept. to Dec., 1893; inspr. of prisons, S.S., 1893; ast. col. sec. and elk. of couns., May, 1897; ag. res. coun., Malacca, May, 1897, to June, 1898, and Sept., 1900; ag. col. treasr., S.S., Jan., 1900; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept., 1901; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Oct., 1901; confirmed, Dec., 1901; lieut.-gov. and ch. sec. to govt., Malta, Aug., 1902.

MERRIMAN, THE HON. JOHN XAVIER.—Comsnr. of pub. wks. and crown lands, C. of Good Hope, 1875-8; has for several years represented the electoral div. of Dordrecht in the Cape legis. assem.; again comsnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., 1881 to 1884; treas.-gen., 1890-3; mem. of Jameson raid comtce., 1896; treas.-gen. in the Schreiner ministry, 1898 to 1900; mem. for Victoria West, 1904.

MESSER, C. McL.—Ed. Merchant Taylors' schl., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; exhibitioner, 1892; scholar, 1893; 16th wrangler math. tripes, 1896; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; ag. land offr., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; ag. col. treasr., 1901; passed cadet, 1902; ag. regist.-gen., 1902; ast. land offr., N.T., 1903; and mem. of land ct., 1903-4.

METHUEN, LIEUT.-GEN. LORD, K.C.B., K.C.V.O., C.M.G. (186).—B. 1846; entered army, 1864; served in Ashanti campaign, 1873-4; in Egypt, 1882; in the Bechuanaland expdn., 1884-5; inspected Bechuanaland pol., 1890; commanding a div. in S. Africa, 1899.

METZGEN, CARL A.—Temporary clk., survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1885; clk. to att.-gen., 1887; ag. clk. to clk. of ct., Sept. to Nov., 1887; rev. offr., 1-88; 3rd clk., treasy., customs, and post office, 1889; 2nd clk., treasy., etc., Feb., 1891; capt., Belize fire brigade, June, 1889, to Feb., 1891; clk. to auditor, Dec., 1891; 4th clk., treasy., Sept., 1892; ag. 3rd clk., Nov., 1-93; audit examr., col. sec. dept., Feb., 1894; ch. clk., treasy. and customs dept., May, 1897; ag. regist.-gen., and ag. auditor, 30th May, 1905; auditor-gen., 7th Nov., 1905.

METZGER, S. AINH.—Apptd., after compet. exam., ast. master model schl., S. Leone, Aug., 1875; resign., and joined mercantile service; extra offr., H.M. customs, July, 1884; extra clk. gov.'s office, June, 1886; with Sir S. Rowe to Yonni exped., 1886; clk. sup. ct. and sheriff's offr., 1886; dep. and ag. master and regisr., sup. ct., greater part of 1892-7; ag. solr. and advoc., sup. ct., 1896; ag. regist.-gen., 1896-6; ag. curator intestate estates, 1897; under-sheriff, Dec., 1897; sec., revision comtce., 1896; ag. dist. comsnr., Waterloo dist., June to Dec., 1899; J.P., S. Leone.
MEWS, ARTHUR.—Aect., gov. telegraphs, Newfndld., 1883; clk. to fincl. sec., 1894; fincl. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1896; dep. col. sec., 1898; regisr. of joint stock companies, 1899.

MIALL, EDWARD.—Entered public service, Canada, 1870; ast. comsnr., inld. rev., 1872; attended fishery comn., Halifax, 1877; mem. of Pacific rly. coman. of inquiry, 1880-1; dep. min., comanr. of inld. rev., and comanr. of standards, Jan., 1883.

MICALLEF, RICHARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; entd. the Malta serv., Nov., 1863; clk. in office of charitable institutions, Dec., 1864; clk., sanitary office and sec. to the med. bd., Jan., 1881; dep. recr.-gen., dep. comptroller of charitable instit., and dep. comanr., Monte di Pietà, Gozo, Apr., 1884; ast. sec. to govt. for Gozo, Sept., 1885; comptroller of charitable instit., with seat in coun. of govt., Nov., 1886, and a seat in the exec. coun., Mar., 1888.

MICHELL, ROLAND LYONS N.—Ed. Chr. Ch., Oxford (open studentship, 1865); tutor to Prince Ibrahim Pasha, son of the Khedive of Egypt, 1870; received decoration of the Osmanian (4th class) from the Khedive Ismail, 1878; and of the Medjidieh from the late Khedive Tewfik, 1879; chief of statistical dept., Cadastral survey, Egypt, 1879; comsnr., Limasol, Cyprus, Aug., 1879; also dep. comsnr., high court, and pol. mag.; is sheriff of dist.

MICHELL, WALTER CECIL.—Ed. Godolphin schl. and Merton Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1887; cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1887; ag. collr. and mag., Ulu Pahang, 1888; passed Malay final, Sept., 1889; dist. offr., Balik, Pulau, Oct., 1891; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1895; also dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1896; 2nd mag. Singapore, May, 1897; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., S.S., Aug., 1897; also collr. of ld. rev., Singapore, Sept., 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Dec., 1898, to Dec., 1899; official assignee, S.S., Dec., 1901; ag. comsnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1903; comsnr., ct. of requests, Sing., Sept., 1903, but continued to act as 1st mag.

MIDDLETON, JOHN PAGE, B.A.—Ed. Uppingham and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June 1874, Norfolk and S.E. circuits; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast, Jan., 1882; pres. of dist. ct., Limasol, Cyprus, Sept., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1892; ag. ch. just., June and July, 1893; ag. judge of consular ct., Constantinople, May to July, 1894; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1895; received thanks of S. of S. Foreign Affairs in connection with Ottoman Dominions (Courts) O. in C., 1896; puisne judge, Ceylon, 1902.

MILLER, F. A.—Ch. clk. crown law office, S. Leone, 1891; dep. master, sup. ct., Sept. to Oct., 1895; 1st clk. col. secretariat, July, 1896; ch. clk. col. secretariat, Apr., 1879; gov.'s clk. and clk. of legis. coun., 1896; J.P. for colony.
MILLER, GRANVILLE G.—Master of titles, Queensland, Nov., 1882; dist. ct. judge, Aug., 1879.

MILLER, WILLIAM AKERMAN.—Assoc. M. Inst. C.E.; ed. King Henry VIII.'s school, Coventry, and Mason's Coll., Birmingham; pupil and ast. to E. Pritchard, M.I.C.E., Birmingham; 2nd cls. supt., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cls. ditto, 1896; col. engr. and survr. gen., Dominica, 1902; chrmn., telephone bd., Roseau, 1902; mem., Roseau town bd., 1902; sub-intendant, crown lands, 1903.

MILLS, Sir Richard, K.C.B. (1901), C.B. (1893); V.D.—B. 1830; acctnt. to the treasury, 1859-72; treasur. offr. of accts, 1872-88; asstt. comptroller and auditor, Sept., 1888; comptroller and auditor-gen., Apr., 1896, to 1900; late lieutenant-col. comdg. (hon. col.) 12th Middlesex (Civ. Ser.) R.V.; was hon. auditor of certain crown cols. ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for Cola.; retired 1900.

MILNER, The Rt. Hon. Viscount (1902), 1st Baron (U.K.), creat. 1901). P.C., G.C.B. (1901), G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.B.—B. 1854; ed. at Oxford; scholar Balliol; 1st class lit. hum., 1876; Hertford scholar, 1874; Craven scholar, 1877; Eldon scholar, 1878; Derby scholar, 1878; Fellow of New Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1881; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. G. J. Goschen, Chanc. of Excheq., 1887-9; under-sec. of finance in Egypt, 1889-92; chmn. bd. inland rev., 1892; author of "England in Egypt"; gov. Cape (1897-1901) and High Comsnr. S. Africa, 1897; admnstr. of Transvaal and Orange River Colony, as well as High Comsnr. S. Africa, 1901-1905.
MILNER, P.A.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the Civil Ser., and assigned to the C. O., July, 1900.

MILTON, Sir W. H., K.C.M.G. (1903), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., 1st Jan., 1879; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., Apr., 1883; sec. to the tender bd., 1881; clk. to exec. coun., 1885; ch. clk. and acctnt., col. sec.'s office, 1891; sec. to prime min., Mar., 1894; detached for serv. in Rhodesia as ch. sec. and sec. for native aff., Aug., 1896; transf'd. to Rhod. serv., Oct. 1897; ag. admr. Rhod., July, 1897, to Nov., 1898; apptd. admnstr. Mashonaland and senr. admnstr. S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1898; admnstr. S. Rhodesia, 20th Dec., 1902.

MINNOW, Joseph Lazarus.—Extra clk., customs, Elmina, 1872; scouts' interp. and commissariat issuer during Ashanti war, 1873-4; office keeper, customs, Cape Coast, 1874; clk. col. sec., 1874; 1st landing waiter and dep. regier. and interp., Axim, 1875; clk. and examining offr., Salt Pond, 1880; sub-coll. of customs, Chamah, 1884; Cape Coast, 1886.

MINTO, The Right Hon. The Earl of (4th Earl, cr. 1813), Viscount Melgund (U.K.), Baron of Minto (1797, Gt. Brit.), Bart. (1710, Scot.), P.C. (1902), G.C.M.G. (1898), Gilbert John Elliot.—B. 1845; succeeded 1891; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1886; ensign and lieut. Scots Guards, 1867-70, is col. comdg. S. Scotland inftry. vol. brig. (V.D.), and a D.L. for cos. of Roxburgh and Selkirk, served in Kurram Valley, Afghan campaign, 1879 (medal); accompanied Sir Fredk. (now Lord) Roberts to S. Africa as priv. sec., 1881; served in mounted inftry. in Egyptian campaign, 1882 (wounded Aug. 24th); afterwards commanded mntd. inftry, in Cairo (mentioned in despatches, medal and 4th class Medjidie); was sec. and mil. sec. to Marquis of Lansdowne, gov.-gen. of Canada, 1883-6; served as ch. of staff in N.W. rebellion, Canada, 1885 (mentioned in despatches and medal); gov.-gen. of Canada, 1888-1904; viceroy of India, 1905.

MITCHELL, G. P. S.—Asst. clk., educn. dept., Trinidad, Aug., 1880; 2nd clk. to asst. dir. of pub. wks., Feb., 1884; clk., N. div. of the P.W.D., May, 1886; clk. to warden of St. Ann's and Diego Martin Warder Unions, July, 1893; bandmr. Trinidad Field Artill. Volr. band, June, 1896.

MITCHELL, Robert W. S., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1840; asst. clk., immigrn. dept., Trinidad, 1858; warden of the dist. of Montserrat, and comsnr. of crown lands, 1867; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1869; was a J.P., mem. of the cent. road bd., and of the bd. of health; asst. protector of immigrts, Mauritius, 1872; received the thanks of H.M.'s roy. comsnrs. for special services rendered during their inquiry into the treatment of Indian immigrts. in that col.; acted as agt.-gen. of immigr. in Trinidad in 1873; govt. immigr. agt. in India, Oct., 1873; ag. immigr. agt. for Jamaica, 1876; agt.-gen. for immigrts., Br. Guiana, 1881; immigrn. agt. for Br. Guiana, in Calcutta, 1884; in 1885 modified immigr. rules under Act of 1883, and in 1886 obtained extension of Indian P.O. money order system to Br. Guiana and other W. Ind. cols.

MOFFAT, John B.—Priv. sec. to col. sec., Cape, 1890; prim. clk. col. sec.'s office, 1894: ag. ch. clk., 1895, and again 1896; ch. clk., native affairs offr., and accounting offr., prime minister's dept., Oct., 1897; civ. comsnr. and res. mag., Mafeking, Nov., 1900; comsnr. of taxes, July, 1904.

MOFFAT, Rev. John Smith, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1835; native comsnr., N.W. Border, Transval, 1880-1; R. M., Basutoland, Mar., 1882, to Apr., 1884; R. M. Taungs, Br. Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; asst. comsnr. for the protectorate, 1887.

MOFFAT, Robert Unwin, C.M.G. (1890).—Med. offr., Uganda Prot., 19th Dec., 1892; prim. med. offr., 3rd Jan., 1898; ditto, East Africa and Uganda Prots., 1st Apr., 1903; sen. med. offr., Uganda Prot., 21st Feb., 1904.

MOFFETT, Frank J.—Ed. Yorkshire Coll., Leeds, Univ. Coll., N. Wales, and schl. of elec. engineering, Hanover Sq., W.; B.A. Univ. Lond.; A.I.E.E.; asst. elec. engrnr. to the col. of Lagos, Feb., 1897; chief elec. engrnr., Nov., 1887.
MOIR, Chas. Edward.—Ag. 4th landing waiter, customs, St. Kitts, July, 1873, to Jan., 1875; 3rd landing waiter, May, 1875; 2nd ditto, Dec., 1878; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Aug., 1883.
MOIR, S. E.—Clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Mar., 1906.
MOLESWORTH, H.—Judge, ct. of insolvency, Victoria, 1886; formerly co. ct. judge.

MOLONEY, Sir C. Alfred, C.M.G. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1848; entered Mil.Coll., Sandhurst 1866; ensign, Feb., 1867; lieut., Dec., 1868; capt., Nov., 1874; acted as civil comdt. of the Quah dist. S. Leone, 1867-8; A.D.C. to Sir James Walker, C.B., gov., Bahamas, Feb., 1871; priv. sec. to Capt. G. C. Strahan, admnstr. Bahamas, Apr., 1871, to May, 1873; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Gov. Hennessy, June to Nov., 1873, when he embarked for service on the G. Coast; served in the Ashantee campaign, 1873-4 (medal); priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Gov. Strahan, July, 1874; ag. auditor, G.C. Col., Feb., 1875; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab., 1875; asst. col. sec., 1877; col. sec., 1879; admnstr. govt. of Lagos, 1878 to 1880, and again in 1881; admnstr. of the govt. of the col., 1882; resumed the admnstr. of Lagos, 1882-3; admnstr. of the Gambia, 1884; admnstr. of Lagos, 1885; gov., 1887; gov., Br. Honduras, 1891; gov. of Windward, 1897; gov. Trinidad and Tobago, 1900-1904; ret., 1904; author of "Forestry of West Africa."
MOMPLE, François Joseph Robert.—M.B.C.M., D.P.H. (Edin.); health offr., Mauritius, 7th Sept., 1898; ag. asst. sanitary warden, 4th Apr., 1900; 1st san. warden, 11th Apr., 1904.

MONCRIEFF, Alexander Bain, M.I.C.E., M. Ann. S. E.—Entered pub. wks. dept. of S. Australia as engnrng. draftman, Nov., 1874; rly. res. engrnr., Dec., 1879; engrnr.-in-ch. for rly. and waterwks., also engrnr. for harbours and jetties, May, 1888; mem. of rly. advisory bd. and chmn. of S.A. supply and tender bd.; engrn.-in-chief and chmn. supply and tender bd., 1895-1899.

MONPLAISIR, VOLMAR JOSEPH.—Entd. Saint Lucia serv. as super. clk., P.O., 21st July, 1896; clk., P.O., 9th Mar., 1899; ag. postmr. in 1901 and 1902; ch. clk., P.O., Grenada, 8th Jan., 1903; dep. postmr., 5th Aug., 1903; postmr., Saint Vincent, 22nd Oct., 1904.
MONTAGUE, AUBREY.—Ed. City of London schl.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B. (Lond.), 1n98; med. offr., Fiji, Sept., 1898.

MONTIZAMBERT, FREDERICK, I.S.O., M.D. (Edin.), F.R.C.S.E., D.C.L.—B. 1843; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Toronto, and Edin. Univ.; entd. pub. health serv. of Canada, 1866; med. supt., St. Lawrence serv., 1869; gen. supt., 1894; dir-gen., 1899; served as P.M.O., Quebec military dist., during Fenian raid, 1866 (medal and clasp); hon. fell. of Incorp. Soc. of offrs. of health of England; hon. fell. of the société française d'hygiène; mem. of national acad. of medicine of Mexico; has been president, and is a life mem. of exec. coun. of American pub. health assoc.; mem. exec. comttee. of Canadian assoc. for prevention of consumption; mem. exec. coun. Canadian branch British Red Cross Soc.; dir-gen. of pub. health and sanitary adviser of govt. of Canada; has published many papers and reports on pub. health subjects.
MOOR, THE HON. F. R., M.L.A.—Sec. for native affairs, Natal, 1893; also in 1899.

MOOR, SIR RALPH DENHAM RAYMENT, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G.
