TRUMP, J., A.M.I.C.E.—Pioneer office, pub. wks., Ceylon, 1878; suptdg. offr., 1879; in charge of convicts, Colombo Breakwater, 1880-3; engaged in rly. and wharf constr. in native states, S.S., 1883-6; dist. engnr., Kuila, Perak, 1886; dep. state engnr., Feb., 1887; ag. state engnr., Oct., 1886, to Oct., 1887.

TRUTCH, SIR JOSEPH W., K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1877), F.R.G.S., and M.I.C.E.—B. 1826; ch. comsnr. of lands and wks., and survr.-gen. of Br. Columbia, May, 1864; was a mem. of the exec. and legis. couns. of that col.; was apptd. in 1870 to proceed to Canada as one of the delegs. from Br. Columbia to confer with the govt. on the terms on which the col. should be admitted into the Dominion; lieut.-gov. of the prov. of Br. Columbia, July, 1871, to July, 1876; res. agt. of Dominion govt. in Br. Columbia, 1879-88.
TUCKER, A. L.—Pres. of hd. of land and wks., and comsnr. of crown lands, Victoria, 1883 to 1885; M.I.A. for Fitzroy.

TUDOR, DANIEL THOMAS.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; eq. dr.; also practised on West. Circ., Bristol, Bath, Somerset, C.C.C., Middx., and N. Lond. Sessions; served as one of the comsnrs. on the Saint Vincent pol. inquiry comsn., Sept., 1903; atty.-gen. of Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; also ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apl. and May, 1904; ag. ch. just., Grenada, May to Oct., 1904.

TUFTER, THE HON. SIR C., BART. (creat. 1888), G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1867), M.D., L.R.C.S., Edin. (1843).—Born 2nd July, 1821; is an M.A. and D.C.L. of Acadia Coll., Nova Scotia; is gov. of Dalhousie Coll., Halifax (apptd. by Act of Parlmt., 1862); pres. of the Canadian med. assoc. from its formation, 1867, until 1870; mem. of exec. coun., and prov. sec. N.S., 1857-1860; and from 1863 to June, 1867; prime min. of that prov. from 1864 until he ret. from office with his govt. on the Union Act coming into force, 1st July, 1867; deleg. to England on public business from N.S. govt., 1858 and 1865; from the Dominion govt., Mar., 1868; leader of the delegation from N.S. to the union conferees at Charlottetown, 1864; to that in Quebec in same year, and to final conferees in London, to complete terms of union, 1866-7; holds patent of rank and precedence from Her Majesty Queen Victoria as an exec. coun., N.S.; sworn of the P.C., June, 1870, and pres. of that body until 1st July, 1872, when app'td. min. of int. rev.; min. of customs, Feb., 1873; resig. office with Sir John Macdonald, 5th Nov., 1873; min. of pub. wks., Oct., 1878; min. of rly. and canals, 1879; represented Cumberland in parlmt. for 29 years in N.S. Assem., from 1855 until confederation in 1867, and in the Commons from that year until he resig. his seat in the cabinet and was app'td. high commr. for Canada, in London, May 24th, 1884; acted as exec. commr. for Dominion at the Antwerp Exhibn., 1885, and the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., London, 1886; resigued office in Canadian ministry as finance min., 1887; one of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries at fisheries conference at Washington, 1887; re-app'td. high commr., May, 1888; joint plenip., with the Marquis of Dufferin and Sir J. A. Crowe, for negotiating a commercial arrangement between Canada and France, 1892; S. of S. for Canada, Jan., 1896; premier, Mar. to June, 1896.

TUPPER, Hon. Sir Chas. Hibbert, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1855; son of Sir C. Tupper, mem. of N.S. bar; grad. Harvard Law schl.; mem. house of commons, Canada, since 1882; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. agt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the Behring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1896; resig., 1896.

TURNER, The Rt. Hon. Sir George, P.C., K.C.M.G. (1897), LL.D.—B. 1851; premier and treas. of Victoria, 1894-9 and 1900-1; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee 1897; pres. of conf. of premiers at Melbourne, 1899; joined 1st Commonwealth Ministry as treasurer, Jan., 1901; treas. in 2nd (Deakin) ministry; treas. in 4th (Reid-Turner) coalition ministry, 1904.

TURNER, GEORGE.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., D.P.H. (Cantab.); lecturer on Hygiene, Guy's hosp., 1882-1895; examnr. for D.P.H., roy. coll. of phys. and surgs., Lond., 1890-1895; employed by L.G.B. to report upon diphtheria, 1884; and by L.C.C. to inquire into epidemics of enteric fever and the sanitation of River Lea, 1894-95; med. offr. of health, Cape, 1895-1900; mem. of Cape med. coun., 1898-1900; dir. of govt. rinderpest experimental statn., Kimberley, 1897-98; med. offr. of health, Transvaal, 1st Aug., 1900; M.L.C.; census commr., Transvaal, 1904.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. Unvrs.; M.A. Abdn., 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; M.D., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick children, Edin., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infirmary, Edin., 1880; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp., 1881; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell. obstetrical soc., Edin.; asst. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar, 1882; also surg. civ. prison, med. offr. lunatic asylum, and dist. med. offr., 1882; pub. vaccinator, pd. surg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.

TUTCHER, W. J., F.L.S.—Ed. at Merchant Venturers' schl., Bristol; entered Royal Gardens, Kew, 1888; asst. supt., botan. and aerostern. dept., Hong Kong, 1891; actg. supt. in 1893, 1894, 1897, 1900 to 1901, and 1902 to 1903; late hon. lecturer on botany, coll. of med. for Chinese.
TWYNAM, E.—Entered the survey dept. of N.S. Wales as a rly. survr. in 1855; app'td. dist. survr. at Goulburn, 1863; ag. surv. gen., 1889.

TYSER, C. R.—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. commr. and leg. advdr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leeward, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1896; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evliak Law, 1899; and of the Mejjellé, 1901.
TYTLER, R. DUFF.—Supt. of immigrtn. Ceylon, 1st July, 1903.

UDAL, JOHN SYMONDS.—Called to the bar, In. Tem., Michaelmas term, 1875, W. circuit; J.P. for co. of Dorset; dep. county court judge, 1885-6-7; atty.-gen. of Fiji, Aug., 1888; Admty. advoc., Sept., 1889; ag. ch. justice for short periods in 1891-2-8; ag. legal asst. C.O., Feb. to Dec., 1900; ag. ch. justice, Leeward Islands, Dec., 1900; ch. just., June, 1904.

USHER, HENRY CHARLES, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; Clk., col. sec.'s office, Honduras, June, 1859; in public treas., July, 1865; provost-marshal, July, 1869; pol. mag., now called dist. mag., Belize dist., Nov., 1878; in charge of the public treas., June, 1866, to July, 1867, and Apr. to June, 1869; and of Belize gaol, May to Aug., 1872; has acted as regsr. of shipping, survr. of shipping, inquisitor of wreck and casualty, and inspr. of pol.; was marshal of the V.-A. ct., is ex-officio judge of the summary ct., and also coroner of the col., commr. of currency under Ord. No. 32 of 1894; ag. col. treas., collr. of cust., and postmr., harbmr., and suptdt. of mercantile marine on many occasions from 1883 to 1900; er-officio regsr. of births and deaths, 1885 to 1892; suptdt. fire brigade, 1886 to 1895; commr. on special duty to the Cays, Aug. to Sept., 1889; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1890; ret., June, 1903.

VALANTIN, W. ADOLPHE.—Asst. clk. and messenger, Queen's advoc.'s chambers, S. Leone, Jan., 1878; acted as asst. sec. to Br. commrs. on the N.W. Liberian Bndry. Com. in 1879; clk. to crown solr., June, 1890; acted as clk. to ch. just., 1882; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1883; dep. sheriff, Mar., 1890; clk. to ch. just., Aug., 1891; dep. coroner, July, 1896; clk. atty.-gen.'s chambers, Dec., 1897; curator, intestate estates, Jan., 1902.

VANDER BECK, J. N., I.S.O. (1903).—Extra clk., col. secy.'s office, Singapore, Sept., 1871; 8th clk., Jan., 1873, and attached to govt. house; 6th clk., Dec., 1875; guardian of govt. house, May, 1877; 5th clk., col. secy.'s office, Apl., 1879; 4th clk., June, 1879; 3rd clk., June, 1880; clk., govt. house, Dec., 1883.

VANE, F. W., I.S.O. (1903).—Employed in the G.P.O., Mar., 1871; asst. P.M.G. and inspr. of post offices, Ceylon, Apr., 1872; acted as sec. loan bd., Apr. to Dec., 1874; acted as P.M.G. and dir. of telegs. on several occasions; commr. of loan bd., Apl. to Dec., 1887; controller of govt. stores, 20th Jan., 1900.

VANE, HENRY G. B.—Asst. audr., Perak, Dec., 1884; ag. asst. audr.-gen. S.S., Apr., 1887; audr., Sungei Ujong and Negri Semblan, May, 1888; treas., pol. mag., etc., Sungei Ujong, Oct., 1890; audr., Selangor, Dec., 1892; state audr., Perak, Apr., 1895.

VAN NOOTEN, E. H. H.—4th clk., Savings bank, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1881; 6th clk., treas., May, 1884; 5th clk., Feb., 1886; 3rd class clk., July, 1888; 2nd class, Apr., 1891; 1st class, Jun. 1897; ag. asct. sup. ct. of civ. justice and asct. registr.'s office, Apr., 1898, to May, 1899.
VAN RENEN, H.—2nd asst. survr.-gen., Cape, Sept., 1897; ag. survr.-gen., May to Dec., 1904.
VAN ZIJL, JAMES STRATEN.—Controller, P.O. savings bank, late O.F.S., Sept., 1897; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.
VARDON, JOSEPH.—M.L.C., S. Aust., 1900; commr. of pub. wks. and min. of industry, 1904; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 1st Mar. to 26th July, 1905.

VAUDIN, WILLIAM MARSHALL.—Ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, P. W. D., Maur., Jan., 1888; sworn ld. surv., May, 1890; ag. asst. govt. surv., May to Oct., 1890; 2nd learner, P. W. D., April, 1891; ag. asst. surv., May, 1891, to July, 1892; first learner, P. W. D., June, 1893, actg. asst. govt. surv., Dec., 1897, to Sept., 1900; asst. surv., Seychelles, Dec., 1900; superint. of wks. and surveys, Aug., 1901.; chmn., local bd. of health, central dist., Apr., 1902.
VECCHIO, THOMAS JOSEPH.—Marshal, sup. ct., bkptcy., ct., and V.-A. ct., Gibraltar, 1866; also interp., 1866-76.
VELGE, C. E.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Michaelmas, 1870; regier., sup. ct., Singapore, May, 1875; ditto, S. Stltnts., 1st Mar., 1896.

VELLA, TOMMASO.—Entd. Malta service, Dec., 1866; clk., May, 1866; dep. collr. of cust., 1892; dep. postmtr., 1896; collr. of cust. and supt. of ports, Sept., 1902; postmtr.-gen., May, 1903; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

VENN, HARRY WHITALL.—Mem. of the legis. assem. of W. Australia; joined the 1st ministry under responsible govt., Dec., 1890, as comnr. of rly. and dir. of pub. wks.; returned in 1890 for the Wellington dist. as mem. of the legis. coun. and has since represented that constituency; chmn. of the comrn. on agricul., 1887-91; left office, 1896.

VENNING, ALFRED REID.—Unoffl. J.P., and dep. coroner, Matale dist., Ceylon, 1876; treas. and collr. of customs, Selangor, Oct., 1884; also chmn. sanitary Bd. Kuala Lumpur, June, 1890; special comnr. to report on Cocos and Keeling Is., 1893; ag. govt. sec., Selangor, Oct., 1893, to July, 1894; ag. clk. mag., Selangor, Mar., 1897; sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1897; fed. sec., Fed. Malay States, Aug., 1903.

VENNING, G.—Apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bld., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. surv., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transfd. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903.
VERITY, G. B.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Br. Hond., 6th July, 1904; ag. 1st cls. clk., ditto, 1st Sept., 1905.

VERNON, HON. F. G.—Ed. in Eng. for R.E.; came to Br. Columbia, 1863; elected to local legis. lature, 1875; mem. exec. coun. and ch. comnr. of lands and wks., 1876; ret., 1878; re-elected, 1878, and remained in legislature till 1882, then ret.; re-elected in 1887, and again apptd. ch. comnr. of pub. wks. and lands and mem. of exec. coun. for prov.; agt.-gen. for Br. Columbia in London, 1895.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES.—Ed. at Clifton and Oxford; scholar of Balliol, 1895-9; 1stcl. Moda., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Sec.'s dept. inland revenue, Nov., 1899; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Aug., 13th, 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot comnr., 1903.
VICKERS, EDWARD.—Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1864; called to the bar, In. Tem., Jan., 1868; advoc. in the high ct., Bombay, Dec., 1868; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1888.

VIGORS, CHARLES THORNTON DOGNE.—Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; ast. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, 1884; ditto, Galle, 1886; ag. off. ast. to govt. agt., S. prov., from Sept., 1886, to Mar., 1888; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Apr., 1888; Tangalla, Dec., 1889; landing survr. cust., Colombo, Nov., 1888, resumed duties, Feb., 1890; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1894; ast. govt. agt., Matara, May, 1894, resumed same, Jan., 1896; Kalutara, May, 1898; govt. agt., Uva, Aug., 1900; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, 13th May, 1902.

VILLIERS, FRANCIS JOHN, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1851; entered the C.O., Sept., 1869; priv. sec. to the admstr. of Griqualand W., Oct., 1875, and clk. of the coun.; ag. col. sec., May, 1877, to Oct., 1889; mem. of legis. coun., 1879; engaged on special service in connection with the annexation of the prov. to the Cape Col. from Nov., 1880, to May, 1881; audr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882; ag. govt. sec., at various times between 1883 and 1896; ret., 1898.

VIRET, ALFRED PERCIVAL.—Rev. offr., Dominica, May, 1882; sec. bds. of health and quarantine, Aug., 1884; sec. to local comte., Ind. and Col. Exhibtn., 1886; confidl. clk. to pres., and clk. exec. coun., May, 1886; govt. offr., treas. and quarantine offr., June, 1891; 1st cl. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1897; ast. col. treas., S. Leone, July, 1899; acted as col. treas., S. Leone, from July to Oct., 1899, and from Jan. to Sept., 1901; acted as col. P.M.G., from June to July, 1900, and from Oct. to Mar., 1902; apptd. collector of customs, 3rd Nov., 1902.
VITALIS, NICHOLAS.—Ch. interp. and clk. to comnr., Larnaka, Cyprus, July, 1878; ditto, Nicosia, Mar., 1879; clk. and interp. to Queen's advoc., Feb., 1882.
VON DOUSSA, LOUIS.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., 1899-1902; M.L.C., 1903-5; atty.-gen., 1903-4.

WACE, HERBERT, C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1851; ed. at Westminster Schl.; Ceylon writer, 1873; ast. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, 1876; ast. govt. agt., Matara, 1886; N'Elia, 1887; ag. govt. agt., Ratnapura, 1889; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1889; comnr. of requesta, Colombo, 1890; govt. agt., Ratnapura, 1891; ag. govt. agt., Matara, May, 1886; 2nd ast. col. sec., Mar., 1889; com. of requesta, Colombo, Dec., 1890; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1892; ag. govt. agt., Kurunegala, 1896; govt. agt., C. Province, Jan., 1900.
WAINWRIGHT, HAMILTON.—Supernum. clk., audit office, Trinidad, 1872; in treasy., 1873; 7th clk., treasy., 1874; ch. clk., col. hosp., 1874; ag. sup. lun. asyl., 1878-9; 2nd clk., customs, 1883.

WAKEMAN, GEORGE HERBERT.—Ed. Bradfield Coll., Berks, and Camb.; admitted solvr., sup. ct., England, July, 1891; ast. land offr., Hong Kong, June, 1900; J.P., Dec., 1900; ag. dep. land offr. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Apr. to Dec., 1901; ag. land offr. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Jan. to Nov., 1902, and Jan. to Aug., 1906; comnr. for oaths, sup. ct., Hong Kong, and comnr. for taking acknowledgts. of married women, Aug., 1905; land offr. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1906.

WALCOTT, H. B.—Excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treasy. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treasy. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; ch. clk., audit office, Dec. 1893; ag. postmr., 1896-5; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk., col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, 1897; audr., Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treasur. Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treas., and mem. ex. and leg. couns., dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. comsnr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regis. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comtee., mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and leg. couns., Br. Hond., Mar., 1903; J.P., Apl., 1903; ag. col. sec., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905.

WALCOTT, REV. J. EVANS, S.C.C.—Ed. Codrington Coll. (Barbados); theolog. exhib., 1865; Rawle scholar, 1867, etc.; headmnr., St. Lucy Mid. Schl., 1869; curate, St. Silas and St. Albans, 1872; public librarian, 1877; compiled classified catalogue, 1879.
WALKEM, G. A., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and ch. comsnr. of lands and wks., Br. Columbia, Canada, 1874 to 1878; apptd. to the bench of sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1882.

WALKER, SIR EDWARD NOEL, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1842; ed. Chelt. Coll.; in office of gov.-in-ch. of Windward Is., Feb., 1862; priv. sec., May, 1866; previously priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov. of St. Vincent from Nov., 1864; asst. govt. sec. and clk. of the ct. of policy and combined ct., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1867; acted as govt. sec. on several occasions; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, May, 1874; col. sec., June, 1883; ditto, Ceylon, 1887-1901; admstd. govt., Mar., 1888, Jan., 1889, Apr., 1893, Dec., 1894, Oct., 1895; lieut.-gov., Dec., 1890; resumed duties as col. sec. at various dates from July, 1888, to Feb., 1896; admstd. govt., Mar., 1896; retd., 1901.

WALKER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR F. W. E. F., G.C.M.G., K.C.B., C.B. (1879).—B. 1844; entered army, 1862; A.D.C., Mauritius, 1866-7; mil. sec., Cape, 1873-8; served in S. African wars, 1878-9 and 1899; and in Bechuanaland expdn., 1884-5.

WALKER, HENRY.—2nd asst., land dept., Br. N. Borneo, Jan., 1883; asst. supt., lands and pub. wks., Kudat, July, 1883; comsnr., land, and recorder of lands, May, 1884; mem. coun., Oct., 1883; ag. supt., P.W. dept., Jan., 1887.
WALKER, HOPSON PUCKNEY, B.A.—Jesus Coll., Camb., 1853; LL.B. (Lond.), M.A. (Melb.); barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.), 1888; ch. clk., sup. ct., Melbourne, Dec., 1883.

WALKER, JAMES HUTCHINSON.—Ed. Abdn. Univ., M.A. (nat. sci. hon.), 1876; M.B., C.M. (highest hon., Murray scholar, and gold medal), 1878; M.D. 1884; asst. prof., botany, Abdn., 1876; prin. med. offr., N. Borneo, Aug., 1881.

WALKER, JOHN BAYLDON.—Called to the bar, In. Tem., Nov., 1875; went N.-E. circuit; pol. mag. and coroner, S. Leone, 1890; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1891; ag. Q.A., June, 1892; and extraord. mem. legis. coun., Sept., 1892; received thanks of coun., 1893, for preparing Freetown municipality order; ag. Queen's advoc., May, 1894; judge, Turks Is., 1895; ag. comsnr., 1895; ch. just., island of St. Vincent, Feb., 1898; S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1901; ch. just., St. Lucia, 1902.

WALKER, LT.-COL. R.S. FROWD, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1850; entered Sandhurst, 1869; ensign 28th foot, 1871; capt., June, 1881; hon. lieut.-col., 1889; A.D.C. to gov., S.S., 1878-9; attached to Perak armed pol., Mar., 1879; dep. comsnr. of the force, Feb., 1880, and in command from Nov., 1882; acted on various occasions as pol. mag., asst. res., and as sec. to gov.; has received the thanks of govt. several times for his action in dealing with Chinese and coolie disturbances; in command of Pahang expdny. force, 1892 and 1894; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1895; organised Malay States Guides, 1896; and since commands the regt.; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1893, to Jan., 1900; ditto, Perak, Apr., 1900, to July, 1901.

WALL, J. DARRELL.—Clk. and storekeeper, Holberton Instlt., Antigua, Nov., 1891; shortld. reporter to W. Ind. Roy. Coman., 1897; aud. def. force accts.; ag. ch. clk. to aud.-gen., Leeward and Fedrl. acct., June, 1899; also clk. to trade and income tax comsnrs., Sept., 1899; ch. clk. to aud.-gen. and Fedrl. acct., May, 1900.

WALLACE, WM., C.M.G. (1897).—Senior ex. offr., Roy. Niger Co., 1886; later as agt.-gen., until 1900, when Niger territs. were handed over to H.M. govt.; concld. numerous treaties with native potentates, one with the Sultan of Sokoto, 1894; commanded or served with over 30 expdtns.; deputy high comsnr. and senior res., N. Nigeria, 1900; admtd. govt., May to Dec., 1901, and May to Nov., 1903 (3 W. African medals, 5 clasps, coronation medal).
WALLACE, WILLIAM HENRY.—Ent. govt. ser., 1882; sec. of mines, Tasmania, 1898.

WALLIS, C. BRAITHWAITE, F.G.S., F.R.G.S., etc.—Ed. privately from Eton, and at Shrewsbury and Mil. Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 4th Manchester regt., 1894; lieut., 1895; instr. of musketry, 1896; capt., 1897; "Special mention" certif., schl. of instruction, and "extra" certif., schl. of musk., 1896; instr. of musk. to 3rd Border regt., 1896, and to 3rd Roy. Welsh Fus., 1897; specially commended by G.O.C. dist. for performance of duties; nominated for admission to R.I. Constab., 1897; apptd. to S. Leone Front. Force, Jan., 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., 1898; Mendiland, Sherbro', and Karene expdn., 1898; Protectorate expdn., 1899; twice mentd. in despatches; in command of a column; rec. thanks of gov., West Africa medal and clasp; transf'd. to 1st batt., Cameronians, 1899; to India, Oct., 1899; selec. for serv. in Somaliland, 1901; asst. dist. comsnr. S. Leone, 1901; ag. comsnr., Apr., 1902, to Mar., 1903; awarded silver medal and certif., R. Humane Soc., Dec., 1901, for saving life in West Africa; again selected for service in Somaliland, June, 1903; J.P. for S. Leone; F.R.C.I.; author of "The Advance of our West African Empire," and "West African Warfare" (Unwin, 1903).

WALLIS, HENRY RICHARD.—Asst. agt., Chinde, 15th Sept., 1893; Brit. vice-consul, Fort Johnston, 1st Oct., 1897; 1st cls. asst., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Jan. to Nov., 1899; recd. comsn. as H.M. vice-consul, 9th June, 1900; ag. consul, Mozambique, Feb. to Dec., 1901; apptd. vice-consul, Tete, 1st Apl., 1901; ag. dep. comsnr., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; asst. dep. comsnr., 1st Apl., 1904.

WALPOLE, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, M.A., KT. BACH. (1897).—B. 1848; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., Jan., 1873; went the home (afterwards the S.-E.) circuit; pres., dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1882; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1889; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; acted as ch. just., June to Sept., 1890, and June to Nov., 1890; ag. ch. just., Gibraltar, Nov., 1892, to Nov., 1893; ch. just., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ret., 1897.
WALSH, FRANK.—Entered N.S. Wales civ.ser., legis. assem. dept., 1874; parly. librarian, 1889.

WALTER, W. G. C.—Served in R. Navy from July, 1877, to Feb., 1886; apptd. to Roy. Niger Co.'s constabulary, Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1894; took part in expedns. of 1886-1894, Nigeria med. and clasp; harbour mr., Klang, Selangor, F.M.S., Jan., 1895; dep. regisr., imports and exports, Klang, Feb., 1895; 1st cl. mag., July, 1895; conservator of the port of Klang and regisr. of imports and exports, Dec., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Klang, on occasions from Feb., 1897, to Feb., 1901.
WALTON, HON. EDGAR HARRIS.—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904; is proprietor and editor of "Eastern Province Herald."

WALTON, GEORGE O'DONNELL.—Matriculated a student of Lond. Univ., June, 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 17th Nov., 1893; ag. on several occasions as pol. mag., Barbados; judge of the petty debt court of Bridgetown; judge of the asst. ct. of appeal; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist., "A" from 21st July, 1902, to 14th Feb., 1903; pol. mag., dist., "F," 9th Dec., 1902.

WARD, HENRY THOS. SIMPSON, M.I.C.E.—Suptdg. offr. of irrigtn., Ceylon, 21st May, 1876; dist. engrnr., June, 1886; prov. engnr., 1st Nov., 1894; financial and office asst., 7th May, 1895; asst dir. of pub. wks., 23rd Jan., 1898; dir. of irrigtn., 15th May, 1904.

WARD, W. F.—Assoc. Roy. Schl. of Mines, Lond. (1876);—Analyst, etc., to the govt. of Tasmania, 1882; asst. to Dr. T. Percy and Prof. W. C. Roberts-Austen, etc., in chemical and metallurgical laboratories of Roy. Schl. of Mines, Royal Mint, and Normal Schl. of Science, S. Kensington (1876-80); special apptmt. to Potosi Gold Mining Co., Venezuela (1881); in charge of chemical testing of smoke-consuming appliances at exhibition, S. Kensington (1881).

WARE, FABIAN.—Ed. priv. and Unis. of Lond. and Paris (B.-es-cs., 1894); asst. mast. in secondy. schl., Bradford gram. schl., 1895-9; examnr. in French to civ. serv. comsnr., 1898-1901; prov. inspr. of secondy. schl. to bd. of edcu., 1901; representative of educn. comtee. of royal comsnr. at Paris exhibtn., 1900; ag. asst. dir. of edcu., O.R.C., 28th June, 1901; asst. dir. of edcu., Transvaal, 17th Oct., 1901; dir. of edcu., 1st July, 1903; corres. mem. of nat. educn. assoc. of U.S.A.; author of articles on German educn. in spec. rpts. of bd. of edcu., 1898-9; transltn. of "Mon Testament" (Père Hyacinthe), 1895; "Educnl. Reform—The Task of the Bd. of Educn." 1900; "Educnl. Foundtns. of Trade and Industry," 1901.

WARNER, GEORGE BRUNTON.—Extra clerical asst., G.P.O., P.M. Burg., from Sept. 1st, 1-81, to Feb. 16th, 1882; 3rd class clk. civ. serv., and filled the post of 7th clk., G.P.O., Feb. 16th, 1882; as 6th clk., Jan. 18th, 1883; clk., col. engineer's dept., Jan. 13th, 1886; border customs offr., De Jager's Drift, Jan. 8th, 1887; issuer of native passes, Dundee div., Jan. 16th, 1888; sheep inspr., De Jager's Drift, May 7th, 1888; J.P., Dundee div., Dec. 13th, 1892; chief examining offr., Customs Point, Durban, Nov. 21st, 1896; ag. clk. of the court, Newcastle, June 16th, 1900; clk. of the court, Newcastle, Aug. 9th, 1900; J.P., Newcastle div., Aug. 16th, 1900; deputy registrar special court, Newcastle div., Aug. 23rd, 1900, in terms of sec. 25 of Act No. XIV., 1900; has acted as asst. and ag. mag. of Newcastle on many occasions; also for the following periods:—Act. asst. mag. from Nov. 19th, 1900, to March 24th, 1901; ag. mag. from June 6th, 1901, to Sept. 8th, 1901; ag. asst. mag., Sept. 9th, 1901.
WARNER, GEORGE VALENTINE.—Clerical asst., comsnr.'s off., St. Kitts, 1891; 2nd clk., treasy., Apl., 1896; rev. offr., 1896; acctnt., treasy., 1897.
WARREN, A.—Apptd. asst., treasy., O.R.C., 23rd July, 1900; asst. bookkeeper, funds dept., 1st July, 1903.

WARREN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR CHARLES, R.E., K.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1840; ed. Chelt. Coll.; was specially employed in various military and administrative capacities in Griqualand W. and the Cape Col. in 1876-8; lieut.-col. for distinguished service; adminstd. govt. of Griqualand W., 1879 to 1880; served in Egyptian campaign, 1882, and brought to justice the murderers of Prof. Palmer and his party; H.M.'s special comsnr. in Bechuanaland, 1884-5, with local rank of maj.-gen., comdg. expdn.; apptd. to command at Suakim, with rank of maj.-gen., and as gov.-gen., 1886; comsnr. of metropol. pol., 1886-8; C.R.E., and in command of troops with rank of maj.-gen., Singapore, 1889 to 1894; commanding a div. in war against Transvaal and Orange F. S., 1899-1900.

WARREN, COL. FALKLAND GEORGE EDGEWORTH, R.A., C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1834; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and R.M.A., Woolwich; 2nd lieut., R.A., 1852; capt., 1859; major, 1871; lieut.-col., 1877; col., 1881; served in Indian Mutiny campaigns, 1857, 1858, and 1859; Umbeyla campaign, 1862 and 1863; Bhootan campaign, 1865 and 1866; Six times mentioned in despatches; medals and four clasps; asst. comsnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, July, 1878; comsnr., Limassol, Oct., 1878; ch. sec. to govt., Aug., 1879; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ret. 1890.
WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, F.R.G.S.—Dist. surv., Ceylon, 1st Oct., 1879; office asst., 16th June, 1885; 4th oh. survr., 1st Jan., 1895; asst. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; has acted as survr.-gen.

WATKINS, FREDERICK HENRY, I.S.O. (1904).—Acted as rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; inspr. of schl., St. Vincent, 1883; headmnr. of gram. schl., St. Vincent, 1887; inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., 1889; ag. pol. mag., Grenadines, 1886; reported on St. Lucia schl., 1888; adjt., St. Kitts-Nevis volr. forces, 1897-9; comsnr. of Montserrat, Jan., 1900.
WATSON, E. C.—M.H.A., Trinity, Newfild., 1885-9; supt. fisheries, 1898; dep. min. for marine and fisheries, 1898.
WATSON, JAMES.—Col. treas., N.S.W., Dec., 1878, to Jan., 1883.
WATSON, J. J.—Res. mag., Jansenville div., Cape Col., Jan., 1880.

WATSON, REGINALD GEORGE.—Ed. at Haileybury; cadet, S. S., 1883; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Smith, 1884-5; attached to land office, Malacca, 1887; ag. 2nd astt. prot. Chinese, Singapore, 1888; ag. astt. ditto, 1888-9; dist. offr., South, prov. Wesleyan, 1889; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1889; 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1890; prot. of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1891; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1895, and Mar., 1898; sen. mag., Selangor, July, 1896; sec. to govt., Selangor, Apr., 1899; ag. sen. mag., Perak, June, 1899; senior mag., Sept., 1901; ag. sec. to res. Perak, May, 1902; comsnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., 27th Feb., 1904.
WATT, JAMES.—Ed. at Oxford; classical schl., Balliol Coll., 1889; B.A. 1895; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr., Southern Nigeria, 1899; dist. comsnr., Feb., 1902.
WATTLEY, ERNEST CHARL.—Ed. Gram. Schl., St.Kitts, Crawford Coll., Maidenhead, and London

WENTZEL, CHAS. AUGUSTUS.—Legal adviser to mil. gov., Pretoria, June, 1900; rea. mag., Johannesburg, Apl., 1901; senior mem. of spec. crim. ct., J'burg., Apl., 1901-3; mem. gold law comsn., 1902; chrmn. of J'burg. insanitary area improvmnt. scheme comsn., 1902-3; ch. mag. for Witwatersrand dist., May, 1902; advocate of sup. ct., Transvaal.

WERRY, CAPT. FRANCIS EDWARD.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Uppingham schl.; capt. 4th batt. Essex regt.; dist. commsnr., Lagos, July, 1902; ag. commsnr. of pol., Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. trav. commsnr., N.E. dist., Jan. to June, 1903.
WESSELS, J. W.—Legal adviser to commdr.-in-chief, July, 1900, to Apl., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, May, 1902.
WESTBROOK, WALTER FRANCIS.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1880; supt. of the registry, 1892; ch. registr., 1896.
WETMORE, A. R.—Atty.-gen., N. Brunswick, Sept., 1867; judge, sup. ct. of judicature of the prov., May, 1870; also sole judge, ct. of divorce causes, 1881.

WETMORE, EDWARD LUDOW.—Ed. at King's Coll., Fredericton, 1869; barrister, N. Brunswick, 1864; dep. clk. of crown, 1869-72; mayor, Fredericton, 1874-77; Q.C., 1881; elected to legislature, 1882-6; leader of Opposition, 1883-6, when he was not re-elected; judge, N.-W. Territories, 1887.
WHEELER, CHARLES HENRY, M.D. (Queen's Univ., Irel.), L.M.R.H.D.—Surgeon in the navy, Aug., 1881; dist. surg., Krian, Perak, Jan., 1886; J.P. for Krian, 1887.
WHEELER, R. P.—Apptd. typist, Imperial military railways, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1900; ditto, refugee camps, 17th July, 1901; ditto, treas., 15th Nov., 1902.
WHEELER, W.—C.M.G. (1904); Treas. Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 28th Feb., 1892.

WHITE, LT. COL. FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; clk., dept. of just., Canada, 1869; transf. to dept. of S. of S. as clk. in-charge of mounted pol. branch, Apr., 1876; ch. clk., Sept., 1878; comptroller of N.-W. mounted pol. force of Canada, Nov., 1878; dep. head, July, 1883; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir John Macdonald, 1880 to 1882; commsnr. of N. W. Territories, 24th Aug., 1906.

WHITE, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR GEORGE S., D.C.L., G.C.B., G.C.S.I. (1898), G.C.M.G. (1901), G.C.I.E. (1893), G.C.V.O. (1900).—B. 1835; commander-in-chief, India, 1893-8; Q.M.G., 1898-9; commanding troops in Natal at outbreak of S. Afr. War, 1899; defended Ladysmith, Nov., 1899, to end of Feb., 1900; gov. Gibraltar, 1900-1906.

WHITE, HERBERT.—Ed. Shrewsbury Schl. cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1879; pol. mag., Avisawela, 1886; astt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 1896; agt., govt. avla., 1895; 2nd astt. col. sec., and sec. cent. irrign. bd., 1896; agt. govt. agt., S. prov., 1896 to Jan., 1897; prin. astt. col. sec., and sec. cent. irrign. bd., May, 1899; mayor of Colombo, May, 1900; commsnr. of requests, Dec., 1901; agt., pol. mag., Colombo, 20th Feb., 1908; registr.-gen., 30th Mar., 1903; commsnr. of requests, 27th Nov., 1903.

WHITE, LT.-COL. WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1830; clk. in G.P.O., London, Feb., 1846; res. Apr., 1854; ch. clk. in money order branch of P.O. dept., Canada (on its establishment), Dec., 1854; sec. of the dept., Jan., 1861, and a commsnr. to inquire into the organisation of the civ. ser., June, 1880; author of a "Post Office Gazetteer" of the Dominion; and of the "Annals of Canada," published in the "Canadian Monthly"; is lieut.-col. comdg. 43rd batt. of Canadian militia; and a J.P.; comdg. Canadian team sent to Wimbledon in 1884; dep. P.M.G., July, 1888.

WHITE, W. ASTHUR.—Apptd. after open compet. exam. for home civil serv. to P.O., Jan., 1890; 2nd div. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., July, 1892; clk., controller's office, Mar., 1898; supt. of registrtn., G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1903; ag. supt. of mails, Oct., 1903; acctnt., postal dept., F.M.S., 1905.

WHITEHOUSE, ALFRED ASHMALL.—Consular agt., Niger Coast Protectorate, Oct., 1894; dist. commr., 1896; served with Benin City expdn., 1897 (medal and clasp); received thanks of S. of S. for work in Kwa Ibo country, 1897; ditto, for work along German bdry., and in Eko country, 1899; trav. commr., S. Nigeria, 1900; polit. offr. with expdn., in Ubinni country, 1901; served with Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp); polit. offr. with expdn. against Bibi-Kala, 1903; polit. offr. Ibibio expdn., 1904; has acted on several occasions as vice-consul and divisional commr.

WHITEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR WILLIAM V., P.C., K.C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1828; atty.-gen. and premier of Newfld., 1878 to 1884, and again 1889; offl. deleg. to London on Fisheries question, 1890; deleg. from legislature on same question, 1891; resig. with his ministry, Apr., 1894; again took office Feb., 1895; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; resig., 1897.
WHITFIELD, H. S.—Ck. and examr., audit dept., W. Australia, 1886; ch. clk., Aug., 1891.
WHITING, JOHN BEERY.—Entd. public serv., S. Australia, 1873; sec., state children's dept., 1886; sec. and regist. of stock in ag.-gen.'s dept., London, 1903.

WHITTY, JAMES LIONEL.—Ed. Portarlington, and Gram. schl., Tipperary; passed exam. in Chinyanja; clk. in treasry., Brit. Cent. Africa Prot., 21st May, 1901; asst. treasr., Somaliland Prot., 31st Mar., 1904.

WHYHAM, WILLIAM HENRY.—Entered R.N., 1864; ret., July, 1873, and joined the Leeward Is. colonial yacht "Union"; dist. mag., Dominica, Nov., 1874; nominated mem. of legis. assem., Mar., 1877; dist. mag. and inspr. of prisons, Antigua, Feb., 1878; ag. pres. of Nevis, 1882; hon. commr. for Antigua at Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. legis. coun., Antigua, 1887; ag.-pres., Dominica, Apr. to Nov., 1889; escheator-gen., 1890; ag. commr., Montserrat, May to Sept., 1894; May to June, 1898; pres. legis. coun., and mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1896; ag. admstr., Dominica, July, 1896, to Feb., 1897, and Apr. to Sept., 1899; visiting just. gaol, 1898; offl. mem. legis. coun., July, 1898; non-elective mem. legis. coun., L. Is., Dec., 1898; ag. admstr., Dominica, July to Nov., 1900; ag. aud.-gen., L. Is., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; June-July, 1900; Nov., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. mem. exec. coun., L. Is., 1st Mar., 1902.
WHYTE, J. W.—Solr. to lands titles commrs., Tasmania, 1888; recorder of titles; reg. of public trusts, collr. of stamp duties, and dep. regist. of deeds, 1884.

WIDDUP, JOHN CHAS. PONSONBY.—L.R.C.S., 1869; L.M. and L.R.C.P., 1870; supernum. med. offr., Br. Guiana med. ser., 1881; govt. med. offr., Canye dist., 1886; health offr., pol. prison, and port surg., New Amsterdam, 1889; mem. Br. med. assoc.

WIGLEY, F. S.—Mem. of assem., St. Kitts, Mar., 1886; notary public, Aug., 1886; called to the bar, June, 1887; master and examr. in chancery, Oct., 1887; ag. pol. mag. and coroner for the Basseterre dist. in 1870 and 1872; ag. att.-gen. in 1870 and 1872; mem. of the exec. coun., Oct., 1870; visiting just. of the gaol, Apr., 1872; nominated mem. of the legis. assem., May, 1873; dist. mag. for dist. C, 1877; offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1879; now mag. and coroner, dist. C; ag. pres., 1885-86; ag. pres., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1885, 1886, 1887, 1888; mem. exec. coun., 1893; ag. commr., 1893 and 1895; ag. admstr., 1898, 1899 and 1900; ditto (under dormant comm.), 1901-1902.
WILBRAHAM, D. F., B.A. (Oxon).—Master sup. ct. and regiar.-gen., S. Leone, 1896; ag. ch. just., June to Nov., 1902; also ag. atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1902.
WILHELM, THOS. A.—Entered col. service, S. Leone, 1878; chief draftsman, survey dept., Mar., 1895.
WILKIE, DAVID, F.I.P.S.—Ed. at Lincoln middle schl., and Chr. hosp., London; served in S. African campaign, 1900-1; corporal, S.A.C., 1903; clerk to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1903.

WILKINSON, DAVID.—Ch. interpreter of Fiji, 10th Oct., 1874, to Sept., 1875; sec. to land claims commr., Dec., 1876; native commr., Nov., 1876; mem. native regist. bd., 1877; accompanied Dep. High Commr. Maudaley as special political agt. to king and chiefs of Tonga, 1878; accompanied Sir A. Gordon to Tonga, 1879; resig., 1882; commr. native lands, Fiji, June, 1894.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transfed. to C.O., June, 1896; temp. staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; promoted to higher grade, 1st Apr., 1905.

WILKINSON, ERNEST FRED. WHALLEY.—C.E., demonstrator and lecturer in engineering, Mason Coll., Birmingham; ch. asst. engrnr., Hull corptn.; ch. engrnr., P.W.D., Siam; apptd. sen. supt. of wks., G. Coast, July, 1897; apptd. ag. 1st asst. dir. of wks., Nov., 1897, to May, 1898; ag. dir. pub. wks., Dec. 1899, to June, 1900; extraordinary M.L.C., Feb., 1900; ex. mem., exec. coun., Feb., 1900; apptd. asst. dir. pub. wks., Oct., 1899; ag. dir. pub. wks., June, 1901, to Mar., 1902.

WILKINSON, J. E.—Ck., stores acctng. dept., Cape govt. rlys., Jan., 1901; joined impl. mil. authorities, 30th Apr., 1901; ck. and typist, land sttlmnt. dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1902; asst. acctnt., 1902; examr. of acctns., audit dept., 1st May, 1903.

WILKINSON, RICHARD JAMES.—Ed. Trim. Coll., Camb. (exhibition); cadet, S. S., Oct., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1890, to Aug., 1891; ditto, Nibong Tebal, June to Aug., 1892; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Dec., 1892, to Mar., 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1893; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Sept., 1894, to May, 1895; ag. asst. admtr.-gen., July, 1895; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 3rd mag., Singapore, Mar. to Apr., 1896; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Apr., 1896; also ag. supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1897; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., July, 1897; ag. dist. offr., P.W., Aug., 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr. of schls., S. Stltmsts., Aug., 1898; apptd. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, but still ag. inspr. of schls., Jan., 1899; ag. supt. educn., Penang, Aug., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1902; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Apr., 1903; inspr. of schls., F.M.S., Oct., 1903.
WILLCOCKS, LIEUT.-COL. SIR JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1900, after relief of Coomassie), C.M.G.
WILLEY, ARTHUR.—D.Sc. (Lond.) ; F.R.S.; Director of Colombo museum, Ceylon, 26th Apr., 1902.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR HENRY.—Writer, crown lands office, W: Australia, 1879; 2nd elk., 1881; clk., gov.'s office, 1883; acted as priv. sec. to Sir Frederick Broome in 1883 and 1884; clk. to coun., May, 1884; ch. elk., P.O., 1889; ch. elk., premier's dept., 1900; elk. to exec. coun., 1902.

WILLIAMS, A. JAY.—Appkd. by F.O. Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 5th Jan., 1896; ast. sec. (new post), 20th Aug., 1897; ag. sec. to adminstr., May to Oct., 1897, and June, 1899, to Apr., 1901; sec. to adminstr., 1st Apr., 1901.

WILLIAMS, C. RIBY, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1857; supervisor of customs, G. C. Coll., 1884; on special mission to Aquamoo and Crepepe, 1886; dist. comsnr., 1887; in charge Salaga recruiting expdn.; comsnr. with Tavievo expeditionary force, and on special mission to King of Buem, 1888; on special mission to E. Wassaw, 1889; controller of customa, Dec., 1890; on special mission to Krobo, 1891; ch. comsnr., Anglo-German bindry, comsnr., 1892; treasur., Nov., 1896; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1897.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY CHAMPNEYS.—Ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; grad. with hns., 1897; priv. sec., Barbados, 1898; attached to high comsnr.'s staff, Cape Town and Johannesburg, 1900-01; elk. to sec., O.R.C., admin., Feb., 1901; sec. for permits, Jan., 1902; elk. to col. sec., June, 1902; ch. elk. and editor, Agricultural Journal, July, 1903, and sec. agric. bd., Oct., 1913; also ag. regiser. of brands; sec. to dep. of agric., 1st July, 1904.

WILLIAMS, SIR HARTLEY, KT. BACH. (1894).—B. 1844; second son of Sir E. Eyre Williams, a former judge of Victoria; ed. at Repton and Trin. Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1865; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1867, and immediately went to Australia and commenced practice; judge of the sup. ct. of Victoria, 1881, and is now the sen. puisne judge; was a vice-pres. of the Victorian free educ. league; and is pres. of the Victorian league for promoting the opening of museums, etc., on Sundays; ret. on pension, 1896.

WILLIAMS, JAMES ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1856; messenger and copyist, secretariat, Lagos, Dec., 1871; elk. and keeper of debtors' prison, June, 1875; 2nd elk. gov.'s office, G. Coast, Dec., 1875; ch. elk., 1877; elk., leg. coun., 1886; dist. comsnr., Dec., 1890.
WILLIAMS, JOHN, F.C.S.—Asst. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1893.

WILLIAMS, JOSHUA STRANGE, M.A., LL.M.—Ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st class in law tripos, 1868; jun. optime and chancellor's agal studies medallist, 1869; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1869; dist. land-regiar., Canterbury, N.Z., 1871; regist.-gen. of land under the Land Transfer Act, 1879; puisne judge, sup. ct., Otago, Mar., 1875.
WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clk. to res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1902.

WILLIAMS, RALPH CHAMPNEYS, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; ed. at Kossel; explored parts of Patagonia and Terra del Fuego, 1873-4; and Cent. Africa, 1883-4; head of civil intell. dept., Bechuanaland expdn., 1884; author of "The British Lion in Bechuanaland"; Br. consul. offr., S. African republic, 1887; Br. agt. there (with letter of credence), 1888; col. treas., and mem. of sanitary comsnr., Gibraltar, 1890; also contract offr., 1891; and also capt. of the port, 1895; recd. certif. and med. from the Italian govt. for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia," Mar. 17, 1891; col. sec., Barbados, 1897; res. comsnr., Bechuanaland Protec., 1901.
WILLIAMS, W. — Draughtsman, P.W.D., Cyprus, Apr., 1880; ast. supt. of wks., 1891; 2nd div. engr., Dec., 1898; ag. 1st div. engr., Mar., 1903; apptd. ditto, Jan., 1904; ag. dir. pub. wks., May, 1906.
WILLIAMS, W. R.—Med. offr., Dist. C., Dominica, Jan., 1881.
WILLIAMSON, G. ALEXANDER.—M.A., M.B., M.D., Aberd. univ.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1895.

WILLIAMSON, VICTOR ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1882).—Ed. at Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford, of which he is M.A.; barrister-at-law of the In. Tem., and goes the N.E. circuit; one of the royal comsnrs. who in 1872 and 1873 inquired into the treatment of Indian imigrts. in Mauritius; served for more than two years in Fiji without remuneration as chmn. of comsnr. for settling titles to land; was mem. of exec. and legis. coun., 1879-81; sec. to roy. comsn. on London bd. of wks., 1888.

WILLIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER, M.A.—Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; held for 3 yrs. from 1890 the Frank Stuart studentship for botan. research; was sen. astt. to regius prof. of botany, Glasgow Univ., and lecturer in botany, Queen Margaret's Coll., Glasgow; dir. of royal bot. gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1896; on special duty to Fed. Malay States, Mar., 1904; author of "A Manual Dictionary of Flowering Plants and Ferns," and numerous scientific and economic papers; editor of "Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Peradeniya," and of "Circulars and Agricultural Journal of Royal Botanical Gardens, Ceylon."

WILMOT, ROBERT DUNCAN.—Mem. of exec. coun., N. Brunswick, from 1851 to 1854, and from 1856 to 1857; again in his own govt. from 1866 until the union; surrv.-gen. from 1851 to 1854; prov. sec. from 1856 to 1857; mayor of St. John, 1849; deleg. to confederate coun. of trade, Quebec, 1868; and to the col. cocece, for the union of the B.N.A. cols., London, 1866-7; comsnr. on behalf of Canada to the Philadelphia Exhibn., 1875; sat for St. John (city and co.) in N. B. assem. from 1846 until 1861, and from 1855 until the union; called to the senate, May, 1867; speaker of the senate, 1878: lieut.-gov., N. B., 1880 to 1885.
WILSON, A.—Engr. in charge of ports and harbours, and immigr. agt., Victoria, July, 1882.

WILSON, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1895).—B. 1838; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond; home civ. serv., 1856-61; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, 1861-6; special comsnr. to report upon the salmon fisheries of N.B.; capt. and adjt. 1st York militia, N.B., 1863; also at camp of instruction, 1865-6; major, St. John volr. batt. when on service on the American frontier, 1866; priv. sec. to gov. of Trinidad, 1866-9; supt. of prisons, 1869-70; stip. mag. E. dist. of St. George, 1870-8; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain and W. dist. of St. George, Sept., 1873-4; comsnr. of the N. prov. and sub-intendant of crown lands, 1878 to 1897; mem. of exec. coun., 1894; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, various dates from Apr., 1875, to Jan., 1897; raised and organised Trinidad volr. force, 1879 to 1890; col. comdg. L.I. volrs., 1890-7, and is now hon. col.; gov. of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1897; ret. May, 1903.
WILSON, GEORGE, C.B. (1899).—Sub-comsnr., Uganda Prot., 30th Aug., 1894; dep. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1902.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang.

WILSON, HENRY FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry comsnr., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriage Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal asst. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal asst. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 1903.

WILSON, JOHN PLOWRIGHT.—Entd. serv. of Uganda Prot. from Imperial Br. E. Africa Co. as asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1893; collr., 1st Aug., 1895; sub-comsnr., 29th July, 1899; I. B. E. A. Co.'s medal, Uganda, 1891-2; East and Central Africa medal, Uganda, 1897-8.

WILSON, LESLIE ORME.—R.M.L.I.; joined Royal Marines as 2nd lieut., 1st Feb., 1890; lieut., 1st Jan., 1896; capt., 1st Apr., 1901; served in H.M.S. "Hibernia" and H.M.S. "Australia"; asst. instructor of musketry, Gravesend, 1901; served with naval brigade in South Africa, 1899-1900 (ment. in desp., D.S.O., medal with five clasps); A.D.C. to gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th July, 1903.

WILSON, T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the G.P.O., Apr., 1882; transf. to the C.O., Dec., 1883; asst. registr., July, 1896; higher grade, Jan., 1898.

WILSON, T. R.—Ck. to comsnr. of crown lands, co. of Bourke, Victoria, Dec., 1852; ch. clk. med. dept., Mar., 1855; sec. to cent. board of health, Aug., 1865; ch. ck. ch. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; under-sec. Victoria, 1881; J.P., 1883; hon. mem., Br. Med. Assoc., 1890.

WILSON, HON. WALTER HORATIO, C.M.G., M.L.C., Queensland.—Born Ruabon, N. Wales, 1839; ed. Ellesmere, Shrop.; arrived in Victoria, 1853; solr. sup. ct. 1863; admitted in Queensland, 1865; called to legis. coun., 1885; postmr.-gen. in ministry of Sir W. Griffith; leadership of legis. coun. without portfolio in the Griffith-McIlwraith govt., Aug., 1890; postmr.-gen. and sec. of pub. instru., May, 1893; continued as such in the Nelson ministry, to Oct., 1894; acted as atty.-gen., Mar., 1897, to Jan., 1898; retained position of postmr.-gen. in the premiership of the Hon. T. J. Byrnes; min. of just. and postmr.-gen. in the ministry of the Hon. J. R. Dickson; subsequently min. of educn.

WINDHAM, WM.—Ck., res. comsnr.'s office, Zulu native reserve, Apr., 1883; studt. interp., native affairs office, Natal, Mar., 1884; ck. and registr. to special comsnr. for Zulu affairs, Oct., 1885; ck. and interp. to res. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, June, 1887; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1889; ck., exec. coun., Natal, Sept., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Natal, Oct., 1889; sec. for Zululand, Dec., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Zululand, Feb., 1890; gov't. sec., Zululand, Feb., 1894; asst. under-sec. for Zululand affairs, 1897; registr. of deeds and registr.-gen., Natal, Mar., 1898; (compiled abstract of opinions and court decisions upon transactions in deeds office, 1878-1888); sec. to native affairs dept., Transvaal, 23rd Sept., 1901.

WINGFIELD, SIR EDWARD, K.C.B. (1899), C.B. (1889).—B. 1834; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford, B.C.L. and M.A. (Oxon); called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, 10th June, 1859; went the home circuit; apptd. asst. under-S. of S. for Cols., 19th July, 1878; permanent under-S. of S., 1st Mar., 1897, to 28th Feb., 1900; sec. to Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1897-1900.

WINGFIELD, MAURICE E.—Ed. at Winchester and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1896; sec. to royal coman., hut tax, S. Leone, 1898; ag. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., and mem. ex. and leg. couns., Falkland Is., 1899-1900; priv. sec. to gov., of Queensland, 1902; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 1903.

WINFIELD, JOHN, M.A. (Oxon) (Worcester Coll.)—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1896; ag. Queen's advocate, July, 1898, to May, 1899; rlyv. comsnr., Aro, Jan.-July, 1900; ag. atty.-gen., July-Sept., 1900; atty.-gen., So. Nig., Dec., 1900; compiled laws of So. Nig., 1900-1901; puisne judge, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. just., June, 1905.
WINSTON, J. H.—Sub-inigr. of roads, Dominica, Mar., 1874; inspr. of roads and ranger of crown lands, Sept., 1882; dist. gov't. offr.

WINTER, SIR FRANCIS PRATT, KT. (1900), C.M.G.(1892).—B. 1848; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, 1887-88; ch. judicial offr. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Br. N. Guinea, 1888; ag. admstr., Sept., 1894, to June, 1895, and Oct., 1898, to Mar., 1899; ret., 1903.

WINTER, SIR J. S., K.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.-B. 1845; speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1877-8; mem. exec. coun., Feb., 1879; solr.-gen., Oct., 1882; atty.-gen., 1886; agt. for Newfoundland at fishery confce., Washington, 1887; resig. office of atty.-gen., 1889; puisne judge, 1893; resig. 1896; prime min., 1897-1900; atty.-gen., 1897; mem. of delegn. to imperial govt. re French treaties and other matters, 1898; min. of just. and mem. of treasy. bd., 1898; high comsnr. to internat. confce. between Gt. Brit. and U.S.A. at Quebec and Washington, 1898.

WISE, ALFRED GASCOYNE, LL.B.—Ed. at Repton, at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1878; ck. to ch. just. at Hong Kong, 1882 (resig., 1883); pol. mag., Jan., 1884; ag. registr. of sup. ct., official trustee, registr. of companies and land offr., Mar., 1885; registr., sup. ct., 1892; ag. puisne judge, Apr., 1889, to Sept., 1890; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1895; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1895; ag. ch. just., June, 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to June, 1902; chairman, squatter's board, Aug., 1902.
WISE, Bernhard Ringrove.—Atty.-gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1887, to Feb., 1888.
WITHERS, A. K.—Ed. at Bedford schl.; served in survey dept. under Govt. of Egypt; confd. clk. to admstr. of Gambia, 1897.

WITTEENOOM, Hon. Sir E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901.

WODEHOUSE, Clarence B.—Entd. Sandhurst Coll., 1875; gazetted to 77th Regt., 1876; resigned coman., 1880; loco commdt. of police, Cyprus, 1st Jan., 1894; ag. comanr., Limassol, 12th July, 1894; ditto, Papho, 1st Apr., 1896; comanr., Papho, 7th June, 1895.

WODEHOUSE, H. E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1845; ed. Repton Schl.; Hong Kong cadet, 1867; student interp., 1869; sup. of Chinese pol., 1870; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1875; pol. mag., 1880; hon. sec. to the local coman. in connection with the Indian and Col. Exhibn., and special comanr. in London, 1886; mem. exec. coun., May, 1895; retired.

WODEHOUSE, P. P. J.—Ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; in charge of census, Dec., 1900; asst. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab pol., 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902.

WOLFE, E. D. C.—Tonbridge Schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langs., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge Schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langs., tripos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; registr. land ct., Oct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1902; ag. asst. registr.-gen., Nov., 1902; sec. to pub. wks. inquiry coman., 1902; collr. of crown rent, New Territory, Feb., 1903; registr. land ct., Mar., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and asst. supt. of pol., 1903-4; collr. of crown rent, N.T., 1904.

WOLFERTSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPE.—Ed. Eton, and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith, Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr. Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and registr. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903.

WOLFF, Rt. Hon. Sir Henry Drummond, P.C. (1885), G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1878); K.C.B. (1879); K.C.M.G. (1862).—B. 1830; entered foreign service in 1846; was attached, 1852, to H.M.'s legation at Florence, where he served for a short time as ag. chargé d'affaires; attached, 1866, to the Earl of Westmorland's special mission to Brussels; asst. priv. secretary to the Earl of Malmesbury, Feb., 1858, S. of S. for foreign affairs; in Nov., priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. Sir E. B. Lytton, S. of S. for cols.; C.M.G. in Apr., 1859, and hon. king-at-arms of the Order of St. M. and St. G.; public sec. to the lord high comanr. of the Ionian Islands, June, 1859; in the same year a mem. of a coman. to inquire into the state of pub. depts.; in 1860, mem. of a coman. to inquire and report on state of Ionian finances; represented Ionian govt. at the internat. statistical congress, London, 1860; was apptd., 1861, by the Ionian govt., vice-pres. of a coman. to organise, in 1862, an exhibit. of the works of Ionian art and industry, and in the same year mem. of a coman. to inquire into and prepare a project of law for regulating system of public instruction in the Ionian Is.; comanr. for the Ionian Is. internat. exhibn., 1862; unsuccessfully contested Christchurch, 1868; elected for that borough, 1874; apptd. in 1878 to be H.M.'s comanr. on the European coman., for the organisation of E. Roumelia; mem. for Portsmouth, 1880 to 1885; was apptd. envoy extraord. and min. plenip. to the Sultan on a special mission, with particular reference to affairs of Egypt, 3rd Aug., 1886; high comanr. in Egypt, 1885-87; min. at Teheran, 1888; ambassador Madrid, 1892-1900.

WOLLASTON, H. N. P., I.S.O. (1903), LL.D., J.P.—B. 1846; apptd. to dept. of trade and customs (after exam.), 1863; dep. regar. of shipping, 1886; grad., with honours, in law, Melb. Univ., 1884; called to bar same year; promoted to be ch. clk., customs dept., 1889; is also paid counsel to marine board of Victoria; apptd. permanent head of Victorian dept. of trade and customs, 1891; apptd. comp. gen. of commonwealth dept. of trade and customs, and permanent head, 1901.

WOLSELEY, Field-Marshal Viscount (created 1885), Sir Garnet Joseph Wolseley, G.C.B. (1880), K.C.M.G. (1870), G.C.M.G. and K.C.B. (1874), Baron (1882), K.P. (1885).—B. 1833; ensign, Mar., 1852; capt., Jan., 1855; major, 90th foot, Mar., 1858; lieut.-col., Apr., 1859; col., June, 1863; maj.-gen., 1874; lieut.-gen., Apr., 1878; gen., 1882; field-marshal, 1896; served with 80th foot in the Burmese war of 1852-53, severely wounded (medal); served with distinction in the Crimea and taking of Sebastopol, with the 90th L.I., and was severely wounded in a sortie, after which he received the Legion of Honour, the 6th class of the Medjidie etc.; was also at the siege and capture of Lucknow and defence of Alumbagh, and numerous actions in Oudh, when he was made brevet lieut.-col.; served in China on the staff of the qrtmr.-gen. throughout the Chinese campaign of 1860, for which he also received a medal and two clasps: dep. qrtmr.-gen. in Canada, Oct., 1867; asst. adjt.-gen. at headqrs., 1871; apptd. for special service on the G. Coast in connection with the Ashantee war, Aug., 1873; brought the Ashantee war to a successful conclusion, and received the thanks of the House of Lords and the House of Commons for his services; was made a G.C.M.G. and a K.C.B., and a major-gen.; and inspr.-gen. of auxiliary forces, Apr., 1874; specially apptd. to administer temporarily the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Sept., 1876; acted as vice-pres. of the London S. African confe., Aug., 1876; mem. of the Indian coun., 1876; comanr. and comdr.-in-ch. Cyprus, July, 1878; gov. and comdr.-in-ch. Natal, and of the Transvaal, and comdr. of the forces in S. Africa, May, 1879, and high comanr. for S. E. Africa; qrtmr.-gen., Apr., 1880; adjt.-gen., Mar., 1882; commanded the forces in Egypt, with the temporary rank of gen., in 1882, and received the thanks of both houses of parlmt., and a peerage; and from the Khedive of Egypt the Grand Order of the Osmanieh; commanded the Nile expedit. and other operations in Egypt and the Soudan for the relief of Gen. Gordon, 1884-5; received the thanks of both houses of parl.: was raised to the rank of viscount, and made K.P.;
WOODS, W. W.—Ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and Oxford, B.A. (1901); clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Somerset House, 2nd Sept., 1901; astt. auditor, G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902.

WOODWARD, LIONEL MABBOT.—Ed. at Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 1st class honours, classical tripus; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1892; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam; in Tamil, July, 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Nov., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag. and astt. Indian imigrn. agt., Singapore, July, 1892; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1893; sheriff and dep. regist. sup. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. offr. assignee, S. Stltms., and regist. of deeds, Apr., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. gen. dist. offr., Prov. Well., Oct., 1900; astt. regist., supreme court, Penang, May, 1902; ag. solr.-gen., May, 1903, dep. pub. proscr., Mar., 1904; seconded for spec. serv. in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., May, 1905.

WRAGG, SIR W. T., KT. BACH. (1891), M.A., D.C.L., Oxon (1894).—B., 1842; open classical exhibn., Linc. Coll., Oxon, Apr., 1863, and open classical scholarship at Worcester Coll., May, 1863; B.A., 1stclass honours, 1867; mem. of the In. Tem., Apr., 1866; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1868; comsnr., or requests, Harrispattu, July, 1868; in charge of the office of the dir. of pub. instruction, Mar., 1869; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1869; pol. mag., Panwila, 1869; pol. mag. of Gampola, Pussellawa, and Nawalapitya, 1871; dist. judge, Chilaw, June, 1872; called to the bar by the In. Tem., Jan., 1879; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1879; ditto, Badulla, 1879; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, July, 1883; pres. of comsn. to inquire into the working of the Ind. imigrn. laws in Natal, 1885-7; pres. of special judicial comsn. for trial of Zulu chiefs, 1888-9; ret., 1898.

WRAGGE, CLEMENT L., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc.—Born 1852; ed. at Uttoxeter gram. schl., and in London as a solr.; went to Adelaide in 1876, and was engaged in the survr.-gen.'s dept. on surveys in the Flinders Ranges and the Murray Scrub; gathered a large collection of specimens in ethnography, conchology, geology, etc., which he presented to the town of Stafford, where it is known as the Wragge museum; devoted his attention to meteorology, and established three observatories in the Churnet Valley and moorlands of N. Staffs., chiefly to investigate problems in climatology; in 1881-2 established and worked the first observatory on summit of Ben Nevis, 4,406 feet high, and a station in connection at Fort William; in Jan., 1884, he established the Torrens Observatory at Wakerville, near Adelaide, and in Oct., another on the summit of Mount Lofty, S. Australia; apptd. meteor. observer of Queensland, Jan., 1887.

WRAY, CECIL, F.G.S., M.R.P.S.—Local corres., Anth. inst.; entered pub. wks. dept., Perak, Oct., 1881; ag. ch. olk. of treasy., May, 1882; astt. to mag. and collr., Kinta dist., 1882; ag. collr. and mag. B. Padang, 1886; collr. and mag., Batang Padang dist., 1887; ag. supt., Lower Perak, Aug., 1889; ag. mag. and collr., Kinta, Apr., 1892; mag. and collr., Krian, June, 1892; ag. dist. mag., Lower Perak, Dec., 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1896; ag. sen. mag., Perak, Apr., 1897; ag. chief mag., Selangor, Sept., 1897; ch. mag., Selangor, Apr., 1899; dist. mag., Kinta, Jan., 1900; dist. offr., Kinta, Jan., 1903; ag. BRITISH RES., Pahang, 3rd Mar., 1904; British Res., Pahang, 14th Sept., 1904.

WRAY, CHAS. JAMES HILL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Surgeon, imigr. service, Queensland, 1876; health offr., Brisbane, 1879; med. offr., penal estabt., St. Helens, and boys' reformatory, Lytton, and visiting surg. benevolent asylum, Dunwich, 1879; inspr. of orphanages, 1882; sec. to central bd. of health, 1884.

WRAY, LEONARD, I.S.O. (1903), M.I.E.E., F.Z.S., M.R.P.S., cor. M.P.S., local corres. A.I., A.M.A.—B. 1862; entered pub. wks. dept., Perak govt. service, June, 1881; supt., Govt. Hill Garden, Larut, Jan., 1882; and curator, Perak state museum, Jan., 1883; also state geologist, Jan., 1890; dir. of museums, F.M.S., Mar., 1904.

WRENFORDSLEY, SIR HENRY THOMAS, KNT. (1883).—Ed. in France, and at Trim Coll., Dub.; was a mem. of the old Norfolk circuit; dep. cty. ct. judge for the metropol. dists. of Marylebone, Brompton, and Brentford, 1876; 2nd puisane judge, Mauritius, 1877; procureur and advoc.-gen., 1878; ch. just., W. Australia, 1880; represtd. col. at intercoln. confce. of the Australian cols., held at Sydney, 1881; ch. just. of Fiji and ch. judicial comsnr. of the W. Pacific, Oct., 1882-3; admst. govt. of W. Australia from Feb. to June, 1883; acted for some time as judge, sup. ct., Tasmania; puisane judge, Victoria, 1888; ch. just., Leeward Is., Oct., 1891; retired, 1901.
WRIGHT, ARTHUR.—Supt., printing dept., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1893.
WRIGHT, ERIC BLACKWOOD.—Barr.-at-law, Inner Temp.; ch. just., Seychelles, 13th May, 1905.

WRIGHT, G. H. BATESON.—Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1875; Denyer theological schol.; Ken- nicott Hebrew schol., 1876; Houghton Syriac prize; Pusey and Ellerton Hebrew schol., 1877; M.A., 1878; D.D., 1891; apptd. headmr. Queen's College, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1881; ag. inspr. of schls., Feb. to Dec., 1883; author of "Critical Translation of Book of Job"; "Was Israel ever in Egypt?" and various school books; J.P., 1886.

WRIGHT, HON. J. ARTHUR, C.E., M.I.C.E. C.E.I.—Comsnr. of rly., dir. of pub. wks., and engnr.-in-ch., mem. of legis. coun. and exec. coun. of W. Australia, 1885-90; temporary collr. of internal and cust. revenue at Albany, W. Aust., Nov., 1897; ag. govt. res., Albany, Jan., 1898; govt. res., Apr., 1899.
WRIGHT, R. T.—Inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, Mar., 1883; inspr. of pol., prot. of imigrts., and sec. to the bd. of health, Grenada, Apr., 1885; harbourm., Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1892.
WRIGHT, WM.—Ck. to pub. trustee, S. Aust., 1885; ch. ck. to pub. trustee, and ch. ck. succession duties dept., 1891; pub. trustee and curator of convicts' estates, 1903.

WRIGHTSON, WALSH, C.M.G. (1900), M.I.C.E.—B. 1852; served articles with Chas. Fowler, Leeds; asst. to boro' engineer, Leeds, 1872; dist. engineer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1875; seconded for serv. on Nanuoya Rly. extensions, 1883; spec. serv. to restore Kalawewa irrigation tank, 1884; on successful completion, promoted prov. engr., N.W. Prov., 1888; asst. to dir. pub. wks., 1892; prov. engr., E. Prov., 1894; dir. pub. wks., Trinidad, and offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1895; apptd. mem. ex. coun., June, 1895; res. engr., harbour, water and sewerage wks., 1896.

WRIXON, THE HON. SIR HENRY JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1839; atty.-gen., Victoria, Feb., 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; mem. of Federation Confce., 1891; represented Victoria at Col. Confce., 1894; author of "Socialism: Notes on a Political Tour."
WROUGHTON, LEWIS.—Sub.-inspr., Basuto-land mtd. pol., 1884; ag. asst. comsnr., Quilting, 1890; asst. comsnr., Basutoiland, 1891; ag. govt. sec., 1901; govt. sec., 1902.

WURTELE, J. S. C., D.C.L.—Treas. of prov. of Quebec, Canada, 1882-3; speaker Quebec legis. assem., 1884 to 1886; judge of the sup. ct., 1886; offr. of the legion of honour, 1882; offr. of public instruction 1881; judge, ct. of appeal, Quebec, Sept., 1892.

WYATT, ROBT.—Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1895; B. Hond. pol., June, 1896; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Corosal, 26th Apr. to 11th July, 1905; ag. supt. pol., 14th July, 1905; J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899.
WYLIE, THOS. ALEXANDER.—Asst. traffic supt., Ceylon rlyws., 17th May, 1888; acctnt., 11th May, 1894; now asst. gen. magr. and acctnt.
WYNNE, C. O., L.R.C.P. and S.E.—Dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., May, 1903.

YOUl, SIR JAMES ARNDELL, K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1810; a respected Australian colonist who, after a series of patient and very interesting experiments, succeeded in introducing salmon and trout into the rivers in Tasmania, and also made the first shipment of salmon ova to Otago, N.Z.; received a vote of thanks from the prov. govt., accompanied by a handsome silver vase; political agt. for Tasmania, 1861-3; was seven years hon. sec. and treas. to the Australian Assoc., which succeeded in prevailing on the Impl. Govt. to establish a mail service to Australia, via Red Sea, and in getting the Australian sovereign made legal tender throughout the Br. dominions; ag. agrt.-gen. for Tasmania, Feb. to Oct., 1881.

YOUNG, ALFRED KARNEY.—B.A., Magd. Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1895-7; dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, 1898; registr. gen., June, 1898; ag. suptdt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903.

YOUNG, CAPT. ARTHUR H., C.M.G. (1897).—B.1854; late 27th Inniskilling; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdtr., mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; asst. comsnr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comsnr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admst. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan.-Apl., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connection with relief work, Nov., 1902.

YOUNG, ROBT. HEYDEN.—Sub-survr., survey dept., S. Sttlnts., 28th Jan., 1878; computer and draftsman, Malacca, 1st May, 1881; dist. survr., Oct., 1882; ch. survr., 12th July, 1888; senior survr., Jan., 1891; in charge of re-survey of Singapore, 4th May, 1903.

YOUNG, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govs. of B. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. govt. sec., B. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt. Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901.
